3.2.4.2 [Governing Attribute Declaration and Governing Type Definition](#sec-gov-attribute)
3.2.4.3 [Schema-Validity Assessment (Attribute)](#sec-cvs-assess-attr)
3.2.5 [Attribute Declaration Information Set Contributions](#psvi_ad)
3.2.5.1 [Assessment Outcome (Attribute)](#sec-sic-a-outcome)
3.2.5.2 [Validation Failure (Attribute)](#sec-sic-attr-error-code)
3.2.5.3 [Attribute Declaration](#sec-sic-attr-decl)
3.2.5.4 [Attribute Validated by Type](#sec-sic-attrType)
3.2.6 [Constraints on Attribute Declaration Schema Components](#coss-attribute)
3.2.6.1 [Attribute Declaration Properties Correct](#sec-a-props-correct)
3.2.6.2 [Simple Default Valid](#sec-cos-valid-simple-default)
3.2.6.3 [xmlns Not Allowed](#sec-no-xmlns)
3.2.6.4 [xsi: Not Allowed](#sec-no-xsi)
3.2.7 [Built-in Attribute Declarations](#builtin-ads)
3.2.7.1 [xsi:type](#sec-sc-xsi.type)
3.2.7.2 [xsi:nil](#sec-sc-xsi.nil)
3.2.7.3 [xsi:schemaLocation](#sec-sc-xsi.schemaLocation)
3.2.7.4 [xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation](#sec-sc-xsi.noNamespaceSchemaLocation)
Attribute declarations provide for:

- Local [·validation·](#key-vn) of attribute information item values using a simple type definition;
- Specifying default or fixed values for attribute information items.
Example
```
<xs:attribute name="age" type="xs:positiveInteger" use="required"/>
```

The XML representation of an attribute declaration.
#### <a id="Attribute_Declaration_details"></a>3.2.1 The Attribute Declaration Schema Component

The attribute declaration schema component has the following properties:

Schema Component: <a id="ad"></a>Attribute Declaration, a kind of [Annotated Component](#ac)<a id="ad-annotations"></a>{annotations} A sequence of [Annotation](#a) components. <a id="ad-name"></a>{name} An xs:NCName value. Required.<a id="ad-target_namespace"></a>{target namespace} An xs:anyURI value. Optional.<a id="ad-type_definition"></a>{type definition} A [Simple Type Definition](#std) component. Required.<a id="ad-scope"></a>{scope} A [Scope](#sc_a) property record. Required.<a id="ad-value_constraint"></a>{value constraint} A [Value Constraint](#vc_a) property record. Optional.<a id="ad-inheritable"></a>{inheritable} An xs:boolean value. Required.Property Record: <a id="sc_a"></a>Scope<a id="sc_a-variety"></a>{variety} One of {global, local}. Required.<a id="sc_a-parent"></a>{parent} Either a [Complex Type Definition](#ctd) or a [Attribute Group Definition](#agd). Required if [{variety}](#sc_a-variety) is ***local***, otherwise must be [·absent·](#key-null)Property Record: <a id="vc_a"></a>Value Constraint<a id="vc_a-variety"></a>{variety} One of {default, fixed}. Required.<a id="vc_a-value"></a>{value} An [·actual value·](#key-vv). Required. <a id="vc_a-lexical_form"></a>{lexical form} A character string. Required.
The [{name}](#ad-name) property must match the local part of the names of attributes being [·validated·](#key-vn).

The value of each attribute validated must conform to the supplied [{type definition}](#ad-type_definition).

A [·non-absent·](#key-null) value of the [{target namespace}](#ad-target_namespace) property provides for [·validation·](#key-vn) of namespace-qualified attribute information items (which must be explicitly prefixed in the character-level form of XML documents). [·Absent·](#key-null) values of [{target namespace}](#ad-target_namespace)[·validate·](#key-vn) unqualified (unprefixed) items.

For an attribute declaration *A*, if *A*.[{scope}](#ad-scope).[{variety}](#sc_a-variety) = ***global***, then *A*is available for use throughout the schema. If *A*.[{scope}](#ad-scope).[{variety}](#sc_a-variety) = ***local***, then *A*is available for use only within (the [Complex Type Definition](#ctd) or [Attribute Group Definition](#agd)) *A*.[{scope}](#ad-scope).[{parent}](#sc_a-parent).

The [{value constraint}](#ad-value_constraint) property reproduces the functions of XML default and `#FIXED`attribute values. A [{variety}](#vc_a-variety) of ***default***specifies that the attribute is to appear unconditionally in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi), with [{value}](#vc_a-value) and [{lexical form}](#vc_a-lexical_form) used whenever the attribute is not actually present; ***fixed***indicates that the attribute value if present must be equal or identical to [{value}](#vc_a-value), and if absent receives [{value}](#vc_a-value) and [{lexical form}](#vc_a-lexical_form) as for ***default***. Note that it is *values*that are checked, not strings, and that the test is for either equality or identity.

See [Annotations (§3.15)](#cAnnotations) for information on the role of the [{annotations}](#ad-annotations) property.

**Note:**A more complete and formal presentation of the semantics of [{name}](#ad-name), [{target namespace}](#ad-target_namespace) and [{value constraint}](#ad-value_constraint) is provided in conjunction with other aspects of complex type [·validation·](#key-vn) (see [Element Locally Valid (Complex Type) (§3.4.4.2)](#cvc-complex-type).)
[[XML Infoset]](#ref-xmlinfo) distinguishes attributes with names such as `xmlns`or `xmlns:xsl`from ordinary attributes, identifying them as [[namespace attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element). Accordingly, it is unnecessary and in fact not possible for schemas to contain attribute declarations corresponding to such namespace declarations, see [xmlns Not Allowed (§3.2.6.3)](#no-xmlns). No means is provided in this specification to supply a default value for a namespace declaration.

#### <a id="declare-attribute"></a>3.2.2 XML Representation of Attribute Declaration Schema Components

The XML representation for an attribute declaration schema component is an [<attribute>](#element-attribute) element information item. It specifies a simple type definition for an attribute either by reference or explicitly, and may provide default information. The correspondences between the properties of the information item after the appropriate [·pre-processing·](#key-pre-processing) and the properties of the component are given in this section.

Attribute declarations can appear at the top level of a schema document, or within complex type definitions, either as complete (local) declarations, or by reference to top-level declarations, or within attribute group definitions. For complete declarations, top-level or local, the `type`attribute is used when the declaration can use a built-in or pre-declared simple type definition. Otherwise an anonymous [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) is provided inline. When no simple type definition is referenced or provided, the default is [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType), which imposes no constraints at all.

XML Representation Summary: `attribute`Element Information Item<a id="element-attribute"></a><attribute
default = [string](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#string)
fixed = [string](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#string)
form = (*qualified*| *unqualified*)
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
name = [NCName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NCName)
ref = [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
targetNamespace = [anyURI](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anyURI)
type = [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
use = (*optional*| *prohibited*| *required*) : optional
inheritable = [boolean](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#boolean)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, [simpleType](#element-simpleType)?)
</attribute>
An [<attribute>](#element-attribute) element maps to an attribute declaration, and allows the type definition of that declaration to be specified either by reference or by explicit inclusion.

Top-level [<attribute>](#element-attribute) elements (i.e. those which appear within the schema document as children of [<schema>](#element-schema) elements) produce ***global***attribute declarations; [<attribute>](#element-attribute)s within [<attributeGroup>](#element-attributeGroup) or [<complexType>](#element-complexType) produce either attribute uses which contain ***global***attribute declarations (if there's a `ref`attribute) or local declarations (otherwise). For complete declarations, top-level or local, the `type`attribute is used when the declaration can use a built-in or user-defined global type definition. Otherwise an anonymous [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) is provided inline.

**Note:**Children of [<override>](#element-override) are not strictly speaking top-level declarations, but they will become top-level declarations if they override corresponding declarations in the [·target set·](#key-targetset) of their parent. See [Overriding component definitions (<override>) (§4.2.5)](#override-schema) for details.
Attribute information items [·validated·](#key-vn) by a top-level declaration must be qualified with the [{target namespace}](#ad-target_namespace) of that declaration. If the [{target namespace}](#ad-target_namespace) is [·absent·](#key-null), the item must be unqualified. Control over whether attribute information items [·validated·](#key-vn) by a local declaration must be similarly qualified or not is provided by the `form`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), whose default is provided by the `attributeFormDefault`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) on the enclosing [<schema>](#element-schema), via its determination of [{target namespace}](#ad-target_namespace).

The names for top-level attribute declarations are in their own [·symbol space·](#key-symbolSpace). The names of locally-scoped attribute declarations reside in symbol spaces local to the type definition which contains them.

The following sections specify several sets of XML mapping rules which apply in different circumstances.
- If the [<attribute>](#element-attribute) element information item has [<schema>](#element-schema) as its parent, then it maps to a global [Attribute Declaration](#ad) as described in [Mapping Rules for Global Attribute Declarations (§3.2.2.1)](#dcl.att.global).
- If the [<attribute>](#element-attribute) element information item has [<complexType>](#element-complexType) or [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) as an ancestor, and the `ref`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is absent, and the `use`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is not `"prohibited"`, then it maps both to an [Attribute Declaration](#ad) and to an [Attribute Use](#au) component, as described in [Mapping Rules for Local Attribute Declarations (§3.2.2.2)](#dcl.att.local). On [Attribute Use](#au) components, see [Attribute Uses (§3.5)](#cAttributeUse).
- If the [<attribute>](#element-attribute) element information item has [<complexType>](#element-complexType) or [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) as an ancestor, and the `ref`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is [·present·](#key-nonnull), and the `use`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is not `"prohibited"`, then it maps to an [Attribute Use](#au) component, as described in [Mapping Rules for References to Top-level Attribute Declarations (§3.2.2.3)](#ref.att.local).
- If the [<attribute>](#element-attribute) element information item has `use='prohibited'`, then it does not map to, or correspond to, any schema component at all. **Note:**The `use`attribute is not allowed on top-level [<attribute>](#element-attribute) elements, so this can only happen with [<attribute>](#element-attribute) elements appearing within a [<complexType>](#element-complexType) or [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) element.
##### <a id="dcl.att.global"></a>3.2.2.1 Mapping Rules for Global Attribute Declarations

<a id="xr.att3"></a>
If the [<attribute>](#element-attribute) element information item has [<schema>](#element-schema) as its parent, the corresponding schema component is as follows:

See below.

#### <a id="sec-src-attdecl"></a>3.2.3 Constraints on XML Representations of Attribute Declarations

<a id="src-attribute"></a>**Schema Representation Constraint: Attribute Declaration Representation OK**
In addition to the conditions imposed on [<attribute>](#element-attribute) element information items by the schema for schema documents, **all**of the following also apply:1 `default`and `fixed`must not both be present.2 If `default`and `use`are both present, `use`must have the [·actual value·](#key-vv)`optional`.3 If the item's parent is not [<schema>](#element-schema), then **all**of the following must be true:3.1 One of `ref`or `name`is present, but not both.3.2 If `ref`is present, then all of [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType), `form`and `type`are absent.4 The `type`attribute and a [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) child element must not both be present.5 If `fixed`and `use`are both present, `use`must not have the [·actual value·](#key-vv)`prohibited`. 6<a id="att-with-ns"></a> If the `targetNamespace`attribute is present then **all**of the following must be true:6.1 The `name`attribute is present. 6.2 The `form`attribute is absent. 6.3 If the ancestor [<schema>](#element-schema) does not have a `targetNamespace`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) or its [·actual value·](#key-vv) is different from the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of `targetNamespace`of [<attribute>](#element-attribute), then **all**of the following are true:6.3.1 [<attribute>](#element-attribute) has [<complexType>](#element-complexType) as an ancestor 6.3.2<a id="att-with-ns-must-be-old"></a> There is a [<restriction>](#element-complexContent..restriction) ancestor between the [<attribute>](#element-attribute) and the nearest [<complexType>](#element-complexType) ancestor, and the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `base`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of [<restriction>](#element-complexContent..restriction) does not [·match·](#key-en-match) the name of [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType).
#### <a id="sec-cvc-attdecl"></a>3.2.4 Attribute Declaration Validation Rules

##### <a id="sec-cvc-attribute"></a>3.2.4.1 Attribute Locally Valid

Informally, an attribute in an XML instance is locally [·valid·](#key-vn) against an attribute declaration if and only if (a) the name of the attribute matches the name of the declaration, (b) after whitespace normalization its [·normalized value·](#key-nv) is locally valid against the type declared for the attribute, and (c) the attribute obeys any relevant value constraint. Additionally, for `xsi:type`, it is required that the type named by the attribute be present in the schema. A logical prerequisite for checking the local validity of an attribute against an attribute declaration is that the attribute declaration itself and the type definition it identifies both be present in the schema.

Local validity of attributes is tested as part of schema-validity [·assessment·](#key-va) of attributes (and of the elements on which they occur), and the result of the test is exposed in the [[validity]](#a-validity) property of the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi).

A more formal statement is given in the following constraint.

<a id="cvc-attribute"></a>**Validation Rule: Attribute Locally Valid**
For an attribute information item *A*to be locally [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to an attribute declaration *D***all**of the following must be true:1<a id="c-a1"></a>*D*is not [·absent·](#key-null) (see [Missing Sub-components (§5.3)](#conformance-missing) for how this can fail to be the case) and *D*and *A*have the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname).2<a id="c-a2"></a>*D*.[{type definition}](#ad-type_definition) is not [·absent·](#key-null).3<a id="c-sva"></a>*A*'s [·initial value·](#key-iv) is locally [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to *D*.[{type definition}](#ad-type_definition) as per [String Valid (§3.16.4)](#cvc-simple-type).4 If *D*.[{value constraint}](#ad-value_constraint) is present and *D*.[{value constraint}](#ad-value_constraint).[{variety}](#vc_a-variety) = ***fixed***, then *A*'s [·actual value·](#key-vv) is equal or identical to *D*.[{value constraint}](#ad-value_constraint).[{value}](#vc_a-value).5 If *D*is the built-in declaration for `xsi:type`([Attribute Declaration for the 'type' attribute (§3.2.7.1)](#xsi.type)), then *A*'s [·actual value·](#key-vv)[·resolves·](#src-resolve) to a type definition.
##### <a id="sec-gov-attribute"></a>3.2.4.2 Governing Attribute Declaration and Governing Type Definition

<a id="key-governing-ad"></a>[Definition:] In a given schema-validity [·assessment·](#key-va) episode, the [·governing·](#key-governing) declaration of an attribute (its **governing attribute declaration**) is the first of the following which applies: 1<a id="c-ad"></a>A declaration which was stipulated by the processor (see [Assessing Schema-Validity (§5.2)](#validation_outcome)).2 Its [·context-determined declaration·](#key-dd);3<a id="c-adbyr"></a>A declaration [·resolved·](#cvc-resolve-instance) to by its [[local name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute) and [[namespace name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute), provided the attribute is not [·skipped·](#key-skipped) and the processor has not stipulated a type definition at the start of [·assessment·](#key-va). If none of these applies, the attribute has no [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad) (or, in equivalent words, the [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad) is [·absent·](#key-null)). **Note:**<a id="anchor11764e"></a> As a consequence, unless [·skipped·](#key-skipped) or stipulated otherwise, attributes named `xsi:type`, `xsi:nil`, `xsi:schemaLocation`, or `xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation`are always governed by their corresponding built-in declarations (see [Built-in Attribute Declarations (§3.2.7)](#builtin-ads)). <a id="key-governing-type-att"></a>[Definition:] The **governing type definition**of an attribute, in a given schema-validity [·assessment·](#key-va) episode, is the first of the following which applies: 1 A type definition stipulated by the processor (see [Assessing Schema-Validity (§5.2)](#validation_outcome)).2 The [{type definition}](#ad-type_definition) of the [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad). If neither of these applies, there is no [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-att) (or, in equivalent words, it is [·absent·](#key-null)).
##### <a id="sec-cvs-assess-attr"></a>3.2.4.3 Schema-Validity Assessment (Attribute)

Schema-validity assessment of an attribute information item involves identifying its [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad) and checking its local validity against the declaration. If the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-att) is not present in the schema, then assessment is necessarily incomplete.

<a id="cvc-assess-attr"></a>**Validation Rule: Schema-Validity Assessment (Attribute)**
The schema-validity assessment of an attribute information item depends on its local [·validation·](#key-vn) alone.For an attribute information item's schema-validity to have been assessed **all**of the following must be true:1<a id="c-caa-ad"></a>A [·non-absent·](#key-null) attribute declaration is known for it, namely its [·governing·](#key-governing) declaration. 2 Its local [·validity·](#key-vn) with respect to that declaration has been evaluated as per [Attribute Locally Valid (§3.2.4.1)](#cvc-attribute).3 Both clause [1](#c-a1) and clause [2](#c-a2) of [Attribute Locally Valid (§3.2.4.1)](#cvc-attribute) are satisfied.
<a id="key-svaa"></a>[Definition:]For attribute information items, there is no difference between assessment and strict assessment, so the attribute information item has been **strictly assessed**if and only if its schema-validity has been assessed.

#### <a id="psvi_ad"></a>3.2.5 Attribute Declaration Information Set Contributions

##### <a id="sec-sic-a-outcome"></a>3.2.5.1 Assessment Outcome (Attribute)

<a id="sic-a-outcome"></a>**Schema Information Set Contribution: Assessment Outcome (Attribute)**
If the schema-validity of an attribute information item has been assessed as per [Schema-Validity Assessment (Attribute) (§3.2.4.3)](#cvc-assess-attr), then in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) it has properties as follows:PSVI Contributions forattribute information items
**<a id="a-validation_context"></a>[validation context]**
: The nearest ancestor element information item with a [[schema information]](#e-schema_information) property.

**<a id="a-validity"></a>[validity]**
: The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **it was [·strictly assessed·](#key-svaa), **then **the appropriate **case**among the following:1.1 **If **it was locally [·valid·](#key-vn) as defined by [Attribute Locally Valid (§3.2.4.1)](#cvc-attribute), **then *****valid***;1.2 **otherwise *****invalid***.2 **otherwise *****notKnown***.

**<a id="a-validation_attempted"></a>[validation attempted]**
: The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **it was [·strictly assessed·](#key-svaa), **then *****full***;2 **otherwise *****none***.

**<a id="a-schema_specified"></a>[schema specified]**
: ***infoset***. See [Attribute Default Value (§3.4.5.1)](#sic-attrDefault) for the other possible value.

##### <a id="sec-sic-attr-error-code"></a>3.2.5.2 Validation Failure (Attribute)

<a id="sic-attr-error-code"></a>**Schema Information Set Contribution: Validation Failure (Attribute)**
If and only if the local [·validity·](#key-vn), as defined by [Attribute Locally Valid (§3.2.4.1)](#cvc-attribute) above, of an attribute information item has been assessed, then in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) the item has a property:PSVI Contributions forattribute information items
**<a id="a-schema_error_code"></a>[schema error code]**
: The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the item is [·invalid·](#key-vn), **then **a list. Applications wishing to provide information as to the reason(s) for the [·validation·](#key-vn) failure are encouraged to record one or more error codes (see [Outcome Tabulations (normative) (§B)](#outcomes)) herein.2 **otherwise **[·absent·](#key-null).

##### <a id="sec-sic-attr-decl"></a>3.2.5.3 Attribute Declaration

<a id="sic-attr-decl"></a>**Schema Information Set Contribution: Attribute Declaration**
If and only if a [·governing·](#key-governing) declaration is known for an attribute information item then in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) the attribute information item has a property:PSVI Contributions forattribute information items
**<a id="a-declaration"></a>[attribute declaration]**
: An [·item isomorphic·](#key-iso) to the [·governing·](#key-governing) declaration component itself.

**<a id="a-schema_default"></a>[schema default]**
: If the attribute information item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to an [Attribute Use](#au) then the [{lexical form}](#vc_a-lexical_form) of the [·effective value constraint·](#key-evc), otherwise the [{lexical form}](#vc_a-lexical_form) of the declaration's [{value constraint}](#ad-value_constraint).

##### <a id="sec-sic-attrType"></a>3.2.5.4 Attribute Validated by Type

<a id="sic-attrType"></a>**Schema Information Set Contribution: Attribute Validated by Type**
If and only if a [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-att) is known for an attribute information item, then in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) the attribute information item has the properties:PSVI Contributions forattribute information items
**<a id="a-schema_normalized_value"></a>[schema normalized value]**
: If the attribute's [·normalized value·](#key-nv) is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-att), then the [·normalized value·](#key-nv) as [·validated·](#key-vn), otherwise [·absent·](#key-null).

**<a id="a-schema_actual_value"></a>[schema actual value]**
: If the [[schema normalized value]](#a-schema_normalized_value) is not [·absent·](#key-null), then the corresponding [·actual value·](#key-vv); otherwise [·absent·](#key-null).

**<a id="a-type_definition"></a>[type definition]**
: An [·item isomorphic·](#key-iso) to the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-att) component.

**<a id="a-type_definition_type"></a>[type definition type]**
: ***simple***.

**<a id="a-type_definition_namespace"></a>[type definition namespace]**
: The [{target namespace}](#std-target_namespace) of the [·type definition·](#td).

**<a id="a-type_definition_anonymous"></a>[type definition anonymous]**
: ***true***if the [{name}](#std-name) of the [·type definition·](#td) is [·absent·](#key-null), otherwise ***false***.

**<a id="a-type_definition_name"></a>[type definition name]**
: The [{name}](#std-name) of the [·type definition·](#td), if the [{name}](#std-name) is not [·absent·](#key-null). If the [·type definition·](#td)'s [{name}](#std-name) property is [·absent·](#key-null), then schema processors may, but need not, provide a value which uniquely identifies this type definition among those with the same target namespace. It is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) whether a processor provides a name for such a type definition. If a processor does provide a value in this situation, the choice of what value to use is [·implementation-dependent·](#key-impl-dep).

**Note:**The [type definition type], [type definition namespace], [type definition name], and [type definition anonymous] properties are redundant with the [type definition] property; they are defined for the convenience of implementations which wish to expose those specific properties but not the entire type definition. <a id="anchor10662c"></a>If the attribute's [·initial value·](#key-iv) is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-att) as defined by [String Valid (§3.16.4)](#cvc-simple-type) and the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-att) has [{variety}](#std-variety)***union***, then there are four additional properties:PSVI Contributions forattribute information items
**<a id="a-member_type_definition"></a>[member type definition]**
: an [·item isomorphic·](#key-iso) to the [·validating type·](#key-vtype) of the [[schema actual value]](#a-schema_actual_value).

**<a id="a-member_type_definition_namespace"></a>[member type definition namespace]**
: The [{target namespace}](#std-target_namespace) of the [·validating type·](#key-vtype).

**<a id="a-member_type_definition_anonymous"></a>[member type definition anonymous]**
: ***true***if the [{name}](#std-name) of the [·validating type·](#key-vtype) is [·absent·](#key-null), otherwise ***false***.

**<a id="a-member_type_definition_name"></a>[member type definition name]**
: The [{name}](#std-name) of the [·validating type·](#key-vtype), if it is not [·absent·](#key-null). If it is [·absent·](#key-null), schema processors may, but need not, provide a value unique to the definition. It is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) whether a processor provides a name for such a type definition. If a processor does provide a value in this situation, the choice of what value to use is [·implementation-dependent·](#key-impl-dep).

The first ([·item isomorphic·](#key-iso)) alternative above is provided for applications such as query processors which need access to the full range of details about an item's [·assessment·](#key-va), for example the type hierarchy; the second, for lighter-weight processors for whom representing the significant parts of the type hierarchy as information items might be a significant burden. If **all**of the following are true:1 the attribute's [·normalized value·](#key-nv) is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-att); 2 **One**of the following is true:2.1 the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-att) has [{variety}](#std-variety) = ***list***; 2.2 the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-att) has [{variety}](#std-variety) = ***union***and the [·validating type·](#key-vtype) of the [[schema actual value]](#a-schema_actual_value) has [{variety}](#std-variety) = ***list***; 3 the [{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition) of the list type (from the previous clause) has [{variety}](#std-variety) = ***union***; then there is an additional property: PSVI Contributions forattribute information items
**<a id="a-member_type_definitions"></a>[member type definitions]**
: a sequence of [Simple Type Definition](#std) components, with the same length as the [[schema actual value]](#a-schema_actual_value), each one an [·item isomorphic·](#key-iso) to the [·validating type·](#key-vtype) of the corresponding item in the [[schema actual value]](#a-schema_actual_value).

See also [Attribute Default Value (§3.4.5.1)](#sic-attrDefault), [Match Information (§3.4.5.2)](#sic-match-info) and [Schema Information (§3.17.5.1)](#sic-schema), which describe other information set contributions related to attribute information items.

#### <a id="coss-attribute"></a>3.2.6 Constraints on Attribute Declaration Schema Components

All attribute declarations (see [Attribute Declarations (§3.2)](#cAttribute_Declarations)) must satisfy the following constraints.

##### <a id="sec-a-props-correct"></a>3.2.6.1 Attribute Declaration Properties Correct

<a id="a-props-correct"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Attribute Declaration Properties Correct**
**All**of the following must be true:1 The values of the properties of an attribute declaration are as described in the property tableau in [The Attribute Declaration Schema Component (§3.2.1)](#Attribute_Declaration_details), modulo the impact of [Missing Sub-components (§5.3)](#conformance-missing).2 if there is a [{value constraint}](#ad-value_constraint), then it is a valid default with respect to the [{type definition}](#ad-type_definition) as defined in [Simple Default Valid (§3.2.6.2)](#cos-valid-simple-default). **Note:**The use of [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID) and related types together with value constraints goes beyond what is possible with XML DTDs, and should be avoided if compatibility with DTDs is desired.
##### <a id="sec-cos-valid-simple-default"></a>3.2.6.2 Simple Default Valid

<a id="cos-valid-simple-default"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Simple Default Valid**
For a Value Constraint *V*to be a valid default with respect to a [Simple Type Definition](#std)*T***all**of the following must be true:1 *V*.[{lexical form}](#vc_a-lexical_form) is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to *T*as defined by [Datatype Valid](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#cvc-datatype-valid) in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2).2 *V*.[{lexical form}](#vc_a-lexical_form) maps to *V*.[{value}](#vc_a-value) in the value space of *T*.
##### <a id="sec-no-xmlns"></a>3.2.6.3 xmlns Not Allowed

<a id="no-xmlns"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: `xmlns`Not Allowed**
The [{name}](#ad-name) of an attribute declaration must not match `xmlns`.**Note:**The [{name}](#ad-name) of an attribute is an [·NCName·](#gloss-NCName), which implicitly prohibits attribute declarations of the form `xmlns:*`.
##### <a id="sec-no-xsi"></a>3.2.6.4 xsi: Not Allowed

<a id="no-xsi"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: `xsi:`Not Allowed**
The [{target namespace}](#ad-target_namespace) of an attribute declaration, whether local or top-level, must not match `http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance`(unless it is one of the four built-in declarations given in the next section).**Note:**This reinforces the special status of these attributes, so that they not only *need*not be declared to be allowed in instances, but in consequence of the rule just given must not be declared. **Note:**It is legal for [Attribute Use](#au)s that refer to `xsi:`attributes to specify default or fixed value constraints (e.g. in a component corresponding to a schema document construct of the form `<xs:attribute ref="xsi:type" default="xs:integer"/>`), but the practice is not recommended; including such attribute uses will tend to mislead readers of the schema document, because the attribute uses would have no effect; see [Element Locally Valid (Complex Type) (§3.4.4.2)](#cvc-complex-type) and [Attribute Default Value (§3.4.5.1)](#sic-attrDefault) for details.
#### <a id="builtin-ads"></a>3.2.7 Built-in Attribute Declarations

There are four attribute declarations present in every schema by definition:

##### <a id="sec-sc-xsi.type"></a>3.2.7.1 xsi:type

The `xsi:type`attribute is used to signal use of a type other than the declared type of an element. See [xsi:type (§2.7.1)](#xsi_type).

<a id="xsi.type"></a>Attribute Declaration for the 'type' attribute**Property****Value**[{name}](#ad-name)`type`[{target namespace}](#ad-target_namespace)`http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance`[{type definition}](#ad-type_definition)The built-in [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) simple type definition[{scope}](#ad-scope)A [Scope](#sc_a) as follows: **Property****Value**[{variety}](#sc_a-variety)***global***[{parent}](#sc_a-parent)[·absent·](#key-null)[{value constraint}](#ad-value_constraint)[·absent·](#key-null)[{annotations}](#ad-annotations)[· the empty sequence ·](#key-null)
##### <a id="sec-sc-xsi.nil"></a>3.2.7.2 xsi:nil

The `xsi:nil`attribute is used to signal that an element's content is "nil" (or "null"). See [xsi:nil (§2.7.2)](#xsi_nil).

<a id="xsi.nil"></a>Attribute Declaration for the 'nil' attribute**Property****Value**[{name}](#ad-name)`nil`[{target namespace}](#ad-target_namespace)`http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance`[{type definition}](#ad-type_definition)The built-in [boolean](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#boolean) simple type definition[{scope}](#ad-scope)A [Scope](#sc_a) as follows: **Property****Value**[{variety}](#sc_a-variety)***global***[{parent}](#sc_a-parent)[·absent·](#key-null)[{value constraint}](#ad-value_constraint)[·absent·](#key-null)[{annotations}](#ad-annotations)[· the empty sequence ·](#key-null)
##### <a id="sec-sc-xsi.schemaLocation"></a>3.2.7.3 xsi:schemaLocation

The `xsi:schemaLocation`attribute is used to signal possible locations of relevant schema documents. See [xsi:schemaLocation, xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation (§2.7.3)](#xsi_schemaLocation).

<a id="xsi.schemaLocation"></a>Attribute Declaration for the 'schemaLocation' attribute**Property****Value**[{name}](#ad-name)`schemaLocation`[{target namespace}](#ad-target_namespace)`http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance`[{type definition}](#ad-type_definition)An anonymous simple type definition, as follows: **Property****Value**[{name}](#std-name)[·absent·](#key-null)[{target namespace}](#std-target_namespace)`http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance`[{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition)The built in [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType)[{facets}](#std-facets)[·absent·](#key-null)[{variety}](#std-variety)***list***[{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition)The built-in [anyURI](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anyURI) simple type definition[{annotations}](#std-annotations)[· the empty sequence ·](#key-null)[{scope}](#ad-scope)A [Scope](#sc_a) as follows: **Property****Value**[{variety}](#sc_a-variety)***global***[{parent}](#sc_a-parent)[·absent·](#key-null)[{value constraint}](#ad-value_constraint)[·absent·](#key-null)[{annotations}](#ad-annotations)[· the empty sequence ·](#key-null)
##### <a id="sec-sc-xsi.noNamespaceSchemaLocation"></a>3.2.7.4 xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation

The `xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation`attribute is used to signal possible locations of relevant schema documents. See [xsi:schemaLocation, xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation (§2.7.3)](#xsi_schemaLocation).

<a id="xsi.noNamespaceSchemaLocation"></a>Attribute Declaration for the 'noNamespaceSchemaLocation' attribute**Property****Value**[{name}](#ad-name)`noNamespaceSchemaLocation`[{target namespace}](#ad-target_namespace)`http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance`[{type definition}](#ad-type_definition)The built-in [anyURI](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anyURI) simple type definition[{scope}](#ad-scope)A [Scope](#sc_a) as follows: **Property****Value**[{variety}](#sc_a-variety)***global***[{parent}](#sc_a-parent)[·absent·](#key-null)[{value constraint}](#ad-value_constraint)[·absent·](#key-null)[{annotations}](#ad-annotations)[· the empty sequence ·](#key-null)
### <a id="cElement_Declarations"></a>3.3 Element Declarations

3.3.1 [The Element Declaration Schema Component](#Element_Declaration_details)
3.3.2 [XML Representation of Element Declaration Schema Components](#declare-element)
3.3.2.1 [Common Mapping Rules for Element Declarations](#dcl.elt.common)
3.3.2.2 [Mapping Rules for Top-Level Element Declarations](#dcl.elt.global)
3.3.2.3 [Mapping Rules for Local Element Declarations](#dcl.elt.local)
3.3.2.4 [References to Top-Level Element Declarations](#ref.elt.global)
3.3.2.5 [Examples of Element Declarations](#dcl.elt.exx)
3.3.3 [Constraints on XML Representations of Element Declarations](#sec-src-element)
3.3.4 [Element Declaration Validation Rules](#eldec_vr)
3.3.4.1 [Selected and Instance-specified Type Definitions](#sec-sistd)
3.3.4.2 [Type Override and Valid Substitutability](#sec-type-override)
3.3.4.3 [Element Locally Valid (Element)](#sec-cvc-elt)
3.3.4.4 [Element Locally Valid (Type)](#sec-cvc-type)
3.3.4.5 [Validation Root Valid (ID/IDREF)](#sec-cvc-id)
3.3.4.6 [Schema-Validity Assessment (Element)](#sec-cvc-assess-elt)
3.3.5 [Element Declaration Information Set Contributions](#edisc)
3.3.5.1 [Assessment Outcome (Element)](#sec-sic-e-outcome)
3.3.5.2 [Validation Failure (Element)](#sec-sic-elt-error-code)
3.3.5.3 [Element Declaration](#sec-sic-elt-decl)
3.3.5.4 [Element Validated by Type](#sec-sic-eltType)
3.3.5.5 [Element Default Value](#sec-sic-eltDefault)
3.3.5.6 [Inherited Attributes](#Inherited_attributes)
3.3.6 [Constraints on Element Declaration Schema Components](#coss-element)
3.3.6.1 [Element Declaration Properties Correct](#sec-e-props-correct)
3.3.6.2 [Element Default Valid (Immediate)](#sec-cos-valid-default)
3.3.6.3 [Substitution Group OK (Transitive)](#sec-cos-equiv-derived-ok-rec)
3.3.6.4 [Substitution Group](#sec-cos-equiv-class)
Element declarations provide for:

- Local [·validation·](#key-vn) of element information item values using a type definition;
- Specifying default or fixed values for element information items;
- Establishing uniquenesses and reference constraint relationships among the values of related elements and attributes;
- Controlling the substitutability of elements through the mechanism of [·element substitution groups·](#key-equivalenceClass).
Example
```
<xs:element name="PurchaseOrder" type="PurchaseOrderType"/>

<xs:element name="gift">
 <xs:complexType>
  <xs:sequence>
   <xs:element name="birthday" type="xs:date"/>
   <xs:element ref="PurchaseOrder"/>
  </xs:sequence>
 </xs:complexType>
</xs:element>
```

XML representations of several different types of element declaration
#### <a id="Element_Declaration_details"></a>3.3.1 The Element Declaration Schema Component

The element declaration schema component has the following properties:

Schema Component: <a id="ed"></a>, a kind of [Term](#t)<a id="ed-annotations"></a>{annotations} A sequence of [Annotation](#a) components. <a id="ed-name"></a>{name} An xs:NCName value. Required.<a id="ed-target_namespace"></a>{target namespace} An xs:anyURI value. Optional.<a id="ed-type_definition"></a>{type definition} A [Type Definition](#td) component. Required.<a id="ed-type_table"></a>{type table} A [Type Table](#tt) property record. Optional.<a id="ed-scope"></a>{scope} A [Scope](#sc_e) property record. Required.<a id="ed-value_constraint"></a>{value constraint} A [Value Constraint](#vc_e) property record. Optional.<a id="ed-nillable"></a>{nillable} An xs:boolean value. Required.<a id="ed-identity-constraint_definitions"></a>{identity-constraint definitions} A set of [Identity-Constraint Definition](#icd) components. <a id="ed-substitution_group_affiliations"></a>{substitution group affiliations} A set of [Element Declaration](#ed) components. <a id="ed-substitution_group_exclusions"></a>{substitution group exclusions} A subset of {extension, restriction}.<a id="ed-disallowed_substitutions"></a>{disallowed substitutions}
A subset of {*substitution*, *extension*, *restriction*}.

<a id="ed-abstract"></a>{abstract} An xs:boolean value. Required.Property Record: <a id="tt"></a>Type Table<a id="tt-alternatives"></a>{alternatives} A sequence of [Type Alternative](#tac) components. <a id="tt-default_type_definition"></a>{default type definition} A [Type Alternative](#tac) component. Required.Property Record: <a id="sc_e"></a>Scope<a id="sc_e-variety"></a>{variety} One of {global, local}. Required.<a id="sc_e-parent"></a>{parent} Either a [Complex Type Definition](#ctd) or a [Model Group Definition](#mgd). Required if [{variety}](#sc_e-variety) is ***local***, otherwise must be [·absent·](#key-null)Property Record: <a id="vc_e"></a>Value Constraint<a id="vc_e-variety"></a>{variety} One of {default, fixed}. Required.<a id="vc_e-value"></a>{value} An [·actual value·](#key-vv). Required. <a id="vc_e-lexical_form"></a>{lexical form} A character string. Required.
The [{name}](#ed-name) property must match the local part of the names of element information items being [·validated·](#key-vn).

For an element declaration *E*, if *E*.[{scope}](#ed-scope).[{variety}](#sc_e-variety) = ***global***, then *E*is available for use throughout the schema. If *E*.[{scope}](#ed-scope).[{variety}](#sc_e-variety) = ***local***, then *E*is available for use only within (the [Complex Type Definition](#ctd) or [Model Group Definition](#mgd)) *E*.[{scope}](#ed-scope).[{parent}](#sc_e-parent).

A [·non-absent·](#key-null) value of the [{target namespace}](#ed-target_namespace) property provides for [·validation·](#key-vn) of namespace-qualified element information items. [·Absent·](#key-null) values of [{target namespace}](#ed-target_namespace)[·validate·](#key-vn) unqualified items.

An element information item is normally required to satisfy the [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition). For such an item, schema information set contributions appropriate to the [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) are added to the corresponding element information item in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi). The type definition against which an element information item is validated (its [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem)) can be different from the declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition). The [{type table}](#ed-type_table) property of an [Element Declaration](#ed), which governs conditional type assignment, and the `xsi:type`attribute of an element information item (see [xsi:type (§2.7.1)](#xsi_type)) can cause the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) and the declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) to be different.

If [{nillable}](#ed-nillable) is ***true***, then an element with no text or element content can be [·valid·](#key-vn) despite a [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) which would otherwise require content, if it carries the attribute `xsi:nil`with the value `true`(see [xsi:nil (§2.7.2)](#xsi_nil)). Formal details of element [·validation·](#key-vn) are described in [Element Locally Valid (Element) (§3.3.4.3)](#cvc-elt).

<a id="key-nilled"></a>[Definition:]An element information item *E*is **nilled**with respect to some element declaration *D*if and only if **all**of the following are true:1 *E*has `xsi:nil`= ***true***. 2 *D*.[{nillable}](#ed-nillable) = ***true***. If *E*is said to be [·nilled·](#key-nilled) without the identity of *D*being clear from the context, then *D*is assumed to be *E*'s [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed).
[{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint) establishes a default or fixed value for an element. If a [{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint) with [{variety}](#vc_e-variety) = ***default***is present, and if the element being [·validated·](#key-vn) is empty, then for purposes of calculating the [[schema normalized value]](#e-schema_normalized_value) and other contributions to the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) the element is treated as if [{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint).[{lexical form}](#vc_e-lexical_form) was used as the content of the element. If ***fixed***is specified, then the element's content must either be empty, in which case ***fixed***behaves as ***default***, or its value must be equal or identical to [{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint).[{value}](#vc_e-value).

**Note:**When a default value is supplied and used, as described in the second sentence of the preceding paragraph, the default value is used to calculate the [[schema normalized value]](#e-schema_normalized_value), etc., but the actual content of the element is not changed: the element contained no character information items in the input information set, and it contains none in the PSVI.**Note:**The provision of defaults for elements goes beyond what is possible in XML DTDs, and does not exactly correspond to defaults for attributes. In particular, an element with a non-empty [{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint) whose simple type definition includes the empty string in its lexical space will nonetheless never receive that value, because the [{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint) will override it.
[{identity-constraint definitions}](#s-identity-constraint_definitions) express constraints establishing uniquenesses and reference relationships among the values of related elements and attributes. See [Identity-constraint Definitions (§3.11)](#cIdentity-constraint_Definitions).

The [{substitution group affiliations}](#ed-substitution_group_affiliations) property of an element declaration indicates which [·substitution groups·](#key-eq), if any, it can potentially be a member of. Potential membership is transitive but not symmetric; an element declaration is a potential member of any group named in its [{substitution group affiliations}](#ed-substitution_group_affiliations), and also of any group of which any entry in its [{substitution group affiliations}](#ed-substitution_group_affiliations) is a potential member. Actual membership may be blocked by the effects of [{substitution group exclusions}](#ed-substitution_group_exclusions) or [{disallowed substitutions}](#ed-disallowed_substitutions), see below.

An empty [{substitution group exclusions}](#ed-substitution_group_exclusions) allows a declaration to be named in the [{substitution group affiliations}](#ed-substitution_group_affiliations) of other element declarations having the same declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) or some type [·derived·](#key-derived) therefrom. The explicit values of [{substitution group exclusions}](#ed-substitution_group_exclusions), ***extension***or ***restriction***, rule out element declarations having types whose derivation from [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) involves any ***extension***steps, or ***restriction***steps, respectively.

The supplied values for [{disallowed substitutions}](#ed-disallowed_substitutions) determine whether an element declaration appearing in a [·content model·](#key-contentModel) will be prevented from additionally [·validating·](#key-vn) elements (a) with an [xsi:type (§2.7.1)](#xsi_type) that identifies an ***extension***or ***restriction***of the type of the declared element, and/or (b) from [·validating·](#key-vn) elements which are in the [·substitution group·](#key-eq) headed by the declared element. If [{disallowed substitutions}](#ed-disallowed_substitutions) is empty, then all [·derived·](#key-derived) types and [·substitution group·](#key-eq) members are allowed.

Element declarations for which [{abstract}](#ed-abstract) is ***true***can appear in content models only when substitution is allowed; such declarations must not themselves ever be used to [·validate·](#key-vn) element content.

See [Annotations (§3.15)](#cAnnotations) for information on the role of the [{annotations}](#ed-annotations) property.

#### <a id="declare-element"></a>3.3.2 XML Representation of Element Declaration Schema Components

The XML representation for an element declaration schema component is an [<element>](#element-element) element information item. It specifies a type definition for an element either by reference or explicitly, and may provide occurrence and default information. The correspondences between the properties of the information item after the appropriate [·pre-processing·](#key-pre-processing) and the properties of the component(s) it corresponds to are given in this section.

XML Representation Summary: `element`Element Information Item<a id="element-element"></a><element
abstract = [boolean](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#boolean): false
block = (*#all*| List of (*extension*| *restriction*| *substitution*))
default = [string](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#string)
final = (*#all*| List of (*extension*| *restriction*))
fixed = [string](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#string)
form = (*qualified*| *unqualified*)
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
maxOccurs = ([nonNegativeInteger](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#nonNegativeInteger) | *unbounded*)  : 1
minOccurs = [nonNegativeInteger](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#nonNegativeInteger): 1
name = [NCName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NCName)
nillable = [boolean](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#boolean): false
ref = [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
substitutionGroup = List of [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
targetNamespace = [anyURI](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anyURI)
type = [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, (([simpleType](#element-simpleType) | [complexType](#element-complexType))?, [alternative](#element-alternative)*, ([unique](#element-unique) | [key](#element-key) | [keyref](#element-keyref))*))
</element>
An [<element>](#element-element) element information item in a schema document maps to an element declaration and allows the type definition of that declaration to be specified either by reference or by explicit inclusion.

Top-level [<element>](#element-element) elements (i.e. those which appear within the schema document as children of [<schema>](#element-schema) elements) produce ***global***element declarations; [<element>](#element-element)s within [<group>](#element-group) or [<complexType>](#element-complexType) produce either particles which contain ***global***element declarations (if there's a `ref`attribute) or local declarations (otherwise). For complete declarations, top-level or local, the `type`attribute is used when the declaration can use a built-in or user-defined global type definition. Otherwise an anonymous [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) or [<complexType>](#element-complexType) is provided inline.

**Note:**Children of [<override>](#element-override) are not strictly speaking top-level declarations, but they will become top-level declarations if they override corresponding declarations in the [·target set·](#key-targetset) of their parent. See [Overriding component definitions (<override>) (§4.2.5)](#override-schema) for details.
Element information items [·validated·](#key-vn) by a top-level declaration must be qualified with the [{target namespace}](#ed-target_namespace) of that declaration. If the [{target namespace}](#ed-target_namespace) is [·absent·](#key-null), the item must be unqualified. Control over whether element information items [·validated·](#key-vn) by a local declaration must be similarly qualified or not is provided by the `form`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), whose default is provided by the `elementFormDefault`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) on the enclosing [<schema>](#element-schema), via its determination of [{target namespace}](#ed-target_namespace).

The names for top-level element declarations are in a separate [·symbol space·](#key-symbolSpace) from the symbol spaces for the names of type definitions, so there can (but need not be) a simple or complex type definition with the same name as a top-level element. The names of locally-scoped element declarations need not be unique and thus reside in no symbol space at all (but the element declarations are constrained by [Element Declarations Consistent (§3.8.6.3)](#cos-element-consistent)).

Note that the above allows for two levels of defaulting for unspecified type definitions. An [<element>](#element-element) with no referenced or included type definition will correspond to an element declaration which has the same type definition as the first substitution-group head named in the `substitutionGroup`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType). This has the important consequence that the minimum valid element declaration, that is, one with only a `name`attribute and no contents, is also (nearly) the most general, validating any combination of text and element content and allowing any attributes, and providing for recursive validation where possible.

See [XML Representation of Identity-constraint Definition Schema Components (§3.11.2)](#declare-key) for [<key>](#element-key), [<unique>](#element-unique) and [<keyref>](#element-keyref).

The following sections specify several sets of XML mapping rules which apply in different circumstances.
- If the [<element>](#element-element) element information item has [<schema>](#element-schema) as its parent, it maps to an [Element Declaration](#ed) using the mappings described in [Common Mapping Rules for Element Declarations (§3.3.2.1)](#dcl.elt.common) and [Mapping Rules for Top-Level Element Declarations (§3.3.2.2)](#dcl.elt.global).
- If the [<element>](#element-element) element information item has [<complexType>](#element-complexType) or [<group>](#element-group) as an ancestor, and the `ref`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is absent, and it does not have `minOccurs=maxOccurs=0`, then it maps both to a [Particle](#p), as described in [Mapping Rules for Local Element Declarations (§3.3.2.3)](#dcl.elt.local), and also to an [Element Declaration](#ed), using the mappings described in [Common Mapping Rules for Element Declarations (§3.3.2.1)](#dcl.elt.common) and [Mapping Rules for Local Element Declarations (§3.3.2.3)](#dcl.elt.local).
- If the [<element>](#element-element) element information item has [<complexType>](#element-complexType) or [<group>](#element-group) as an ancestor, and the `ref`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is present, and it does not have `minOccurs=maxOccurs=0`, then it maps to a [Particle](#p) as described in [References to Top-Level Element Declarations (§3.3.2.4)](#ref.elt.global).
- If the [<element>](#element-element) element information item has `minOccurs=maxOccurs=0`, then it maps to no component at all.**Note:**The `minOccurs`and `maxOccurs`attributes are not allowed on top-level [<element>](#element-element) elements, so in valid schema documents this will happen only when the [<element>](#element-element) element information item has [<complexType>](#element-complexType) or [<group>](#element-group) as an ancestor.
##### <a id="dcl.elt.common"></a>3.3.2.1 Common Mapping Rules for Element Declarations

The following mapping rules apply in all cases where an [<element>](#element-element) element maps to an [Element Declaration](#ed) component.

XML Mapping Summary for [Element Declaration](#Element_Declaration_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{name}](#ed-name)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `name`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).[{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) The first of the following that applies: 1 The type definition corresponding to the [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) or [<complexType>](#element-complexType) element information item in the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if either is present. 2 The type definition [·resolved·](#src-resolve) to by the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `type`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if it is present. 3 The declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) of the [Element Declaration](#ed)[·resolved·](#src-resolve) to by the first [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) in the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `substitutionGroup`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present. 4 [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType). [{type table}](#ed-type_table)A [Type Table](#tt) corresponding to the [<alternative>](#element-alternative) element information items among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if any, as follows, otherwise [·absent·](#key-null). **Property****Value**[{alternatives}](#tt-alternatives) A sequence of [Type Alternative](#tac)s, each corresponding, in order, to one of the [<alternative>](#element-alternative) elements which have a `test`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element). [{default type definition}](#tt-default_type_definition) Depends upon the final [<alternative>](#element-alternative) element among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element). If it has no `test`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), the final [<alternative>](#element-alternative) maps to the [{default type definition}](#tt-default_type_definition); if it does have a `test`attribute, it is covered by the rule for [{alternatives}](#tt-alternatives) and the [{default type definition}](#tt-default_type_definition) is taken from the declared type of the [Element Declaration](#ed). So the value of the [{default type definition}](#tt-default_type_definition) is given by the appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the [<alternative>](#element-alternative) has no `test`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), **then **a [Type Alternative](#tac) corresponding to the [<alternative>](#element-alternative).2 **otherwise **(the [<alternative>](#element-alternative) has a `test`) a [Type Alternative](#tac) with the following properties: **Property****Value**[{test}](#tac-test)[·absent·](#key-null).[{type definition}](#tac-type_definition)the [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) property of the parent [Element Declaration](#ed).[{annotations}](#tac-annotations)the empty sequence.[{nillable}](#ed-nillable)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `nillable`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise ***false***.[{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint)If there is a `default`or a `fixed`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), then a [Value Constraint](#vc_e) as follows, otherwise [·absent·](#key-null). <a id="l-std"></a>[Definition:]Use the name **effective simple type definition**for the declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition), if it is a simple type definition, or, if [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition).[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***simple***, for [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition).[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{simple type definition}](#ct-simple_type_definition), or else for the built-in [string](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#string) simple type definition).**Property****Value**[{variety}](#vc_e-variety)either ***default***or ***fixed***, as appropriate[{value}](#vc_e-value)the [·actual value·](#key-vv) (with respect to the [·effective simple type definition·](#l-std)) of the [[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)[{lexical form}](#vc_e-lexical_form)the [·normalized value·](#key-nv) (with respect to the [·effective simple type definition·](#l-std)) of the [[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)[{identity- constraint definitions}](#ed-identity-constraint_definitions)A set consisting of the identity-constraint-definitions corresponding to all the [<key>](#element-key), [<unique>](#element-unique) and [<keyref>](#element-keyref) element information items in the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if any, otherwise the empty set.[{substitution group affiliations}](#ed-substitution_group_affiliations)A set of the element declarations [·resolved·](#src-resolve) to by the items in the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `substitutionGroup`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise the empty set.[{disallowed substitutions}](#ed-disallowed_substitutions)A set depending on the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `block`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise on the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `blockDefault`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the ancestor [<schema>](#element-schema) element information item, if present, otherwise on the empty string. Call this the **EBV**(for effective block value). Then the value of this property is the appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the **EBV**is the empty string, **then **the empty set;2 **If **the **EBV**is `#all`, **then **`{`***extension***, ***restriction***, ***substitution***`}`;3 **otherwise **a set with members drawn from the set above, each being present or absent depending on whether the [·actual value·](#key-vv) (which is a list) contains an equivalently named item. **Note:**Although the `blockDefault`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of [<schema>](#element-schema)may include values other than ***extension***, ***restriction***or ***substitution***, those values are ignored in the determination of [{disallowed substitutions}](#ed-disallowed_substitutions) for element declarations (they *are*used elsewhere).[{substitution group exclusions}](#ed-substitution_group_exclusions)As for [{disallowed substitutions}](#ed-disallowed_substitutions) above, but using the `final`and `finalDefault`[[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) in place of the `block`and `blockDefault`[[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and with the relevant set being `{`***extension***, ***restriction***`}`.[{abstract}](#ed-abstract)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `abstract`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise ***false***.[{annotations}](#ed-annotations) The [·annotation mapping·](#key-am-one) of the [<element>](#element-element) element and any of its [<unique>](#element-unique), [<key>](#element-key) and [<keyref>](#element-keyref)[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) with a `ref`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), as defined in [XML Representation of Annotation Schema Components (§3.15.2)](#declare-annotation).
##### <a id="dcl.elt.global"></a>3.3.2.2 Mapping Rules for Top-Level Element Declarations

<a id="xr.elt4"></a>
If the [<element>](#element-element) element information item has [<schema>](#element-schema) as its parent, it maps to a global [Element Declaration](#ed), using the mapping given in [Common Mapping Rules for Element Declarations (§3.3.2.1)](#dcl.elt.common), supplemented by the following.

XML Mapping Summary for [Element Declaration](#Element_Declaration_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{target namespace}](#ed-target_namespace)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `targetNamespace`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the parent [<schema>](#element-schema) element information item, or [·absent·](#key-null) if there is none.[{scope}](#ed-scope)A [Scope](#sc_e) as follows **Property****Value**[{variety}](#sc_e-variety)***global***[{parent}](#sc_e-parent)[·absent·](#key-null)
##### <a id="dcl.elt.local"></a>3.3.2.3 Mapping Rules for Local Element Declarations

<a id="xr.elt6"></a>
If the [<element>](#element-element) element information item has [<complexType>](#element-complexType) or [<group>](#element-group) as an ancestor, and the `ref`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is absent, and it does not have `minOccurs=maxOccurs=0`, then it maps both to a [Particle](#p) and to a local [Element Declaration](#ed) which is the [{term}](#p-term) of that [Particle](#p). The [Particle](#p) is as follows:

XML Mapping Summary for [Particle](#Particle_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `minOccurs`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise `1`.[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs)***unbounded***, if the `maxOccurs`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) equals ***unbounded***, otherwise the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `maxOccurs`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise `1`.[{term}](#p-term)A (local) element declaration as given below.[{annotations}](#p-annotations) The same annotations as the [{annotations}](#ed-annotations) of the [{term}](#p-term).
<a id="xr.elt8"></a>
The [<element>](#element-element) element also maps to an element declaration using the mapping rules given in [Common Mapping Rules for Element Declarations (§3.3.2.1)](#dcl.elt.common), supplemented by those below:

XML Mapping Summary for [Element Declaration](#Element_Declaration_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{target namespace}](#ed-target_namespace) The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **`targetNamespace`is present , **then **its [·actual value·](#key-vv). 2 **If **`targetNamespace`is not present and **one**of the following is true2.1 `form`= `qualified`2.2 `form`is absent and the [<schema>](#element-schema) ancestor has `elementFormDefault`= `qualified`**then **the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `targetNamespace`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the ancestor [<schema>](#element-schema) element information item, or [·absent·](#key-null) if there is none. 3 **otherwise **[·absent·](#key-null). [{scope}](#ed-scope) A [Scope](#sc_e) as follows: **Property****Value**[{variety}](#sc_e-variety)***local***[{parent}](#sc_e-parent)If the [<element>](#element-element) element information item has [<complexType>](#element-complexType) as an ancestor, the [Complex Type Definition](#ctd) corresponding to that item, otherwise (the [<element>](#element-element) element information item is within a named [<group>](#element-group) element information item), the [Model Group Definition](#mgd) corresponding to that item.
##### <a id="ref.elt.global"></a>3.3.2.4 References to Top-Level Element Declarations

<a id="xr.elt10"></a>
If the [<element>](#element-element) element information item has [<complexType>](#element-complexType) or [<group>](#element-group) as an ancestor, and the `ref`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is present, and it does not have `minOccurs=maxOccurs=0`, then it maps to a [Particle](#p) as follows.

XML Mapping Summary for [Particle](#Particle_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `minOccurs`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise `1`.[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs)***unbounded***, if the `maxOccurs`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) equals ***unbounded***, otherwise the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `maxOccurs`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise `1`.[{term}](#p-term)The (top-level) element declaration [·resolved·](#src-resolve) to by the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `ref`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).[{annotations}](#p-annotations) The [·annotation mapping·](#key-am-one) of the [<element>](#element-element) element, as defined in [XML Representation of Annotation Schema Components (§3.15.2)](#declare-annotation).
##### <a id="dcl.elt.exx"></a>3.3.2.5 Examples of Element Declarations

Example
```
<xs:element name="unconstrained"/>

<xs:element name="emptyElt">
 <xs:complexType>
  <xs:attribute ...>. . .</xs:attribute>
 </xs:complexType>
</xs:element>

<xs:element name="contextOne">
 <xs:complexType>
  <xs:sequence>
   <xs:element name="myLocalElement" type="myFirstType"/>
   <xs:element ref="globalElement"/>
  </xs:sequence>
 </xs:complexType>
</xs:element>

<xs:element name="contextTwo">
 <xs:complexType>
  <xs:sequence>
   <xs:element name="myLocalElement" type="mySecondType"/>
   <xs:element ref="globalElement"/>
  </xs:sequence>
 </xs:complexType>
</xs:element>
```

The first example above declares an element whose type, by default, is [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType). The second uses an embedded anonymous complex type definition.The last two examples illustrate the use of local element declarations. Instances of `myLocalElement`within `contextOne`will be constrained by `myFirstType`, while those within `contextTwo`will be constrained by `mySecondType`. **Note:**The possibility that differing attribute declarations and/or content models would apply to elements with the same name in different contexts is an extension beyond the expressive power of a DTD in XML.Example
```
 <xs:complexType name="facet">
  <xs:complexContent>
   <xs:extension base="xs:annotated">
    <xs:attribute name="value" use="required"/>
   </xs:extension>
  </xs:complexContent>
 </xs:complexType>

 <xs:element name="facet" type="xs:facet" abstract="true"/>

 <xs:element name="encoding" substitutionGroup="xs:facet">
  <xs:complexType>
   <xs:complexContent>
    <xs:restriction base="xs:facet">
     <xs:sequence>
      <xs:element ref="annotation" minOccurs="0"/>
     </xs:sequence>
     <xs:attribute name="value" type="xs:encodings"/>
    </xs:restriction>
   </xs:complexContent>
  </xs:complexType>
 </xs:element>

 <xs:element name="period" substitutionGroup="xs:facet">
  <xs:complexType>
   <xs:complexContent>
    <xs:restriction base="xs:facet">
     <xs:sequence>
      <xs:element ref="annotation" minOccurs="0"/>
     </xs:sequence>
     <xs:attribute name="value" type="xs:duration"/>
    </xs:restriction>
   </xs:complexContent>
  </xs:complexType>
 </xs:element>

 <xs:complexType name="datatype">
  <xs:sequence>
   <xs:element ref="facet" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>
  </xs:sequence>
  <xs:attribute name="name" type="xs:NCName" use="optional"/>
  . . .
 </xs:complexType>
```

An example from a previous version of the schema for datatypes. The `facet`type is defined and the `facet`element is declared to use it. The `facet`element is abstract -- it's *only*defined to stand as the head for a [·substitution group·](#key-eq). Two further elements are declared, each a member of the `facet`[·substitution group·](#key-eq). Finally a type is defined which refers to `facet`, thereby allowing *either*`period`or `encoding`(or any other member of the group).ExampleThe following example illustrates conditional type assignment to an element, based on the value of one of the element's attributes. Each instance of the `message`element will be assigned either to type `messageType`or to a more specific type derived from it. The type `messageType`accepts any well-formed XML or character sequence as content, and carries a `kind`attribute which can be used to describe the kind or format of the message. The value of `kind`is either one of a few well known keywords or, failing that, any string.
```
<xs:complexType name="messageType" mixed="true">
 <xs:sequence>
  <xs:any processContents="skip" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>
 </xs:sequence>
 <xs:attribute name="kind">
  <xs:simpleType>
   <xs:union>
    <xs:simpleType>
     <xs:restriction base="xs:string">
      <xs:enumeration value="string"/>
      <xs:enumeration value="base64"/>
      <xs:enumeration value="binary"/>
      <xs:enumeration value="xml"/>
      <xs:enumeration value="XML"/>
     </xs:restriction>
    </xs:simpleType>
    <xs:simpleType>
     <xs:restriction base="xs:string"/>
    </xs:simpleType>
   </xs:union>
  </xs:simpleType>
 </xs:attribute>
 <xs:anyAttribute processContents="skip"/>
</xs:complexType>
```

Three restrictions of `messageType`are defined, each corresponding to one of the three well-known formats: `messageTypeString`for `kind="string"`, `messageTypeBase64`for `kind="base64"`and `kind="binary"`, and `messageTypeXML`for `kind="xml"`or `kind="XML"`.
```
<xs:complexType name="messageTypeString">
 <xs:simpleContent>
  <xs:restriction base="messageType">
   <xs:simpleType>
    <xs:restriction base="xs:string"/>
   </xs:simpleType>
  </xs:restriction>
 </xs:simpleContent>
</xs:complexType>

<xs:complexType name="messageTypeBase64">
 <xs:simpleContent>
  <xs:restriction base="messageType">
   <xs:simpleType>
    <xs:restriction base="xs:base64Binary"/>
   </xs:simpleType>
  </xs:restriction>
 </xs:simpleContent>
</xs:complexType>

<xs:complexType name="messageTypeXML">
 <xs:complexContent>
  <xs:restriction base="messageType">
   <xs:sequence>
    <xs:any processContents="strict"/>
   </xs:sequence>
  </xs:restriction>
 </xs:complexContent>
</xs:complexType>
```

<a id="anchor6013"></a> The `message`element itself uses `messageType`both as its declared type and as its default type, and uses `test`attributes on its [<alternative>](#element-alternative)[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) to assign the appropriate specialized message type to messages with the well known values for the `kind`attribute. Because the declared type and the default type are the same, the last [<alternative>](#element-alternative) (without the `test`attribute) can be omitted.
```
<xs:element name="message" type="messageType">
  <xs:alternative test="@kind='string'" type="messageTypeString"/>
  <xs:alternative test="@kind='base64'" type="messageTypeBase64"/>
  <xs:alternative test="@kind='binary'" type="messageTypeBase64"/>
  <xs:alternative test="@kind='xml'"    type="messageTypeXML"/>
  <xs:alternative test="@kind='XML'"    type="messageTypeXML"/>
  <xs:alternative                       type="messageType"/>
</xs:element>
```

#### <a id="sec-src-element"></a>3.3.3 Constraints on XML Representations of Element Declarations

<a id="src-element"></a>**Schema Representation Constraint: Element Declaration Representation OK**
In addition to the conditions imposed on [<element>](#element-element) element information items by the schema for schema documents: **all**of the following must be true:1 `default`and `fixed`are not both present.2 If the item's parent is not [<schema>](#element-schema), then **all**of the following are true:2.1 One of `ref`or `name`is present, but not both.2.2 <a id="anchor8458"></a>If `ref`is present, then no unqualified attributes are present other than `minOccurs`, `maxOccurs`, and `id`, and no children in the Schema namespace (`xs`) other than [<annotation>](#element-annotation).3 The [<element>](#element-element) element does not have both a [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) or [<complexType>](#element-complexType) child and a `type`attribute.4<a id="ed-with-ns"></a> If `targetNamespace`is present then **all**of the following are true:4.1 `name`is present. 4.2 `form`is not present. 4.3 If the ancestor [<schema>](#element-schema) does not have a `targetNamespace`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) or its [·actual value·](#key-vv) is different from the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of `targetNamespace`of [<element>](#element-element), then **all**of the following are true:4.3.1 [<element>](#element-element) has [<complexType>](#element-complexType) as an ancestor 4.3.2<a id="ed-with-ns-must-be-old"></a> There is a [<restriction>](#element-complexContent..restriction) ancestor between the [<element>](#element-element) and the nearest [<complexType>](#element-complexType) ancestor, and the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `base`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of [<restriction>](#element-complexContent..restriction) does not [·match·](#key-en-match) the name of [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType). 5 Every [<alternative>](#element-alternative) element but the last has a `test`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element); the last [<alternative>](#element-alternative) element may have such an [[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).
#### <a id="eldec_vr"></a>3.3.4 Element Declaration Validation Rules

When an element is [·assessed·](#key-va), it is first checked against its [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed), if any; this in turn entails checking it against its [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem). The second step is recursive: the element's [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) are [·assessed·](#key-va) in turn with respect to the declarations assigned to them by their parent's [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem).

##### <a id="sec-sistd"></a>3.3.4.1 Selected and Instance-specified Type Definitions

The [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) of an element is normally the declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) associated with the [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed), but this may be [·overridden·](#key-overrides) using conditional type assignment in the [Element Declaration](#ed) or using an [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd), or both. When the element is declared with conditional type assignment, the [·selected type definition·](#key-selected-type) is used as the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) unless [·overridden·](#key-overrides) by an [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd).

<a id="key-selected-type"></a>[Definition:]The **selected type definition***S*of an element information item *E*is a type definition associated with *E*in the following way. Let *D*be the [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) of *E*.
