Then: 1<a id="hack-will-it-work-haha-yes"></a> If *D*has a [{type table}](#ed-type_table), then *S*is the type [·conditionally selected·](#key-cta-select) for *E*by *D*.[{type table}](#ed-type_table). 2 If *D*has no [{type table}](#ed-type_table), then *S*is *D*.[{type definition}](#ed-type_definition). If *E*has no [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed), then *E*has no selected type definition.**Note:**It is a consequence of [Element Declaration Properties Correct (§3.3.6.1)](#e-props-correct) that if *D*is valid, then *S*will be [·validly substitutable·](#key-val-sub-type) for *D*'s declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition), or else that *S*will be [·xs:error·](#key-error).<a id="key-cta-select"></a>[Definition:] Given a [Type Table](#tt)*T*and an element information item *E*, *T***conditionally selects**a type *S*for *E*in the following way. The [{test}](#tac-test) expressions in *T*'s [{alternatives}](#tt-alternatives) are evaluated, in order, until one of the [Type Alternative](#tac)s [·successfully selects·](#key-cta-ta-select) a type definition for *E*, or until all have been tried without success. If any [Type Alternative](#tac)[·successfully selects·](#key-cta-ta-select) a type definition, none of the following [Type Alternative](#tac)s are tried. Then the type *S***conditionally selected**for *E*by *T*is as described in the appropriate **case**among the following:1<a id="hack-to-force-template-match-2"></a> If at least one [Type Alternative](#tac) in *T*.[{alternatives}](#tt-alternatives)[·successfully selects·](#key-cta-ta-select) a type definition for *E*, then *S*is the type definition selected by the first such [Type Alternative](#tac). 2 If no [Type Alternative](#tac) in *T*.[{alternatives}](#tt-alternatives)[·successfully selects·](#key-cta-ta-select) a type definition, then *S*is *T*.[{default type definition}](#tt-default_type_definition).[{type definition}](#tac-type_definition). <a id="key-itd"></a>[Definition:]An **instance-specified type definition**is a type definition associated with an element information item in the following way: 1<a id="xsi-type-att"></a>Among the element's attribute information items is one named `xsi:type`.2 The [·normalized value·](#key-nv) of that attribute information item is a qualified name [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to the built-in [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) simple type, as defined by [String Valid (§3.16.4)](#cvc-simple-type). 3 The [·actual value·](#key-vv) (a [·QName·](#gloss-QName)) [·resolves·](#cvc-resolve-instance) to a type definition. It is this type definition which is the **instance-specified type definition**.
##### <a id="sec-type-override"></a>3.3.4.2 Type Override and Valid Substitutability

<a id="key-overrides"></a>[Definition:]An [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd)*S*is said to **override**another type definition *T*if and only if all of the following are true: 1 *S*is the [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd) on some element information item *E*. A [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) may or may not be known for *E*.2 *S*is [·validly substitutable·](#key-val-sub-type) for *T*, subject to the blocking keywords of the [{disallowed substitutions}](#ed-disallowed_substitutions) of *E*'s [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed), if any, or [·validly substitutable without limitation·](#key-val-sub-type-absolute) for *T*(if no [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) is known).**Note:**Typically, *T*would be the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) for *E*if it were not overridden. (This will be the case if *T*was stipulated by the processor, as described in [Assessing Schema-Validity (§5.2)](#validation_outcome), or *E*has a [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) and *T*is its declared type, or *T*is the [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) of *E*.) **Note:**The use of the term "[·override·](#key-overrides)" to denote the relation between an [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd)*S*and another type *T*has nothing to do with the [<override>](#element-override) element; the two mechanisms are distinct and unrelated.<a id="key-val-sub-type"></a>[Definition:]A type definition *S*is **validly substitutable**for another type *T*, subject to a set of blocking keywords *K*(typically drawn from the set {***substitution***, ***extension***, ***restriction***, ***list***, ***union***} used in the [{disallowed substitutions}](#ed-disallowed_substitutions) and [{prohibited substitutions}](#ctd-prohibited_substitutions) of element declarations and type definitions), if and only if either
- *S*and *T*are both complex type definitions and *S*is validly derived from *T*subject to the blocking keywords in the union of *K*and *T*. [{prohibited substitutions}](#ctd-prohibited_substitutions), as defined in [Type Derivation OK (Complex) (§3.4.6.5)](#cos-ct-derived-ok)
or
- *S*is a complex type definition, *T*is a simple type definition, and *S*is validly [·derived·](#key-derived) from *T*subject to the blocking keywords in *K*, as defined in [Type Derivation OK (Complex) (§3.4.6.5)](#cos-ct-derived-ok)
or
- *S*is a simple type definition and *S*is validly [·derived·](#key-derived) from *T*subject to the blocking keywords in *K*, as defined in [Type Derivation OK (Simple) (§3.16.6.3)](#cos-st-derived-ok).
<a id="key-val-sub-type-absolute"></a>[Definition:]If the set of keywords controlling whether a type *S*is [·validly substitutable·](#key-val-sub-type) for another type *T*is the empty set, then *S*is said to be **validly substitutable**for *T***without limitation**or **absolutely**. The phrase **validly substitutable**, without mention of any set of blocking keywords, means "validly substitutable without limitation".

Sometimes one type *S*is [·validly substitutable·](#key-val-sub-type) for another type *T*only if *S*is derived from *T*by a chain of restrictions, or if *T*is a union type and *S*a member type of the union. The concept of [·valid substitutability·](#key-val-sub-type) is appealed to often enough in such contexts that it is convenient to define a term to cover this specific case. <a id="key-val-sub-type-restricts"></a>[Definition:]A type definition *S*is **validly substitutable as a restriction**for another type *T*if and only if *S*is [·validly substitutable·](#key-val-sub-type) for *T*, subject to the blocking keywords {***extension***, ***list***, ***union***}.

##### <a id="sec-cvc-elt"></a>3.3.4.3 Element Locally Valid (Element)

The concept of local validity of an element information item against an element declaration is an important part of the schema-validity [·assessment·](#key-va) of elements. (The other important part is the recursive [·assessment·](#key-va) of attributes and descendant elements.) Local validity partially determines the element information item's [[validity]](#e-validity) property, and fully determines the [[local element validity]](#e-local_elem_validity) property, in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi).

Informally, an element is locally valid against an element declaration when:
1. The declaration is present in the schema and the name of the element matches the name of the declaration.
2. The element is declared concrete (i.e. not abstract).
3. Any `xsi:nil`attribute on the element obeys the rules. The element is allowed to have an `xsi:nil`attribute only if the element is declared nillable, and `xsi:nil = 'true'`is allowed only if the element itself is empty. If the element declaration specifies a fixed value for the element, `xsi:nil='true'`will make the element invalid.
4. Any `xsi:type`attribute present names a type which is [·validly substitutable·](#key-val-sub-type) for the element's declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition).
5. The element's content satisfies the appropriate constraints: If the element is empty and the declaration specifies a default value, the default is checked against the appropriate type definitions. Otherwise, the content of the element is checked against the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem); additionally, if the element declaration specifies a fixed value, the content is checked against that value.
6. The element satisfies all the identity constraints specified on the element declaration.
7. Additionally, on the [·validation root·](#key-vr), document-level ID and IDREF constraints are checked.
The following validation rule gives the normative formal definition of local validity of an element against an element declaration.

<a id="cvc-elt"></a>**Validation Rule: Element Locally Valid (Element)**
For an element information item *E*to be locally [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to an element declaration *D***all**of the following must be true:1<a id="c-ea"></a>*D*is not [·absent·](#key-null) and *E*and *D*have the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname).2 *D*.[{abstract}](#ed-abstract) = ***false***.3 **One**of the following is true:3.1 *D*.[{nillable}](#ed-nillable) = ***false***, and *E*has no `xsi:nil`attribute.3.2<a id="c-nl"></a>*D*.[{nillable}](#ed-nillable) = ***true***and **one**of the following is true3.2.1 *E*has no `xsi:nil`attribute information item.3.2.2 *E*has `xsi:nil`= `false`.3.2.3<a id="c-nl-11"></a>*E*has `xsi:nil`= `true`(that is, *E*is [·nilled·](#key-nilled)), and **all**of the following are true:3.2.3.1 *E*has no character or element information item [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).3.2.3.2 *D*has no [{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint) with [{variety}](#vc_e-variety) = ***fixed***.4<a id="c-itd"></a><a id="anchor11764a"></a> If *E*has an [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd)*T*, then *T*[·overrides·](#key-overrides) the [·selected type definition·](#key-selected-type) of *E*. 5<a id="c-evagtd"></a>The appropriate **case**among the following is true:5.1<a id="c-evc"></a>**If ***D*has a [{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint), and *E*has neither element nor character [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), and *E*is not [·nilled·](#key-nilled) with respect to *D*, **then ****all**of the following are true:5.1.1 If *E*'s [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) is an [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd), then *D*.[{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint) is a valid default for the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) as defined in [Element Default Valid (Immediate) (§3.3.6.2)](#cos-valid-default). 5.1.2 The element information item with *D*.[{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint).[{lexical form}](#vc_e-lexical_form) used as its [·normalized value·](#key-nv) is locally [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) as defined by [Element Locally Valid (Type) (§3.3.4.4)](#cvc-type).5.2 **If ***D*has no [{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint), or *E*has either element or character [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), or *E*is [·nilled·](#key-nilled) with respect to *D*, **then ****all**of the following are true:5.2.1 *E*is locally [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) as defined by [Element Locally Valid (Type) (§3.3.4.4)](#cvc-type).5.2.2 If *D*.[{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint).[{variety}](#vc_e-variety) = ***fixed***and *E*is not [·nilled·](#key-nilled) with respect to *D*, then **all**of the following are true:5.2.2.1 *E*has no element information item [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).5.2.2.2 The appropriate **case**among the following is true:5.2.2.2.1 **If ***E*'s [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) is a [Complex Type Definition](#ctd) with [{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***mixed***, **then **the [·initial value·](#key-iv) of *E*matches *D*.[{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint).[{lexical form}](#vc_e-lexical_form).5.2.2.2.2 **If ***E*'s [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) is a [Simple Type Definition](#std) or a [Complex Type Definition](#ctd) with [{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***simple***, **then **the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of *E*is equal or identical to *D*.[{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint).[{value}](#vc_e-value). 6 *E*is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to each of the [{identity-constraint definitions}](#s-identity-constraint_definitions) as per [Identity-constraint Satisfied (§3.11.4)](#cvc-identity-constraint).7 If *E*is the [·validation root·](#key-vr), then it is [·valid·](#key-vn) per [Validation Root Valid (ID/IDREF) (§3.3.4.5)](#cvc-id).**Note:**If an element has an `xsi:type`attribute, the type definition indicated by that attribute normally takes precedence over the [·selected type definition·](#key-selected-type) which would otherwise govern the element. <a id="anchor11764b"></a> If an `xsi:type`attribute is present and [·resolves·](#cvc-resolve-instance) to a known type definition, but fails to [·override·](#key-overrides) the [·selected type definition·](#key-selected-type), then *E*is not locally valid against *D*, since *E*has failed to satisfy clause [4](#c-itd). In this case (or if `xsi:type`fails to [·resolve·](#cvc-resolve-instance)), the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) of the element is the [·selected type definition·](#key-selected-type) of its [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed), and the element is validated against that type as described in clause [5](#c-evagtd). The local validity of the element with respect to the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) is recorded in the [[local type validity]](#e-local_type_validity) property. The use of the [·selected type definition·](#key-selected-type) when the [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd) cannot be used allows useful validation to proceed in some cases (not all) even when the schema is incomplete. It also helps ensure consistent typing for sub-elements with the same name.
##### <a id="sec-cvc-type"></a>3.3.4.4 Element Locally Valid (Type)

The following validation rule specifies formally what it means for an element to be locally valid against a type definition. This concept is appealed to in the course of checking an element's local validity against its [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem). It is also part of schema-validity [·assessment·](#key-va) of an element when the element is [·laxly assessed·](#key-lva), by checking its local validity against `xs:anyType`.

Informally, local validity against a type requires first that the type definition be present in the schema and not declared abstract. For a simple type definition, the element must lack attributes (except for namespace declarations and the special attributes in the `xsi`namespace) and child elements, and must be type-valid against that simple type definition. For a complex type definition, the element must be locally valid against that complex type definition.

<a id="cvc-type"></a>**Validation Rule: Element Locally Valid (Type)**
For an element information item *E*to be locally [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to a type definition *T***all**of the following must be true:1<a id="c-ct"></a>*T*is not [·absent·](#key-null);2 If *T*is a complex type definition, then *T*.[{abstract}](#ctd-abstract) = ***false***.3 The appropriate **case**among the following is true:3.1 **If ***T*is a simple type definition, **then ****all**of the following are true:3.1.1 *E*.[[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is empty, except for attributes named `xsi:type`, `xsi:nil`, `xsi:schemaLocation`, or `xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation`.3.1.2 *E*has no element information item [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).3.1.3<a id="c-sv1"></a>If *E*is not [·nilled·](#key-nilled), then the [·initial value·](#key-iv) is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to *T*as defined by [String Valid (§3.16.4)](#cvc-simple-type).3.2 **If ***T*is a complex type definition, **then ***E*is locally [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to *T*as per [Element Locally Valid (Complex Type) (§3.4.4.2)](#cvc-complex-type);
##### <a id="sec-cvc-id"></a>3.3.4.5 Validation Root Valid (ID/IDREF)

The following validation rule specifies document-level ID/IDREF constraints checked on the [·validation root·](#key-vr) if it is an element; this rule is not checked on other elements. Informally, the requirement is that each ID identifies a single element within the [·validation root·](#key-vr), and that each IDREF value matches one ID.

<a id="cvc-id"></a>**Validation Rule: Validation Root Valid (ID/IDREF)**
For an element information item *E*which is the [·validation root·](#key-vr) to be [·valid·](#key-vn)**all**of the following must be true:1 There is no **ID/IDREF binding**in *E*.[[ID/IDREF table]](#e-ii_table) whose [[binding]](#iib-binding) is the empty set.2<a id="c-uba"></a>There is no **ID/IDREF binding**in *E*.[[ID/IDREF table]](#e-ii_table) whose [[binding]](#iib-binding) has more than one member.See [ID/IDREF Table (§3.17.5.2)](#sic-id) for the definition of **ID/IDREF binding**.**Note:**The first clause above is violated when there is a reference to an undefined ID. The second is violated when there is a multiply-defined ID. The cases are separated out to ensure that distinct error codes (see [Outcome Tabulations (normative) (§B)](#outcomes)) are associated with these two cases.**Note:**Since an element governed by type `xs:ID`provides a unique identifier for the element's parent element, it is not useful to have an element governed by `xs:ID`when the element has no parent element or when the parent element lies outside the scope of validation.In the following examples, `DOC`and `Y`are governed by type [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType), the element `X`and the attribute `xml:id`are governed by `xs:ID`, and the element `Z`is governed by a complex type with simple content derived from `xs:ID`.
- In the document `<DOC><X>abcd</X></DOC>`, the ID value '`abcd`' will normally be bound to the `DOC`element. But if the `X`element is the validation root, then '`abcd`' will have no element binding, because `DOC`is outside the scope of the validation episode. So the first clause is violated and the document is invalid.
- The superficially similar case `<DOC><Y xml:id="abcd"/></DOC>`will, in contrast, be valid whether the `DOC`element or the `Y`element is the validation root. The ID/IDREF table will have one entry in either case, binding '`abcd`' to the `Y`element.
- For the document `<DOC><Z xml:id="abcd">abcd</Z></DOC>`, if `Z`is the validation root, then the ID/IDREF table for the document will have a single entry for '`abcd`' and will be valid. The single binding comes from the `xml:id`attribute; the content of `Z`produces no binding, just as the content of `X`above produces no binding.But if `DOC`is the validation root, then the ID/IDREF table for the document will have two entries for '`abcd`' (one, from the `xml:id`attribute, binding '`abcd`' to the `Z`element, one from the content of `Z`binding '`abcd`' to the `DOC`element) and will be invalid.
**Note:**Although this rule applies at the [·validation root·](#key-vr), in practice processors, particularly streaming processors, will perhaps wish to detect and signal the clause [2](#c-uba) case as it arises.**Note:**This reconstruction of [[XML 1.1]](#ref-xml-1.1)'s `ID/IDREF`functionality is imperfect in that if the [·validation root·](#key-vr) is not the document element of an XML document, the results will not necessarily be the same as those a validating parser would give were the document to have a DTD with equivalent declarations.
##### <a id="sec-cvc-assess-elt"></a>3.3.4.6 Schema-Validity Assessment (Element)

This section gives the top-level rule for [·assessment·](#key-va) of an element information item. Informally:
1. Assessment begins with the identification of a [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) for the element and then checks that the element is locally valid against the declaration; if no [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) is available, a [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) can be used instead.
2. The element's attributes are to be [·assessed·](#key-va) recursively, unless they match a ***skip***wildcard and are thus [·skipped·](#key-skipped).
3. The element's children are to be [·assessed·](#key-va) recursively, unless they match a ***skip***wildcard and are thus [·skipped·](#key-skipped). For each child element, the [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) is the one identified in the course of checking the local validity of the parent, unless that declaration is not available. If the [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) is not available, the element may still be [·strictly assessed·](#key-sva) if a [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) can be identified (e.g. via the `xsi:type`attribute), otherwise the element will be [·laxly assessed·](#key-lva).
<a id="key-governing-ed"></a>[Definition:] The **governing element declaration**of an element information item *E*, in a given schema-validity [·assessment·](#key-va) episode, is the first of the following which applies: 1<a id="elem-stipulated"></a>A declaration stipulated by the processor (see [Assessing Schema-Validity (§5.2)](#validation_outcome)).2<a id="elem-cdd"></a>*E*'s [·context-determined declaration·](#key-dd).3<a id="elem-wc-split"></a>A declaration [·resolved·](#cvc-resolve-instance) to by *E*'s [[local name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and [[namespace name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), provided that *E*is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) either to a ***strict***[·wildcard particle·](#key-wp) or to a ***lax***[·wildcard particle·](#key-wp). 4<a id="elem-wc"></a>A declaration [·resolved·](#cvc-resolve-instance) to by *E*'s [[local name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and [[namespace name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), provided that **none**of the following is true: 4.1<a id="no-id-if-skipped"></a>*E*is [·skipped·](#key-skipped)4.2 the processor has stipulated a type definition for *E*4.3 a [·non-absent·](#key-null)[·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) exists for *E*If none of these applies, *E*has no [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) (or, in equivalent words, *E*'s [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) is [·absent·](#key-null)). <a id="key-governing-type-elem"></a>[Definition:] The **governing type definition**of an element information item *E*, in a given schema-validity [·assessment·](#key-va) episode, is the first of the following which applies: 1<a id="stipulated-plus-xsitype"></a>An [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd) which [·overrides·](#key-overrides) a type definition stipulated by the processor (see [Assessing Schema-Validity (§5.2)](#validation_outcome)).2<a id="stipulated"></a>A type definition stipulated by the processor (see [Assessing Schema-Validity (§5.2)](#validation_outcome)).3<a id="governing-plus-xsitype"></a>An [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd) which [·overrides·](#key-overrides) the [·selected type definition·](#key-selected-type) of *E*.4<a id="gov-no-xsitype"></a>The [·selected type definition·](#key-selected-type) of *E*.5 The value [·absent·](#key-null) if *E*is [·skipped·](#key-skipped). 6 An [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd) which [·overrides·](#key-overrides) the [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype). 7 The [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype).8 An [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd). If none of these applies, there is no [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) (or, in equivalent words, it is [·absent·](#key-null)). <a id="cvc-assess-elt"></a>**Validation Rule: Schema-Validity Assessment (Element)**
The schema-validity assessment of an element information item *E*is performed as follows:1 If *E*has a [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) or a [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem), then *E*must be [·strictly assessed·](#key-sva).2 If *E*is [·skipped·](#key-skipped), then *E*must not be [·assessed·](#key-va).3 Otherwise, *E*must be [·laxly assessed·](#key-lva).<a id="key-sva"></a>[Definition:]An element information item *E*is said to be **strictly assessed**if and only if **all**of the following are true:1<a id="c-xd"></a>**One**of the following is true:1.1<a id="c-ed"></a>**All**of the following are true:1.1.1<a id="dummy-id-to-force-special-list-processing-for-parent"></a>A [·non-absent·](#key-null) element declaration is known for *E*, namely its [·governing·](#key-governing) declaration. 1.1.2 *E*'s local [·validity·](#key-vn) with respect to that declaration has been evaluated as per [Element Locally Valid (Element) (§3.3.4.3)](#cvc-elt).1.1.3 If that evaluation involved the evaluation of [Element Locally Valid (Type) (§3.3.4.4)](#cvc-type), clause [1](#c-ct) thereof is satisfied.1.2<a id="c-td"></a>**All**of the following are true:1.2.1 *E*does not have a [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed). 1.2.2 A [·non-absent·](#key-null) type definition is known for *E*, namely its [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem)1.2.3<a id="another-dummy-id-to-force-special-list-processing-for-parent"></a>The local [·validity·](#key-vn) of *E*with respect to its [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) has been evaluated as per [Element Locally Valid (Type) (§3.3.4.4)](#cvc-type).2<a id="c-deepa"></a> For each of the attribute information items among *E*.[[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), the appropriate **case**among the following is true:2.1 **If **the attribute has a [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad) , **then **its schema-validity is assessed with respect to that declaration, as defined in [Schema-Validity Assessment (Attribute) (§3.2.4.3)](#cvc-assess-attr). 2.2 **otherwise **its schema-validity is not assessed. 3<a id="c-deepe"></a> For each of the element information items among its [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), the appropriate **case**among the following is true:3.1 **If **the child has a [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) or a [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) , **then **its schema-validity is assessed with respect to that declaration or type definition, as defined in [Schema-Validity Assessment (Element) (§3.3.4.6)](#cvc-assess-elt). 3.2 **If **the child is [·attributed to·](#key-att-to) a ***skip***[Wildcard](#w) , **then **its schema-validity is not assessed. 3.3 **otherwise **its schema-validity is [·laxly assessed·](#key-lva) with respect to [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType). <a id="key-lva"></a>[Definition:]The schema validity of an element information item *E*is said to be **laxly assessed**if and only if **both**of the following are true:1 *E*has neither a [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) nor a [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem).2 *E*is locally [·validated·](#key-vn) with respect to [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType) as defined in [Element Locally Valid (Type) (§3.3.4.4)](#cvc-type), and the schema-validity of *E*'s [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is assessed as described in clause [2](#c-deepa) and clause [3](#c-deepe) of [Schema-Validity Assessment (Element) (§3.3.4.6)](#cvc-assess-elt).**Note:**It follows from the definitions given that no element information item can be both [·strictly assessed·](#key-sva) and [·laxly assessed·](#key-lva) in the same schema-validity [·assessment·](#key-va) episode.
#### <a id="edisc"></a>3.3.5 Element Declaration Information Set Contributions

##### <a id="sec-sic-e-outcome"></a>3.3.5.1 Assessment Outcome (Element)

<a id="sic-e-outcome"></a>**Schema Information Set Contribution: Assessment Outcome (Element)**
If and only if the schema-validity of an element information item has been assessed as per [Schema-Validity Assessment (Element) (§3.3.4.6)](#cvc-assess-elt), then in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) it has properties as follows:PSVI Contributions forelement information items
**<a id="e-validation_context"></a>[validation context]**
: The nearest ancestor element information item with a [[schema information]](#e-schema_information) property (or this element item itself if it has such a property).

**<a id="e-validity"></a>[validity]**
: The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **it was [·strictly assessed·](#key-sva), **then **the appropriate **case**among the following:1.1 **If ****all**of the following are true:1.1.1 **One**of the following is true:1.1.1.1 clause [1.1](#c-ed) of [Schema-Validity Assessment (Element) (§3.3.4.6)](#cvc-assess-elt) applied and the item was locally [·valid·](#key-vn) as defined by [Element Locally Valid (Element) (§3.3.4.3)](#cvc-elt);1.1.1.2 clause [1.2](#c-td) of [Schema-Validity Assessment (Element) (§3.3.4.6)](#cvc-assess-elt) applied and the item was locally [·valid·](#key-vn) as defined by [Element Locally Valid (Type) (§3.3.4.4)](#cvc-type).1.1.2 Neither its [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) nor its [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) contains an information item (element or attribute respectively) whose [validity] is ***invalid***.1.1.3 Neither its [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) nor its [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) contains an information item (element or attribute respectively) which is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a ***strict***[·wildcard particle·](#key-wp) and whose [validity] is ***notKnown***.**then *****valid***;1.2 **otherwise *****invalid***.2 **otherwise *****notKnown***.

**<a id="e-validation_attempted"></a>[validation attempted]**
: The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **it was [·strictly assessed·](#key-sva) and neither its [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) nor its [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) contains an information item (element or attribute respectively) whose [validation attempted] is not ***full***, **then *****full***;2 **If **it was not [·strictly assessed·](#key-sva) and neither its [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) nor its [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) contains an information item (element or attribute respectively) whose [validation attempted] is not ***none***, **then *****none***;3 **otherwise *****partial***.

##### <a id="sec-sic-elt-error-code"></a>3.3.5.2 Validation Failure (Element)

<a id="sic-elt-error-code"></a>**Schema Information Set Contribution: Validation Failure (Element)**
If and only if the local [·validity·](#key-vn), as defined by [Element Locally Valid (Element) (§3.3.4.3)](#cvc-elt) above and/or [Element Locally Valid (Type) (§3.3.4.4)](#cvc-type) below, of an element information item has been assessed, then in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) the item has a property:PSVI Contributions forelement information items
**<a id="e-schema_error_code"></a>[schema error code]**
: The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the item is [·invalid·](#key-vn), **then **a list. Applications wishing to provide information as to the reason(s) for the [·validation·](#key-vn) failure are encouraged to record one or more error codes (see [Outcome Tabulations (normative) (§B)](#outcomes)) herein.2 **otherwise **[·absent·](#key-null).

**<a id="e-subsequence_valid"></a>[subsequence-valid]**
: The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the element information item is locally [·invalid·](#key-vn), because unexpected attributes or elements were found among its [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)**and**clause [1](#c-cvct) of [Element Locally Valid (Complex Type) (§3.4.4.2)](#cvc-complex-type) would be satisfied, if those unexpected attributes and children (those with [[match information]](#e-match_info) = ***none***) were removed, **then *****true***2 **otherwise *****false***

**<a id="e-idcs"></a>[failed identity constraints]**
: A list of [Identity-Constraint Definition](#icd)s that are not satisfied by the element information item, as defined by [Identity-constraint Satisfied (§3.11.4)](#cvc-identity-constraint). **Note:**In principle, the value of this property includes all of the [Identity-Constraint Definition](#icd)s which are not satisfied for this element item; in practice, some processors will expose a subset of the items in this value, rather than the full value. For example, a processor could choose not to check further identity constraints after detecting the first failure.

**<a id="e-assertions"></a>[failed assertions]**
: A list of [Assertion](#as)s that are not satisfied by the element information item, as defined by [Assertion Satisfied (§3.13.4.1)](#cvc-assertion). **Note:**In principle, the value of this property includes all of the [Assertion](#as)s which are not satisfied by this element item; in practice, some processors will expose a subset of the items in this value, rather than the full value. For example, a processor could choose not to check further assertions after detecting the first failure.

##### <a id="sec-sic-elt-decl"></a>3.3.5.3 Element Declaration

<a id="sic-elt-decl"></a>**Schema Information Set Contribution: Element Declaration**
If and only if a [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) is known for an element information item, then in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) the element information item has the properties:PSVI Contributions forelement information items
**<a id="e-declaration"></a>[element declaration]**
: an [·item isomorphic·](#key-iso) to the [·governing·](#key-governing) declaration component itself

**<a id="e-nil"></a>[nil]**
: ***true***if clause [3.2.3](#c-nl-11) of [Element Locally Valid (Element) (§3.3.4.3)](#cvc-elt) above is satisfied, otherwise ***false***

**<a id="e-expected-ed"></a>[expected element declaration]**
: if the element information item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to an [·element particle·](#key-ep), then the [{term}](#p-term) of that [Particle](#p), otherwise [·absent·](#key-null)**Note:**The [[element declaration]](#e-declaration) either is the same as or is in the [·substitution group·](#key-eq) of the [[expected element declaration]](#e-expected-ed).

**<a id="e-declared_type"></a>[declared type]**
: an [·item isomorphic·](#key-iso) to the declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) of the [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed)

**<a id="e-local_elem_validity"></a>[local element validity]**
: The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the item was locally [·valid·](#key-vn) as defined by [Element Locally Valid (Element) (§3.3.4.3)](#cvc-elt), **then *****valid***2 **otherwise **(the item was locally [·invalid·](#key-vn) as defined by [Element Locally Valid (Element) (§3.3.4.3)](#cvc-elt)) ***invalid***.

##### <a id="sec-sic-eltType"></a>3.3.5.4 Element Validated by Type

<a id="sic-eltType"></a>**Schema Information Set Contribution: Element Validated by Type**
If and only if a [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) is known for an element information item, then in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) the item has the properties:PSVI Contributions forelement information items
**<a id="e-schema_normalized_value"></a>[schema normalized value]**
: The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the element information item is not [·nilled·](#key-nilled) and either the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) is a simple type definition or its [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) has [{variety}](#ct-variety)***simple***, **then **the appropriate **case**among the following:1.1 **If **clause [5.1](#c-evc) of [Element Locally Valid (Element) (§3.3.4.3)](#cvc-elt) above has applied, **then **the [{lexical form}](#vc_e-lexical_form) of the [{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint)1.2 **If **clause [5.1](#c-evc) of [Element Locally Valid (Element) (§3.3.4.3)](#cvc-elt) above has *not*applied and the element's [·initial value·](#key-iv) is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to the simple type definition as defined by [String Valid (§3.16.4)](#cvc-simple-type), **then **the [·normalized value·](#key-nv) of the item as [·validated·](#key-vn)1.3 **otherwise **[·absent·](#key-null). 2 **otherwise **[·absent·](#key-null).

**<a id="e-schema_actual_value"></a>[schema actual value]**
: If the [[schema normalized value]](#e-schema_normalized_value) is not [·absent·](#key-null), then the corresponding [·actual value·](#key-vv) ; otherwise [·absent·](#key-null).

**<a id="e-type_definition"></a>[type definition]**
: An [·item isomorphic·](#key-iso) to the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) component itself.

**<a id="e-type_definition_type"></a>[type definition type]**
: ***simple***or ***complex***, depending on the [[type definition]](#e-type_definition).

**<a id="e-type_definition_namespace"></a>[type definition namespace]**
: [[type definition]](#e-type_definition).{target namespace}.

**<a id="e-type_definition_anonymous"></a>[type definition anonymous]**
: ***true***if [[type definition]](#e-type_definition).{name} is [·absent·](#key-null), otherwise ***false***.

**<a id="e-type_definition_name"></a>[type definition name]**
: If [[type definition]](#e-type_definition).{name} is not [·absent·](#key-null), then [[type definition]](#e-type_definition).{name}, otherwise schema processors may, but need not, provide a value unique to the definition. It is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) whether a processor provides a name for such a type definition. If a processor does provide a value in this situation, the choice of what value to use is [·implementation-dependent·](#key-impl-dep).

**<a id="e-type_fallback"></a>[type fallback]**
: A keyword indicating whether the expected type definition was unavailable and the element had a fallback type as its [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem)
- ***declared***if the element information item has a [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) which has no [{type table}](#ed-type_table), and also an `xsi:type`attribute which fails to [·resolve·](#cvc-resolve-instance) to a type definition that [·overrides·](#key-overrides) the declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition)
- ***selected***if the element information item has a [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) with a [{type table}](#ed-type_table) and also has an `xsi:type`attribute which fails to [·resolve·](#cvc-resolve-instance) to a type definition that [·overrides·](#key-overrides) the [·selected type definition·](#key-selected-type)
- ***lax***if the element was [·laxly assessed·](#key-lva) using [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType)
- ***none***otherwise

**<a id="e-type_alternative"></a>[type alternative]**
: If the element's [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) does not have a [{type table}](#ed-type_table), then [·absent·](#key-null); otherwise the first [Type Alternative](#tac) that [·successfully selected·](#key-cta-ta-select) the element's [·selected type definition·](#key-selected-type), if any; otherwise the [{default type definition}](#tt-default_type_definition).

**<a id="e-local_type_validity"></a>[local type validity]**
: The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the element information item was locally [·valid·](#key-vn) as defined by [Element Locally Valid (Type) (§3.3.4.4)](#cvc-type), **then *****valid***2 **otherwise **(the item was locally [·invalid·](#key-vn) as defined by [Element Locally Valid (Type) (§3.3.4.4)](#cvc-type)) ***invalid***.

**<a id="e-descendant_validity"></a>[descendant validity]**
: The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **neither its [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) nor its [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) contains an information item **I**(element or attribute respectively) where either **I**'s [[validity]](#e-validity) is ***invalid***or **I**is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a strict [·wildcard particle·](#key-wp) and **I**'s [[validity]](#e-validity) is ***notKnown***, **then *****valid***;2 **otherwise *****invalid***.

**Note:**The [type definition type], [type definition namespace], [type definition name], and [type definition anonymous] properties are redundant with the [type definition] property; they are defined for the convenience of implementations which wish to expose those specific properties but not the entire type definition.**Note:**When clause [5.1](#c-evc) of [Element Locally Valid (Element) (§3.3.4.3)](#cvc-elt) above applies and the default or fixed value constraint [{value}](#vc_a-value) is of type [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) or [NOTATION](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NOTATION), it is [·implementation-dependent·](#key-impl-dep) whether [·namespace fixup·](#term-ns-fixup) occurs; if it does not, the prefix used in the lexical representation (in [[schema normalized value]](#e-schema_normalized_value)) will not necessarily map to the namespace name of the value (in [[schema actual value]](#e-schema_actual_value)). To reduce problems and confusion, users may prefer to ensure that the required namespace information item is present in the input infoset. <a id="anchor10662d"></a>If the [[schema normalized value]](#e-schema_normalized_value) is not [·absent·](#key-null) and the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) is a simple type definition with [{variety}](#std-variety)***union***, or its [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) has [{variety}](#ct-variety)***simple***and [{simple type definition}](#ct-simple_type_definition) a simple type definition with [{variety}](#std-variety)***union***, then there are four additional properties:PSVI Contributions forelement information items
**<a id="e-member_type_definition"></a>[member type definition]**
: An [·item isomorphic·](#key-iso) to the [·validating type·](#key-vtype) of the [[schema actual value]](#e-schema_actual_value).

**<a id="e-member_type_definition_namespace"></a>[member type definition namespace]**
: The [{target namespace}](#std-target_namespace) of the [·validating type·](#key-vtype).

**<a id="e-member_type_definition_anonymous"></a>[member type definition anonymous]**
: ***true***if the [{name}](#std-name) of the [·validating type·](#key-vtype) is [·absent·](#key-null), otherwise ***false***.

**<a id="e-member_type_definition_name"></a>[member type definition name]**
: The [{name}](#std-name) of the [·validating type·](#key-vtype), if it is not [·absent·](#key-null). If it is [·absent·](#key-null), schema processors may, but need not, provide a value unique to the definition. It is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) whether a processor provides a name for such a type definition. If a processor does provide a value in this situation, the choice of what value to use is [·implementation-dependent·](#key-impl-dep).

The [[type definition]](#e-type_definition) property is provided for applications such as query processors which need access to the full range of details about an item's [·assessment·](#key-va), for example the type hierarchy; the [[type definition type]](#e-type_definition_type), [[type definition namespace]](#e-type_definition_namespace), [[type definition name]](#e-type_definition_name), and [[type definition anonymous]](#e-type_definition_anonymous) properties are defined for the convenience of those specifying lighter-weight interfaces, in which exposing the entire type hierarchy and full component details might be a significant burden. If **all**of the following are true:1 the [[schema normalized value]](#e-schema_normalized_value) is not [·absent·](#key-null); 2 **One**of the following is true:2.1 the simple type definition used to validate the [·normalized value·](#key-nv) (either the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) or its [{simple type definition}](#ct-simple_type_definition)) has [{variety}](#std-variety) = ***list***; 2.2 the simple type definition has [{variety}](#std-variety) = ***union***and the [·validating type·](#key-vtype) of the [[schema actual value]](#a-schema_actual_value) has [{variety}](#std-variety) = ***list***; 3 the [{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition) of the list type (from the previous clause) has [{variety}](#std-variety) = ***union***; then there is an additional property: PSVI Contributions forelement information items
**<a id="e-member_type_definitions"></a>[member type definitions]**
: a sequence of [Simple Type Definition](#std) components, with the same length as the [[schema actual value]](#e-schema_actual_value), each one an [·item isomorphic·](#key-iso) to the [·validating type·](#key-vtype) of the corresponding item in the [[schema actual value]](#e-schema_actual_value).

Also, if the declaration has a [{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint), the item has a property:PSVI Contributions forelement information items
**<a id="e-schema_default"></a>[schema default]**
: The [{lexical form}](#vc_e-lexical_form) of the declaration's [{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint).

Note that if an element is [·laxly assessed·](#key-lva), then the [[type definition]](#e-type_definition) and [[member type definition]](#e-member_type_definition) properties, or their alternatives, are based on [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType).
##### <a id="sec-sic-eltDefault"></a>3.3.5.5 Element Default Value

<a id="sic-eltDefault"></a>**Schema Information Set Contribution: Element Default Value**
If and only if the local [·validity·](#key-vn), as defined by [Element Locally Valid (Element) (§3.3.4.3)](#cvc-elt) above, of an element information item has been assessed, in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) the item has a property:PSVI Contributions forelement information items
**<a id="e-schema_specified"></a>[schema specified]**
: The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **clause [5.1](#c-evc) of [Element Locally Valid (Element) (§3.3.4.3)](#cvc-elt) above applies, **then *****schema***. 2 **otherwise *****infoset***.

See also [Match Information (§3.4.5.2)](#sic-match-info), [Identity-constraint Table (§3.11.5)](#sic-key), [Validated with Notation (§3.14.5)](#sic-notation-used), and [Schema Information (§3.17.5.1)](#sic-schema), which describe other information set contributions related to element information items.

##### <a id="Inherited_attributes"></a>3.3.5.6 Inherited Attributes

<a id="sic-inheritedAttrs"></a>**Schema Information Set Contribution: Inherited Attributes**
<a id="key-p-inherited"></a>[Definition:]An attribute information item *A*, whether explicitly specified in the input information set or defaulted as described in [Attribute Default Value (§3.4.5.1)](#sic-attrDefault), is **potentially inherited**by an element information item *E*if and only if **all**of the following are true:1 *A*is among the [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of one of *E*'s ancestors. 2 *A*and *E*have the same [validation context]. 3 **One**of the following is true:3.1 *A*is [·attributed to·](#key-att-to) an [Attribute Use](#au) whose [{inheritable}](#au-inheritable) = ***true***. 3.2 *A*is *not*[·attributed to·](#key-att-to) any [Attribute Use](#au) but *A*has a [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad) whose [{inheritable}](#ad-inheritable) = ***true***. If and only if an element information item *P*is not [·skipped·](#key-skipped) (that is, it is either [·strictly·](#key-sva) or [·laxly·](#key-lva) assessed), in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) each of *P*'s element information item [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)*E*which is not [·attributed to·](#key-att-to) a ***skip***[Wildcard](#w), has a property:PSVI Contributions forelement information items
**<a id="e-inherited_attributes"></a>[inherited attributes]**
: A list of attribute information items. An attribute information item *A*is included if and only if **all**of the following are true:1 *A*is [·potentially inherited·](#key-p-inherited) by *E*. 2 Let *O*be *A*'s [[owner element]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute). *A*does not have the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) as another attribute which is also [·potentially inherited·](#key-p-inherited) by *E*and whose [[owner element]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute) is a descendant of *O*.

#### <a id="coss-element"></a>3.3.6 Constraints on Element Declaration Schema Components

All element declarations (see [Element Declarations (§3.3)](#cElement_Declarations)) must satisfy the following constraint.

##### <a id="sec-e-props-correct"></a>3.3.6.1 Element Declaration Properties Correct

<a id="e-props-correct"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Element Declaration Properties Correct**
For any element declaration *E*, **all**of the following must be true:1 The values of *E*'s properties . are as described in the property tableau in [The Element Declaration Schema Component (§3.3.1)](#Element_Declaration_details), modulo the impact of [Missing Sub-components (§5.3)](#conformance-missing).2 If *E*has a [·non-absent·](#key-null)[{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint), then *E*.[{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint) is a valid default with respect to *E*.[{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) as defined in [Element Default Valid (Immediate) (§3.3.6.2)](#cos-valid-default).3 If *E*.[{substitution group affiliations}](#ed-substitution_group_affiliations) is non-empty, then *E*.[{scope}](#ed-scope).[{variety}](#sc_e-variety) = ***global***.4<a id="c-vs-sg"></a>For each member *M*of *E*.[{substitution group affiliations}](#ed-substitution_group_affiliations), *E*.[{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) is [·validly substitutable·](#key-val-sub-type) for *M*.[{type definition}](#ed-type_definition), subject to the blocking keywords in *M*.[{substitution group exclusions}](#ed-substitution_group_exclusions). 5 There are no circular substitution groups. That is, it is not possible to return to *E*by repeatedly following any member of the [{substitution group affiliations}](#ed-substitution_group_affiliations) property.6 If *E*.[{type table}](#ed-type_table) exists, then for each [Type Alternative](#tac) in *E*.[{type table}](#ed-type_table).[{alternatives}](#tt-alternatives), the [{test}](#tac-test) property is not [·absent·](#key-null). 7 If *E*.[{type table}](#ed-type_table) exists, then for each [{type definition}](#tac-type_definition)*T*in *E*.[{type table}](#ed-type_table).[{alternatives}](#tt-alternatives), and also for *E*.[{type table}](#ed-type_table).[{default type definition}](#tt-default_type_definition).[{type definition}](#tac-type_definition), **one**of the following is true7.1 *T*is [·validly substitutable·](#key-val-sub-type) for *E*.[{type definition}](#ed-type_definition), subject to the blocking keywords of *E*.[{disallowed substitutions}](#ed-disallowed_substitutions).7.2 *T*is the type [·xs:error·](#key-error).
##### <a id="sec-cos-valid-default"></a>3.3.6.2 Element Default Valid (Immediate)

This and the following sections define relations appealed to elsewhere in this specification.

<a id="cos-valid-default"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Element Default Valid (Immediate)**
For a [Value Constraint](#vc_e)*V*to be a valid default with respect to a type definition *T*the appropriate **case**among the following must be true:1 **If ***T*is a simple type definition or a complex type definition with [{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***simple***, **then ***V*is a valid default with respect either to *T*(if *T*is simple) or (if *T*is complex) to *T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{simple type definition}](#ct-simple_type_definition) as defined by [Simple Default Valid (§3.2.6.2)](#cos-valid-simple-default). 2 **If ***T*is a complex type definition with [{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) ≠ ***simple***, **then ****all**of the following are true:2.1 *T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***mixed***.2.2 The particle *T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{particle}](#ct-particle) is [·emptiable·](#cd-emptiable) as defined by [Particle Emptiable (§3.9.6.3)](#cos-group-emptiable).
##### <a id="sec-cos-equiv-derived-ok-rec"></a>3.3.6.3 Substitution Group OK (Transitive)

<a id="cos-equiv-derived-ok-rec"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Substitution Group OK (Transitive)**
For an element declaration (call it **M**, for member) to be substitutable for another element declaration (call it **H**, for head) at least **one**of the following must be true:1 **M**and **H**are the same element declaration.2 **All**of the following are true:2.1 **H**. [{disallowed substitutions}](#ed-disallowed_substitutions) does not contain ***substitution***.2.2 There is a chain of [{substitution group affiliations}](#ed-substitution_group_affiliations) properties from **M**to **H**, that is, either **M**.[{substitution group affiliations}](#ed-substitution_group_affiliations) contains **H**, or **M**.[{substitution group affiliations}](#ed-substitution_group_affiliations) contains a declaration whose [{substitution group affiliations}](#ed-substitution_group_affiliations) contains **H**, or . . .2.3 The set of all [{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method)s involved in the [·derivation·](#key-derived) of *M*.[{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) from *H*.[{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) does not intersect with the union of (1) *H*.[{disallowed substitutions}](#ed-disallowed_substitutions), (2) *H*.[{type definition}](#ed-type_definition).[{prohibited substitutions}](#ctd-prohibited_substitutions) (if **H**.[{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) is complex, otherwise the empty set), and (3) the [{prohibited substitutions}](#ctd-prohibited_substitutions) (respectively the empty set) of any intermediate declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition)s in the [·derivation·](#key-derived) of *M*.[{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) from *H*.[{type definition}](#ed-type_definition).
##### <a id="sec-cos-equiv-class"></a>3.3.6.4 Substitution Group

<a id="key-validly-sub"></a>[Definition:]One element declaration is **substitutable**for another if together they satisfy constraint [Substitution Group OK (Transitive) (§3.3.6.3)](#cos-equiv-derived-ok-rec).

<a id="key-eq"></a>[Definition:] Every element declaration (call this **HEAD**) in the [{element declarations}](#s-element_declarations) of a schema defines a **substitution group**, a subset of those [{element declarations}](#s-element_declarations). An element declaration is in the **substitution group**of **HEAD**if and only if it is [·substitutable·](#key-validly-sub) for **HEAD**.

### <a id="Complex_Type_Definitions"></a>3.4 Complex Type Definitions

3.4.1 [The Complex Type Definition Schema Component](#Complex_Type_Definition_details)
3.4.2 [XML Representation of Complex Type Definition Schema Components](#declare-type)
3.4.2.1 [Common Mapping Rules for Complex Type Definitions](#dcl.ctd.common)
3.4.2.2 [Mapping Rules for Complex Types with Simple Content](#dcl.ctd.ctsc)
3.4.2.3 [Mapping Rules for Complex Types with Complex Content](#dcl.ctd.ctcc)
3.4.2.4 [Mapping Rule for Attribute Uses Property](#dcl.ctd.attuses)
3.4.2.5 [Mapping Rule for Attribute Wildcard Property](#dcl.ctd.anyatt)
3.4.2.6 [Examples of Complex Type Definitions](#dcl.ctd.exx)
3.4.3 [Constraints on XML Representations of Complex Type Definitions](#sec-src-ct)
3.4.4 [Complex Type Definition Validation Rules](#formal-complex-type)
3.4.4.1 [Locally Declared Type](#sec-cdtt)
3.4.4.2 [Element Locally Valid (Complex Type)](#sec-cvc-complex-type)
3.4.4.3 [Element Sequence Locally Valid (Complex Content)](#sec-cvc-complex-content)
3.4.4.4 [Attribution](#sec-particle-attribution)
3.4.5 [Complex Type Definition Information Set Contributions](#sec-ctdsic)
3.4.5.1 [Attribute Default Value](#sec-sic-attrDefault)
3.4.5.2 [Match Information](#sec-sic-match-info)
3.4.6 [Constraints on Complex Type Definition Schema Components](#coss-ct)
3.4.6.1 [Complex Type Definition Properties Correct](#sec-ct-props-correct)
3.4.6.2 [Derivation Valid (Extension)](#sec-cos-ct-extends)
3.4.6.3 [Derivation Valid (Restriction, Complex)](#sec-derivation-ok-restriction)
3.4.6.4 [Content Type Restricts (Complex Content)](#sec-cos-content-act-restrict)
3.4.6.5 [Type Derivation OK (Complex)](#sec-cos-ct-derived-ok)
3.4.7 [Built-in Complex Type Definition](#builtin-ctd)
Complex Type Definitions provide for:

- Constraining element information items by providing [Attribute Declaration (§2.2.2.3)](#Attribute_Declaration)s governing the appearance and content of [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)
- Constraining element information item [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) to be empty, or to conform to a specified element-only or mixed content model, or else constraining the character information item [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) to conform to a specified simple type definition.
- Constraining elements and attributes to exist, not to exist, or to have specified values, with [Assertion (§2.2.4.3)](#Assertion)s.
- Using the mechanisms of [Type Definition Hierarchy (§2.2.1.1)](#Type_Derivation) to [·derive·](#key-derived) a complex type from another simple or complex type.
- Specifying [·post-schema-validation infoset contributions·](#gloss-sic) for elements.
- Limiting the ability to [·derive·](#key-derived) additional types from a given complex type.
- Controlling the permission to substitute, in an instance, elements of a [·derived·](#key-derived) type for elements declared in a content model to be of a given complex type.
Example
```
<xs:complexType name="PurchaseOrderType">
  <xs:sequence>
   <xs:element name="shipTo" type="USAddress"/>
   <xs:element name="billTo" type="USAddress"/>
   <xs:element ref="comment" minOccurs="0"/>
   <xs:element name="items"  type="Items"/>
  </xs:sequence>
  <xs:attribute name="orderDate" type="xs:date"/>
 </xs:complexType>
```

The XML representation of a complex type definition.
#### <a id="Complex_Type_Definition_details"></a>3.4.1 The Complex Type Definition Schema Component

A complex type definition schema component has the following properties:

Schema Component: <a id="ctd"></a>Complex Type Definition, a kind of [Type Definition](#td)<a id="ctd-annotations"></a>{annotations} A sequence of [Annotation](#a) components. <a id="ctd-name"></a>{name} An xs:NCName value. Optional.<a id="ctd-target_namespace"></a>{target namespace} An xs:anyURI value. Optional.<a id="ctd-base_type_definition"></a>{base type definition} A [type definition](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#) component. Required.<a id="ctd-final"></a>{final}
A subset of {*extension*, *restriction*}.

<a id="ctd-context"></a>{context} Required if [{name}](#ctd-name) is [·absent·](#key-null), otherwise must be [·absent·](#key-null).
Either an [Element Declaration](#ed) or a [Complex Type Definition](#ctd).

<a id="ctd-derivation_method"></a>{derivation method} One of {extension, restriction}. Required.<a id="ctd-abstract"></a>{abstract} An xs:boolean value. Required.<a id="ctd-attribute_uses"></a>{attribute uses} A set of [Attribute Use](#au) components. <a id="ctd-attribute_wildcard"></a>{attribute wildcard} A [Wildcard](#w) component. Optional.<a id="ctd-content_type"></a>{content type} A [Content Type](#ct) property record. Required.<a id="ctd-prohibited_substitutions"></a>{prohibited substitutions} A subset of {extension, restriction}.<a id="ctd-assertions"></a>{assertions} A sequence of [Assertion](#as) components. Property Record: <a id="ct"></a>Content Type<a id="ct-variety"></a>{variety} One of {empty, simple, element-only, mixed}. Required.<a id="ct-particle"></a>{particle} A [Particle](#p) component. Required if [{variety}](#ct-variety) is ***element-only***or ***mixed***, otherwise must be [·absent·](#key-null).<a id="ct-open_content"></a>{open content} An [Open Content](#oc) property record. Optional if [{variety}](#ct-variety) is ***element-only***or ***mixed***, otherwise must be [·absent·](#key-null).<a id="ct-simple_type_definition"></a>{simple type definition} A [Simple Type Definition](#std) component. Required if [{variety}](#ct-variety) is ***simple***, otherwise must be [·absent·](#key-null).Property Record: <a id="oc"></a>Open Content<a id="oc-mode"></a>{mode} One of {interleave, suffix}. Required.<a id="oc-wildcard"></a>{wildcard} A [Wildcard](#w) component. Required.
Complex type definitions are identified by their [{name}](#ctd-name) and [{target namespace}](#ctd-target_namespace). Except for anonymous complex type definitions (those with no [{name}](#ctd-name)), since type definitions (i.e. both simple and complex type definitions taken together) must be uniquely identified within an [·XSD schema·](#key-schema), no complex type definition can have the same name as another simple or complex type definition. Complex type [{name}](#ctd-name)s and [{target namespace}](#ctd-target_namespace)s are provided for reference from instances (see [xsi:type (§2.7.1)](#xsi_type)), and for use in the XML representation of schema components (specifically in [<element>](#element-element)). See [References to schema components across namespaces (<import>) (§4.2.6)](#composition-schemaImport) for the use of component identifiers when importing one schema into another.

**Note:**The [{name}](#ctd-name) of a complex type is not *ipso facto*the [[(local) name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the element information items [·validated·](#key-vn) by that definition. The connection between a name and a type definition is described in [Element Declarations (§3.3)](#cElement_Declarations).
As described in [Type Definition Hierarchy (§2.2.1.1)](#Type_Derivation), each complex type is [·derived·](#key-derived) from a [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition) which is itself either a [Simple Type Definition (§2.2.1.2)](#Simple_Type_Definition) or a [Complex Type Definition (§2.2.1.3)](#Complex_Type_Definition). [{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method) specifies the means of [·derivation·](#key-derived) as either ***extension***or ***restriction***(see [Type Definition Hierarchy (§2.2.1.1)](#Type_Derivation)).

A complex type with an empty specification for [{final}](#ctd-final) can be used as a [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition) for other types [·derived·](#key-derived) by either of extension or restriction; the explicit values ***extension***, and ***restriction***prevent further [·derivations·](#key-derived) by extension and restriction respectively. If all values are specified, then <a id="key-ct-final"></a>[Definition:]the complex type is said to be **final**, because no further [·derivations·](#key-derived) are possible. Finality is *not*inherited, that is, a type definition [·derived·](#key-derived) by restriction from a type definition which is final for extension is not itself, in the absence of any explicit `final`attribute of its own, final for anything.

The [{context}](#ctd-context) property is only relevant for anonymous type definitions, for which its value is the component in which this type definition appears as the value of a property, e.g. [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition).

Complex types for which [{abstract}](#ctd-abstract) is ***true***have no valid instances and thus cannot be used in the normal way as the [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) for the [·validation·](#key-vn) of element information items (if for some reason an abstract type is identified as the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) of an element information item, the item will invariably be invalid). It follows that such abstract types must not be referenced from an [xsi:type (§2.7.1)](#xsi_type) attribute in an instance document. Abstract complex types can be used as [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition)s, or even as the declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition)s of element declarations, provided in every case a concrete [·derived·](#key-derived) type definition is used for [·validation·](#key-vn), either via [xsi:type (§2.7.1)](#xsi_type) or the operation of a [·substitution group·](#key-eq).

[{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses) are a set of attribute uses. See [Element Locally Valid (Complex Type) (§3.4.4.2)](#cvc-complex-type) and [Attribute Locally Valid (§3.2.4.1)](#cvc-attribute) for details of attribute [·validation·](#key-vn).

[{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard)s provide a more flexible specification for [·validation·](#key-vn) of attributes not explicitly included in [{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses). See [Element Locally Valid (Complex Type) (§3.4.4.2)](#cvc-complex-type), [The Wildcard Schema Component (§3.10.1)](#Wildcard_details) and [Wildcard allows Expanded Name (§3.10.4.2)](#cvc-wildcard-name) for formal details of attribute wildcard [·validation·](#key-vn).

[{content type}](#ctd-content_type) determines the [·validation·](#key-vn) of [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of element information items. Informally:
- A [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) with [{variety}](#ct-variety)***empty***[·validates·](#key-vn) elements with no character or element information item [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).
- A [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) with [{variety}](#ct-variety)***simple***[·validates·](#key-vn) elements with character-only [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)using its [{simple type definition}](#ct-simple_type_definition).
- A [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) with [{variety}](#ct-variety)***element-only***[·validates·](#key-vn) elements with [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) that conform to the [·content model·](#key-contentModel) supplied by its [{particle}](#ct-particle).
- A [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) with [{variety}](#ct-variety)***mixed***[·validates·](#key-vn) elements whose element [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) (i.e. specifically ignoring other [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) such as character information items) conform to the [·content model·](#key-contentModel) supplied by its [{particle}](#ct-particle).
- A [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) with [·non-absent·](#key-null)[{open content}](#ct-open_content)[·validates·](#key-vn) elements with some [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) conforming to the [·content model·](#key-contentModel) and others conforming to the [{open content}](#ct-open_content).
**Note:**Not all combinations of [{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method) and [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) are compatible with all properties of the [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition). For example, it is not allowed to derive a complex type with complex content from a simple type. The XML mapping rules specified in the following section (in particular clause [5](#c-ctsc-bad) of the rule for the [{simple type definition}](#ct-simple_type_definition) in the rule for [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) of complex types with simple content, and clause [4.1](#c-ctr) and clause [4.2.1](#c-ctes) of the rule for [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) for complex types with complex content) do not detect such incompatible combinations of properties; in such cases the mapping rules will build a complex type regardless of the fact that the properties specified are incompatible. But the resulting complex type does not satisfy component rules outlined in [Derivation Valid (Extension) (§3.4.6.2)](#cos-ct-extends) or [Derivation Valid (Restriction, Complex) (§3.4.6.3)](#derivation-ok-restriction). <a id="anchor6382"></a> The [{prohibited substitutions}](#ctd-prohibited_substitutions) property of a complex type definition *T*determines whether type definitions derived from *T*are or are not [·validly substitutable·](#key-val-sub-type) for *T*. Examples include (but are not limited to) the substitution of another type definition:
- as the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) of an element instance *E*, when *T*is the [·selected type definition·](#key-selected-type) of *E*(often, the declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) of *E*'s [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed)); this can occur when *E*specifies a type definition using the `xsi:type`attribute; see [xsi:type (§2.7.1)](#xsi_type);
- as the [·selected type definition·](#key-selected-type) of an element instance *E*, when *T*is the declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) of *E*'s [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed); this can occur when conditional type assignment is used; see [Type Alternatives (§3.12)](#cTypeAlternative);
- as the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) of element instances whose [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) is included in a model group only [·implicitly·](#key-impl-cont), by virtue of being included in the [·substitution group·](#key-eq) of some element declaration present [·directly·](#key-contain-dpt)[·indirectly·](#key-contain-ipx) in the model group, whose declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) is *T*.
- as the [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) of an [Element Declaration](#ed)*E1*where
  - *E1*is contained in a [Complex Type Definition](#ctd)*D*
  - *D*is derived from another [Complex Type Definition](#ctd)*B*
  - *B*contains an [Element Declaration](#ed)*E2*that has the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) as *E1*
  - *E2*has *T*as its [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition).

If [{prohibited substitutions}](#ctd-prohibited_substitutions) is empty, then all such substitutions are allowed; if it contains the keyword ***restriction***, then no type definition is [·validly substitutable·](#key-val-sub-type) for *T*if its derivation from *T*involves any restriction steps; if [{prohibited substitutions}](#ctd-prohibited_substitutions) contains the keyword ***extension***, then no type definition is [·validly substitutable·](#key-val-sub-type) for *T*if its derivation from *T*involves any extension steps.
[{assertions}](#ctd-assertions) constrain elements and attributes to exist, not to exist, or to have specified values. Though specified as a sequence, the order among the assertions is not significant during assessment. See [Assertions (§3.13)](#cAssertions).

See [Annotations (§3.15)](#cAnnotations) for information on the role of the [{annotations}](#ctd-annotations) property.

#### <a id="declare-type"></a>3.4.2 XML Representation of Complex Type Definition Schema Components

The XML representation for a complex type definition schema component is a [<complexType>](#element-complexType) element information item.

The XML representation for complex type definitions with a [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) with [{variety}](#ct-variety)***simple***is significantly different from that of those with other [{content type}](#ctd-content_type)s, and this is reflected in the presentation below, which describes the mappings for the two cases in separate subsections. Common mapping rules are factored out and given in separate sections. As always, the mapping rules given here apply after, not before, the appropriate [·pre-processing·](#key-pre-processing).

XML Representation Summary: `complexType`Element Information Item<a id="element-complexType"></a><complexType
abstract = [boolean](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#boolean): false
block = (*#all*| List of (*extension*| *restriction*))
final = (*#all*| List of (*extension*| *restriction*))
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
mixed = [boolean](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#boolean)
name = [NCName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NCName)
defaultAttributesApply = [boolean](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#boolean): true
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, ([simpleContent](#element-simpleContent) | [complexContent](#element-complexContent) | ([openContent](#element-openContent)?, ([group](#element-group) | [all](#element-all) | [choice](#element-choice) | [sequence](#element-sequence))?, (([attribute](#element-attribute) | [attributeGroup](#element-attributeGroup))*, [anyAttribute](#element-anyAttribute)?), [assert](#element-assert)*)))
</complexType>**Note:**It is a consequence of the concrete syntax given above that a top-level type definition need consist of no more than a name, i.e. that `<complexType name="anyThing"/>`is allowed. **Note:**Aside from the simple coherence requirements outlined below, the requirement that type definitions identified as restrictions actually *be*restrictions — that is, the requirement that they accept as valid only a subset of the items which are accepted as valid by their base type definition — is enforced in [Constraints on Complex Type Definition Schema Components (§3.4.6)](#coss-ct).The following sections describe different sets of mapping rules for complex types; some are common to all or many source declarations, others only in specific circumstances.
- If the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) source declaration has a [<simpleContent>](#element-simpleContent) element as a child, then it maps to a [Complex Type Definition](#ctd) using the mapping rules in
  - [Mapping Rules for Complex Types with Simple Content (§3.4.2.2)](#dcl.ctd.ctsc),
  - [Common Mapping Rules for Complex Type Definitions (§3.4.2.1)](#dcl.ctd.common),
  - [Mapping Rule for Attribute Uses Property (§3.4.2.4)](#dcl.ctd.attuses), and
  - [Mapping Rule for Attribute Wildcard Property (§3.4.2.5)](#dcl.ctd.anyatt).

- If the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) source declaration has a [<complexContent>](#element-complexContent) element as a child, then it maps to a [Complex Type Definition](#ctd) using the mapping rules in
  - [Mapping Rules for Complex Types with Explicit Complex Content (§3.4.2.3.1)](#dcl.ctd.ctcc.explicit),
  - [Mapping Rules for Content Type Property of Complex Content (§3.4.2.3.3)](#dcl.ctd.ctcc.common),
  - [Common Mapping Rules for Complex Type Definitions (§3.4.2.1)](#dcl.ctd.common),
  - [Mapping Rule for Attribute Uses Property (§3.4.2.4)](#dcl.ctd.attuses), and
  - [Mapping Rule for Attribute Wildcard Property (§3.4.2.5)](#dcl.ctd.anyatt).

- If the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) source declaration has neither a [<simpleContent>](#element-simpleContent) nor a [<complexContent>](#element-complexContent) element as a child, then it maps to a [Complex Type Definition](#ctd) using the mapping rules in
  - [Mapping Rules for Complex Types with Implicit Complex Content (§3.4.2.3.2)](#dcl.ctd.ctcc.implicit),
  - [Mapping Rules for Content Type Property of Complex Content (§3.4.2.3.3)](#dcl.ctd.ctcc.common),
  - [Common Mapping Rules for Complex Type Definitions (§3.4.2.1)](#dcl.ctd.common),
  - [Mapping Rule for Attribute Uses Property (§3.4.2.4)](#dcl.ctd.attuses), and
  - [Mapping Rule for Attribute Wildcard Property (§3.4.2.5)](#dcl.ctd.anyatt).

Where convenient, the mapping rules are described exclusively in terms of the schema document's information set. The mappings, however, depend not only upon the source declaration but also upon the schema context. Some mappings, that is, depend on the properties of other components in the schema. In particular, several of the mapping rules given in the following sections depend upon the [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition) having been identified before they apply.

##### <a id="dcl.ctd.common"></a>3.4.2.1 Common Mapping Rules for Complex Type Definitions

<a id="xr.ct1"></a>
Whichever alternative for the content of [<complexType>](#element-complexType) is chosen, the following property mappings apply. Except where otherwise specified, attributes and child elements are to be sought among the [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) element.

XML Mapping Summary for [Complex Type Definition](#Complex_Type_Definition_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{name}](#ctd-name)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `name`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) if present, otherwise [·absent·](#key-null).[{target namespace}](#ctd-target_namespace)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `targetNamespace`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the [<schema>](#element-schema) ancestor element information item if present, otherwise [·absent·](#key-null).[{abstract}](#ctd-abstract)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `abstract`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise ***false***.[{prohibited substitutions}](#ctd-prohibited_substitutions)A set corresponding to the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `block`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise to the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `blockDefault`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the ancestor [<schema>](#element-schema) element information item, if present, otherwise on the empty string. Call this the **EBV**(for effective block value).
