Then the value of this property is the appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the **EBV**is the empty string, **then **the empty set;2 **If **the **EBV**is `#all`, **then **`{`***extension***, ***restriction***`}`;3 **otherwise **a set with members drawn from the set above, each being present or absent depending on whether the [·actual value·](#key-vv) (which is a list) contains an equivalently named item. **Note:**Although the `blockDefault`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of [<schema>](#element-schema)may include values other than ***restriction***or ***extension***, those values are ignored in the determination of [{prohibited substitutions}](#ctd-prohibited_substitutions) for complex type definitions (they *are*used elsewhere).[{final}](#ctd-final)As for [{prohibited substitutions}](#ctd-prohibited_substitutions) above, but using the `final`and `finalDefault`[[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) in place of the `block`and `blockDefault`[[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).[{context}](#ctd-context)<a id="anchor11290b"></a> If the `name`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is present, then [·absent·](#key-null), otherwise (among the ancestor element information items there will be a nearest [<element>](#element-element)), the [Element Declaration](#ed) corresponding to the nearest [<element>](#element-element) information item among the the ancestor element information items. [{assertions}](#ctd-assertions) A sequence whose members are [Assertion](#as)s drawn from the following sources, in order: 1 The [{assertions}](#ctd-assertions) of the [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition).2 [Assertion](#as)s corresponding to all the [<assert>](#element-assert) element information items among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of [<complexType>](#element-complexType), [<restriction>](#element-simpleContent..restriction) and [<extension>](#element-simpleContent..extension), if any, in document order. [{annotations}](#ctd-annotations) The [·annotation mapping·](#key-am-set) of the set of elements containing the [<complexType>](#element-complexType), the [<openContent>](#element-openContent)[[child]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, the [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup)[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, the [<simpleContent>](#element-simpleContent) and [<complexContent>](#element-complexContent)[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, and their [<restriction>](#element-simpleContent..restriction) and [<extension>](#element-simpleContent..extension)[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, and their [<openContent>](#element-openContent) and [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup)[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, as defined in [XML Representation of Annotation Schema Components (§3.15.2)](#declare-annotation). **Note:**If the [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition) is a complex type definition, then the [{assertions}](#ctd-assertions) always contain members of the [{assertions}](#ctd-assertions) of the [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition), no matter which alternatives are chosen in the XML representation, [<simpleContent>](#element-simpleContent) or [<complexContent>](#element-complexContent), [<restriction>](#element-simpleContent..restriction) or [<extension>](#element-simpleContent..extension).
##### <a id="dcl.ctd.ctsc"></a>3.4.2.2 Mapping Rules for Complex Types with Simple Content

<a id="xr.ct3"></a>
When the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) source declaration has a [<simpleContent>](#element-simpleContent) child, the following elements are relevant (as are [<attribute>](#element-attribute), [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup), and [<anyAttribute>](#element-anyAttribute)), and the property mappings are as below, supplemented by the mappings in [Common Mapping Rules for Complex Type Definitions (§3.4.2.1)](#dcl.ctd.common), [Mapping Rule for Attribute Uses Property (§3.4.2.4)](#dcl.ctd.attuses), and [Mapping Rule for Attribute Wildcard Property (§3.4.2.5)](#dcl.ctd.anyatt). Note that either [<restriction>](#element-simpleContent..restriction) or [<extension>](#element-simpleContent..extension)must appear in the content of [<simpleContent>](#element-simpleContent).

XML Representation Summary: `simpleContent`Element Information Item et al.<a id="element-simpleContent"></a><simpleContent
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, ([restriction](#element-simpleContent..restriction) | [extension](#element-simpleContent..extension)))
</simpleContent><a id="element-simpleContent..restriction"></a><restriction
**base**= [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, ([simpleType](#element-simpleType)?, ([minExclusive](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-minExclusive) | [minInclusive](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-minInclusive) | [maxExclusive](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-maxExclusive) | [maxInclusive](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-maxInclusive) | [totalDigits](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-totalDigits) | [fractionDigits](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-fractionDigits) | [length](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-length) | [minLength](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-minLength) | [maxLength](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-maxLength) | [enumeration](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-enumeration) | [whiteSpace](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-whiteSpace) | [pattern](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-pattern) | [assertion](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-assertion) | *{any with namespace: ##other}*)*)?, (([attribute](#element-attribute) | [attributeGroup](#element-attributeGroup))*, [anyAttribute](#element-anyAttribute)?), [assert](#element-assert)*)
</restriction><a id="element-simpleContent..extension"></a><extension
**base**= [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, (([attribute](#element-attribute) | [attributeGroup](#element-attributeGroup))*, [anyAttribute](#element-anyAttribute)?), [assert](#element-assert)*)
</extension>
When the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) element has a [<simpleContent>](#element-simpleContent) child, then the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) element maps to a complex type with simple content, as follows.

##### <a id="dcl.ctd.ctcc"></a>3.4.2.3 Mapping Rules for Complex Types with Complex Content

<a id="xr.ct10"></a>
When the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) element does not have a [<simpleContent>](#element-simpleContent) child element, then it maps to a complex type with complex content. The following elements are relevant (as are the [<attribute>](#element-attribute), [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup), and [<anyAttribute>](#element-anyAttribute) elements, which are described more fully in [XML Representation of Attribute Declaration Schema Components (§3.2.2)](#declare-attribute), [Mapping Rule for Attribute Uses Property (§3.4.2.4)](#dcl.ctd.attuses), and [XML Representation of Wildcard Schema Components (§3.10.2)](#declare-openness), respectively, and which are not repeated here), and the additional property mappings are as below, supplemented by the mappings in [Common Mapping Rules for Complex Type Definitions (§3.4.2.1)](#dcl.ctd.common), [Mapping Rule for Attribute Uses Property (§3.4.2.4)](#dcl.ctd.attuses), [Mapping Rule for Attribute Wildcard Property (§3.4.2.5)](#dcl.ctd.anyatt), [Mapping Rules for Local Attribute Declarations (§3.2.2.2)](#dcl.att.local), and [Mapping Rules for References to Top-level Attribute Declarations (§3.2.2.3)](#ref.att.local). Note that either [<restriction>](#element-complexContent..restriction) or [<extension>](#element-complexContent..extension)must appear in the content of [<complexContent>](#element-complexContent), but their content models are different in this case from the case above when they occur as children of [<simpleContent>](#element-simpleContent).

<a id="xr.ct11"></a>

XML Representation Summary: `complexContent`Element Information Item et al.<a id="element-complexContent"></a><complexContent
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
mixed = [boolean](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#boolean)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, ([restriction](#element-complexContent..restriction) | [extension](#element-complexContent..extension)))
</complexContent><a id="element-complexContent..restriction"></a><restriction
**base**= [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, [openContent](#element-openContent)?, ([group](#element-group) | [all](#element-all) | [choice](#element-choice) | [sequence](#element-sequence))?, (([attribute](#element-attribute) | [attributeGroup](#element-attributeGroup))*, [anyAttribute](#element-anyAttribute)?), [assert](#element-assert)*)
</restriction><a id="element-complexContent..extension"></a><extension
**base**= [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, [openContent](#element-openContent)?, (([group](#element-group) | [all](#element-all) | [choice](#element-choice) | [sequence](#element-sequence))?, (([attribute](#element-attribute) | [attributeGroup](#element-attributeGroup))*, [anyAttribute](#element-anyAttribute)?), [assert](#element-assert)*))
</extension><a id="element-openContent"></a><openContent
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
mode = (*none*| *interleave*| *suffix*) : interleave
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, [any](#element-any)?)
</openContent>
Complex types with complex content can be the image of two different forms of [<complexType>](#element-complexType) element: one with a [<complexContent>](#element-complexContent) child (discussed in [Mapping Rules for Complex Types with Explicit Complex Content (§3.4.2.3.1)](#dcl.ctd.ctcc.explicit)), and one with neither [<simpleContent>](#element-simpleContent) nor [<complexContent>](#element-complexContent) as a child (discussed in [Mapping Rules for Complex Types with Implicit Complex Content (§3.4.2.3.2)](#dcl.ctd.ctcc.implicit)). The mapping of the [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) is the same in both cases; it is described in [Mapping Rules for Content Type Property of Complex Content (§3.4.2.3.3)](#dcl.ctd.ctcc.common).

###### <a id="dcl.ctd.ctcc.explicit"></a>3.4.2.3.1 Mapping Rules for Complex Types with Explicit Complex Content

When the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) source declaration has a [<complexContent>](#element-complexContent) child, the following mappings apply, supplemented by those specified in [Mapping Rules for Content Type Property of Complex Content (§3.4.2.3.3)](#dcl.ctd.ctcc.common), [Common Mapping Rules for Complex Type Definitions (§3.4.2.1)](#dcl.ctd.common), [Mapping Rule for Attribute Uses Property (§3.4.2.4)](#dcl.ctd.attuses), and [Mapping Rule for Attribute Wildcard Property (§3.4.2.5)](#dcl.ctd.anyatt).

XML Mapping Summary for [Complex Type Definition with complex content](#Complex_Type_Definition_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition)The type definition [·resolved·](#src-resolve) to by the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `base`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)[{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method)If the [<restriction>](#element-complexContent..restriction) alternative is chosen, then ***restriction***, otherwise (the [<extension>](#element-complexContent..extension) alternative is chosen) ***extension***.
###### <a id="dcl.ctd.ctcc.implicit"></a>3.4.2.3.2 Mapping Rules for Complex Types with Implicit Complex Content

When the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) source declaration has neither [<simpleContent>](#element-simpleContent) nor [<complexContent>](#element-complexContent) as a child, it is taken as shorthand for complex content restricting [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType). The mapping rules specific to this situation are as follows; the mapping rules for properties not described here are as given in [Mapping Rules for Content Type Property of Complex Content (§3.4.2.3.3)](#dcl.ctd.ctcc.common), [Common Mapping Rules for Complex Type Definitions (§3.4.2.1)](#dcl.ctd.common), [Mapping Rule for Attribute Uses Property (§3.4.2.4)](#dcl.ctd.attuses), and [Mapping Rule for Attribute Wildcard Property (§3.4.2.5)](#dcl.ctd.anyatt).

XML Mapping Summary for [Complex Type Definition with complex content](#Complex_Type_Definition_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition)[·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType)[{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method)***restriction***
###### <a id="dcl.ctd.ctcc.common"></a>3.4.2.3.3 Mapping Rules for Content Type Property of Complex Content

For complex types with complex content, the [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) property is calculated as follows. (For the [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) on complex types with simple content, see [Mapping Rules for Complex Types with Simple Content (§3.4.2.2)](#dcl.ctd.ctsc).)

**Note:**The mapping rule below refers here and there to elements not necessarily present within a [<complexType>](#element-complexType) source declaration. For purposes of evaluating tests like "If the `abc`attribute is present on the `xyz`element", if no `xyz`element information item is present, then no `abc`attribute is present on the (non-existent) `xyz`element.When the mapping rule below refers to "the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)", then for a [<complexType>](#element-complexType) source declaration with a [<complexContent>](#element-complexContent) child, then the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of [<extension>](#element-complexContent..extension) or [<restriction>](#element-complexContent..restriction) (whichever appears as a child of [<complexContent>](#element-complexContent)) are meant. If no [<complexContent>](#element-complexContent) is present, then the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) source declaration itself are meant. The mapping rule also refers to the value of the [{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method) property, whose value is determined as specified in the preceding sections.XML Mapping Summary for **the `mixed`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is present on [<complexType>](#element-complexType), **then **its [·actual value·](#key-vv);1.3 **otherwise ##### <a id="dcl.ctd.attuses"></a>3.4.2.4 Mapping Rule for Attribute Uses Property

Any [<complexType>](#element-complexType) source declaration can have [<attribute>](#element-attribute) and [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) elements as children, or descendants. The [<attribute>](#element-attribute) element is described in [XML Representation of Attribute Declaration Schema Components (§3.2.2)](#declare-attribute) and will not be repeated here.

XML Representation Summary: `attributeGroup`Element Information Item<a id="element-simpleContent..attributeGroup"></a><attributeGroup
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
**ref**= [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?)
</attributeGroup>
The [<attribute>](#element-attribute) and [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) elements map to the [{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses) property of the [Complex Type Definition](#ctd) component as described below. This mapping rule is the same for all complex type definitions.

**Note:**In the following rule, references to "the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)" refer to the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the [<extension>](#element-simpleContent..extension) or [<restriction>](#element-simpleContent..restriction) element (whichever appears as a child of [<simpleContent>](#element-simpleContent) or [<complexContent>](#element-complexContent) in the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) source declaration), if present, otherwise to the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) source declaration itself. The rule also refers to the value of the [{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method) property, which is described elsewhere. XML Mapping Summary for [Complex Type Definition (Attribute Uses)](#Complex_Type_Definition_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses)
If the [<schema>](#element-schema) ancestor has a `defaultAttributes`attribute, and the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) element does not have `defaultAttributesApply`= `false`, then the [{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses) property is computed as if there were an [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup)[[child]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) with empty content and a `ref`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) whose [·actual value·](#key-vv) is the same as that of the `defaultAttributes`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) appearing after any other [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup)[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element). Otherwise proceed as if there were no such [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup)[[child]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).

Then the value is a union of sets of attribute uses as follows 1<a id="c-add1"></a>The set of attribute uses corresponding to the [<attribute>](#element-attribute)[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if any.2<a id="c-add2"></a>The [{attribute uses}](#agd-attribute_uses) of the attribute groups [·resolved·](#src-resolve) to by the [·actual value·](#key-vv)s of the `ref`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup)[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if any.3 The attribute uses "inherited" from the [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition)*T*, as described by the appropriate **case**among the following:3.1 **If ***T*is a complex type definition and [{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method) = ***extension***, **then **the attribute uses in *T*.[{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses) are inherited.3.2 **If ***T*is a complex type definition and [{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method) = ***restriction***, **then **the attribute uses in *T*.[{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses) are inherited, with the exception of those with an [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) whose [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) is **one**of the following:3.2.1<a id="att-locally-specified"></a>the [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) of the [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) of an attribute use which has already been included in the set, following the rules in clause [1](#c-add1) or clause [2](#c-add2) above;3.2.2<a id="att-prohibited"></a>the [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) of the [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) of what would have been an attribute use corresponding to an [<attribute>](#element-attribute)[[child]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if the [<attribute>](#element-attribute) had not had `use`= ***prohibited***.**Note:**This sub-clause handles the case where the base type definition *T*allows the attribute in question, but the restriction prohibits it.3.3 **otherwise **no attribute use is inherited.<a id="xr.ctd.n3bis"></a>**Note:**The *only*substantive function of the value ***prohibited***for the `use`attribute of an [<attribute>](#element-attribute) is in establishing the correspondence between a complex type defined by restriction and its XML representation. It serves to prevent inheritance of an identically named attribute use from the [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition). Such an [<attribute>](#element-attribute) does not correspond to any component, and hence there is no interaction with either explicit or inherited wildcards in the operation of [Complex Type Definition Validation Rules (§3.4.4)](#formal-complex-type) or [Constraints on Complex Type Definition Schema Components (§3.4.6)](#coss-ct). It is pointless, though not an error, for the `use`attribute to have the value ***prohibited***in other contexts (e.g. in complex type extensions or named model group definitions), in which cases the <attribute> element is simply ignored, provided that it does not violate other constraints in this specification.
##### <a id="dcl.ctd.anyatt"></a>3.4.2.5 Mapping Rule for Attribute Wildcard Property

The [{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard) property of a [Complex Type Definition](#ctd) depends on the [<anyAttribute>](#element-anyAttribute) element which may be present within the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) element or within the attribute groups referred to within [<complexType>](#element-complexType). The [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) element is described in the preceding section [Mapping Rule for Attribute Uses Property (§3.4.2.4)](#dcl.ctd.attuses) and will not be repeated here.

**Note:**The following mapping rule is the same for all complex type definitions. References to "the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)" refer to the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the [<extension>](#element-simpleContent..extension) or [<restriction>](#element-simpleContent..restriction) element (whichever appears as a child of [<simpleContent>](#element-simpleContent) or [<complexContent>](#element-complexContent) in the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) source declaration), if present, otherwise to the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) source declaration itself. The rule also refers to the value of the [{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method) property, which is described elsewhere. XML Mapping Summary for [Complex Type Definition (Attribute Wildcard)](#Complex_Type_Definition_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard)
If the [<schema>](#element-schema) ancestor has a `defaultAttributes`attribute, and the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) element does not have `defaultAttributesApply`= `false`, then the [{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard) property is computed as if there were an [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup)[[child]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) with empty content and a `ref`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) whose [·actual value·](#key-vv) is the same as that of the `defaultAttributes`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) appearing after any other [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup)[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element). Otherwise proceed as if there were no such [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup)[[child]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).

1 <a id="key-eaw"></a>[Definition:]Let the **complete wildcard**be the [Wildcard](#w) computed as described in [Common Rules for Attribute Wildcards (§3.6.2.2)](#declare-attributeGroup-wildcard).2 The value is then determined by the appropriate **case**among the following:2.1 **If **[{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method) = ***restriction***, **then **the [·complete wildcard·](#key-eaw);2.2 **If **[{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method) = ***extension***, **then **2.2.1 <a id="key-baw"></a>[Definition:]let the **base wildcard**be defined as the appropriate **case**among the following:2.2.1.1 **If **the [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition) is a complex type definition with an [{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard), **then **that [{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard).2.2.1.2 **otherwise **[·absent·](#key-null).2.2.2 The value is then determined by the first **case**among the following which applies:2.2.2.1 **If **the [·base wildcard·](#key-baw) is [·absent·](#key-null), **then **the [·complete wildcard·](#key-eaw);2.2.2.2 **If **the [·complete wildcard·](#key-eaw) is [·absent·](#key-null), **then **the [·base wildcard·](#key-baw);2.2.2.3 **otherwise **a wildcard whose [{process contents}](#w-process_contents) and [{annotations}](#w-annotations) are those of the [·complete wildcard·](#key-eaw), and whose [{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint) is the wildcard union of the [{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint) of the [·complete wildcard·](#key-eaw) and of the [·base wildcard·](#key-baw), as defined in [Attribute Wildcard Union (§3.10.6.3)](#cos-aw-union).
##### <a id="dcl.ctd.exx"></a>3.4.2.6 Examples of Complex Type Definitions

Example: Three ways to define a type for length
The following declaration defines a type for specifications of length by creating a complex type with simple content, with `xs:nonNegativeInteger`as the type of the content, and a `unit`attribute to give the unit of measurement.

```
<xs:complexType name="length1">
 <xs:simpleContent>
  <xs:extension base="xs:nonNegativeInteger">
   <xs:attribute name="unit" type="xs:NMTOKEN"/>
  </xs:extension>
 </xs:simpleContent>
</xs:complexType>

<xs:element name="width" type="length1"/>
```

An instance using this type might look like this:

```
  <width unit="cm">25</width>
```

A second approach to defining length uses two elements, one for size and one for the unit of measure. The definition of the type and the declaration of the element might look like this:

```
<xs:complexType name="length2">
 <xs:complexContent>
  <xs:restriction base="xs:anyType">
   <xs:sequence>
    <xs:element name="size" type="xs:nonNegativeInteger"/>
    <xs:element name="unit" type="xs:NMTOKEN"/>
   </xs:sequence>
  </xs:restriction>
 </xs:complexContent>
</xs:complexType>

<xs:element name="depth" type="length2"/>
```

An instance using this method might look like this:

```
  <depth>
   <size>25</size><unit>cm</unit>
  </depth>
```

A third definition of type leaves the base type implicit; at the component level, the following declaration is equivalent to the preceding one.

```
<xs:complexType name="length3">
 <xs:sequence>
  <xs:element name="size" type="xs:nonNegativeInteger"/>
  <xs:element name="unit" type="xs:NMTOKEN"/>
 </xs:sequence>
</xs:complexType>
```

Example
```
<xs:complexType name="personName">
 <xs:sequence>
  <xs:element name="title" minOccurs="0"/>
  <xs:element name="forename" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>
  <xs:element name="surname"/>
 </xs:sequence>
</xs:complexType>

<xs:complexType name="extendedName">
 <xs:complexContent>
  <xs:extension base="personName">
   <xs:sequence>
    <xs:element name="generation" minOccurs="0"/>
   </xs:sequence>
  </xs:extension>
 </xs:complexContent>
</xs:complexType>

<xs:element name="addressee" type="extendedName"/>

  <addressee>
   <forename>Albert</forename>
   <forename>Arnold</forename>
   <surname>Gore</surname>
   <generation>Jr</generation>
  </addressee>
```

A type definition for personal names, and a definition [·derived·](#key-derived) by extension which adds a single element; an element declaration referencing the [·derived·](#key-derived) definition, and a [·valid·](#key-vn) instance thereof.Example
```
<xs:complexType name="simpleName">
 <xs:complexContent>
  <xs:restriction base="personName">
   <xs:sequence>
    <xs:element name="forename" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>
    <xs:element name="surname"/>
   </xs:sequence>
  </xs:restriction>
 </xs:complexContent>
</xs:complexType>

<xs:element name="who" type="simpleName"/>

   <who>
    <forename>Bill</forename>
    <surname>Clinton</surname>
   </who>
```

A simplified type definition [·derived·](#key-derived) from the base type from the previous example by restriction, eliminating one optional child and fixing another to occur exactly once; an element declared by reference to it, and a [·valid·](#key-vn) instance thereof.Example
```
<xs:complexType name="paraType" mixed="true">
 <xs:choice minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded">
  <xs:element ref="emph"/>
  <xs:element ref="strong"/>
 </xs:choice>
 <xs:attribute name="version" type="xs:decimal"/>
</xs:complexType>
```

An illustration of the abbreviated form, with the `mixed`attribute appearing on `complexType`itself.Example
```
<xs:complexType name="name">
  <xs:openContent>
    <xs:any namespace="##other" processContents="skip"/>
  </xs:openContent>
  <xs:sequence>
    <xs:element name="given" type="xs:string"/>
    <xs:element name="middle" type="xs:string" minOccurs="0"/>
    <xs:element name="family" type="xs:string"/>
  </xs:sequence>
</xs:complexType>
```

A complex type definition that allows three explicitly declared child elements, in the specified order (but not necessarily adjacent), and furthermore allows additional elements of any name from any namespace other than the target namespace to appear anywhere in the children. ExampleTo restrict away a local element declaration that [·competes·](#key-compete) with a wildcard, use a wildcard in the derived type that explicitly disallows the element's [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname). For example:
```
<xs:complexType name="computer">
 <xs:all>
  <xs:element name="CPU" type="CPUType"/>
  <xs:element name="memory" type="memoryType"/>
  <xs:element name="monitor" type="monitorType"/>
  <xs:element name="speaker" type="speakerType"
              minOccurs="0"/>
  <!-- Any additional information about the computer -->
  <xs:any processContents="lax"
          minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>
 </xs:all>
</xs:complexType>

<xs:complexType name="quietComputer">
 <xs:complexContent>
  <xs:restriction base="computer">
   <xs:all>
    <xs:element name="CPU" type="CPUType"/>
    <xs:element name="memory" type="memoryType"/>
    <xs:element name="monitor" type="monitorType"/>
    <!-- Any additional information about the computer -->
    <xs:any processContents="lax" notQName="speaker"
            minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>
   </xs:all>
  </xs:restriction>
 </xs:complexContent>
</xs:complexType>
```

The restriction type `quietComputer`has a ***lax***wildcard, which [·matches·](#key-wc-match) any element but one with the name `speaker`.Without the specification of the `notQName`attribute, the wildcard would [·match·](#key-wc-match) elements named `speaker`, as well. In that case, the restriction would be valid only if there is a top-level declaration for `speaker`that also has type `speakerType`or a type derived from it. Otherwise, there would be instances locally valid against the restriction `quietComputer`that are not locally valid against the base type `computer`. For example, if there is no `notQName`attribute on the wildcard and no top-level declaration for `speaker`, then the following is allowed by `quietComputer`, but not by `computer`:
```
   <speaker xsi:type="xs:string"/>
```

The specific rule violated in this case is clause [2](#ctr-child-type-subsumption) of constraint [Content type restricts (Complex Content) (§3.4.6.4)](#cos-content-act-restrict)
#### <a id="sec-src-ct"></a>3.4.3 Constraints on XML Representations of Complex Type Definitions

<a id="src-ct"></a>**Schema Representation Constraint: Complex Type Definition Representation OK**
In addition to the conditions imposed on [<complexType>](#element-complexType) element information items by the schema for schema documents, **all**of the following also apply:1<a id="simple-content-rules"></a>If the [<simpleContent>](#element-simpleContent) alternative is chosen, the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) element must not have `mixed`= ***true***. 2 <a id="anchor11222bx"></a> If [<restriction>](#element-simpleContent..restriction) is present under [<simpleContent>](#element-simpleContent), then no facet-specifying element other than `xs:enumeration`, `xs:pattern`, or `xs:assertion`may appear more than once among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of [<restriction>](#element-restriction). 3 If [<openContent>](#element-openContent) is present and has `mode`≠ `'none'`, then there must be an [<any>](#element-any) among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of [<openContent>](#element-openContent). 4 If [<openContent>](#element-openContent) is present and has `mode`= `'none'`, then there must not be an [<any>](#element-any) among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of [<openContent>](#element-openContent). 5<a id="ccmixed-eq-ctmixed"></a> If the [<complexContent>](#element-complexContent) alternative is chosen and the `mixed`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is present on both [<complexType>](#element-complexType) and [<complexContent>](#element-complexContent), then [·actual values·](#key-vv) of those [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)must be the same.
#### <a id="formal-complex-type"></a>3.4.4 Complex Type Definition Validation Rules

##### <a id="sec-cdtt"></a>3.4.4.1 Locally Declared Type

This section defines the concept of [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype); this concept plays a role in determining whether restrictions and extensions of complex type definitions are legitimate. The [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) is also used to help determine the [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) and [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) of an element information item.

<a id="key-ldtype"></a>[Definition:] Every [Complex Type Definition](#ctd) determines a partial functional mapping from element or attribute information items (and their [expanded names](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname)) to type definitions. This mapping serves as a **locally declared type**for elements and attributes which are allowed by the [Complex Type Definition](#ctd).

The attribute case is simpler and will be taken first.

<a id="key-ldt-att"></a>[Definition:] For a given [Complex Type Definition](#ctd)*CTD*and a given attribute information item *A*, the [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) of *A*within *CTD*is the appropriate **case**among the following:1<a id="dummy3"></a>**If ***CTD*is [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType) , **then **[·absent·](#key-null). 2 **If ***A*has the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) as some attribute declaration *D*which is the [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) of some [Attribute Use](#au) contained by *CTD*'s [{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses) , **then **the [{type definition}](#ad-type_definition) of *D*. 3 **otherwise **the [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) of *A*within *CTD*.[{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition).
The definition for elements is slightly more complex.

<a id="key-ldt-elem"></a>[Definition:] For a given [Complex Type Definition](#ctd)*CTD*and a given element information item *E*, the [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) of *E*within *CTD*is the appropriate **case**among the following:1<a id="dummy1"></a>**If ***CTD*is [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType) , **then **[·absent·](#key-null). 2 **If ***E*has the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) as some element declaration *D*which is [·contained·](#key-contain-xpx) by *CTD*'s content model, whether [·directly·](#key-contain-dpt), [·indirectly·](#key-contain-ipx), or [·implicitly·](#key-impl-cont) , **then **the declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) of *D*. 3 **otherwise **the [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) of *E*within *CTD*.[{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition). **Note:**The constraint [Element Declarations Consistent (§3.8.6.3)](#cos-element-consistent) ensures that even if there is more than one such declaration *D*, there will be just one type definition. **Note:**The reference to [·implicit·](#key-impl-cont) containment ensures that if *E*has a [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed)[·substitutable·](#key-validly-sub) for a declaration [·contained·](#key-contain-xpx) by *CTD*'s content model, a [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) is defined for *E*.
##### <a id="sec-cvc-complex-type"></a>3.4.4.2 Element Locally Valid (Complex Type)

<a id="cvc-complex-type"></a>**Validation Rule: Element Locally Valid (Complex Type)**
For an element information item *E*to be locally [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to a complex type definition *T***all**of the following must be true:1<a id="c-cvct"></a>If *E*is not [·nilled·](#key-nilled), then **all**of the following are true:1.1 **If ***T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***empty***, **then ***E*has no character or element information item [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).1.2<a id="c-sv2"></a>**If ***T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***simple***, **then ***E*has no element information item [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), and the [·initial value·](#key-iv) of *E*is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to *T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{simple type definition}](#ct-simple_type_definition) as defined by [String Valid (§3.16.4)](#cvc-simple-type).1.3 **If ***T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***element-only***, **then ***E*has no character information item [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) other than those whose [[character code]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.character) is defined as a [white space](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml11/#NT-S) in [[XML 1.1]](#ref-xml-1.1). 1.4 **If ***T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***element-only***or *T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***mixed***, **then **the sequence of element information items in *E*.[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if any, taken in order, is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to *T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type), as defined in [Element Sequence Locally Valid (Complex Content) (§3.4.4.3)](#cvc-complex-content).2<a id="c-aam"></a>For each attribute information item *A*in *E*.[[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) excepting those named `xsi:type`, `xsi:nil`, `xsi:schemaLocation`, or `xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation`(see [Built-in Attribute Declarations (§3.2.7)](#builtin-ads)), the appropriate **case**among the following is true:2.1<a id="c-ctma"></a>**If **there is among the [{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses) an attribute use *U*whose [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) has the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) as *A*, **then ***A*is locally [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to *U*as per [Attribute Locally Valid (Use) (§3.5.4)](#cvc-au). In this case *U*.[{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) is the [·context-determined declaration·](#key-dd) for *A*with respect to [Schema-Validity Assessment (Attribute) (§3.2.4.3)](#cvc-assess-attr) and [Assessment Outcome (Attribute) (§3.2.5.1)](#sic-a-outcome). Also *A*is [·attributed to·](#key-att-to)*U*.2.2<a id="c-avaw"></a>**otherwise ****all**of the following are true:2.2.1 There is an [{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard).2.2.2 *A*is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to it as defined in [Item Valid (Wildcard) (§3.10.4.1)](#cvc-wildcard). In this case *A*is [·attributed to·](#key-att-to) the [{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard). 3<a id="c-ra"></a>For each attribute use *U*in *T*.[{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses), if *U*.[{required}](#au-required) = ***true***, then *U*.[{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) has the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) as one of the attribute information items in *E*.[[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).**Note:**It is a consequence that (with few exceptions) each such *U*will have the matching attribute information item [·attributed to·](#key-att-to) it by clause [2.1](#c-ctma) above. The exceptions are uses of `xsi:type`and the other attributes named in clause [2.1](#c-ctma); no [·attribution·](#key-att-to) is performed for them. 4 For each [·defaulted attribute·](#key-dflt-att)*A*belonging to *E*, the [{lexical form}](#vc_a-lexical_form) of *A*'s [·effective value constraint·](#key-evc) is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to *A*.[{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration).[{type definition}](#a-type_definition) as defined by [String Valid (§3.16.4)](#cvc-simple-type). **Note:**When an [{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard) is present, this does *not*introduce any ambiguity with respect to how attribute information items for which an attribute use is present amongst the [{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses) whose name and target namespace match are [·assessed·](#key-va). In such cases the attribute use *always*takes precedence, and the [·assessment·](#key-va) of such items stands or falls entirely on the basis of the attribute use and its [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration). This follows from the details of clause [2](#c-aam).5 For each element information item in *E*.[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and each attribute information item in *E*.[[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if neither the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) nor the [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) is [·absent·](#key-null), then the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) is the same as, or is [·validly substitutable·](#key-val-sub-type) for, the [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype), [·without limitation·](#key-val-sub-type-absolute). 6<a id="c-as"></a>*E*is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to each of the assertions in *T*.[{assertions}](#ctd-assertions) as per [Assertion Satisfied (§3.13.4.1)](#cvc-assertion).<a id="key-dflt-att"></a>[Definition:]A **defaulted attribute**belonging to an element information item *E*[·governed by·](#key-governing-type-elem) a complex type *T*is any [Attribute Use](#au)*U*for which **all**of the following are true:1 *U*is a member of *T*.[{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses). 2 *U*.[{required}](#au-required) = ***false***. 3 *U*'s [·effective value constraint·](#key-evc) is not [·absent·](#key-null). 4 *U*.[{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) is not one of the [Attribute Declaration](#ad)s from [Built-in Attribute Declarations (§3.2.7)](#builtin-ads). 5 *U*.[{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) does not match any of the attribute information items in *E*.[[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) as per clause [2.1](#c-ctma) of [Element Locally Valid (Complex Type) (§3.4.4.2)](#cvc-complex-type) above.
##### <a id="sec-cvc-complex-content"></a>3.4.4.3 Element Sequence Locally Valid (Complex Content)

<a id="cvc-complex-content"></a>**Validation Rule: Element Sequence Locally Valid (Complex Content)**
For a sequence *S*(possibly empty) of element information items to be locally [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to a [Content Type](#ct)*CT*, the appropriate **case**among the following must be true:1 **If ***CT*.[{open content}](#ct-open_content) is [·absent·](#key-null) , **then ***S*is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to *CT*.[{particle}](#ct-particle), as defined in [Element Sequence Locally Valid (Particle) (§3.9.4.2)](#cvc-particle). 2 **If ***CT*.[{open content}](#ct-open_content).[{mode}](#oc-mode) = ***suffix***, **then ***S*can be represented as two subsequences *S1*and *S2*(either can be empty) such that **all**of the following are true:2.1 *S*= *S1*+ *S2*2.2 *S1*is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to *CT*.[{particle}](#ct-particle), as defined in [Element Sequence Locally Valid (Particle) (§3.9.4.2)](#cvc-particle). 2.3 If *S2*is not empty, let *E*be the first element in *S2*, then *S1*+ *E*does *not*have a [·path·](#key-path) in *CT*.[{particle}](#ct-particle)2.4 Every element in *S2*is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to the wildcard *CT*.[{open content}](#ct-open_content).[{wildcard}](#oc-wildcard), as defined in [Item Valid (Wildcard) (§3.10.4.1)](#cvc-wildcard). 3 **otherwise **(*CT*.[{open content}](#ct-open_content).[{mode}](#oc-mode) = ***interleave***) *S*can be represented as two subsequences *S1*and *S2*(either can be empty) such that **all**of the following are true:3.1 *S*is a member of *S1*× *S2*(where × is the interleave operator, see [All-groups (§3.8.4.1.3)](#all-mg)) 3.2 *S1*is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to *CT*.[{particle}](#ct-particle), as defined in [Element Sequence Locally Valid (Particle) (§3.9.4.2)](#cvc-particle). 3.3 For every element *E*in *S2*, let *S3*be the longest prefix of *S1*where members of *S3*are before *E*in *S*, then *S3*+ *E*does *not*have a [·path·](#key-path) in *CT*.[{particle}](#ct-particle)3.4 Every element in *S2*is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to the wildcard *CT*.[{open content}](#ct-open_content).[{wildcard}](#oc-wildcard), as defined in [Item Valid (Wildcard) (§3.10.4.1)](#cvc-wildcard).
<a id="loc-locallyValid-seq"></a>[Definition:] A sequence of element information items is **locally valid**with respect to a [Content Type](#ct) if and only if it satisfies [Element Sequence Locally Valid (Complex Content) (§3.4.4.3)](#cvc-complex-content) with respect to that [Content Type](#ct).

##### <a id="sec-particle-attribution"></a>3.4.4.4 Attribution

<a id="anchor5748"></a><a id="key-att-to"></a>[Definition:] During [·validation·](#key-vn) of an element information item against its (complex) [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem), associations between element and attribute information items among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) on the one hand, and the attribute uses, attribute wildcard, particles and open content property record on the other, are established. The element or attribute information item is **attributed to**the corresponding component.

When an attribute information item has the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) as the [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) of an [Attribute Use](#au), then the item is **attributed to**that attribute use. Otherwise, if the item matches an attribute wildcard, as described in [Item Valid (Wildcard) (§3.10.4.1)](#cvc-wildcard), then the item is **attributed to**that wildcard. Otherwise the item is *not***attributed to**any component.

When a sequence *S*of [[child]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) element information items are checked against the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem)'s [{content type}](#ctd-content_type)*CT*, let *S1*and *S2*be subsequences of *S*such that
1. *S*is a member of *S1*× *S2*
2. *S1*is a prefix of some element sequence that is [·locally valid·](#loc-locallyValid-seq) with respect to *CT*, as defined in [Element Sequence Locally Valid (Complex Content) (§3.4.4.3)](#cvc-complex-content).
3. for every element *E*in *S2*, let *S3*be the longest prefix of *S1*where members of *S3*are before *E*in *S*, then *S3*+ *E*is *not*a prefix of any element sequence that is [·locally valid·](#loc-locallyValid-seq) with respect to *CT*.
Then members of *S1*that form a [·validation-path·](#key-vpath) in *CT*.[{particle}](#ct-particle) (see [Element Sequence Locally Valid (Complex Content) (§3.4.4.3)](#cvc-complex-content)) are **attributed to**the particles they match up with in the [·validation-path·](#key-vpath). Other members of *S1*are **attributed to**the [{open content}](#ct-open_content) of *CT*. Members of *S2*are *not***attributed to**any component. **Note:**The above definition makes sure that [·attribution·](#key-att-to) happens even when the sequence of element information items is not [·locally valid·](#loc-locallyValid-seq) with respect to a [Content Type](#ct). For example, if a complex type definition has the following content model:
```
   <xs:sequence>
     <xs:element name="a"/>
     <xs:element name="b"/>
     <xs:element name="c"/>
   </xs:sequence>
```

and an input sequence
```
   <a/><b/><d/>
```

Then the element <a> is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to the particle whose term is the "a" element declaration. Similarly, <b> is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to the "b" particle.
<a id="key-dd"></a>[Definition:] During [·validation·](#key-vn), associations between element and attribute information items among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) on the one hand, and element and attribute declarations on the other, are also established. When an item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to an [·element particle·](#key-ep), then it is associated with the declaration which is the [{term}](#p-term) of the particle. Similarly, when an attribute information item is [·attributed to·](#key-att-to) an [Attribute Use](#au), then the item is associated with the [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) of that [Attribute Use](#au). Such declarations are called the **context-determined declarations**. See clause [2.1](#c-ctma) (in [Element Locally Valid (Complex Type) (§3.4.4.2)](#cvc-complex-type)) for attribute declarations, clause [2](#c-cdde) (in [Element Sequence Locally Valid (Particle) (§3.9.4.2)](#cvc-particle)) for element declarations.

#### <a id="sec-ctdsic"></a>3.4.5 Complex Type Definition Information Set Contributions

##### <a id="sec-sic-attrDefault"></a>3.4.5.1 Attribute Default Value

<a id="sic-attrDefault"></a>**Schema Information Set Contribution: Attribute Default Value**
For each [·defaulted attribute·](#key-dflt-att), the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) has an attribute information item whose properties are as below added to the [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the element information item.In addition, if necessary [·namespace fixup·](#term-ns-fixup) is performed on the element information item for the [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration)'s [{target namespace}](#ad-target_namespace).[[local name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute)The [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration)'s [{name}](#ad-name).[[namespace name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute)The [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration)'s [{target namespace}](#ad-target_namespace).[[prefix]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute)If the [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) has a [·non-absent·](#key-null)[{target namespace}](#ad-target_namespace)*N*and the [[in-scope namespaces]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) property binds *N*to one or more prefixes, then a namespace prefix bound to *N*in the [[in-scope namespaces]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) property of the element information item in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi). If more than one prefix is bound to *N*in the [[in-scope namespaces]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), it is [·implementation-dependent·](#key-impl-dep) which of those prefixes is used. If the [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) has a [·non-absent·](#key-null)[{target namespace}](#ad-target_namespace)*N*and no prefix is bound to *N*in the [[in-scope namespaces]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) property, then (as described in the discussion of [·namespace fixup·](#term-ns-fixup)) an [·implementation-dependent·](#key-impl-dep) prefix. **Note:**In this case (i.e. when [·namespace fixup·](#term-ns-fixup) is performed), the [[in-scope namespaces]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) property is also augmented and an appropriate namespace attribute is added to the parent element's [[namespace attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element). If the [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration)'s [{target namespace}](#ad-target_namespace) is [·absent·](#key-null), then [·absent·](#key-null). [[normalized value]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute)The [·effective value constraint·](#key-evc)'s [{lexical form}](#vc_a-lexical_form).[[owner element]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute)The element information item being assessed.[[schema normalized value]](#a-schema_normalized_value)The [·effective value constraint·](#key-evc)'s [{lexical form}](#vc_a-lexical_form).[[schema actual value]](#a-schema_actual_value)The [·effective value constraint·](#key-evc)'s [{value}](#vc_a-value).[[schema default]](#a-schema_default)The [·effective value constraint·](#key-evc)'s [{lexical form}](#vc_a-lexical_form).[[validation context]](#a-validation_context)The nearest ancestor element information item with a [[schema information]](#e-schema_information) property.[[validity]](#a-validity)***valid***.[[validation attempted]](#a-validation_attempted)***full***.[[schema specified]](#a-schema_specified)***schema***.The added items also have [[type definition]](#a-type_definition) (and [[member type definition]](#a-member_type_definition) and [[member type definitions]](#a-member_type_definitions) if appropriate) properties, and their lighter-weight alternatives, as specified in [Attribute Validated by Type (§3.2.5.4)](#sic-attrType).<a id="anchor6445"></a><a id="term-ns-fixup"></a>[Definition:]When default values are supplied for attributes, **namespace fixup**may be required, to ensure that the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) includes the namespace bindings needed and maintains the consistency of the namespace information in the infoset. To perform namespace fixup on an element information item *E*for a namespace *N*: 1 If the [[in-scope namespaces]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of *E*binds a prefix to *N*, no namespace fixup is needed; the properties of *E*are not changed.2 Otherwise, first select some prefix *P*which is not bound by the [[in-scope namespaces]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of *E*(the choice of prefix is [·implementation-dependent·](#key-impl-dep)).3 Add an entry to the [[in-scope namespaces]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of *E*binding *P*to *N*.4 Add a namespace attribute to the [[namespace attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of *E*.5 Maintain the consistency of the information set by adjusting the namespace bindings on the descendants of *E*. This may be done in either of two ways:5.1 Add the binding of *P*to *N*to the [[in-scope namespaces]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of all descendants of *E*, except where that binding is overridden by another binding for *P*.5.2 Add to the [[namespace attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of each child of *E*a namespace attribute which undeclares the binding for *P*(i.e. a namespace attribute for prefix *P*whose [·normalized value·](#key-nv) is the empty string), unless that child already has a namespace declaration for prefix *P*. Note that this approach is possible only if [[XML Namespaces 1.1]](#ref-xml-namespaces-1.1) is in use, rather than [[Namespaces in XML 1.0]](#ref-xml-namespaces-1.0).The choice between the two methods of maintaining consistency in the information set is [·implementation-dependent·](#key-impl-dep). If the [·namespace fixup·](#term-ns-fixup) is occasioned by a defaulted attribute with a non-absent target namespace, then (as noted above), the [[prefix]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute) of the attribute information item supplied in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) is set to *P*.**Note:**When a default value of type [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) or [NOTATION](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NOTATION) is applied, it is [·implementation-dependent·](#key-impl-dep) whether [·namespace fixup·](#term-ns-fixup) occurs; if it does not, the prefix used in the lexical representation (in [[normalized value]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute) or [[schema normalized value]](#a-schema_normalized_value)) will not necessarily map to the namespace name of the value (in [[schema actual value]](#a-schema_actual_value)). To reduce problems and confusion, users may prefer to ensure that the required namespace information item is present in the input infoset.
##### <a id="sec-sic-match-info"></a>3.4.5.2 Match Information

<a id="sic-match-info"></a>**Schema Information Set Contribution: Match Information**
To allow users of the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) to distinguish element information items which are [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to [·element particles·](#key-ep) from those [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to [·wildcard particles·](#key-wp), if and only if the local [·validity·](#key-vn) of an element information item has been assessed as defined by [Element Locally Valid (Complex Type) (§3.4.4.2)](#cvc-complex-type), then each attribute information item in its [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) has the following properties in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi):PSVI Contributions forattribute information items
**<a id="a-attribution"></a>[attribute attribution]**
: The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the attribute information item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to an [Attribute Use](#au), **then **an [·item isomorphic·](#key-iso) to the [Attribute Use](#au).2 **If **the attribute information item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to an [{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard), **then **an [·item isomorphic·](#key-iso) to the attribute wildcard.3 **otherwise **(the item is not [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to an [Attribute Use](#au) or an [{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard)) [·absent·](#key-null).

**<a id="a-match_info"></a>[match information]**
: A keyword indicating what kind of component the attribute information item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to. The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to an [Attribute Use](#au), **then *****attribute***2 **If **the item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a ***strict***[{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard), **then *****strict***3 **If **the item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a ***lax***[{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard), **then *****lax***4 **If **the item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a ***skip***[{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard), **then *****skip***5 **otherwise **(the item is not [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to an [Attribute Use](#au) or an [{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard)) ***none***

And each element information item in its [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) has the following properties in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi):PSVI Contributions forelement information items
**<a id="e-attribution"></a>[element attribution]**
: The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the element information item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to an [·element particle·](#key-ep) or a [·wildcard particle·](#key-wp), **then **an [·item isomorphic·](#key-iso) to the [Particle](#p).2 **If **the item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to an [Open Content](#oc), **then **an [·item isomorphic·](#key-iso) to the [Open Content](#oc).3 **otherwise **(the item is not [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a [Particle](#p) or an [Open Content](#oc)) [·absent·](#key-null).

**<a id="e-match_info"></a>[match information]**
: A keyword indicating what kind of [Particle](#p) the item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to. The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to an [·element particle·](#key-ep), **then *****element***2 **If **the item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a ***strict***[·wildcard particle·](#key-wp), **then *****strict***3 **If **the item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a ***lax***[·wildcard particle·](#key-wp), **then *****lax***4 **If **the item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a ***skip***[·wildcard particle·](#key-wp), **then *****skip***5 **If **the item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to an [Open Content](#oc), **then *****open***6 **otherwise **(the item is not [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a [Particle](#p) or an [Open Content](#oc)) ***none***

#### <a id="coss-ct"></a>3.4.6 Constraints on Complex Type Definition Schema Components

All complex type definitions (see [Complex Type Definitions (§3.4)](#Complex_Type_Definitions)) must satisfy the following constraints.

##### <a id="sec-ct-props-correct"></a>3.4.6.1 Complex Type Definition Properties Correct

<a id="ct-props-correct"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Complex Type Definition Properties Correct**
**All**of the following must be true:1 The values of the properties of a complex type definition are as described in the property tableau in [The Complex Type Definition Schema Component (§3.4.1)](#Complex_Type_Definition_details), modulo the impact of [Missing Sub-components (§5.3)](#conformance-missing).2 If the [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition) is a simple type definition, the [{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method) is ***extension***.3 There are no circular definitions, except for that of [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType). That is, it is possible to reach the definition of [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType) by repeatedly following the [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition).4 No two distinct members of the [{attribute uses}](#agd-attribute_uses) have [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration)s with the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname). 5 If [{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{open content}](#ct-open_content) is [·non-absent·](#key-null), then [{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) is either ***element-only***or ***mixed***.
##### <a id="sec-cos-ct-extends"></a>3.4.6.2 Derivation Valid (Extension)

<a id="cos-ct-extends"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Derivation Valid (Extension)**
For every complex type *T*with [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition)*B*where *T*.[{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method) = ***extension***, the appropriate **case**among the following must be true:1 **If ***B*is a complex type definition, **then ****all**of the following are true:1.1 *B*.[{final}](#ctd-final) does not contain ***extension***.1.2<a id="c-cte"></a>*B*.[{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses) is a subset of *T*.[{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses). That is, for every attribute use *U*in *B*.[{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses), there is an attribute use in *T*.[{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses) whose properties, recursively, are identical to those of *U*. 1.3 If *B*has an [{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard), then *T*also has one, and *B*.[{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard).[{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint) is a subset of *T*.[{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard).[{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint), as defined by [Wildcard Subset (§3.10.6.2)](#cos-ns-subset).1.4 **One**of the following is true:1.4.1 *B*and *T*both have [{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***simple***and both have the same [{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{simple type definition}](#ct-simple_type_definition). 1.4.2 *B*and *T*both have [{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***empty***.1.4.3 **All**of the following are true:1.4.3.1 *T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***element-only***or ***mixed***.1.4.3.2 **One**of the following is true:1.4.3.2.1 *B*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***empty***.1.4.3.2.2 **All**of the following are true:1.4.3.2.2.1 Both *B*and *T*have [{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***mixed***or both have [{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***element-only***.1.4.3.2.2.2 *T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{particle}](#ct-particle) is a [·valid extension·](#cd-model-extension) of *B*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{particle}](#ct-particle), as defined in [Particle Valid (Extension) (§3.9.6.2)](#cos-particle-extend).1.4.3.2.2.3 **One or more**of the following is true:1.4.3.2.2.3.1 *B*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{open content}](#ct-open_content) (call it **BOT**) is [·absent·](#key-null). 1.4.3.2.2.3.2 *T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{open content}](#ct-open_content) (call it **EOT**) has [{mode}](#oc-mode)***interleave***. 1.4.3.2.2.3.3 Both **BOT**and **EOT**have [{mode}](#oc-mode)***suffix***. 1.4.3.2.2.4 If neither **BOT**nor **EOT**is [·absent·](#key-null), then **BOT**.[{wildcard}](#oc-wildcard).[{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint) is a subset of **EOT**.[{wildcard}](#oc-wildcard).[{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint), as defined by [Wildcard Subset (§3.10.6.2)](#cos-ns-subset).1.5 It is in principle possible to [·derive·](#key-derived)*T*in two steps, the first an extension and the second a restriction (possibly vacuous), from that type definition among its ancestors whose [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition) is [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType).**Note:**This requirement ensures that nothing removed by a restriction is subsequently added back by an extension in an incompatible way (for example, with a conflicting type assignment or value constraint).Constructing the intermediate type definition to check this constraint is straightforward: simply re-order the [·derivation·](#key-derived) to put all the extension steps first, then collapse them into a single extension. If the resulting definition can be the basis for a valid restriction to the desired definition, the constraint is satisfied.1.6<a id="c-vs-ctd-e"></a> For any element or attribute information item, its [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) within *T*is [·validly substitutable·](#key-val-sub-type) for the [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) within *B*, [·without limitation·](#key-val-sub-type-absolute), if neither is [·absent·](#key-null). 1.7 *B*.[{assertions}](#ctd-assertions) is a prefix of *T*.[{assertions}](#ctd-assertions).2 **If ***B*is a simple type definition, **then ****all**of the following are true:2.1 *T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***simple***and *T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{simple type definition}](#ct-simple_type_definition) = *B*.2.2 *B*.[{final}](#std-final) does not contain ***extension***. .
<a id="cd-ct-extension"></a>[Definition:] A complex type **T**is a **valid extension**of its [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition) if and only if **T**.[{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method) = ***extension***and *T*satisfies the constraint [Derivation Valid (Extension) (§3.4.6.2)](#cos-ct-extends).

##### <a id="sec-derivation-ok-restriction"></a>3.4.6.3 Derivation Valid (Restriction, Complex)

<a id="derivation-ok-restriction"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Derivation Valid (Restriction, Complex)**
For every complex type *T*with [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition)*B*where *T*.[{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method) = ***restriction***, **all**of the following must be true:1 *B*is a complex type definition whose [{final}](#ctd-final) does not contain ***restriction***.2 **One or more**of the following is true:2.1 *B*is [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType).2.2 **All**of the following are true:2.2.1 *T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***simple***2.2.2 **One**of the following is true:2.2.2.1 Let *S**B*be *B*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{simple type definition}](#ct-simple_type_definition), and *S**T*be *T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{simple type definition}](#ct-simple_type_definition). Then *S**T*is validly derived from *S**B*as defined in [Type Derivation OK (Simple) (§3.16.6.3)](#cos-st-derived-ok).2.2.2.2 *B*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***mixed***and *B*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{particle}](#ct-particle) is a [Particle](#p) which is [·emptiable·](#cd-emptiable) as defined in [Particle Emptiable (§3.9.6.3)](#cos-group-emptiable).2.3 **All**of the following are true:2.3.1 *T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***empty***. 2.3.2 **One**of the following is true:2.3.2.1 *B*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***empty***.2.3.2.2 *B*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***element-only***or ***mixed***, and *B*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{particle}](#ct-particle) is a [Particle](#p) which is [·emptiable·](#cd-emptiable) as defined in [Particle Emptiable (§3.9.6.3)](#cos-group-emptiable).2.4 **All**of the following are true:2.4.1 **One**of the following is true:2.4.1.1 *T*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***element-only***and *B*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***element-only***or ***mixed***. 2.4.1.2 *T*and *B*both have [{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***mixed***.2.4.2<a id="T.ct.restricts.B.ct"></a>The [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) of *T*[·restricts·](#pt-actual-restriction) that of *B*as defined in [Content type restricts (Complex Content) (§3.4.6.4)](#cos-content-act-restrict).3<a id="c-ran"></a>For every element information item *E*, if the [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of *E*satisfy clause [2](#c-aam) and clause [3](#c-ra) of [Element Locally Valid (Complex Type) (§3.4.4.2)](#cvc-complex-type) with respect to *T*, then they also satisfy the same clauses with respect to *B*, and for every attribute information item *A*in *E*.[[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), *B*'s [·default binding·](#key-dft-binding) for *A*[·subsumes·](#loc-testSubP) that defined by *T*.4<a id="c-vs-ctd-r"></a> For any element or attribute information item, its [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) within *T*is [·validly substitutable·](#key-val-sub-type) for its [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) within *B*, subject to the blocking keywords {***extension***, ***list***, ***union***}, if the item has a [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) both in *T*and in *B*. 5 *B*.[{assertions}](#ctd-assertions) is a prefix of *T*.[{assertions}](#ctd-assertions).<a id="cd-ct-restriction"></a>[Definition:]A complex type definition with [{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method) = ***restriction***is a **valid restriction**of its [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition) if and only if the constraint [Derivation Valid (Restriction, Complex) (§3.4.6.3)](#derivation-ok-restriction) is satisfied. **Note:**Valid restriction involves both a subset relation on the set of elements valid against *T*and those valid against *B*, and a derivation relation, explicit in the type hierarchy, between the types assigned to attributes and child elements by *T*and those assigned to the same attributes and children by *B*.
The constraint just given, like other constraints on schemas, must be satisfied by every complex type *T*to which it applies.

However, under certain conditions conforming processors need not (although they may) detect some violations of this constraint. If (1) the type definition being checked has *T*. [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) . [{particle}](#ct-particle) . [{term}](#p-term) . [{compositor}](#mg-compositor) = ***all***and (2) an implementation is unable to determine by examination of the schema in isolation whether or not clause [2.4.2](#T.ct.restricts.B.ct) is satisfied, then the implementation may provisionally accept the derivation. If any instance encountered in the [·assessment·](#key-va) episode is valid against *T*but not against *T*.[{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition), then the derivation of *T*does not satisfy this constraint, the schema does not conform to this specification, and no [·assessment·](#key-va) can be performed using that schema.

It is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) whether a processor (a) always detects violations of clause [2.4.2](#T.ct.restricts.B.ct) by examination of the schema in isolation, (b) detects them only when some element information item in the input document is valid against *T*but not against *T*.[{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition), or (c) sometimes detects such violations by examination of the schema in isolation and sometimes not. In the latter case, the circumstances in which the processor does one or the other are [·implementation-dependent·](#key-impl-dep).

##### <a id="sec-cos-content-act-restrict"></a>3.4.6.4 Content Type Restricts (Complex Content)

<a id="cos-content-act-restrict"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Content type restricts (Complex Content)**
<a id="pt-actual-restriction"></a>[Definition:]A [Content Type](#ct)**R**(for "restriction") with complex content (i.e. one with a [·non-absent·](#key-null)[{particle}](#ct-particle)) **restricts**another [Content Type](#ct)**B**(for "base") with complex content if and only if **all**of the following are true:1 Every sequence of element information items which is [·locally valid·](#loc-locallyValid-seq) with respect to **R**is also [·locally valid·](#loc-locallyValid-seq) with respect to **B**.2<a id="ctr-child-type-subsumption"></a>For all sequences of element information items **ES**which are [·locally valid·](#loc-locallyValid-seq) with respect to **R**, for all elements **E**in **ES**, **B**'s [·default binding·](#key-dft-binding) for **E**[·subsumes·](#loc-testSubP) that defined by **R**. <a id="key-dft-binding"></a>[Definition:] When a sequence of element information items **ES**is [·locally valid·](#loc-locallyValid-seq) with respect to a [Content Type](#ct)**CT**or when a set of attribute information items **AS**satisfies clause [2](#c-aam) and clause [3](#c-ra) of [Element Locally Valid (Complex Type) (§3.4.4.2)](#cvc-complex-type) with respect to a [Complex Type Definition](#ctd), there is a (partial) functional mapping from the element information items *E*in the sequence *ES*or the attribute information items in *AS*to a **default binding**for the item, where the default binding is an [Element Declaration](#ed), an [Attribute Use](#au), or one of the keywords ***strict***, ***lax***, or ***skip***, as follows: 1 When the item has a [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed), the default binding is that [Element Declaration](#ed).2 When the item has a [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad) and it is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to an [Attribute Use](#au), the default binding is that [Attribute Use](#au). 3 When the item has a [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad) and it is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to an attribute wildcard, the default binding is an [Attribute Use](#au) whose [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) is the [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad), whose [{value constraint}](#au-value_constraint) is [·absent·](#key-null), and whose [{inheritable}](#au-inheritable) is the [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad)'s [{inheritable}](#ad-inheritable) (the other properties in the [Attribute Use](#au) are not relevant). 4 When the item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a ***strict***[·wildcard particle·](#key-wp) or attribute wildcard or an [Open Content](#oc) with a ***strict***[Wildcard](#w) and it does not have a [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) or a [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad), then the default binding is the keyword ***strict***.5 When the item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a ***lax***[·wildcard particle·](#key-wp) or attribute wildcard or an [Open Content](#oc) with a ***lax***[Wildcard](#w) and it does not have a [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) or a [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad), then the default binding is the keyword ***lax***.6 When the item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a ***skip***[·wildcard particle·](#key-wp) or attribute wildcard or an [Open Content](#oc) with a ***skip***[Wildcard](#w) then the default binding is the keyword ***skip***.<a id="loc-testSubP"></a>[Definition:]A [·default binding·](#key-dft-binding)**G**(for general) **subsumes**another [·default binding·](#key-dft-binding)**S**(for specific) if and only if **one**of the following is true1 **G**is ***skip***.2 **G**is ***lax***and **S**is not ***skip***.3 Both **G**and **S**are ***strict***.4 **G**and **S**are both Element Declarations and **all**of the following are
