true:4.1 Either **G**.[{nillable}](#ed-nillable) = ***true***or **S**.[{nillable}](#ed-nillable) = ***false***.4.2 Either **G**has no [{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint), or it is not ***fixed***, or **S**has a ***fixed***[{value constraint}](#ed-value_constraint) with an equal or identical value.4.3 **S**.[{identity-constraint definitions}](#ed-identity-constraint_definitions) is a superset of **G**.[{identity-constraint definitions}](#ed-identity-constraint_definitions).4.4 **S**disallows a superset of the substitutions that **G**does.4.5<a id="c-vs-ct"></a>**S**'s declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) is [·validly substitutable as a restriction·](#key-val-sub-type-restricts) for **G**'s declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition).4.6<a id="c-tt-equiv"></a>**S**.[{type table}](#ed-type_table) and **G**.[{type table}](#ed-type_table) either are both [·absent·](#key-null) or are both [·present·](#key-nonnull) and [·equivalent·](#key-equiv-tt). 5 **G**and **S**are both [Attribute Use](#au)s and **all**of the following are true:5.1 *S*.[{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration).[{type definition}](#ad-type_definition) is validly [·derived·](#key-derived) from *G*.[{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration).[{type definition}](#ad-type_definition), as defined in [Type Derivation OK (Simple) (§3.16.6.3)](#cos-st-derived-ok).5.2 Let **GVC**be *G*'s [·effective value constraint·](#key-evc) and **SVC**be *S*'s [·effective value constraint·](#key-evc), then **one or more**of the following is true:5.2.1 **GVC**is [·absent·](#key-null) or has [{variety}](#vc_a-variety)***default***.5.2.2 **SVC**.[{variety}](#vc_a-variety) = ***fixed***and **SVC**.[{value}](#vc_a-value) is equal or identical to **GVC**.[{value}](#vc_a-value).5.3 *G*.[{inheritable}](#au-inheritable) = *S*.[{inheritable}](#au-inheritable). **Note:**To restrict a complex type definition with a simple base type definition to ***empty***, use a simple type definition with a ***fixed***value of the empty string: this preserves the type information.**Note:**To restrict away a local element declaration that [·competes·](#key-compete) with a wildcard, use a wildcard in the derived type that explicitly disallows the element's [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname). See the example given in [XML Representation of Complex Type Definition Schema Components (§3.4.2)](#declare-type).
##### <a id="sec-cos-ct-derived-ok"></a>3.4.6.5 Type Derivation OK (Complex)

The following constraint defines a relation appealed to elsewhere in this specification.

<a id="cos-ct-derived-ok"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Type Derivation OK (Complex)**
For a complex type definition (call it **D**, for [·derived·](#key-derived)) to be validly [·derived·](#key-derived) from a type definition (call this **B**, for base) subject to the blocking keywords in a subset of {***extension***, ***restriction***} **all**of the following must be true:1 If **B**and **D**are not the same type definition, then the [{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method) of **D**is not in the subset.2 **One or more**of the following is true:2.1<a id="c-tid"></a>**B**= **D**.2.2 **B**= **D**.[{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition).2.3 **All**of the following are true:2.3.1 **D**.[{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition) ≠ [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType).2.3.2 The appropriate **case**among the following is true:2.3.2.1 **If ****D**.[{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition) is complex, **then **it is validly [·derived·](#key-derived) from **B**subject to the subset as defined by this constraint.2.3.2.2 **If ****D**.[{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition) is simple, **then **it is validly [·derived·](#key-derived) from **B**subject to the subset as defined in [Type Derivation OK (Simple) (§3.16.6.3)](#cos-st-derived-ok).**Note:**This constraint is used to check that when someone uses a type in a context where another type was expected (either via `xsi:type`or [·substitution groups·](#key-eq)), that the type used is actually [·derived·](#key-derived) from the expected type, and that that [·derivation·](#key-derived) does not involve a form of [·derivation·](#key-derived) which was ruled out by the expected type.<a id="no-identity"></a>**Note:**The wording of clause [2.1](#c-tid) above appeals to a notion of component identity which is only incompletely defined by this version of this specification. In some cases, the wording of this specification does make clear the rules for component identity. These cases include:
- When they are both top-level components with the same component type, namespace name, and local name;
- When they are necessarily the same type definition (for example, when the two type definitions in question are the type definitions associated with two attribute or element declarations, which are discovered to be the same declaration);
- When they are the same by construction (for example, when an element's type definition defaults to being the same type definition as that of its substitution-group head or when a complex type definition inherits an attribute declaration from its base type definition).
In other cases it is possible that conforming implementations will disagree as to whether components are identical.**Note:**When a complex type definition *S*is said to be "validly [·derived·](#key-derived)" from a type definition *T*, without mention of any specific set of blocking keywords, or with the explicit phrase "without limitation", then what is meant is that *S*is validly derived from *T*, subject to the empty set of blocking keywords, i.e. without any particular limitations.
#### <a id="builtin-ctd"></a>3.4.7 Built-in Complex Type Definition

There is a complex type definition for [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType) present in every schema by definition. It has the following properties:

<a id="any-type-itself"></a>Complex Type Definition of anyType**Property****Value**[{name}](#ctd-name)anyType[{target namespace}](#ctd-target_namespace)http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema[{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition)itself[{derivation method}](#ctd-derivation_method)***restriction***[{content type}](#ctd-content_type)A [Content Type](#ct) as follows: **Property****Value**[{variety}](#ct-variety)***mixed***[{particle}](#ct-particle)a [Particle](#p) with the properties shown below in [Outer Particle for Content Type of anyType (§3.4.7)](#anyType-outer-particle). [{simple type definition}](#ct-simple_type_definition)[·absent·](#key-null)[{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses)The empty set[{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard) a wildcard with the following properties:: **Property****Value**[{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint) A [Namespace Constraint](#nc) with the following properties: **Property****Value**[{variety}](#nc-variety)***any***[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces)The empty set[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names)The empty set[{process contents}](#w-process_contents)***lax***[{final}](#ctd-final)The empty set[{context}](#ctd-context)[·absent·](#key-null)[{prohibited substitutions}](#ctd-prohibited_substitutions)The empty set[{assertions}](#ctd-assertions)The empty sequence[{abstract}](#ctd-abstract)***false***
The outer particle of [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType) contains a sequence with a single term:

<a id="anyType-outer-particle"></a>Outer Particle for Content Type of anyType**Property****Value**[{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs)1[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs)1[{term}](#p-term)a model group with the following properties: **Property****Value**[{compositor}](#mg-compositor)***sequence***[{particles}](#mg-particles) a list containing one particle with the properties shown below in [Inner Particle for Content Type of anyType (§3.4.7)](#anyType-inner-particle).
The inner particle of [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType) contains a wildcard which matches any element:

<a id="anyType-inner-particle"></a>Inner Particle for Content Type of anyType**Property****Value**[{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs)0[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs)***unbounded***[{term}](#p-term)a wildcard with the following properties: **Property****Value**[{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint) A [Namespace Constraint](#nc) with the following properties: **Property****Value**[{variety}](#nc-variety)***any***[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces)The empty set[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names)The empty set[{process contents}](#w-process_contents)***lax*****Note:**This specification does not provide an inventory of built-in complex type definitions for use in user schemas. A preliminary library of complex type definitions is available which includes both mathematical (e.g. `rational`) and utility (e.g. `array`) type definitions. In particular, there is a `text`type definition which is recommended for use as the type definition in element declarations intended for general text content, as it makes sensible provision for various aspects of internationalization. For more details, see the schema document for the type library at its namespace name: [http://www.w3.org/2001/03/XMLSchema/TypeLibrary.xsd](https://www.w3.org/2001/03/XMLSchema/TypeLibrary.xsd).
### <a id="cAttributeUse"></a>3.5 Attribute Uses

3.5.1 [The Attribute Use Schema Component](#AU_details)
3.5.2 [XML Representation of Attribute Use Schema Components](#sec-xrau)
3.5.3 [Constraints on XML Representations of Attribute Uses](#sec-src-au)
3.5.4 [Attribute Use Validation Rules](#sec-cvc-au)
3.5.5 [Attribute Use Information Set Contributions](#sec-ausic)
3.5.6 [Constraints on Attribute Use Schema Components](#coss-attruse)
An attribute use is a utility component which controls the occurrence and defaulting behavior of attribute declarations. It plays the same role for attribute declarations in complex types that particles play for element declarations.

Example
```
<xs:complexType>
 . . .
 <xs:attribute ref="xml:lang" use="required"/>
 <xs:attribute ref="xml:space" default="preserve"/>
 <xs:attribute name="version" type="xs:decimal" fixed="1.0"/>
</xs:complexType>

```

XML representations which all involve attribute uses, illustrating some of the possibilities for controlling occurrence.
#### <a id="AU_details"></a>3.5.1 The Attribute Use Schema Component

The attribute use schema component has the following properties:

Schema Component: <a id="au"></a>Attribute Use, a kind of [Annotated Component](#ac)<a id="au-annotations"></a>{annotations} A sequence of [Annotation](#a) components. <a id="au-required"></a>{required} An xs:boolean value. Required.<a id="au-attribute_declaration"></a>{attribute declaration} An [Attribute Declaration](#ad) component. Required.<a id="au-value_constraint"></a>{value constraint} A [Value Constraint](#vc_au) property record. Optional.<a id="au-inheritable"></a>{inheritable} An xs:boolean value. Required.Property Record: <a id="vc_au"></a>Value Constraint<a id="vc_au-variety"></a>{variety} One of {default, fixed}. Required.<a id="vc_au-value"></a>{value} An [·actual value·](#key-vv). Required. <a id="vc_au-lexical_form"></a>{lexical form} A character string. Required.
[{required}](#au-required) determines whether this use of an attribute declaration requires an appropriate attribute information item to be present, or merely allows it.

[{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) provides the attribute declaration itself, which will in turn determine the simple type definition used.

[{value constraint}](#au-value_constraint) allows for local specification of a default or fixed value. This must be consistent with that of the [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration), in that if the [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) specifies a fixed value, the only allowed [{value constraint}](#au-value_constraint) is the same fixed value, or a value equal or identical to it.

See [Annotations (§3.15)](#cAnnotations) for information on the role of the [{annotations}](#au-annotations) property.

#### <a id="sec-xrau"></a>3.5.2 XML Representation of Attribute Use Schema Components

Attribute uses correspond to all uses of [<attribute>](#element-attribute) which allow a `use`attribute. These in turn correspond to *two*components in each case, an attribute use and its [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) (although note the latter is not new when the attribute use is a reference to a top-level attribute declaration). The appropriate mapping is described in [XML Representation of Attribute Declaration Schema Components (§3.2.2)](#declare-attribute).

#### <a id="sec-src-au"></a>3.5.3 Constraints on XML Representations of Attribute Uses

None as such.

#### <a id="sec-cvc-au"></a>3.5.4 Attribute Use Validation Rules

<a id="key-evc"></a>[Definition:] The **effective value constraint**of an attribute use *U*is *U*.[{value constraint}](#au-value_constraint), if present, otherwise *U*.[{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration).[{value constraint}](#ad-value_constraint), if present, otherwise the **effective value constraint**is [·absent·](#key-null).

<a id="cvc-au"></a>**Validation Rule: Attribute Locally Valid (Use)**
For an attribute information item to be [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to an attribute use its [·actual value·](#key-vv)must be equal or identical to the [{value}](#vc_a-value) of the attribute use's [{value constraint}](#au-value_constraint), if it is present and has [{variety}](#vc_a-variety)***fixed***.
#### <a id="sec-ausic"></a>3.5.5 Attribute Use Information Set Contributions

None as such.

#### <a id="coss-attruse"></a>3.5.6 Constraints on Attribute Use Schema Components

All attribute uses (see [Attribute Uses (§3.5)](#cAttributeUse)) must satisfy the following constraints.

<a id="au-props-correct"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Attribute Use Correct**
**All**of the following must be true:1 The values of the properties of an attribute use *U*are as described in the property tableau in [The Attribute Use Schema Component (§3.5.1)](#AU_details), modulo the impact of [Missing Sub-components (§5.3)](#conformance-missing).2 If *U*.[{value constraint}](#au-value_constraint) is not [·absent·](#key-null), then it is a valid default with respect to *U*.[{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration).[{type definition}](#ad-type_definition) as defined in [Simple Default Valid (§3.2.6.2)](#cos-valid-simple-default).3 If *U*.[{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) has [{value constraint}](#ad-value_constraint).[{variety}](#vc_a-variety) = ***fixed***and *U*itself has a [{value constraint}](#au-value_constraint), then *U*.[{value constraint}](#au-value_constraint).[{variety}](#vc_au-variety) = ***fixed***and *U*.[{value constraint}](#au-value_constraint).[{value}](#vc_au-value) is identical to *U*.[{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration).[{value constraint}](#ad-value_constraint).[{value}](#vc_a-value).
### <a id="cAttribute_Group_Definitions"></a>3.6 Attribute Group Definitions

3.6.1 [The Attribute Group Definition Schema Component](#Attribute_Group_Definition_details)
3.6.2 [XML Representation of Attribute Group Definition Schema Components](#declare-attributeGroup)
3.6.2.1 [XML Mapping Rule for Named Attribute Groups](#declare-attributeGroup-core)
3.6.2.2 [Common Rules for Attribute Wildcards](#declare-attributeGroup-wildcard)
3.6.3 [Constraints on XML Representations of Attribute Group Definitions](#constrain-attribute-group-xml)
3.6.4 [Attribute Group Definition Validation Rules](#sec-cvc-agd)
3.6.5 [Attribute Group Definition Information Set Contributions](#sec-sic-agd)
3.6.6 [Constraints on Attribute Group Definition Schema Components](#coss-attrGroup)
A schema can name a group of attribute declarations so that they can be incorporated as a group into complex type definitions.

Attribute group definitions do not participate in [·validation·](#key-vn) as such, but the [{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses) and [{attribute wildcard}](#ctd-attribute_wildcard) of one or more complex type definitions may be constructed in whole or part by reference to an attribute group. Thus, attribute group definitions provide a replacement for some uses of XML's [parameter entity](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml11/#dt-PE) facility. Attribute group definitions are provided primarily for reference from the XML representation of schema components (see [<complexType>](#element-complexType) and [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup)).

Example
```
<xs:attributeGroup name="myAttrGroup">
    <xs:attribute . . ./>
    . . .
</xs:attributeGroup>

<xs:complexType name="myelement">
    . . .
    <xs:attributeGroup ref="myAttrGroup"/>
</xs:complexType>
```

XML representations for attribute group definitions. The effect is as if the attribute declarations in the group were present in the type definition.
The example above illustrates the pattern mentioned in [XML Representations of Components (§3.1.2)](#xroc): The same element, in this case `attributeGroup`, serves both to define and to incorporate by reference. In the first `attributeGroup`element in the example, the `name`attribute is required and the `ref`attribute is forbidden; in the second the `ref`attribute is required, the `name`attribute is forbidden.

#### <a id="Attribute_Group_Definition_details"></a>3.6.1 The Attribute Group Definition Schema Component

The attribute group definition schema component has the following properties:

Schema Component: <a id="agd"></a>Attribute Group Definition, a kind of [Annotated Component](#ac)<a id="agd-annotations"></a>{annotations} A sequence of [Annotation](#a) components. <a id="agd-name"></a>{name} An xs:NCName value. Required.<a id="agd-target_namespace"></a>{target namespace} An xs:anyURI value. Optional.<a id="agd-attribute_uses"></a>{attribute uses} A set of [Attribute Use](#au) components. <a id="agd-attribute_wildcard"></a>{attribute wildcard} A [Wildcard](#w) component. Optional.
Attribute groups are identified by their [{name}](#agd-name) and [{target namespace}](#agd-target_namespace); attribute group identities must be unique within an [·XSD schema·](#key-schema). See [References to schema components across namespaces (<import>) (§4.2.6)](#composition-schemaImport) for the use of component identifiers when importing one schema into another.

[{attribute uses}](#agd-attribute_uses) is a set of attribute uses, allowing for local specification of occurrence and default or fixed values.

[{attribute wildcard}](#agd-attribute_wildcard) provides for an attribute wildcard to be included in an attribute group. See above under [Complex Type Definitions (§3.4)](#Complex_Type_Definitions) for the interpretation of attribute wildcards during [·validation·](#key-vn).

See [Annotations (§3.15)](#cAnnotations) for information on the role of the [{annotations}](#agd-annotations) property.

#### <a id="declare-attributeGroup"></a>3.6.2 XML Representation of Attribute Group Definition Schema Components

##### <a id="declare-attributeGroup-core"></a>3.6.2.1 XML Mapping Rule for Named Attribute Groups

The XML representation for an attribute group definition schema component is an [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) element information item. It provides for naming a group of attribute declarations and an attribute wildcard for use by reference in the XML representation of complex type definitions and other attribute group definitions. The correspondences between the properties of the information item after the appropriate [·pre-processing·](#key-pre-processing) and the properties of the component it corresponds to are given in this section.

XML Representation Summary: `attributeGroup`Element Information Item<a id="element-attributeGroup"></a><attributeGroup
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
name = [NCName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NCName)
ref = [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, (([attribute](#element-attribute) | [attributeGroup](#element-attributeGroup))*, [anyAttribute](#element-anyAttribute)?))
</attributeGroup><a id="ag_p1"></a>
When an [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) appears as a child of [<schema>](#element-schema) or [<redefine>](#element-redefine), it corresponds to an attribute group definition as below. When it appears as a child of [<complexType>](#element-complexType) or [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup), it does not correspond to any component as such.

**Note:**If the [<attributeGroup>](#element-attributeGroup) is a child of [<override>](#element-override), and it overrides a corresponding declaration in the [·target set·](#key-targetset) of its parent, it will also correspond to an attribute group definition as shown below. See [Overriding component definitions (<override>) (§4.2.5)](#override-schema) for details. XML Mapping Summary for [Attribute Group Definition](#Attribute_Group_Definition_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{name}](#agd-name)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `name`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)[{target namespace}](#agd-target_namespace)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `targetNamespace`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the [<schema>](#element-schema) ancestor element information item if present, otherwise [·absent·](#key-null).[{attribute uses}](#agd-attribute_uses) The union of the set of attribute uses corresponding to the [<attribute>](#element-attribute)[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if any, with the [{attribute uses}](#agd-attribute_uses) of the attribute groups [·resolved·](#src-resolve) to by the [·actual value·](#key-vv)s of the `ref`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup)[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if any. **Note:**As described below, circular references from [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) to [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) are not errors.[{attribute wildcard}](#agd-attribute_wildcard) The [Wildcard](#w) determined by applying the attribute-wildcard mapping described in [Common Rules for Attribute Wildcards (§3.6.2.2)](#declare-attributeGroup-wildcard) to the [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) element information item. [{annotations}](#agd-annotations) The [·annotation mapping·](#key-am-one) of the [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) element and its [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup)[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, as defined in [XML Representation of Annotation Schema Components (§3.15.2)](#declare-annotation). **Note:**It is a consequence of this rule and the rule in [XML Representation of Complex Type Definition Schema Components (§3.4.2)](#declare-type) that any annotations specified in attribute group references are included in the sequence of [Annotation](#a)s of the enclosing [Complex Type Definition](#ctd) or [Attribute Group Definition](#agd) components.
The rules given above for [{attribute uses}](#agd-attribute_uses) and [{attribute wildcard}](#agd-attribute_wildcard) specify that if an [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) element *A*contains a reference to another attribute group *B*(i.e. *A*'s [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) include an [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) with a `ref`attribute pointing at *B*), then *A*maps to an [Attribute Group Definition](#agd) component whose [{attribute uses}](#agd-attribute_uses) reflect not only the [<attribute>](#element-attribute)[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of *A*but also those of *B*and of any [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) elements referred to in *B*. The same is true for attribute groups referred to from complex types.

Circular reference is *not*disallowed. That is, it is not an error if *B*, or some [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) element referred to by *B*(directly, or indirectly at some remove) contains a reference to *A*. An [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) element involved in such a reference cycle maps to a component whose [{attribute uses}](#agd-attribute_uses) and [{attribute wildcard}](#agd-attribute_wildcard) properties reflect all the [<attribute>](#element-attribute) and [<any>](#element-any) elements contained in, or referred to (directly or indirectly) by elements in the cycle.

**Note:**In version 1.0 of this specification, circular group reference was not allowed except in the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of [<redefine>](#element-redefine). As described above, this version allows it. The effect is to take the transitive closure of the reference relation between [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) elements and take into account all their [{attribute uses}](#agd-attribute_uses) and [{attribute wildcard}](#agd-attribute_wildcard) properties.
##### <a id="declare-attributeGroup-wildcard"></a>3.6.2.2 Common Rules for Attribute Wildcards

The following mapping for attribute-wildcards forms part of the XML mapping rules for different kinds of source declaration (most prominently [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup)). It can be applied to any element which can have an [<anyAttribute>](#element-anyAttribute) element as a child, and produces as a result either a [Wildcard](#w) or the special value [·absent·](#key-null). The mapping depends on the concept of the [·local wildcard·](#key-law): <a id="key-law"></a>[Definition:] The **local wildcard**of an element information item *E*is the appropriate **case**among the following:1<a id="local-wc-yes"></a>**If ***E*has an [<anyAttribute>](#element-anyAttribute) child, **then **the [Wildcard](#w) mapped to by the [<anyAttribute>](#element-anyAttribute) element using the wildcard mapping set out in [XML Representation of Wildcard Schema Components (§3.10.2)](#declare-openness);2 **otherwise **[·absent·](#key-null).The mapping is defined as follows: 1<a id="local-wc"></a> Let *L*be the [·local wildcard·](#key-law)2 Let *W*be a sequence containing all the [·non-absent·](#key-null)[{attribute wildcard}](#agd-attribute_wildcard)s of the attribute groups referenced by *E*, in document order. 3 The value is then determined by the appropriate **case**among the following:3.1 **If ***W*is empty, **then **the [·local wildcard·](#key-law)*L*.3.2 **otherwise **the appropriate **case**among the following:3.2.1<a id="c-awi1"></a>**If ***L*is [·non-absent·](#key-null), **then **a wildcard whose properties are as follows: **Property****Value**[{process contents}](#w-process_contents)*L*.[{process contents}](#w-process_contents)[{annotations}](#w-annotations)*L*.[{annotations}](#w-annotations)[{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint)the wildcard intersection of *L*.[{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint) and of the [{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint)s of all the the wildcards in *W*, as defined in [Attribute Wildcard Intersection (§3.10.6.4)](#cos-aw-intersect)3.2.2<a id="c-awi2"></a>**otherwise **(no [<anyAttribute>](#element-anyAttribute) is present) a wildcard whose properties are as follows: **Property****Value**[{process contents}](#w-process_contents) The [{process contents}](#w-process_contents) of the first wildcard in *W*[{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint) The wildcard intersection of the [{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint)s of all the wildcards in *W*, as defined in [Attribute Wildcard Intersection (§3.10.6.4)](#cos-aw-intersect)[{annotations}](#w-annotations)[·The empty sequence·](#key-null)
#### <a id="constrain-attribute-group-xml"></a>3.6.3 Constraints on XML Representations of Attribute Group Definitions

<a id="src-attribute_group"></a>**Schema Representation Constraint: Attribute Group Definition Representation OK**
None as such.
#### <a id="sec-cvc-agd"></a>3.6.4 Attribute Group Definition Validation Rules

None as such.

#### <a id="sec-sic-agd"></a>3.6.5 Attribute Group Definition Information Set Contributions

None as such.

#### <a id="coss-attrGroup"></a>3.6.6 Constraints on Attribute Group Definition Schema Components

All attribute group definitions (see [Attribute Group Definitions (§3.6)](#cAttribute_Group_Definitions)) must satisfy the following constraint.

<a id="ag-props-correct"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Attribute Group Definition Properties Correct**
**All**of the following must be true:1 The values of the properties of an attribute group definition are as described in the property tableau in [The Attribute Group Definition Schema Component (§3.6.1)](#Attribute_Group_Definition_details), modulo the impact of [Missing Sub-components (§5.3)](#conformance-missing);2 No two distinct members of the [{attribute uses}](#agd-attribute_uses) have [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration)s with the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname).
### <a id="cModel_Group_Definitions"></a>3.7 Model Group Definitions

3.7.1 [The Model Group Definition Schema Component](#Model_Group_Definition_details)
3.7.2 [XML Representation of Model Group Definition Schema Components](#declare-namedModelGroup)
3.7.3 [Constraints on XML Representations of Model Group Definitions](#sec-src-model_group_defn)
3.7.4 [Model Group Definition Validation Rules](#sec-cvc-mg)
3.7.5 [Model Group Definition Information Set Contributions](#sec-sic-mg)
3.7.6 [Constraints on Model Group Definition Schema Components](#coss-groupDef)
A model group definition associates a name and optional annotations with a [Model Group](#mg). By reference to the name, the entire model group can be incorporated by reference into a [{term}](#p-term).

Model group definitions are provided primarily for reference from the [XML Representation of Complex Type Definition Schema Components (§3.4.2)](#declare-type) (see [<complexType>](#element-complexType) and [<group>](#element-group)). Thus, model group definitions provide a replacement for some uses of XML's [parameter entity](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml11/#dt-PE) facility.

Example
```
<xs:group name="myModelGroup">
 <xs:sequence>
  <xs:element ref="someThing"/>
  . . .
 </xs:sequence>
</xs:group>

<xs:complexType name="trivial">
 <xs:group ref="myModelGroup"/>
 <xs:attribute .../>
</xs:complexType>

<xs:complexType name="moreSo">
 <xs:choice>
  <xs:element ref="anotherThing"/>
  <xs:group ref="myModelGroup"/>
 </xs:choice>
 <xs:attribute .../>
</xs:complexType>
```

A minimal model group is defined and used by reference, first as the whole content model, then as one alternative in a choice.
#### <a id="Model_Group_Definition_details"></a>3.7.1 The Model Group Definition Schema Component

The model group definition schema component has the following properties:

Schema Component: <a id="mgd"></a>Model Group Definition, a kind of [Annotated Component](#ac)<a id="mgd-annotations"></a>{annotations} A sequence of [Annotation](#a) components. <a id="mgd-name"></a>{name} An xs:NCName value. Required.<a id="mgd-target_namespace"></a>{target namespace} An xs:anyURI value. Optional.<a id="mgd-model_group"></a>{model group} A [Model Group](#mg) component. Required.
Model group definitions are identified by their [{name}](#mgd-name) and [{target namespace}](#mgd-target_namespace); model group identities must be unique within an [·XSD schema·](#key-schema). See [References to schema components across namespaces (<import>) (§4.2.6)](#composition-schemaImport) for the use of component identifiers when importing one schema into another.

Model group definitions *per se*do not participate in [·validation·](#key-vn), but the [{term}](#p-term) of a particle may correspond in whole or in part to a model group from a model group definition.

[{model group}](#mgd-model_group) is the [Model Group](#mg) for which the model group definition provides a name.

See [Annotations (§3.15)](#cAnnotations) for information on the role of the [{annotations}](#mgd-annotations) property.

#### <a id="declare-namedModelGroup"></a>3.7.2 XML Representation of Model Group Definition Schema Components

The XML representation for a model group definition schema component is a [<group>](#element-group) element information item. It provides for naming a model group for use by reference in the XML representation of complex type definitions and model groups. The correspondences between the properties of the information item after the appropriate [·pre-processing·](#key-pre-processing) and the properties of the component it corresponds to are given in this section.

XML Representation Summary: `group`Element Information Item<a id="element-group"></a><group
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
maxOccurs = ([nonNegativeInteger](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#nonNegativeInteger) | *unbounded*)  : 1
minOccurs = [nonNegativeInteger](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#nonNegativeInteger): 1
name = [NCName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NCName)
ref = [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, ([all](#element-all) | [choice](#element-choice) | [sequence](#element-sequence))?)
</group><a id="xr.mgd1"></a>
If the item has [<schema>](#element-schema) or [<redefine>](#element-redefine) as its parent (in which case there will be a `name`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), then the item maps to a model group definition component with properties as follows:

**Note:**If the item is a child of [<override>](#element-override), it will also have a `name`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element); if it overrides a corresponding declaration in the [·target set·](#key-targetset) of its parent, it will also (in the overridden schema document) map to a component as described below. See [Overriding component definitions (<override>) (§4.2.5)](#override-schema) for details. XML Mapping Summary for [Model Group Definition](#Model_Group_Definition_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{name}](#mgd-name)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `name`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)[{target namespace}](#mgd-target_namespace)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `targetNamespace`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the [<schema>](#element-schema) ancestor element information item if present, otherwise [·absent·](#key-null).[{model group}](#mgd-model_group)A model group which is the [{term}](#p-term) of a particle corresponding to the [<all>](#element-all), [<choice>](#element-choice) or [<sequence>](#element-sequence) among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) (there must be exactly one).[{annotations}](#mgd-annotations) The [·annotation mapping·](#key-am-one) of the [<group>](#element-group) element, as defined in [XML Representation of Annotation Schema Components (§3.15.2)](#declare-annotation).
<a id="xr.mgd3"></a>
Otherwise, if the item has a `ref`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and does *not*have `minOccurs=maxOccurs=0`, then the [<group>](#element-group) element maps to a particle component with properties as follows:

XML Mapping Summary for [Particle](#Particle)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `minOccurs`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise `1`.[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs)***unbounded***, if the `maxOccurs`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) equals ***unbounded***, otherwise the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `maxOccurs`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise `1`.[{term}](#p-term)The [{model group}](#mgd-model_group) of the model group definition [·resolved·](#src-resolve) to by the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `ref`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)[{annotations}](#mgd-annotations) The [·annotation mapping·](#key-am-one) of the [<group>](#element-group) element, as defined in [XML Representation of Annotation Schema Components (§3.15.2)](#declare-annotation).
Otherwise, the [<group>](#element-group) has `minOccurs=maxOccurs=0`, in which case it maps to no component at all.

**Note:**The name of this section is slightly misleading, in that the second, un-named, case above (with a `ref`and no `name`) is not really a named model group at all, but a reference to one. Also note that in the first (named) case above no reference is made to `minOccurs`or `maxOccurs`: this is because the schema for schema documents does not allow them on the child of [<group>](#element-group) when it is named. This in turn is because the [{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs) and [{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) of the particles which *refer*to the definition are what count.
#### <a id="sec-src-model_group_defn"></a>3.7.3 Constraints on XML Representations of Model Group Definitions

None as such.

#### <a id="sec-cvc-mg"></a>3.7.4 Model Group Definition Validation Rules

None as such.

#### <a id="sec-sic-mg"></a>3.7.5 Model Group Definition Information Set Contributions

None as such.

#### <a id="coss-groupDef"></a>3.7.6 Constraints on Model Group Definition Schema Components

All model group definitions (see [Model Group Definitions (§3.7)](#cModel_Group_Definitions)) must satisfy the following constraint.

<a id="mgd-props-correct"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Model Group Definition Properties Correct**
The values of the properties of a model group definition must be as described in the property tableau in [The Model Group Definition Schema Component (§3.7.1)](#Model_Group_Definition_details), modulo the impact of [Missing Sub-components (§5.3)](#conformance-missing).
### <a id="Model_Groups"></a>3.8 Model Groups

3.8.1 [The Model Group Schema Component](#Model_Group_details)
3.8.2 [XML Representation of Model Group Schema Components](#declare-contentModel)
3.8.3 [Constraints on XML Representations of Model Groups](#sec-src-model_group)
3.8.4 [Model Group Validation Rules](#sec-cvc-mgrp)
3.8.4.1 [Language Recognition by Groups](#group-recognition)
3.8.4.2 [Principles of Validation against Groups](#group-validation)
3.8.4.3 [Element Sequence Valid](#sec-cvc-model-group)
3.8.5 [Model Group Information Set Contributions](#sec-sic-mgrp)
3.8.6 [Constraints on Model Group Schema Components](#coss-modelGroup)
3.8.6.1 [Model Group Correct](#sec-mg-props-correct)
3.8.6.2 [All Group Limited](#sec-cos-all-limited)
3.8.6.3 [Element Declarations Consistent](#sec-cos-element-consistent)
3.8.6.4 [Unique Particle Attribution](#sec-cos-nonambig)
3.8.6.5 [Effective Total Range (all and sequence)](#sec-cos-seq-range)
3.8.6.6 [Effective Total Range (choice)](#sec-cos-choice-range)
When the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of element information items are not constrained to be ***empty***or by reference to a simple type definition ([Simple Type Definitions (§3.16)](#Simple_Type_Definitions)), the sequence of element information item [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) content may be specified in more detail with a model group. Because the [{term}](#p-term) property of a particle can be a model group, and model groups contain particles, model groups can indirectly contain other model groups; the grammar for model groups is therefore recursive. <a id="key-contain-dgp"></a>[Definition:]A model group **directly contains**the particles in the value of its [{particles}](#mg-particles) property.<a id="key-contain-igx"></a>[Definition:]A model group **indirectly contains**the particles, groups, wildcards, and element declarations which are [·contained·](#key-contain-xpx) by the particles it [·directly contains·](#key-contain-dgp).<a id="key-contain-xgx"></a>[Definition:]A model group **contains**the components which it either [·directly contains·](#key-contain-dgp) or [·indirectly contains·](#key-contain-igx).

Example
```
<xs:group name="otherPets">
  <xs:all>
   <xs:element name="birds"/>
   <xs:element name="fish"/>
  </xs:all>
 </xs:group>

<xs:all>
 <xs:element ref="cats"/>
 <xs:element ref="dogs"/>
 <xs:group ref="otherPets"/>
</xs:all>

<xs:sequence>
 <xs:choice>
  <xs:element ref="left"/>
  <xs:element ref="right"/>
 </xs:choice>
 <xs:element ref="landmark"/>
</xs:sequence>
```

XML representations for the three kinds of model group, the third nested inside the second.
#### <a id="Model_Group_details"></a>3.8.1 The Model Group Schema Component

The model group schema component has the following properties:

Schema Component: <a id="mg"></a>Model Group, a kind of [Term](#t)<a id="mg-annotations"></a>{annotations} A sequence of [Annotation](#a) components. <a id="mg-compositor"></a>{compositor} One of {all, choice, sequence}. Required.<a id="mg-particles"></a>{particles} A sequence of [Particle](#p) components. specifies a sequential (***sequence***), disjunctive (***choice***) or conjunctive (***all***) interpretation of the [{particles}](#mg-particles). This in turn determines whether the element information item [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)[·validated·](#key-vn) by the model group must:
- (***sequence***) correspond, in order, to the specified [{particles}](#mg-particles);
- (***choice***) correspond to exactly one of the specified [{particles}](#mg-particles);
- (***all***) correspond to the specified [{particles}](#mg-particles). The elements can occur in any order.
When two or more element declarations contained [·directly·](#key-contain-dpt), [·indirectly·](#key-contain-ipx), or [·implicitly·](#key-impl-cont) in the [{particles}](#mg-particles) of a model group have identical names, the type definitions of those declarations must be the same.

See [Annotations (§3.15)](#cAnnotations) for information on the role of the [{annotations}](#mg-annotations) property.

#### <a id="declare-contentModel"></a>3.8.2 XML Representation of Model Group Schema Components

The XML representation for a model group schema component is either an [<all>](#element-all), a [<choice>](#element-choice) or a [<sequence>](#element-sequence) element information item. The correspondences between the properties of those information items after the appropriate [·pre-processing·](#key-pre-processing) and the properties of the component they correspond to are given in this section.

XML Representation Summary: `all`Element Information Item et al.<a id="element-all"></a><all
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
maxOccurs = (*0*| *1*) : 1
minOccurs = (*0*| *1*) : 1
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, ([element](#element-element) | [any](#element-any) | [group](#element-group))*)
</all><a id="element-choice"></a><choice
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
maxOccurs = ([nonNegativeInteger](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#nonNegativeInteger) | *unbounded*)  : 1
minOccurs = [nonNegativeInteger](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#nonNegativeInteger): 1
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, ([element](#element-element) | [group](#element-group) | [choice](#element-choice) | [sequence](#element-sequence) | [any](#element-any))*)
</choice><a id="element-sequence"></a><sequence
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
maxOccurs = ([nonNegativeInteger](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#nonNegativeInteger) | *unbounded*)  : 1
minOccurs = [nonNegativeInteger](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#nonNegativeInteger): 1
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, ([element](#element-element) | [group](#element-group) | [choice](#element-choice) | [sequence](#element-sequence) | [any](#element-any))*)
</sequence><a id="xr.mg3"></a>
Each of the above items corresponds to a particle containing a model group, with properties as follows (unless `minOccurs=maxOccurs=0`, in which case the item corresponds to no component at all):

XML Mapping Summary for [Particle](#Particle_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `minOccurs`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise `1`.[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs)***unbounded***, if the `maxOccurs`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) equals ***unbounded***, otherwise the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `maxOccurs`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise `1`.[{term}](#p-term)A model group as given below.[{annotations}](#p-annotations) The same annotations as the [{annotations}](#mg-annotations) of the model group. See below.
The particle just described has a [Model Group](#mg) as the value of its [{term}](#p-term) property, as follows.

XML Mapping Summary for [Model Group](#Model_Group_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{compositor}](#mg-compositor)One of ***all***, ***choice***, ***sequence***depending on the element information item.[{particles}](#mg-particles)A sequence of particles corresponding to all the [<all>](#element-all), [<choice>](#element-choice), [<sequence>](#element-sequence), [<any>](#element-any), [<group>](#element-group) or [<element>](#element-element) items among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), in order.[{annotations}](#mg-annotations) The [·annotation mapping·](#key-am-one) of the [<all>](#element-all), [<choice>](#element-choice), or [<sequence>](#element-sequence) element, whichever is present, as defined in [XML Representation of Annotation Schema Components (§3.15.2)](#declare-annotation).
#### <a id="sec-src-model_group"></a>3.8.3 Constraints on XML Representations of Model Groups

None as such.

#### <a id="sec-cvc-mgrp"></a>3.8.4 Model Group Validation Rules

In order to define the validation rules for model groups clearly, it will be useful to define some basic terminology; this is done in the next two sections, before the validation rules themselves are formulated.

##### <a id="group-recognition"></a>3.8.4.1 Language Recognition by Groups

Each model group *M*denotes a language *L*(*M*), whose members are the sequences of element information items [·accepted·](#key-accept) by *M*.

Within *L*(*M*) a smaller language *V*(*M*) can be identified, which is of particular importance for schema-validity assessment. The difference between the two languages is that *V*(*M*) enforces some constraints which are ignored in the definition of *L*(*M*). Informally *L*(*M*) is the set of sequences which are accepted by a model group if no account is taken of the schema component constraint [Unique Particle Attribution (§3.8.6.4)](#cos-nonambig) or the related provisions in the validation rules which specify how to choose a unique [·path·](#key-path) in a non-deterministic model group. By contrast, *V*(*M*) takes account of those constraints and includes only the sequences which are [·locally valid·](#key-lvip) against *M*. For all model groups *M*, *V*(*M*) is a subset of *L*(*M*). *L*(*M*) and related concepts are described in this section; *V*(*M*) is described in the next section, [Principles of Validation against Groups (§3.8.4.2)](#group-validation).

<a id="key-path"></a>[Definition:]When a sequence *S*of element information items is checked against a model group *M*, the sequence of [·basic particles·](#key-basic-particle) which the items of *S*match, in order, is a **path**of *S*in *M*. For a given *S*and *M*, the path of *S*in *M*is not necessarily unique. Detailed rules for the matching, and thus for the construction of paths, are given in [Language Recognition by Groups (§3.8.4.1)](#group-recognition) and [Principles of Validation against Particles (§3.9.4.1)](#particle-validation). Not every sequence has a path in every model group, but every sequence accepted by the model group does have a path. <a id="key-complete-path"></a>[Definition:]For a model group *M*and a sequence *S*in *L*(*M*), the path of *S*in *M*is a **complete path**; prefixes of complete paths which are themselves not complete paths are **incomplete paths**. For example, in the model group

```
   <xs:sequence>
    <xs:element name="a"/>
    <xs:element name="b"/>
    <xs:element name="c"/>
   </xs:sequence>
```

the sequences (`<a/><b/><c/>`) and (`<a/><b/>`) have [·paths·](#key-path) (the first a [·complete path·](#key-complete-path) and the second an [·incomplete path·](#key-complete-path)), but the sequences (`<a/><b/><c/><d/>`) and (`<a/><x/>`) do not have paths.

**Note:**It is possible, but unusual, for a model group to have some paths which are neither complete paths, nor prefixes of complete paths. For example, the model group
```
   <xs:sequence>
    <xs:element name="a"/>
    <xs:element name="b"/>
    <xs:choice/>
   </xs:sequence>
```

accepts no sequences because the empty `choice`recognizes no input sequences. But the sequences (`<a/>`) and (`<a/><b/>`) have paths in the model group.
The definitions of *L*(*M*) and [·paths·](#key-path) in *M*, when *M*is a [·basic term·](#key-basic-term) or a [·basic particle·](#key-basic-particle), are given in [Principles of Validation against Particles (§3.9.4.1)](#particle-validation). The definitions for groups are given below.

###### <a id="sec-sequences"></a>3.8.4.1.1 Sequences

This section defines *L*(*M*), the set of [·paths·](#key-path) in *M*, and *V*(*M*), if *M*is a sequence group.

If *M*is a [Model Group](#mg), and the [{compositor}](#mg-compositor) of *M*is ***sequence***, and the [{particles}](#mg-particles) of *M*is the sequence *P*1, *P*2, ..., *P**n*, then *L*(*M*) is the set of sequences *S*= *S*1 + *S*2 + ... + *Sn*(taking "+" as the concatenation operator), where *S**i*is in *L*(*P**i*) for 0 < *i*≤ *n*. The sequence of sequences *S*1, *S*2, ..., *Sn*is a [·partition·](#key-partition) of *S*. Less formally, when *M*is a sequence of *P*1, *P*2, ... *P**n*, then *L*(*M*) is the set of sequences formed by taking one sequence which is accepted by *P*1, then one accepted by *P*2, and so on, up through *P**n*, and then concatenating them together in order.

<a id="key-partition"></a>[Definition:]A **partition**of a sequence is a sequence of sub-sequences, some or all of which may be empty, such that concatenating all the sub-sequences yields the original sequence.

When *M*is a sequence group and *S*is a sequence of input items, the set of [·paths·](#key-path) of *S*in *M*is the set of all paths *Q*= *Q*1 + *Q*2 + ... + *Q**j*, where
- *j*≤ *n*, and
- *S*= *S*1 + *S*2 + ... + *S**j*(i.e. *S*1, *S*2, ..., *S**j*is a [·partition·](#key-partition) of *S*), and
- *S**i*is in *L*(*P**i*) for 0 < *i*< *j*, and
- *Q**i*is a [·path·](#key-path) of *S**i*in *P**i*for 0 < *i*≤ *j*.
Example By this definition, some sequences which do not satisfy the entire model group nevertheless have [·paths·](#key-path) in a model group. For example, given the model group *M*
```
   <xs:sequence>
    <xs:element name="a"/>
    <xs:element name="b"/>
    <xs:element name="c"/>
   </xs:sequence>
```

and an input sequence *S*
```
<a/><b/>
```

where *n*= 3, *j*= 2, then *S*1 is (`<a/>`), *S*2 is (`<b/>`), and *S*has a [·path·](#key-path) in *M*, even though *S*is not in *L*(*M*). The [·path·](#key-path) has two items, first the [Particle](#p) for the `a`element, then the [Particle](#p) for the `b`element.
When *M*is a sequence group, the set *V*(*M*) (the set of sequences [·locally valid·](#key-lvip) against *M*) is the set of sequences *S*which are in *L*(*M*) and which have a [·validation-path·](#key-vpath) in *M*. Informally, *V*(*M*) contains those sequences which are accepted by *M*and for which no element information item is ever [·attributed to·](#key-att-to) a [·wildcard particle·](#key-wp) if it can, in context, instead be [·attributed to·](#key-att-to) an [·element particle·](#key-ep). There will invariably be a [·partition·](#key-partition) of *S*whose members are [·locally valid·](#key-lvip) against [{particles}](#mg-particles) of *M*.

**Note:**For sequences with more than one [·path·](#key-path) in *M*, the [·attributions·](#key-att-to) of the [·validation-path·](#key-vpath) are used in validation and for determining the contents of the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi). For example, if *M*is
```
  <xs:sequence>
   <xs:any minOccurs="0"/>
   <xs:element name="a" minOccurs="0"/>
  </xs:sequence>
```

then the sequence (`<a/>`) has two [·paths·](#key-path) in *M*, one containing just the [·wildcard particle·](#key-wp) and the other containing just the [·element particle·](#key-ep). It is the latter which is a [·validation-path·](#key-vpath) and which determines which [Particle](#p) the item in the input is [·attributed to·](#key-att-to). **Note:**There are model groups for which some members of *L*(*M*) are not in *V*(*M*). For example, if *M*is
```
  <xs:sequence>
   <xs:any minOccurs="0"/>
   <xs:element name="a"/>
  </xs:sequence>
```

then the sequence (`<a/><a/>`) is in *L*(*M*), but not in *V*(*M*), because the validation rules require that the first `a`be [·attributed to·](#key-att-to) the [·wildcard particle·](#key-wp). In a [·validation-path·](#key-vpath) the initial `a`will invariably be [·attributed to·](#key-att-to) the [·element particle·](#key-ep), and so no sequence with an initial `a`can be [·locally valid·](#key-lvip) against this model group.
###### <a id="sec-choices"></a>3.8.4.1.2 Choices

This section defines *L*(*M*), the set of [·paths·](#key-path) in *M*, and *V*(*M*), if *M*is a choice group.

When the [{compositor}](#mg-compositor) of *M*is ***choice***, and the [{particles}](#mg-particles) of *M*is the sequence *P*1, *P*2, ..., *P**n*, then *L*(*M*) is *L*(*P*1) ∪ *L*(*P*2) ∪ ... ∪ *L*(*P**n*), and the set of [·paths·](#key-path) of *S*in *P*is the set *Q*= *Q*1 ∪ *Q*2 ∪ ... ∪ *Q**n*, where *Q**i*is the set of [·paths·](#key-path) of *S*in *P**i*, for 0 < *i*≤ *n*. Less formally, when *M*is a choice of *P*1, *P*2, ... *P**n*, then *L*(*M*) contains any sequence accepted by any of the particles *P*1, *P*2, ... *P**n*, and any [·path·](#key-path) of *S*in any of the particles *P*1, *P*2, ... *P**n*is a [·path·](#key-path) of *S*in *P*.

The set *V*(*M*) (the set of sequences [·locally valid·](#key-lvip) against *M*) is the set of sequences *S*which are in *L*(*M*) and which have a [·validation-path·](#key-vpath) in *M*. In effect, this means that if one of the choices in *M*[·attributes·](#key-att-to) an initial element information item to a [·wildcard particle·](#key-wp), and another [·attributes·](#key-att-to) the same item to an [·element particle·](#key-ep), then the latter choice is used for validation.

**Note:**For example, if *M*is
```
  <xs:choice>
   <xs:any/>
   <xs:element name="a"/>
  </xs:choice>
```

then the [·validation-path·](#key-vpath) for the sequence (`<a/>`) contains just the [·element particle·](#key-ep) and it is to the [·element particle·](#key-ep) that the input element will be [·attributed·](#key-att-to); the alternate [·path·](#key-path) containing just the [·wildcard particle·](#key-wp) is not relevant for validation as defined in this specification.
###### <a id="all-mg"></a>3.8.4.1.3 All-groups

This section defines *L*(*M*), the set of [·paths·](#key-path) in *M*, and *V*(*M*), if *M*is an all-group.

When the [{compositor}](#mg-compositor) of *M*is ***all***, and the [{particles}](#mg-particles) of *M*is the sequence *P*1, *P*2, ..., *P**n*, then *L*(*M*) is the set of sequences *S*= *S*1 × *S*2 × ... × *Sn*(taking "×" as the interleave operator), where for 0 < *i*≤ *n*, *S**i*is in *L*(*P**i*). The set of sequences {*S*1, *S*2, ..., *Sn*} is a [·grouping·](#key-grouping) of *S*. The set of [·paths·](#key-path) of *S*in *P*is the set of all [·paths·](#key-path)*Q*= *Q*1 × *Q*2 × ... × *Q**n*, where *Q**i*is a [·path·](#key-path) of *S**i*in *P**i*, for 0 < *i*≤ *n*.

Less formally, when *M*is an ***all***-group of *P*1, *P*2, ... *P**n*, then *L*(*M*) is the set of sequences formed by taking one sequence which is accepted by *P*1, then one accepted by *P*2, and so on, up through *P**n*, and then interleaving them together. Equivalently, *L*(*M*) is the set of sequences *S*such that the set {*S*1, *S*2, ..., *Sn*} is a [·grouping·](#key-grouping) of *S*, and for 0 < *i*≤ *n*, *S**i*is in *L*(*P**i*).

<a id="key-grouping"></a>[Definition:]A **grouping**of a sequence is a set of sub-sequences, some or all of which may be empty, such that each member of the original sequence appears once and only once in one of the sub-sequences and all members of all sub-sequences are in the original sequence.

For example, given the model group *M*
```
  <xs:all>
   <xs:element name="a" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="5"/>
   <xs:element name="b" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>
   <xs:element name="c" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="5"/>
  </xs:all>
```

and an input sequence *S*
```
<a/><b/><a/>
```

where *n*= 3, then *S*1 is (`<a/><a/>`), *S*2 is (`<b/>`), and the [·path·](#key-path) of *S*in *M*is the sequence containing first the [Particle](#p) for the `a`element, then the [Particle](#p) for the `b`element, then once more the [Particle](#p) for the `a`element.
The set *V*(*M*) (the set of sequences [·locally valid·](#key-lvip) against *M*) is the set of sequences *S*which are in *L*(*M*) and which have a [·validation-path·](#key-vpath) in *M*. In effect, this means that if one of the [Particles](#p) in *M*[·attributes·](#key-att-to) an element information item to a [·wildcard particle·](#key-wp), and a [·competing·](#key-compete)[Particle](#p)[·attributes·](#key-att-to) the same item to an [·element particle·](#key-ep), then the [·element particle·](#key-ep) is used for validation.

**Note:**For example, if *M*is
```
  <xs:all>
   <xs:any/>
   <xs:element name="a"/>
  </xs:all>
```

then *M*accepts sequences of length two, containing one `a`element and one other element. The other element can be anything at all, including a second `a`element. After the first `a`the [·element particle·](#key-ep) accepts no more elements and so no longer [·competes·](#key-compete) with the [·wildcard particle·](#key-wp). So if the sequence (`<a/><a/>`) is checked against *M*, in the [·validation-path·](#key-vpath) the first `a`element will be [·attributed to·](#key-att-to) the [·element particle·](#key-ep) and the second to the [·wildcard particle·](#key-wp). If the intention is not to allow the second `a`, use a wildcard that explicitly disallows it. That is,
```
  <xs:all>
   <xs:any notQName="a"/>
   <xs:element name="a"/>
  </xs:all>
```

Now the sequence (`<a/><a/>`) is not accepted by the particle.
###### <a id="sec-multiplepaths"></a>3.8.4.1.4 Multiple Paths in Groups

It is possible for a given sequence of element information items to have multiple [·paths·](#key-path) in a given model group *M*; this is the case, for example, when *M*is ambiguous, as for example

```
  <xs:choice>
   <xs:sequence>
    <xs:element ref="my:a" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>
    <xs:element ref="my:b"/>
   </xs:sequence>
   <xs:sequence>
    <xs:element ref="my:a"/>
    <xs:element ref="my:b" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>
   </xs:sequence>
  </xs:choice>
```

which can match the sequence (`<a/><b/>`) in more than one way. It may also be the case with unambiguous model groups, if they do not correspond to a [deterministic](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml11/#determinism) expression (as it is termed in [[XML 1.1]](#ref-xml-1.1)) or a "1-unambiguous" expression, as it is defined by [[Brüggemann-Klein / Wood 1998]](#ABK-DW). For example,

```
  <xs:sequence>
   <xs:element name="a" minOccurs="0"/>
   <xs:element name="a"/>
  </xs:sequence>
```

**Note:**Because these model groups do not obey the constraint [Unique Particle Attribution (§3.8.6.4)](#cos-nonambig), they cannot appear in a conforming schema.
##### <a id="group-validation"></a>3.8.4.2 Principles of Validation against Groups

As noted above, each model group *M*denotes a language *L*(*M*), whose members are sequences of element information items. Each member of *L*(*M*) has one or more [·paths·](#key-path) in *M*, as do other sequences of element information items.

By imposing conditions on [·paths·](#key-path) in a model group *M*it is possible to identify a set of [·validation-paths·](#key-vpath) in *M*, such that if *M*is a model group which obeys the [Unique Particle Attribution (§3.8.6.4)](#cos-nonambig) constraint, then any sequence *S*has at most one [·validation-path·](#key-vpath) in *M*. The language *V*(*M*) can then be defined as the set of sequences which have [·validation-paths·](#key-vpath) in *M*.

<a id="key-compete"></a>[Definition:]Two [Particles](#p)*P*1 and *P*2 contained in some [Particle](#p)*P***compete**with each other if and only if some sequence *S*of element information items has two [·paths·](#key-path) in *P*which are identical except that one path has *P*1 as its last item and the other has *P*2.

For example, in the content model

```
  <xs:sequence>
   <xs:element name="a"/>
   <xs:choice>
    <xs:element name="b"/>
    <xs:any/>
   </xs:choice>
  </xs:sequence>
```

the sequence (`<a/><b/>`) has two paths, one (*Q*1) consisting of the [Particle](#p) whose [{term}](#p-term) is the declaration for `a`followed by the [Particle](#p) whose [{term}](#p-term) is the declaration for `b`, and a second (*Q*2) consisting of the [Particle](#p) whose [{term}](#p-term) is the declaration for `a`followed by the [Particle](#p) whose [{term}](#p-term) is the wildcard. The sequences *Q*1 and *Q*2 are identical except for their last items, and so the two [Particles](#p) which are the last items of *Q*1 and *Q*2 are said to [·compete·](#key-compete) with each other.

By contrast, in the content model
```
  <xs:choice>
   <xs:sequence>
    <xs:element name="a"/>
    <xs:element name="b"/>
   </xs:sequence>
   <xs:sequence>
    <xs:element name="c"/>
    <xs:any/>
   </xs:sequence>
  </xs:choice>
```

the [Particles](#p) for `b`and the wildcard do not [·compete·](#key-compete), because there is no pair of [·paths·](#key-path) in *P*which differ only in one having the [·element particle·](#key-ep) for `b`and the other having the [·wildcard particle·](#key-wp).
<a id="key-competing-paths"></a>[Definition:]Two (or more) [·paths·](#key-path) of a sequence *S*in a [Particle](#p)*P*are **competing paths**if and only if they are identical except for their final items, which differ.

<a id="key-vpath"></a>[Definition:]For any sequence *S*of element information items and any particle *P*, a [·path·](#key-path) of *S*in *P*is a **validation-path**if and only if for each prefix of the [·path·](#key-path) which ends with a [·wildcard particle·](#key-wp), the corresponding prefix of *S*has no [·competing path·](#key-competing-paths) which ends with an [·element particle·](#key-ep).

**Note:**It is a consequence of the definition of [·validation-path·](#key-vpath) that for any content model *M*which obeys constraint [Unique Particle Attribution (§3.8.6.4)](#cos-nonambig) and for any sequence *S*of element information items, *S*has at most one [·validation-path·](#key-vpath) in *M*.
<a id="key-lvip"></a>[Definition:]A sequence *S*of element information items is **locally valid**against a particle *P*if and only if *S*has a [·validation-path·](#key-vpath) in *P*. The set of all such sequences is written *V*(*P*).

##### <a id="sec-cvc-model-group"></a>3.8.4.3 Element Sequence Valid

<a id="cvc-model-group"></a>**Validation Rule: Element Sequence Valid**
For a sequence *S*(possibly empty) of element information items to be locally [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to a model group *M*, *S*must be in *V*(*M*). **Note:**It is possible to define groups whose [{particles}](#mg-particles) is empty. When a ***choice***-group *M*has an empty [{particles}](#mg-particles) property, then *L*(*M*) is the empty set. When *M*is a ***sequence***- or ***all***-group with an empty [{particles}](#mg-particles) property, then *L*(*M*) is the set containing the empty (zero-length) sequence.
#### <a id="sec-sic-mgrp"></a>3.8.5 Model Group Information Set Contributions

None as such.

#### <a id="coss-modelGroup"></a>3.8.6 Constraints on Model Group Schema Components

All model groups (see [Model Groups (§3.8)](#Model_Groups)) must satisfy the following constraints.

##### <a id="sec-mg-props-correct"></a>3.8.6.1 Model Group Correct

<a id="mg-props-correct"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Model Group Correct**
**All**of the following must be true:1 The values of the properties of a model group are as described in the property tableau in [The Model Group Schema Component (§3.8.1)](#Model_Group_details), modulo the impact of [Missing Sub-components (§5.3)](#conformance-missing).2<a id="no-circular-groups"></a>There are no circular groups. That is, within the [{particles}](#mg-particles) of a group there is no particle at any depth whose [{term}](#p-term) is the group itself.
##### <a id="sec-cos-all-limited"></a>3.8.6.2 All Group Limited

<a id="cos-all-limited"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: All Group Limited**
When a model group has [{compositor}](#mg-compositor)***all***, then **all**of the following must be true:1 It appears only as the value of one or more of the following properties:1.1 the [{model group}](#mgd-model_group) property of a model group definition.1.2 the [{term}](#p-term) property of a [Particle](#p) with [{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs)`= 1`which is the [{particle}](#ct-particle) of the [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) of a complex type definition.1.3 the [{term}](#p-term) property of a [Particle](#p)*P*with [{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs)`=`[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs)`= 1`, where *P*is among the [{particles}](#mg-particles) of a [Model Group](#mg) whose [{compositor}](#mg-compositor) is ***all***.2 For every particle *P*in its [{particles}](#mg-particles), if *P*.[{term}](#p-term) is a model group, then *P*.[{term}](#p-term).[{compositor}](#mg-compositor) = ***all***.
##### <a id="sec-cos-element-consistent"></a>3.8.6.3 Element Declarations Consistent

<a id="cos-element-consistent"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Element Declarations Consistent**
If the [{particles}](#mg-particles) property contains, either directly, indirectly (that is, within the [{particles}](#mg-particles) property of a contained model group, recursively), or [·implicitly·](#key-impl-cont), two or more element declarations with the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname), then all their type definitions must be the same top-level definition, that is, **all**of the following must be true:1 All their declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition)s have a [·non-absent·](#key-null)[{name}](#ctd-name).2 All their declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition)s have the same [{name}](#ctd-name).3 All their declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition)s have the same [{target namespace}](#ctd-target_namespace).4 All their [{type table}](#ed-type_table)s are either all [·absent·](#key-null) or else all are present and [·equivalent·](#key-equiv-tt).If **all**of the following are true:1 The [{particles}](#mg-particles) property contains (either directly, indirectly, or [·implicitly·](#key-impl-cont)) one or more element declarations with the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname)*Q*; call these element declarations *EDS*. 2 At least **one**of the following is true2.1 The [{particles}](#mg-particles) property contains one or more ***strict***or ***lax***[·wildcard particles·](#key-wp) which [·match·](#key-wc-match)*Q*. 2.2 The [Model Group](#mg) is the [{term}](#p-term) of the [·content model·](#key-contentModel) of some [Complex Type Definition](#ctd)*CTD*and *CTD*.[{content type}](#ctd-content_type) has an [{open content}](#ct-open_content) with a ***strict***or ***lax***[Wildcard](#w) which [·matches·](#key-wc-match)*Q*. 3 There exists a top-level element declaration *G*with the [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname)*Q*. then the [{type table}](#ed-type_table)s of *EDS*and the [{type table}](#ed-type_table) of *G*must either all be [·absent·](#key-null) or else all be present and [·equivalent·](#key-equiv-tt).<a id="key-impl-cont"></a>[Definition:]A list of particles **implicitly contains**an element declaration if and only if a member of the list contains that element declaration in its [·substitution group·](#key-eq).<a id="key-equiv-tt"></a>[Definition:] A [Type Table](#tt)*T1*is **equivalent**to a [Type Table](#tt)*T2*if and only if **all**of the following are true:1 *T1*.[{alternatives}](#tt-alternatives) has the same length as *T2*.[{alternatives}](#tt-alternatives) and their corresponding entries are [·equivalent·](#key-equiv-ta). 2 *T1*.[{default type definition}](#tt-default_type_definition) and *T2*.[{default type definition}](#tt-default_type_definition) are [·equivalent·](#key-equiv-ta). <a id="key-equiv-ta"></a>[Definition:] Any [Type Alternative](#tac) is **equivalent**to itself. Otherwise, any [Type Alternative](#tac)*T1*is **equivalent**to a different [Type Alternative](#tac)*T2*if and only if *T1*.[{test}](#tac-test) and *T2*.[{test}](#tac-test) are true for the same set of input element information items and *T1*.[{type definition}](#tac-type_definition) and *T2*.[{type definition}](#tac-type_definition) accept the same set of input information items as valid. In the general case, equivalence and non-equivalence can be difficult to establish. It is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) under just what conditions a processor detects that two type alternatives are equivalent, but all processors must detect *T1*and *T2*as equivalent if **all**of the following are true:1 *T1*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{namespace bindings}](#x-namespace_bindings) and *T2*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{namespace bindings}](#x-namespace_bindings) have the same number of [Namespace Binding](#nb)s, and for each entry in *T1*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{namespace bindings}](#x-namespace_bindings) there is a corresponding entry in *T2*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{namespace bindings}](#x-namespace_bindings) with the same [{prefix}](#nb-prefix) and [{namespace}](#nb-namespace). 2 *T1*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{default namespace}](#x-default_namespace) and *T2*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{default namespace}](#x-default_namespace) either are both [·absent·](#key-null) or have the same value. 3 *T1*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{base URI}](#x-base_URI) and *T2*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{base URI}](#x-base_URI) either are both [·absent·](#key-null) or have the same value. 4 *T1*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{expression}](#x-expression) and *T2*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{expression}](#x-expression) have the same value. 5 *T1*.[{type definition}](#tac-type_definition) and *T2*.[{type definition}](#tac-type_definition) are the same type definition. A processor may treat two type alternatives as non-equivalent if they do not satisfy the conditions just given and the processor does not detect that they are nonetheless equivalent. **Note:**In the general case, equivalence can be difficult to prove, so the minimum required of implementations is kept relatively simple. Schema authors can avoid interoperability issues by ensuring that any type alternatives for which equivalence must be established do satisfy the tests above. Implementations may recognize cases when differences of namespace bindings, base URIs, and white space in the XPath expression do not affect the meaning of the expression.
##### <a id="sec-cos-nonambig"></a>3.8.6.4 Unique Particle Attribution

<a id="key-ep"></a>[Definition:] An **element particle**is a [Particle](#p) whose [{term}](#p-term) is an [Element Declaration](#ed). <a id="key-wp"></a>[Definition:] A **wildcard particle**is a [Particle](#p) whose [{term}](#p-term) is a [Wildcard](#w). Wildcard particles may be referred to as "strict", "lax", or "skip" particles, depending on the [{process contents}](#w-process_contents) property of their [{term}](#p-term).

<a id="cos-nonambig"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Unique Particle Attribution**
A content model must not contain two [·element particles·](#key-ep) which [·compete·](#key-compete) with each other, nor two [·wildcard particles·](#key-wp) which [·compete·](#key-compete) with each other.**Note:**Content models in which an [·element particle·](#key-ep) and a [·wildcard particle·](#key-wp)[·compete·](#key-compete) with each other are *not*prohibited. In such cases, the [Element Declaration](#ed) is chosen; see the definitions of [·attribution·](#key-att-to) and [·validation-path·](#key-vpath). **Note:**This constraint reconstructs for XSD the equivalent constraints of [[XML 1.1]](#ref-xml-1.1) and SGML. See [Analysis of the Unique Particle Attribution Constraint (non-normative) (§J)](#non-ambig) for further discussion.Since this constraint is expressed at the component level, it applies to content models whose origins (e.g. via type [·derivation·](#key-derived) and references to named model groups) are no longer evident. So particles at different points in the content model are always distinct from one another, even if they originated from the same named model group.**Note:**It is a consequence of [Unique Particle Attribution (§3.8.6.4)](#cos-nonambig), together with the definition of [·validation-path·](#key-vpath), that any sequence *S*of element information items has at most one [·validation-path·](#key-vpath) in any particle *P*. This means in turn that each item in *S*is attributed to at most one particle in *P*. No item can match more than one [Wildcard](#w) or more than one [Element Declaration](#ed) (because no two [·wildcard particles·](#key-wp) and no two [·element particles·](#key-ep)may[·compete·](#key-compete)), and if an item matches both a [·wildcard particle·](#key-wp) and an [·element particle·](#key-ep), it is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) by the rules for [·validation-paths·](#key-vpath) to the [·element particle·](#key-ep).**Note:**Because locally-scoped element declarations sometimes have and sometimes do not have a [{target namespace}](#ed-target_namespace), the scope of declarations is *not*relevant to enforcing either the [Unique Particle Attribution (§3.8.6.4)](#cos-nonambig) constraint or the [Element Declarations Consistent (§3.8.6.3)](#cos-element-consistent) constraint.
##### <a id="sec-cos-seq-range"></a>3.8.6.5 Effective Total Range (all and sequence)

The following constraints define relations appealed to elsewhere in this specification.

<a id="cos-seq-range"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Effective Total Range (***all***and ***sequence***)**
The effective total range of a particle *P*whose [{term}](#p-term) is a group *G*whose [{compositor}](#mg-compositor) is ***all***or ***sequence***is a pair of minimum and maximum, as follows: minimumThe product of *P*.[{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs) and the sum of the [{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs) of every wildcard or element declaration particle in *G*.[{particles}](#mg-particles) and the minimum part of the effective total range of each of the group particles in *G*.[{particles}](#mg-particles) (or `0`if there are no [{particles}](#mg-particles)).maximum***unbounded***if the [{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) of any wildcard or element declaration particle in *G*.[{particles}](#mg-particles) or the maximum part of the effective total range of any of the group particles in *G*.[{particles}](#mg-particles) is ***unbounded***, or if any of those is non-zero and *P*.[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) = ***unbounded***, otherwise the product of *P*.[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) and the sum of the [{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) of every wildcard or element declaration particle in *G*.[{particles}](#mg-particles) and the maximum part of the effective total range of each of the group particles in *G*.[{particles}](#mg-particles) (or `0`if there are no [{particles}](#mg-particles)).
##### <a id="sec-cos-choice-range"></a>3.8.6.6 Effective Total Range (choice)

<a id="cos-choice-range"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Effective Total Range (***choice***)**
The effective total range of a particle *P*whose [{term}](#p-term) is a group *G*whose [{compositor}](#mg-compositor) is ***choice***is a pair of minimum and maximum, as follows:minimumThe product of *P*.[{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs) and the minimum of the [{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs) of every wildcard or element declaration particle in *G*.[{particles}](#mg-particles) and the minimum part of the effective total range of each of the group particles in *G*.[{particles}](#mg-particles) (or `0`if there are no [{particles}](#mg-particles)).maximum***unbounded***if the [{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) of any wildcard or element declaration particle in *G*.[{particles}](#mg-particles) or the maximum part of the effective total range of any of the group particles in *G*.[{particles}](#mg-particles) is ***unbounded***, or if any of those is non-zero and *P*.[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) = ***unbounded***, otherwise the product of *P*.[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) and the maximum of the [{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) of every wildcard or element declaration particle in *G*.[{particles}](#mg-particles) and the maximum part of the effective total range of each of the group particles in *G*.[{particles}](#mg-particles) (or `0`if there are no [{particles}](#mg-particles)).
### <a id="cParticles"></a>3.9 Particles

3.9.1 [The Particle Schema Component](#Particle_details)
3.9.2 [XML Representation of Particle Schema Components](#declare-particles)
3.9.3 [Constraints on XML Representations of Particles](#sec-src-p)
3.9.4 [Particle Validation Rules](#sec-cvc-p)
3.9.4.1 [Principles of Validation against Particles](#particle-validation)
3.9.4.2 [Element Sequence Locally Valid (Particle)](#sec-cvc-particle)
3.9.4.3 [Element Sequence Accepted (Particle)](#sec-cvc-accept)
3.9.5 [Particle Information Set Contributions](#sec-sic-p)
3.9.6 [Constraints on Particle Schema Components](#coss-particle)
3.9.6.1 [Particle Correct](#sec-p-props-correct)
3.9.6.2 [Particle Valid (Extension)](#sec-cos-particle-extend)
3.9.6.3 [Particle Emptiable](#sec-cos-group-emptiable)
As described in [Model Groups (§3.8)](#Model_Groups), particles contribute to the definition of content models.

When an element is validated against a complex type, its sequence of child elements is checked against the content model of the complex type and the children are [·attributed to·](#key-att-to) to [Particles](#p) of the content model.
