The attribution of items to [Particles](#p) determines the calculation of the items' [·context-determined declarations·](#key-dd) and thus partially determines the [·governing element declarations·](#key-governing-ed) for the children: when an element information item is [·attributed to·](#key-att-to) an [·element particle·](#key-ep), that [Particle](#p)'s [Element Declaration](#ed), or an [Element Declaration](#ed)[·substitutable·](#key-validly-sub) for it, becomes the item's [·context-determined declaration·](#key-dd) and thus normally its [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed); when the item is [·attributed to·](#key-att-to) a [·wildcard particle·](#key-wp), the [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) depends on the [{process contents}](#w-process_contents) property of the wildcard and on [QName resolution (Instance) (§3.17.6.3)](#cvc-resolve-instance).

Example
```
<xs:element ref="egg" minOccurs="12" maxOccurs="12"/>

<xs:group ref="omelette" minOccurs="0"/>

<xs:any maxOccurs="unbounded"/>

```

XML representations which all involve particles, illustrating some of the possibilities for controlling occurrence.
#### <a id="Particle_details"></a>3.9.1 The Particle Schema Component

The particle schema component has the following properties:

Schema Component: <a id="p"></a>Particle, a kind of [Component](#c)<a id="p-min_occurs"></a>{min occurs} An xs:nonNegativeInteger value. Required.<a id="p-max_occurs"></a>{max occurs} Either a positive integer or ***unbounded***. Required.<a id="p-term"></a>{term} A [Term](#t) component. Required.<a id="p-annotations"></a>{annotations} A sequence of [Annotation](#a) components.
In general, multiple element information item [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), possibly with intervening character [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) if the content type is ***mixed***, can be [·validated·](#key-vn) with respect to a single particle. When the [{term}](#p-term) is an element declaration or wildcard, [{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs) determines the minimum number of such element [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) that can occur. The number of such children must be greater than or equal to [{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs). If [{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs) is ***0***, then occurrence of such children is optional.

Again, when the [{term}](#p-term) is an element declaration or wildcard, the number of such element [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)must be less than or equal to any numeric specification of [{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs); if [{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) is ***unbounded***, then there is no upper bound on the number of such children.

When the [{term}](#p-term) is a model group, the permitted occurrence range is determined by a combination of [{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs) and [{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) and the occurrence ranges of the [{term}](#p-term)'s [{particles}](#mg-particles).

<a id="key-contain-dpt"></a>[Definition:]A particle **directly contains**the component which is the value of its [{term}](#p-term) property.<a id="key-contain-ipx"></a>[Definition:]A particle **indirectly contains**the particles, groups, wildcards, and element declarations which are contained by the value of its [{term}](#p-term) property.<a id="key-contain-xpx"></a>[Definition:]A particle **contains**the components which it either [·directly contains·](#key-contain-dpt) or [·indirectly contains·](#key-contain-ipx).

See [Annotations (§3.15)](#cAnnotations) for information on the role of the [{annotations}](#p-annotations) property.

#### <a id="declare-particles"></a>3.9.2 XML Representation of Particle Schema Components

<a id="anchor11228"></a> Particles in the schema typically correspond to element information items that can bear `minOccurs`and `maxOccurs`attributes in the schema document:
- Local [<element>](#element-element), see [XML Representation of Element Declaration Schema Components (§3.3.2)](#declare-element)
- Model groups [<all>](#element-all), [<sequence>](#element-sequence), and [<choice>](#element-choice), see [XML Representation of Model Group Schema Components (§3.8.2)](#declare-contentModel)
- Group references [<group>](#element-group), see [XML Representation of Model Group Definition Schema Components (§3.7.2)](#declare-namedModelGroup)
- Wildcard [<any>](#element-any), see [XML Representation of Wildcard Schema Components (§3.10.2)](#declare-openness)
Sometimes particles do not correspond to any of these elements. For example, particles may be synthesized in complex type extension.
#### <a id="sec-src-p"></a>3.9.3 Constraints on XML Representations of Particles

None as such.

#### <a id="sec-cvc-p"></a>3.9.4 Particle Validation Rules

##### <a id="particle-validation"></a>3.9.4.1 Principles of Validation against Particles

Every particle *P*[·recognizes·](#key-accept) some language *L*(*P*). When [{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs) and [{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) of *P*are both 1, *L*(*P*) is the language of *P*'s [{term}](#p-term), as described in [Validation of Basic Terms (§3.9.4.1.2)](#term-recognition). The following section ([Language Recognition for Repetitions (§3.9.4.1.1)](#particle-recognition)) describes how more complicated counts are handled.

###### <a id="particle-recognition"></a>3.9.4.1.1 Language Recognition for Repetitions

When *P*.[{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs) = *P*.[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) = *n*, and *P*.[{term}](#p-term) = *T*, then *L*(*P*) is the set of sequences *S*= *S*1 + *S*2 + ... + *Sn*such that *S**i*is in *L*(*T*) for 0 < *i*≤ *n*. Less formally: *L*(*P*) is the set of sequences which have [·partitions·](#key-partition) into *n*sub-sequences for which each of the *n*subsequences is in the language accepted by the [{term}](#p-term) of *P*.

When *P*.[{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs) = *j*and *P*.[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) = *k*, and *P*.[{term}](#p-term) = *T*, then *L*(*P*) is the set of sequences *S*= *S*1, + *S*2 + ... + *Sn*, i.e. the set of sequences which have [·partitions·](#key-partition) into *n*sub-sequences such that *n*≥ *j*and *n*≤ *k*(or *k*is ***unbounded***) and *S**i*is in *L*(*T*) for 0 < *i*≤ *n*.

When *P*.[{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs) = 0, then *L*(*P*) also includes the empty sequence.

If (1) [Particle](#p)*P*has [{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs) = *j*, [{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) = *k*, and [{term}](#p-term) = *T*, and (2) *S*is a sequence of element information items such that *S*= *S*1 + *S*2 + ... + *Sn*(i.e. *S*1, *S*2, ..., *Sn*is a [·partition·](#key-partition) of *S*), and (3) *n*≤ *k*(or *k*is ***unbounded***), and (4) *S**i*is in *L*(*T*) for 0 < *i*< *n*, then:
- If *T*is a model group, then the set of [·paths·](#key-path) of *S*in *P*is the set of all [·paths·](#key-path)*Q*such that *Q*= *Q*1 + *Q*2 + ... + *Q**n*, where *Q**i*is a [·path·](#key-path) of *S**i*in *T*for 0 < *i*≤ *n*. (For the definition of [·paths·](#key-path) in model groups, see [Language Recognition by Groups (§3.8.4.1)](#group-recognition).)
- If *T*is a [·basic term·](#key-basic-term), then the (sole) [·path·](#key-path) of *S*in *P*is a sequence of *n*occurrences of *P*.
**Note:**Informally: the path of an input sequence *S*in a particle *P*may go through the [·basic particles·](#key-basic-particle) in *P*as many times as is allowed by *P*.[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs). If the path goes through *P*more than once, each time before the last one must correspond to a sequence accepted by *P*.[{term}](#p-term); because the last iteration in the path may not be complete, it need not be accepted by the [{term}](#p-term).
###### <a id="term-recognition"></a>3.9.4.1.2 Validation of Basic Terms

In the preceding section ([Language Recognition for Repetitions (§3.9.4.1.1)](#particle-recognition)), the language *L*(*P*) [·accepted·](#key-accept) by a [Particle](#p)*P*is defined in terms of the language [·accepted·](#key-accept) by *P*'s [{term}](#p-term). This section defines *L*(*T*) for [·basic terms·](#key-basic-term); for the definition of *L*(*T*) when *T*is a group, see [Language Recognition by Groups (§3.8.4.1)](#group-recognition).

<a id="key-L.ed"></a>[Definition:] For any [Element Declaration](#ed)*D*, the language ***L*(*D*)**[·accepted·](#key-accept) by *D*is the set of all sequences of length 1 whose sole member is an element information item which [·matches·](#key-e-d-match)*D*.

<a id="key-e-d-match"></a>[Definition:] An element information item *E***matches**an [Element Declaration](#ed)*D*if and only if **one**of the following is true:1 *E*and *D*have the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname),2 The [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) of *E*[·resolves·](#cvc-resolve-instance) to an element declaration *D*2 which is [·substitutable·](#key-validly-sub) for *D*.<a id="key-en-match"></a>[Definition:]An [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname)*E***matches**an [·NCName·](#gloss-NCName)*N*and a namespace name *NS*(or, equivalently, *N*and *NS***match***E*) if and only if all of the following are true:
- The local name of *E*is identical to *N*.
- Either the namespace name of *E*is identical to *NS*, or else *E*has no namespace name (*E*is an unqualified name) and *NS*is [·absent·](#key-null).
**Note:**For convenience, [expanded names](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) are sometimes spoken of as [·matching·](#key-en-match) a [Type Definition](#td), an [Element Declaration](#ed), an [Attribute Declaration](#ad), or other schema component which has both a {name} and a {target namespace} property (or vice versa, the component is spoken of as [·matching·](#key-en-match) the [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname)), when what is meant is, strictly speaking, that the [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname)[·matches·](#key-en-match) the {name} and {target namespace} properties of the component.
<a id="key-L.w"></a>[Definition:] For any [Wildcard](#w)*W*, the language ***L*(*W*)**[·accepted·](#key-accept) by *W*is the set of all sequences of length 1 whose sole member is an element information item which [·matches·](#key-wc-match)*W*.

<a id="anchor6159-2"></a><a id="key-wc-match"></a>[Definition:]An element information item *E***matches**a [Wildcard](#w)*W*(or a [·wildcard particle·](#key-wp) whose [{term}](#p-term) is *W*) if and only if *E*is locally [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to *W*, as defined in the validation rule [Item Valid (Wildcard) (§3.10.4.1)](#cvc-wildcard).

<a id="key-ns-match"></a>[Definition:]Two namespace names *N*1 and *N*2 are said to **match**if and only if they are identical or both are [·absent·](#key-null).

For principles of validation when the [{term}](#p-term) is a model group instead of a [·basic particle·](#key-basic-particle), see [Language Recognition by Groups (§3.8.4.1)](#group-recognition) and [Principles of Validation against Groups (§3.8.4.2)](#group-validation).

##### <a id="sec-cvc-particle"></a>3.9.4.2 Element Sequence Locally Valid (Particle)

<a id="cvc-particle"></a>**Validation Rule: Element Sequence Locally Valid (Particle)**
For a sequence (possibly empty) of element information items to be locally [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to a [Particle](#p)**all**of the following must be true:1 The sequence must be accepted by the [Particle](#p), as defined in [Element Sequence Accepted (Particle) (§3.9.4.3)](#cvc-accept).
##### <a id="sec-cvc-accept"></a>3.9.4.3 Element Sequence Accepted (Particle)

<a id="cvc-accept"></a>**Validation Rule: Element Sequence Accepted (Particle)**
For a sequence (possibly empty) of element information items to be accepted by a [Particle](#p)*P*, the appropriate **case**among the following must be true:1<a id="c-pw"></a>**If ***P*.[{term}](#p-term) is a wildcard, **then ****all**of the following are true:1.1 The length of the sequence is greater than or equal to *P*.[{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs).1.2 If *P*.[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) is a number, then the length of the sequence is less than or equal to the [{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs).1.3 Each element information item in the sequence is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to the wildcard as defined by [Item Valid (Wildcard) (§3.10.4.1)](#cvc-wildcard). In this case, each element information item in the sequence is [·attributed to·](#key-att-to)*P*and has no [·context-determined declaration·](#key-dd).2<a id="c-cdde"></a>**If ***P*.[{term}](#p-term) is an element declaration *D*, **then ****all**of the following are true:2.1 The length of the sequence is greater than or equal to *P*.[{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs).2.2 If *P*.[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) is a number, then the length of the sequence is less than or equal to the [{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs).2.3 For each element information item *E*in the sequence **one or more**of the following is true:2.3.1 *D*has the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) as *E*.In this case *D*is the [·context-determined declaration·](#key-dd) for *E*with respect to [Schema-Validity Assessment (Element) (§3.3.4.6)](#cvc-assess-elt) and [Assessment Outcome (Element) (§3.3.5.1)](#sic-e-outcome).2.3.2<a id="c-psg"></a>*D*is top-level (i.e. *D*.[{scope}](#ed-scope).[{variety}](#sc_e-variety) = ***global***), its [{disallowed substitutions}](#ed-disallowed_substitutions) does not contain ***substitution***, *E*'s [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname)[·resolves·](#cvc-resolve-instance) to an element declaration *S*— <a id="key-eqd"></a>[Definition:]call this declaration the **substituting declaration**— and [·S·](#key-eqd) is [·substitutable·](#key-validly-sub) for *D*as defined in [Substitution Group OK (Transitive) (§3.3.6.3)](#cos-equiv-derived-ok-rec).In this case [·S·](#key-eqd) is the [·context-determined declaration·](#key-dd) for *E*with respect to [Schema-Validity Assessment (Element) (§3.3.4.6)](#cvc-assess-elt) and [Assessment Outcome (Element) (§3.3.5.1)](#sic-e-outcome).In this case *E*is [·attributed to·](#key-att-to)*P*.**Note:**This clause is equivalent to requiring that the sequence of length 1 containing *E*is in [·L(D)·](#key-L.ed). 3 **If ***P*.[{term}](#p-term) is a model group, **then ****all**of the following are true:3.1 There is a [·partition·](#key-partition) of the sequence into *n*sub-sequences such that *n*is greater than or equal to *P*.[{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs).3.2 If *P*.[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) is a number, *n*is less than or equal to *P*.[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs).3.3 Each sub-sequence in the [·partition·](#key-partition) is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to that model group as defined in [Element Sequence Valid (§3.8.4.3)](#cvc-model-group). In this case, the element information items in each sub-sequence are [·attributed to·](#key-att-to)[Particles](#p) within the model group which is the [{term}](#p-term), as described in [Language Recognition by Groups (§3.8.4.1)](#group-recognition).**Note:**The rule just given does not require that the content model be deterministic. In practice, however, most non-determinism in content models is ruled out by the schema component constraint [Unique Particle Attribution (§3.8.6.4)](#cos-nonambig). Non-determinism can occur despite that constraint for several reasons. In some such cases, some particular element information item may be accepted by either a [Wildcard](#w) or an [Element Declaration](#ed). In such situations, the validation process defined in this specification matches the element information item against the [Element Declaration](#ed), both in identifying the [Element Declaration](#ed) as the item's [·context-determined declaration·](#key-dd), and in choosing alternative paths through a content model. Other cases of non-determinism involve nested particles each of which has [{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) greater than 1, where the input sequence can be partitioned in multiple ways. In those cases, there is no fixed rule for eliminating the non-determinism.**Note:**clause [1](#c-pw) and clause [2.3.2](#c-psg) do not interact: an element information item validatable by a declaration with a substitution group head is *not*validatable by a wildcard which accepts the head's (namespace, name) pair but not its own.
#### <a id="sec-sic-p"></a>3.9.5 Particle Information Set Contributions

None as such.

#### <a id="coss-particle"></a>3.9.6 Constraints on Particle Schema Components

##### <a id="sec-p-props-correct"></a>3.9.6.1 Particle Correct

All particles (see [Particles (§3.9)](#cParticles)) must satisfy the following constraint.

<a id="p-props-correct"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Particle Correct**
**All**of the following must be true:1 The values of the properties of a particle are as described in the property tableau in [The Particle Schema Component (§3.9.1)](#Particle_details), modulo the impact of [Missing Sub-components (§5.3)](#conformance-missing).2 If [{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) is not ***unbounded***, that is, it has a numeric value, then **all**of the following are true:2.1 [{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs) is not greater than [{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs).
##### <a id="sec-cos-particle-extend"></a>3.9.6.2 Particle Valid (Extension)

The following constraint defines a relation appealed to elsewhere in this specification.

<a id="cos-particle-extend"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Particle Valid (Extension)**
<a id="cd-model-extension"></a>[Definition:]For a particle (call it **E**, for extension) to be a **valid extension**of another particle (call it **B**, for base)**one or more**of the following is true:1 They are the same particle.2 *E*.[{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs) = *E*.[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs) = 1 and *E*.[{term}](#p-term) is a ***sequence***group whose [{particles}](#mg-particles)' first member is a particle all of whose properties, recursively, are identical to those of **B**.3 **All**of the following are true:3.1 **E**.[{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs) = **B**.[{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs). 3.2 Both **E**and **B**have ***all***groups as their [{term}](#p-term)s. 3.3 The [{particles}](#mg-particles) of **B**'s ***all***group is a prefix of the [{particles}](#mg-particles) of **E**'s ***all***group.
##### <a id="sec-cos-group-emptiable"></a>3.9.6.3 Particle Emptiable

The following constraint defines a relation appealed to elsewhere in this specification.

<a id="cos-group-emptiable"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Particle Emptiable**
<a id="cd-emptiable"></a>[Definition:]For a particle to be **emptiable****one or more**of the following is true:1 Its [{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs) is `0`.2 Its [{term}](#p-term) is a group and the minimum part of the effective total range of that group, as defined by [Effective Total Range (all and sequence) (§3.8.6.5)](#cos-seq-range) (if the group is ***all***or ***sequence***) or [Effective Total Range (choice) (§3.8.6.6)](#cos-choice-range) (if it is ***choice***), is `0`.
### <a id="Wildcards"></a>3.10 Wildcards

3.10.1 [The Wildcard Schema Component](#Wildcard_details)
3.10.2 [XML Representation of Wildcard Schema Components](#declare-openness)
3.10.2.1 [Mapping from <any> to a Particle](#dcl.wc.p)
3.10.2.2 [Mapping from <any> and <anyAttribute> to a Wildcard Component](#dcl.wc.wc)
3.10.3 [Constraints on XML Representations of Wildcards](#sec-src-wildcard)
3.10.4 [Wildcard Validation Rules](#sec-cvc-w)
3.10.4.1 [Item Valid (Wildcard)](#sec-cvc-wildcard)
3.10.4.2 [Wildcard allows Expanded Name](#sec-cvc-wildcard-name)
3.10.4.3 [Wildcard allows Namespace Name](#sec-cvc-wildcard-namespace)
3.10.5 [Wildcard Information Set Contributions](#sec-sic-w)
3.10.6 [Constraints on Wildcard Schema Components](#coss-wildcard)
3.10.6.1 [Wildcard Properties Correct](#sec-w-props-correct)
3.10.6.2 [Wildcard Subset](#sec-cos-ns-subset)
3.10.6.3 [Attribute Wildcard Union](#sec-cos-aw-union)
3.10.6.4 [Attribute Wildcard Intersection](#sec-cos-aw-intersect)
In order to exploit the full potential for extensibility offered by XML plus namespaces, more provision is needed than DTDs allow for targeted flexibility in content models and attribute declarations. A wildcard provides for [·validation·](#key-vn) of attribute and element information items dependent on their namespace names and optionally on their local names.

Example
```
<xs:any processContents="skip"/>

<xs:any namespace="##other" processContents="lax"/>

<xs:any namespace="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform"
        xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform"
        notQName="xsl:comment xsl:fallback"/>

<xs:any notNamespace="##targetNamespace ##local"/>

<xs:anyAttribute namespace="http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace"/>
```

XML representations of the four basic types of wildcard, plus one attribute wildcard.
#### <a id="Wildcard_details"></a>3.10.1 The Wildcard Schema Component

The wildcard schema component has the following properties:

Schema Component: <a id="w"></a>Wildcard, a kind of [Term](#t)<a id="w-annotations"></a>{annotations} A sequence of [Annotation](#a) components. <a id="w-namespace_constraint"></a>{namespace constraint} A [Namespace Constraint](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#) property record. Required.<a id="w-process_contents"></a>{process contents} One of {skip, strict, lax}. Required.Property Record: <a id="nc"></a>Namespace Constraint<a id="nc-variety"></a>{variety} One of {any, enumeration, not}. Required.<a id="nc-namespaces"></a>{namespaces}
A set each of whose members is either an xs:anyURI value or the distinguished value [·absent·](#key-null). Required.

<a id="nc-disallowed_names"></a>{disallowed names}
A set each of whose members is either an xs:QName value or the keyword ***defined***or the keyword ***sibling***. Required.

[{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint) provides for [·validation·](#key-vn) of attribute and element items that:
1. ([{variety}](#nc-variety)***any***) have any namespace or are not namespace-qualified;
2. ([{variety}](#nc-variety)***not***and [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) a set whose members are either namespace names or [·absent·](#key-null)) have any namespace other than the specified namespaces and/or, if [·absent·](#key-null) is included in the set, are namespace-qualified; (see this [example](#anchor6013a), which accepts only namespace-qualified names distinct from the target namespace; the "`##local`" in the schema document maps to the value [·absent·](#key-null) in the [{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint) property)
3. ([{variety}](#nc-variety)***enumeration***and [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) a set whose members are either namespace names or [·absent·](#key-null)) have any of the specified namespaces and/or, if [·absent·](#key-null) is included in the set, are unqualified.
4. ([{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) contains [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) members) have any [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) other than the specified names.
5. ([{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) contains the keyword ***defined***) have any [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) other than those matching the names of global element or attribute declarations.
6. ([{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) contains the keyword ***sibling***) have any [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) other than those matching the names of element or attribute declarations in the containing complex type definition.
[{process contents}](#w-process_contents) controls the impact on [·assessment·](#key-va) of the information items allowed by wildcards, as follows: strictThere must be a top-level declaration for the item available, or the item must have an `xsi:type`, and the item must be [·valid·](#key-vn) as appropriate.skipNo constraints at all: the item must simply be well-formed XML.laxIf the item has a uniquely determined declaration available, it must be [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to that declaration, that is, [·validate·](#key-vn) if you can, don't worry if you can't.
See [Annotations (§3.15)](#cAnnotations) for information on the role of the [{annotations}](#w-annotations) property.

#### <a id="declare-openness"></a>3.10.2 XML Representation of Wildcard Schema Components

The XML representation for a wildcard schema component is an [<any>](#element-any) or [<anyAttribute>](#element-anyAttribute) element information item.

XML Representation Summary: `any`Element Information Item<a id="element-any"></a><any
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
maxOccurs = ([nonNegativeInteger](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#nonNegativeInteger) | *unbounded*)  : 1
minOccurs = [nonNegativeInteger](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#nonNegativeInteger): 1
namespace = ((*##any*| *##other*) | List of ([anyURI](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anyURI) | (*##targetNamespace*| *##local*)) )
notNamespace = List of ([anyURI](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anyURI) | (*##targetNamespace*| *##local*))
notQName = List of ([QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) | (*##defined*| *##definedSibling*))
processContents = (*lax*| *skip*| *strict*) : strict
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?)
</any>XML Representation Summary: `anyAttribute`Element Information Item<a id="element-anyAttribute"></a><anyAttribute
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
namespace = ((*##any*| *##other*) | List of ([anyURI](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anyURI) | (*##targetNamespace*| *##local*)) )
notNamespace = List of ([anyURI](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anyURI) | (*##targetNamespace*| *##local*))
notQName = List of ([QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) | *##defined*)
processContents = (*lax*| *skip*| *strict*) : strict
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?)
</anyAttribute><a id="any_p1"></a>
An [<any>](#element-any) information item corresponds both to a wildcard component and to a particle containing that wildcard (unless `minOccurs=maxOccurs=0`, in which case the item corresponds to no component at all). The mapping rules are given in the following two subsections. As always, the mapping rules given here apply after, not before, the appropriate [·pre-processing·](#key-pre-processing).

Processors may issue a warning if a list of `xs:anyURI`values in the `namespace`or `noNamespace`attributes includes any values beginning with the string '`##`'.

**Note:**Strings beginning with '`##`' are not IRIs or URIS according to the definitions given in the relevant RFCs (for references see [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2)) and users of this specification are discouraged from using them as namespace names, to avoid confusion with the keyword values defined by this specification.
##### <a id="dcl.wc.p"></a>3.10.2.1 Mapping from <any> to a Particle

The mapping from an [<any>](#element-any) information item to a particle is as follows.

XML Mapping Summary for [Particle](#Particle_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{min occurs}](#p-min_occurs)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `minOccurs`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise `1`.[{max occurs}](#p-max_occurs)***unbounded***, if `maxOccurs`= ***unbounded***, otherwise the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `maxOccurs`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise `1`.[{term}](#p-term)A wildcard as given below.[{annotations}](#p-annotations) The same annotations as the [{annotations}](#w-annotations) of the wildcard. See below.
##### <a id="dcl.wc.wc"></a>3.10.2.2 Mapping from <any> and <anyAttribute> to a Wildcard Component

The mapping from an [<any>](#element-any) information item to a wildcard component is as follows. This mapping is also used for mapping [<anyAttribute>](#element-anyAttribute) information items to wildcards, although in some cases the result of the mapping is further modified, as specified in the rules for [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) and [<complexType>](#element-complexType).

XML Mapping Summary for [Wildcard](#Wildcard_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint) A [Namespace Constraint](#nc) with the following properties: **Property****Value**[{variety}](#nc-variety) the appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the `namespace`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is present, **then **the appropriate **case**among the following:1.1 **If **`namespace`= `"##any"`, **then *****any***;1.2 **If **`namespace`= `"##other"`, **then *****not***;1.3 **otherwise *****enumeration***;2 **If **the `notNamespace`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is present, **then *****not***; 3 **otherwise **(neither `namespace`nor `notNamespace`is present) ***any***.[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) the appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **neither `namespace`nor `notNamespace`is present, **then **the empty set;2 **If **`namespace`= `"##any"`, **then **the empty set;3 **If **`namespace`= `"##other"`, **then **a set consisting of [·absent·](#key-null) and, if the `targetNamespace`[attribute] of the [<schema>](#element-schema) ancestor element information item is present, its [·actual value·](#key-vv);4 **otherwise **a set whose members are namespace names corresponding to the space-delimited substrings of the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `namespace`or `notNamespace`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) (whichever is present), except 4.1 if one such substring is `##targetNamespace`, the corresponding member is the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `targetNamespace`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the [<schema>](#element-schema) ancestor element information item if present, otherwise [·absent·](#key-null);4.2 if one such substring is `##local`, the corresponding member is [·absent·](#key-null).[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) If the `notQName`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is present, then a set whose members correspond to the items in the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `notQName`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), as follows.
- If the item is a [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) value (i.e. an [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname)), then that [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) value is a member of the set.
- If the item is the token "`##defined`", then the keyword ***defined***is a member of the set.
- If the item is the token "`##definedSibling`", then the keyword ***sibling***is a member of the set.
If the `notQName`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is not present, then the empty set. [{process contents}](#w-process_contents)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `processContents`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise ***strict***.[{annotations}](#w-annotations) The [·annotation mapping·](#key-am-one) of the [<any>](#element-any) element, as defined in [XML Representation of Annotation Schema Components (§3.15.2)](#declare-annotation). **Note:**When this rule is used for an attribute wildcard (see [XML Representation of Complex Type Definition Schema Components (§3.4.2)](#declare-type)), the [{annotations}](#w-annotations) is the [·annotation mapping·](#key-am-one) of the [<anyAttribute>](#element-anyAttribute) element.
Wildcards are subject to the same ambiguity constraints ([Unique Particle Attribution (§3.8.6.4)](#cos-nonambig)) as other content model particles: If an instance element could match one of two wildcards, within the content model of a type, that model is in error.

#### <a id="sec-src-wildcard"></a>3.10.3 Constraints on XML Representations of Wildcards

<a id="src-wildcard"></a>**Schema Representation Constraint: Wildcard Representation OK**
In addition to the conditions imposed on [<any>](#element-any) and [<anyAttribute>](#element-anyAttribute) element information items by the schema for schema documents, `namespace`and `notNamespace`attributes must not both be present.
#### <a id="sec-cvc-w"></a>3.10.4 Wildcard Validation Rules

##### <a id="sec-cvc-wildcard"></a>3.10.4.1 Item Valid (Wildcard)

<a id="cvc-wildcard"></a>**Validation Rule: Item Valid (Wildcard)**
<a id="anchor6159-1"></a> For an element or attribute information item *I*to be locally [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to a wildcard *W***all**of the following must be true:1 The [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) of *I*is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to *W*.[{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint), as defined in [Wildcard allows Expanded Name (§3.10.4.2)](#cvc-wildcard-name).2 **If ***W*.[{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint).[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) contains the keyword ***defined***, **then ****both**of the following are true:2.1 **If ***W*is an element wildcard (i.e., *W*appears in a content model), **then **the [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) of *I*does not [·resolve·](#cvc-resolve-instance) to an element declaration. (Informally, no such top-level element is declared in the schema.)2.2 **If ***W*is an attribute wildcard, **then **the [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) of *I*does not [·resolve·](#cvc-resolve-instance) to an attribute declaration.3 **If ****all**of the following are true:3.1 *W*.[{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint).[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) contains the keyword ***sibling***;3.2 *W*is an element wildcard3.3 *I*is an element information item3.4 *I*has a [[parent]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)*P*that is also an element information item3.5 *I*and *P*have the same [[validation context]](#e-validation_context)3.6 *P*has an [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem)*T*(which is always a complex type and contains *W*in its [·content model·](#key-contentModel))**then **the [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) of *I*does not [·match·](#key-en-match) any element declaration [·contained·](#key-contain-xpx) in the content model of *T*, whether [·directly·](#key-contain-dpt), [·indirectly·](#key-contain-ipx), or [·implicitly·](#key-impl-cont).Informally, the keyword ***sibling***disallows any element declared as a possible sibling of the wildcard *W*.
When an element or attribute information item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a wildcard and the preceding constraint ([Item Valid (Wildcard) (§3.10.4.1)](#cvc-wildcard)) is satisfied, then the item has no [·context-determined declaration·](#key-dd). Its [·governing·](#key-governing) declaration, if any, is found by matching its [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) as described in [QName resolution (Instance) (§3.17.6.3)](#cvc-resolve-instance). Note that QName resolution is performed only if the item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a ***strict***or ***lax***wildcard; if the wildcard has a [{process contents}](#w-process_contents) property of ***skip***, then the item has no [·governing·](#key-governing) declaration.

<a id="key-skipped"></a>[Definition:] An element or attribute information item is **skipped**if it is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a ***skip***wildcard or if one of its ancestor elements is.

##### <a id="sec-cvc-wildcard-name"></a>3.10.4.2 Wildcard allows Expanded Name

<a id="cvc-wildcard-name"></a>**Validation Rule: Wildcard allows Expanded Name**
For an [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname)*E*, i.e. a (namespace name, local name) pair, to be [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to a namespace constraint *C***all**of the following must be true:1 The namespace name is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to *C*, as defined in [Wildcard allows Namespace Name (§3.10.4.3)](#cvc-wildcard-namespace);2 *C*.[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) does not contain *E*.
##### <a id="sec-cvc-wildcard-namespace"></a>3.10.4.3 Wildcard allows Namespace Name

<a id="cvc-wildcard-namespace"></a>**Validation Rule: Wildcard allows Namespace Name**
For a value *V*which is either a namespace name or [·absent·](#key-null) to be [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to a namespace constraint *C*(the value of a [{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint)) **one**of the following must be true:1 *C*.[{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***any***.2 *C*.[{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***not***, and *V*is not identical to any of the members of *C*.[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces).3 *C*.[{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***enumeration***, and *V*is identical to one of the members of *C*.[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces).
#### <a id="sec-sic-w"></a>3.10.5 Wildcard Information Set Contributions

None as such.

#### <a id="coss-wildcard"></a>3.10.6 Constraints on Wildcard Schema Components

##### <a id="sec-w-props-correct"></a>3.10.6.1 Wildcard Properties Correct

All wildcards (see [Wildcards (§3.10)](#Wildcards)) must satisfy the following constraint.

<a id="w-props-correct"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Wildcard Properties Correct**
**All**of the following must be true:1 The values of the properties of a wildcard are as described in the property tableau in [The Wildcard Schema Component (§3.10.1)](#Wildcard_details), modulo the impact of [Missing Sub-components (§5.3)](#conformance-missing).2 If [{variety}](#nc-variety) is ***not***, [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) has at least one member.3 If [{variety}](#nc-variety) is ***any***, [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) is empty.4 The namespace name of each [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) member in [{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) is allowed by the [{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint), as defined in [Wildcard allows Namespace Name (§3.10.4.3)](#cvc-wildcard-namespace).5 Attribute wildcards do not contain ***sibling***in their [{namespace constraint}](#w-namespace_constraint).[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names).
##### <a id="sec-cos-ns-subset"></a>3.10.6.2 Wildcard Subset

The following constraints define a relation appealed to elsewhere in this specification.

<a id="cos-ns-subset"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Wildcard Subset**
<a id="key-wc_subset"></a>[Definition:] Given two [Namespace Constraint](#nc)s **sub**and **super**, **sub**is a **wildcard subset**of **super**if and only if**one**of the following is true1 **super**.[{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***any***.2 Both **sub**and **super**have [{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***enumeration***, and **super**.[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) is a superset of **sub**.[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces).3 **sub**.[{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***enumeration***, **super**.[{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***not***, and the [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) of the two are disjoint.4 Both **sub**and **super**have [{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***not***, and **super**.[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) is a subset of **sub**.[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces).<a id="anchor6161"></a> And **all**of the following are true:1 Each [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) member of **super**.[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) is not allowed by **sub**, as defined in [Wildcard allows Expanded Name (§3.10.4.2)](#cvc-wildcard-name).2 If **super**.[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) contains ***defined***, then **sub**.[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) also contains ***defined***. 3 If **super**.[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) contains ***sibling***, then **sub**'s [{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) also contains ***sibling***.
##### <a id="sec-cos-aw-union"></a>3.10.6.3 Attribute Wildcard Union

<a id="cos-aw-union"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Attribute Wildcard Union**
<a id="key-wc_union"></a>[Definition:] Given three [Namespace Constraint](#nc)s *O*, *O1*, and *O2*, *O*is the **wildcard union**of *O1*and *O2*if and only if, first, the [{variety}](#nc-variety) and [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces), and, second, the [{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) of *O*are consistent with *O*being the union of *O1*and *O2*, as that is defined below. The [{variety}](#nc-variety) and [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) of *O*are consistent with *O*being the wildcard union of *O1*and *O2*if and only if **one or more**of the following is true:1 *O*, *O1*, and *O2*all have the same [{variety}](#nc-variety) and [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces).2 Either *O1*.[{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***any***or *O2*.[{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***any***, and *O*.[{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***any***.3 *O*, *O1*, and *O2*all have [{variety}](#nc-variety)***enumeration***, and *O*.[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) is the union of *O1*.[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) and *O2*.[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces).4 *O1*.[{variety}](#nc-variety) = *O2*.[{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***not***, and **one**of the following is true4.1 The intersection of the [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) of *O1*and *O2*is the empty set, and *O*.[{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***any***. 4.2 *O*.[{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***not***, and *O*.[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) is the non-empty intersection of *O1*.[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) and *O2*.[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces). 5 Either *O1*or *O2*has [{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***not***and [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) = *S1*, and the other has [{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***enumeration***and [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) = *S2*, and **one**of the following is true5.1 The set difference *S1*minus *S2*is the empty set, and *O*.[{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***any***. 5.2 *O*.[{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***not***and *O*.[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) is the non-empty set difference *S1*minus *S2*. The [{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) property of *O*is consistent with *O*being the wildcard union of *O1*and *O2*if and only if *O*.[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) includes all and only the following:1 [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) members of *O1*.[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) that are not allowed by *O2*, as defined in [Wildcard allows Expanded Name (§3.10.4.2)](#cvc-wildcard-name).2 [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) members of *O2*.[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) that are not allowed by *O1*. 3 The keyword ***defined***if it is contained in both *O1*.[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) and *O2*.[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names). **Note:**When one of the wildcards has ***defined***in [{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) and the other does not, then ***defined***is *not*included in the union. This may allow QNames that are not allowed by either wildcard. This is to ensure that all unions are expressible. If ***defined***is intended to be included, then it is necessary to have it in both wildcards.In the case where there are more than two [Namespace Constraint](#nc)s to be combined, the wildcard union is determined by identifying the wildcard union of two of them as above, then the wildcard union of the result with the third, and so on as required.
##### <a id="sec-cos-aw-intersect"></a>3.10.6.4 Attribute Wildcard Intersection

<a id="cos-aw-intersect"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Attribute Wildcard Intersection**
<a id="key-wc_intersect"></a>[Definition:] Given three [Namespace Constraint](#nc)s *O*, *O1*, and *O2*, *O*is the **wildcard intersection**of *O1*and *O2*if and only if both its [{variety}](#nc-variety) and [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) properties, on the one hand, and its [{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) property, on the other, are consistent with *O*being the intersection of *O1*and *O2*, as that is defined below. The [{variety}](#nc-variety) and [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) of *O*are consistent with *O*being the wildcard intersection of *O1*and *O2*if and only if **one or more**of the following is true:1 *O*, *O1*, and *O2*have the same [{variety}](#nc-variety) and [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces). 2 Either *O1*or *O2*has [{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***any***and *O*has [{variety}](#nc-variety) and [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) identical to those of the other.3 *O*, *O1*, and *O2*all have [{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***enumeration***, and *O*.[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) is the intersection of the [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) of *O1*and *O2*.4 *O*, *O1*, and *O2*all have [{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***not***, and *O*.[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) is the union of the [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) of *O1*and *O2*.5 Either *O1*or *O2*has [{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***not***and [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) = *S1*and the other has [{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***enumeration***and [{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) = *S2*, and *O*.[{variety}](#nc-variety) = ***enumeration***and *O*.[{namespaces}](#nc-namespaces) = the set difference *S2*minus *S1*. The [{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) property of *O*is consistent with *O*being the wildcard intersection of *O1*and *O2*if and only if *O*.[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) includes all and only the following:1 [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) members of *O1*.[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) whose namespace names are allowed by *O2*, as defined in [Wildcard allows Namespace Name (§3.10.4.3)](#cvc-wildcard-namespace).2 [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) members of *O2*.[{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names) whose namespace names are allowed by *O1*. 3 The keyword ***defined***if it is a member of either [{disallowed names}](#nc-disallowed_names). In the case where there are more than two [Namespace Constraint](#nc)s to be combined, the wildcard intersection is determined by identifying the wildcard intersection of two of them as above, then the wildcard intersection of the result with the third, and so on as required.
### <a id="cIdentity-constraint_Definitions"></a>3.11 Identity-constraint Definitions

3.11.1 [The Identity-constraint Definition Schema Component](#Identity-constraint_Definition_details)
3.11.2 [XML Representation of Identity-constraint Definition Schema Components](#declare-key)
3.11.3 [Constraints on XML Representations of Identity-constraint Definitions](#sec-src-identity-constraint)
3.11.4 [Identity-constraint Definition Validation Rules](#sec-cvc-identity-constraint)
3.11.5 [Identity-constraint Definition Information Set Contributions](#sec-sic-key)
3.11.6 [Constraints on Identity-constraint Definition Schema Components](#coss-identity-constraint)
3.11.6.1 [Identity-constraint Definition Properties Correct](#sec-c-props-correct)
3.11.6.2 [Selector Value OK](#sec-c-selector-xpath)
3.11.6.3 [Fields Value OK](#sec-c-fields-xpaths)
Identity-constraint definition components provide for uniqueness and reference constraints with respect to the contents of multiple elements and attributes.

Example
```
<xs:key name="fullName">
 <xs:selector xpath=".//person"/>
 <xs:field xpath="forename"/>
 <xs:field xpath="surname"/>
</xs:key>

<xs:keyref name="personRef" refer="fullName">
 <xs:selector xpath=".//personPointer"/>
 <xs:field xpath="@first"/>
 <xs:field xpath="@last"/>
</xs:keyref>

<xs:unique name="nearlyID">
 <xs:selector xpath=".//*"/>
 <xs:field xpath="@id"/>
</xs:unique>
```

XML representations for the three kinds of identity-constraint definitions.
#### <a id="Identity-constraint_Definition_details"></a>3.11.1 The Identity-constraint Definition Schema Component

The identity-constraint definition schema component has the following properties:

Schema Component: <a id="icd"></a>Identity-Constraint Definition, a kind of [Annotated Component](#ac)<a id="icd-annotations"></a>{annotations} A sequence of [Annotation](#a) components. <a id="icd-name"></a>{name} An xs:NCName value. Required.<a id="icd-target_namespace"></a>{target namespace} An xs:anyURI value. Optional.<a id="icd-identity-constraint_category"></a>{identity-constraint category} One of {key, keyref, unique}. Required.<a id="icd-selector"></a>{selector} An [XPath Expression](#x) property record. Required.<a id="icd-fields"></a>{fields} A sequence of [XPath Expression](#x) property records. <a id="icd-referenced_key"></a>{referenced key} An [Identity-Constraint Definition](#icd) component. Required if [{identity-constraint category}](#icd-identity-constraint_category) is ***keyref***, otherwise ([{identity-constraint category}](#icd-identity-constraint_category) is ***key***or ***unique***) must be [·absent·](#key-null).
If a value is present, its [{identity-constraint category}](#icd-identity-constraint_category) must be ***key***or ***unique***.

Identity-constraint definitions are identified by their [{name}](#icd-name) and [{target namespace}](#icd-target_namespace); identity-constraint definition identities must be unique within an [·XSD schema·](#key-schema). See [References to schema components across namespaces (<import>) (§4.2.6)](#composition-schemaImport) for the use of component identifiers when importing one schema into another.

Informally, [{identity-constraint category}](#icd-identity-constraint_category) identifies the identity-constraint definition as playing one of three roles:
- (***unique***) the identity-constraint definition asserts uniqueness, with respect to the content identified by [{selector}](#icd-selector), of the tuples resulting from evaluation of the [{fields}](#icd-fields) XPath expression(s).
- (***key***) the identity-constraint definition asserts uniqueness as for ***unique***. ***key***further asserts that all selected content actually has such tuples.
- (***keyref***) the identity-constraint definition asserts a correspondence, with respect to the content identified by [{selector}](#icd-selector), of the tuples resulting from evaluation of the [{fields}](#icd-fields) XPath expression(s), with those of the [{referenced key}](#icd-referenced_key).
These constraints are specified alongside the specification of types for the attributes and elements involved, i.e. something declared as of type integer can also serve as a key. Each constraint declaration has a name, which exists in a single symbol space for constraints. The equality and inequality conditions appealed to in checking these constraints apply to the *values*of the fields selected, not their lexical representation, so that for example `3.0`and `3`would be conflicting keys if they were both decimal, but non-conflicting if they were both strings, or one was a string and one a decimal. When equality and identity differ for the simple types involved, all three forms of identity-constraint test for *either*equality *or*identity of values.

Overall the augmentations to XML's `ID/IDREF`mechanism are:

- Functioning as a part of an identity-constraint is in addition to, not instead of, having a type;
- Not just attribute values, but also element content and combinations of values and content can be declared to be unique;
- Identity-constraints are specified to hold within the scope of particular elements;
- (Combinations of) attribute values and/or element content can be declared to be keys, that is, not only unique, but always present and non-nillable;
- The comparison between ***keyref***[{fields}](#icd-fields) and ***key***or ***unique***[{fields}](#icd-fields) is performed on typed values, not on the lexical representations of those values.
[{selector}](#icd-selector) specifies a restricted XPath ([[XPath 2.0]](#bib-xpath2)) expression relative to instances of the element being declared. This must identify a sequence of element nodes that are contained within the declared element to which the constraint applies.

[{fields}](#icd-fields) specifies XPath expressions relative to each element selected by a [{selector}](#icd-selector). Each XPath expression in the [{fields}](#icd-fields) property must identify a single node (element or attribute), whose content or value, which must be of a simple type, is used in the constraint. It is possible to specify an ordered list of [{fields}](#icd-fields)s, to cater to multi-field keys, keyrefs, and uniqueness constraints.

In order to reduce the burden on implementers, in particular implementers of streaming processors, only restricted subsets of XPath expressions are allowed in [{selector}](#icd-selector) and [{fields}](#icd-fields). The details are given in [Constraints on Identity-constraint Definition Schema Components (§3.11.6)](#coss-identity-constraint).

**Note:**Provision for multi-field keys etc. goes beyond what is supported by `xsl:key`.**Note:**In version 1.0 of this specification [[XSD 1.0 2E]](#schema2e-1), identity constraints used [[XPath 1.0]](#bib-xpath1). They now use [[XPath 2.0]](#bib-xpath2).
See [Annotations (§3.15)](#cAnnotations) for information on the role of the [{annotations}](#icd-annotations) property.

#### <a id="declare-key"></a>3.11.2 XML Representation of Identity-constraint Definition Schema Components

The XML representation for an identity-constraint definition schema component is either a [<key>](#element-key), a [<keyref>](#element-keyref) or a [<unique>](#element-unique) element information item. The correspondences between the properties of those information items after the appropriate [·pre-processing·](#key-pre-processing) and the properties of the component they correspond to are as follows:

XML Representation Summary: `unique`Element Information Item et al.<a id="element-unique"></a><unique
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
name = [NCName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NCName)
ref = [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, ([selector](#element-selector), [field](#element-field)+)?)
</unique><a id="element-key"></a><key
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
name = [NCName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NCName)
ref = [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, ([selector](#element-selector), [field](#element-field)+)?)
</key><a id="element-keyref"></a><keyref
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
name = [NCName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NCName)
ref = [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
refer = [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, ([selector](#element-selector), [field](#element-field)+)?)
</keyref><a id="element-selector"></a><selector
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
**xpath**= *a subset of XPath expression, see below*
xpathDefaultNamespace = ([anyURI](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anyURI) | (*##defaultNamespace*| *##targetNamespace*| *##local*))
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?)
</selector><a id="element-field"></a><field
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
**xpath**= *a subset of XPath expression, see below*
xpathDefaultNamespace = ([anyURI](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anyURI) | (*##defaultNamespace*| *##targetNamespace*| *##local*))
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?)
</field><a id="xr.idc5.bis"></a>
If the `ref`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is absent, the corresponding schema component is as follows:

XML Mapping Summary for [Identity-constraint Definition](#Identity-constraint_Definition_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{name}](#icd-name)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `name`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)[{target namespace}](#icd-target_namespace)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `targetNamespace`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the [<schema>](#element-schema) ancestor element information item if present, otherwise [·absent·](#key-null).[{identity- constraint category}](#icd-identity-constraint_category)One of ***key***, ***keyref***or ***unique***, depending on the item.[{selector}](#icd-selector) An [XPath Expression](#x) property record, as described in section [XML Representation of Assertion Schema Components (§3.13.2)](#declare-assertion), with [<selector>](#element-selector) as the "host element" and `xpath`as the designated expression [[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element). [{fields}](#icd-fields) A sequence of [XPath Expression](#x) property records, corresponding to the [<field>](#element-field) element information item [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), in order, following the rules given in [XML Representation of Assertion Schema Components (§3.13.2)](#declare-assertion), with [<field>](#element-field) as the "host element" and `xpath`as the designated expression [[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element). [{referenced key}](#icd-referenced_key)If the item is a [<keyref>](#element-keyref), the identity-constraint definition [·resolved·](#src-resolve) to by the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `refer`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), otherwise [·absent·](#key-null).[{annotations}](#icd-annotations) The [·annotation mapping·](#key-am-set) of the set of elements containing the [<key>](#element-key), [<keyref>](#element-keyref), or [<unique>](#element-unique) element, whichever is present, and the [<selector>](#element-selector) and [<field>](#element-field)[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, as defined in [XML Representation of Annotation Schema Components (§3.15.2)](#declare-annotation). <a id="xr.idc7.bis"></a>
Otherwise (the `ref`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is present), the corresponding schema component is the identity-constraint definition [·resolved·](#src-resolve) to by the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `ref`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).

Example
```
<xs:element name="vehicle">
 <xs:complexType>
  . . .
  <xs:attribute name="plateNumber" type="xs:integer"/>
  <xs:attribute name="state" type="twoLetterCode"/>
 </xs:complexType>
</xs:element>

<xs:element name="state">
 <xs:complexType>
  <xs:sequence>
   <xs:element name="code" type="twoLetterCode"/>
   <xs:element ref="vehicle" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>
   <xs:element ref="person" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>
  </xs:sequence>
 </xs:complexType>

 <!-- vehicles are keyed by their plate within states -->
 <xs:key name="reg">
  <xs:selector xpath=".//vehicle"/>
  <xs:field xpath="@plateNumber"/>
 </xs:key>
</xs:element>

<xs:element name="root">
 <xs:complexType>
  <xs:sequence>
   . . .
   <xs:element ref="state" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>
   . . .
  </xs:sequence>
 </xs:complexType>

 <!-- states are keyed by their code -->
 <xs:key name="state">
  <xs:selector xpath=".//state"/>
  <xs:field xpath="code"/>
 </xs:key>

 <xs:keyref name="vehicleState" refer="state">
  <!-- every vehicle refers to its state -->
  <xs:selector xpath=".//vehicle"/>
  <xs:field xpath="@state"/>
 </xs:keyref>

 <!-- vehicles are keyed by a pair of state and plate -->
 <xs:key name="regKey">
  <xs:selector xpath=".//vehicle"/>
  <xs:field xpath="@state"/>
  <xs:field xpath="@plateNumber"/>
 </xs:key>

 <!-- people's cars are a reference -->
 <xs:keyref name="carRef" refer="regKey">
  <xs:selector xpath=".//car"/>
  <xs:field xpath="@regState"/>
  <xs:field xpath="@regPlate"/>
 </xs:keyref>

</xs:element>

<xs:element name="person">
 <xs:complexType>
  <xs:sequence>
   . . .
   <xs:element name="car">
    <xs:complexType>
     <xs:attribute name="regState" type="twoLetterCode"/>
     <xs:attribute name="regPlate" type="xs:integer"/>
    </xs:complexType>
   </xs:element>
  </xs:sequence>
 </xs:complexType>
</xs:element>
```

A `state`element is defined, which contains a `code`child and some `vehicle`and `person`children. A `vehicle`in turn has a `plateNumber`attribute, which is an integer, and a `state`attribute. State's `code`s are a key for them within the document. Vehicle's `plateNumber`s are a key for them within states, and `state`and `plateNumber`is asserted to be a ***key***for `vehicle`within the document as a whole. Furthermore, a `person`element has an empty `car`child, with `regState`and `regPlate`attributes, which are then asserted together to refer to `vehicle`s via the `carRef`constraint. The requirement that a `vehicle`'s `state`match its containing `state`'s `code`is not expressed here.Example
```
<xs:element name="stateList">
 <xs:complexType>
  <xs:sequence>
   . . .
   <xs:element name="state" maxOccurs="unbounded">
    <xs:complexType>
     <xs:sequence>
      . . .
      <xs:element name="code" type="twoLetterCode"/>
      . . .
     </xs:sequence>
    </xs:complexType>
   </xs:element>
   . . .
  </xs:sequence>
 </xs:complexType>

 <xs:key ref="state"/> <!-- reuse the key constraint from the above example -->
</xs:element>
```

A list of `state`elements can appear as child elements under `stateList`. A ***key***constraint can be used to ensure that there is no duplicate state `code`. We already defined a ***key***in the above example for the exact same purpose (the key constraint is named "`state`"). We can reuse it directly via the `ref`attribute on the `key`element.
#### <a id="sec-src-identity-constraint"></a>3.11.3 Constraints on XML Representations of Identity-constraint Definitions

<a id="src-identity-constraint"></a>**Schema Representation Constraint: Identity-constraint Definition Representation OK**
In addition to the conditions imposed on [<key>](#element-key), [<keyref>](#element-keyref) and [<unique>](#element-unique) element information items by the schema for schema documents, **all**of the following must be true:1 One of `ref`or `name`is present, but not both. 2 If `name`is present, then [<selector>](#element-selector) appears in [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element). 3 If `name`is present on [<keyref>](#element-keyref), then `refer`is also present. 4 If `ref`is present, then only `id`and [<annotation>](#element-annotation) are allowed to appear together with `ref`. 5 If `ref`is present, then the [{identity-constraint category}](#icd-identity-constraint_category) of the identity-constraint definition [·resolved·](#src-resolve) to by the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `ref`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) matches the name of the element information item.
#### <a id="sec-cvc-identity-constraint"></a>3.11.4 Identity-constraint Definition Validation Rules

<a id="cvc-identity-constraint"></a>**Validation Rule: Identity-constraint Satisfied**
For an element information item *E*to be locally [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to an identity-constraint **all**of the following must be true:1 A data model instance is constructed from the input information set, as described in [[XDM]](#bib-xdm). The [{selector}](#icd-selector), with the element node corresponding to *E*as the context node, evaluates to a sequence of element nodes, as defined in [XPath Evaluation (§3.13.4.2)](#cvc-xpath). <a id="key-tns"></a>[Definition:] The **target node set**is the set of nodes in that sequence, after omitting all element nodes corresponding to element information items that are [·skipped·](#key-skipped).2 Each node in the [·target node set·](#key-tns) is either the context node or an element node among its descendants.3 <a id="anchor11716"></a> For each node *N*in the [·target node set·](#key-tns) and each field *F*in [{fields}](#icd-fields), when *N*is taken as the context node *F*evaluates (as defined in [XPath Evaluation (§3.13.4.2)](#cvc-xpath)) to a sequence of nodes *S*. *S*contains zero or more [·skipped·](#key-skipped) nodes, at most one node whose [·governing·](#key-governing) type definition is either a simple type definition or a complex type definition with [{variety}](#ct-variety)***simple***, and no other nodes. As a consequence, for each field *F*the sequence *S*contains at most one node with a [·non-absent·](#key-null)[schema actual value]. Call that one node's [schema actual value]*V*(*N*,*F*). <a id="key-ks"></a>[Definition:]The **key-sequence**of *N*is the sequence consisting of the values *V*(*N*,*F*), in the order of the [{fields}](#icd-fields) property.**Note:**The use of [schema actual value] in the definition of [·key sequence·](#key-ks) above means that ***default***or ***fixed***value constraints may play a part in [·key sequence·](#key-ks)s.**Note:**In some cases, the [·key-sequence·](#key-ks) of node *N*will be shorter than [{fields}](#icd-fields) in length, for example whenever any field evaluates to the empty sequence or to a sequence consisting only of [·skipped·](#key-skipped) or [·nilled·](#key-nilled) nodes. In such a case, the [·key-sequence·](#key-ks) of *N*does not participate in value comparison, because *N*is not a member of the [·qualified node set·](#key-qns). See below.4 <a id="key-qns"></a>[Definition:]The **qualified node set**is the subset of the [·target node set·](#key-tns) consisting of those nodes whose [·key-sequence·](#key-ks) has the same length as the [{fields}](#icd-fields). The appropriate **case**among the following is true:4.1<a id="c-u"></a>**If **the [{identity-constraint category}](#icd-identity-constraint_category) is ***unique***, **then **no two members of the [·qualified node set·](#key-qns) have [·key-sequences·](#key-ks) whose members are pairwise equal or identical, as defined by [Equality](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#equality) and [Identity](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#identity) in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2).4.2<a id="c-k"></a>**If **the [{identity-constraint category}](#icd-identity-constraint_category) is ***key***, **then ****all**of the following are true:4.2.1 The [·target node set·](#key-tns) and the [·qualified node set·](#key-qns) are equal, that is, every member of the [·target node set·](#key-tns) is also a member of the [·qualified node set·](#key-qns) and *vice versa*.4.2.2 No two members of the [·qualified node set·](#key-qns) have [·key-sequences·](#key-ks) whose members are pairwise equal or identical, as defined by [Equality](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#equality) and [Identity](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#identity) in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2).4.2.3<a id="c-nlbl"></a>No element member of the [·key-sequence·](#key-ks) of any member of the [·qualified node set·](#key-qns) was assessed as [·valid·](#key-vn) by reference to an element declaration whose [{nillable}](#ed-nillable) is ***true***.4.3<a id="cl-krv"></a>**If **the [{identity-constraint category}](#icd-identity-constraint_category) is ***keyref***, **then **for each member of the [·qualified node set·](#key-qns) (call this the **keyref member**), there is a [·node table·](#key-nt) associated with the [{referenced key}](#icd-referenced_key) in the [[identity-constraint table]](#e-id_constraint_table) of *E*(see [Identity-constraint Table (§3.11.5)](#sic-key), which is understood as logically prior to this clause of this constraint, below) and there is an entry in that table whose [·key-sequence·](#key-ks) is equal or identical to the **keyref member's**[·key-sequence·](#key-ks) member for member, as defined by [Equality](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#equality) and [Identity](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#identity) in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2).**Note:**For ***unique***identity constraints, the [·qualified node set·](#key-qns) is allowed to be different from the [·target node set·](#key-tns). That is, a selected unique node may have fields that do not have corresponding [schema actual value]s. **Note:**Because the validation of ***keyref***(see clause [4.3](#cl-krv)) depends on finding appropriate entries in an element information item's [·node table·](#key-nt), and [·node tables·](#key-nt) are assembled strictly recursively from the node tables of descendants, only element information items within the sub-tree rooted at the element information item being [·validated·](#key-vn) can be referenced successfully.**Note:**Although this specification defines a [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) contribution which would enable schema-aware processors to implement clause [4.2.3](#c-nlbl) above ([Element Declaration (§3.3.5.3)](#sic-elt-decl)), processors are not required to provide it. This clause can be read as if in the absence of this infoset contribution, the value of the relevant [{nillable}](#ed-nillable) property must be available.
For purposes of checking identity-constraints, single atomic values are not distinguished from lists with single items. An atomic value *V*and a list *L*with a single item are treated as equal, for purposes of this specification, if *V*is equal to the atomic value which is the single item of *L*. And similarly for identity.

#### <a id="sec-sic-key"></a>3.11.5 Identity-constraint Definition Information Set Contributions

<a id="sic-key"></a>**Schema Information Set Contribution: Identity-constraint Table**
<a id="key-ec"></a>[Definition:]An **eligible identity-constraint**of an element information item is one such that clause [4.1](#c-u) or clause [4.2](#c-k) of [Identity-constraint Satisfied (§3.11.4)](#cvc-identity-constraint) is satisfied with respect to that item and that constraint, or such that any of the element information item [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of that item have an [[identity-constraint table]](#e-id_constraint_table) property whose value has an entry for that constraint.<a id="key-nt"></a>[Definition:]A **node table**is a set of pairs each consisting of a [·key-sequence·](#key-ks) and an element node.Whenever an element information item has one or more [·eligible identity-constraints·](#key-ec), in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) that element information item has a property as follows:PSVI Contributions forelement information items
**<a id="e-id_constraint_table"></a>[identity-constraint table]**
: one **Identity-constraint Binding**information item for each [·eligible identity-constraint·](#key-ec), with properties as follows: PSVI Contributions forIdentity-constraint Binding information items
**<a id="cb-definition"></a>[definition]**
: The [·eligible identity-constraint·](#key-ec).

**<a id="cb-node_table"></a>[node table]**
: A [·node table·](#key-nt) with one entry for every [·key-sequence·](#key-ks) (call it **k**) and node (call it **n**) such that **one**of the following is true1<a id="c-kc"></a>There is an entry in one of the [·node tables·](#key-nt) associated with the [[definition]](#cb-definition) in an **Identity-constraint Binding**information item in at least one of the [[identity-constraint table]](#e-id_constraint_table)s of the element information item [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the element information item whose [·key-sequence·](#key-ks) is **k**and whose node is **n**;2 **n**appears with [·key-sequence·](#key-ks)**k**in the [·qualified node set·](#key-qns) for the [[definition]](#cb-definition). provided no two entries have the same [·key-sequence·](#key-ks) but distinct nodes. Potential conflicts are resolved by not including any conflicting entries which would have owed their inclusion to clause [1](#c-kc) above. Note that if all the conflicting entries arose under clause [1](#c-kc) above, this means no entry at all will appear for the offending [·key-sequence·](#key-ks).

**Note:**The complexity of the above arises from the fact that ***keyref***identity-constraints can be defined on domains distinct from the embedded domain of the identity-constraint they reference, or on domains which are the same but self-embedding at some depth. In either case the [·node table·](#key-nt) for the referenced identity-constraint needs to propagate upwards, with conflict resolution.The **Identity-constraint Binding**information item, unlike others in this specification, is essentially an internal bookkeeping mechanism. It is introduced to support the definition of [Identity-constraint Satisfied (§3.11.4)](#cvc-identity-constraint) above.
#### <a id="coss-identity-constraint"></a>3.11.6 Constraints on Identity-constraint Definition Schema Components

##### <a id="sec-c-props-correct"></a>3.11.6.1 Identity-constraint Definition Properties Correct

All identity-constraint definitions (see [Identity-constraint Definitions (§3.11)](#cIdentity-constraint_Definitions)) must satisfy the following constraint.

<a id="c-props-correct"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Identity-constraint Definition Properties Correct**
**All**of the following must be true:1 The values of the properties of an identity-constraint definition are as described in the property tableau in [The Identity-constraint Definition Schema Component (§3.11.1)](#Identity-constraint_Definition_details), modulo the impact of [Missing Sub-components (§5.3)](#conformance-missing).2 If the [{identity-constraint category}](#icd-identity-constraint_category) is ***keyref***, the cardinality of the [{fields}](#icd-fields) is equal to that of the [{fields}](#icd-fields) of the [{referenced key}](#icd-referenced_key).
##### <a id="sec-c-selector-xpath"></a>3.11.6.2 Selector Value OK

<a id="c-selector-xpath"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Selector Value OK**
**All**of the following must be true:1 The [{selector}](#icd-selector) satisfies the constraint [XPath Valid (§3.13.6.2)](#xpath-valid). 2 **One or more**of the following is true:2.1 Its [{expression}](#x-expression) conforms to the following extended BNF:
| **Selector XPath expressions** |
| --- |
| \| <a id="Selector"></a>[1] \| `Selector` \| ::= \| `Path ( '\\|' Path )*` \| \| --- \| --- \| --- \| --- \| \| <a id="Path"></a>[2] \| `Path` \| ::= \| `('.' '//')? Step ( '/' Step )*` \| \| <a id="Step"></a>[3] \| `Step` \| ::= \| `'.' \\| NameTest` \| \| <a id="NameTest"></a>[4] \| `NameTest` \| ::= \| `QName \\| '*' \\| NCName ':*'` \| |

2.2 Its [{expression}](#x-expression) is an XPath expression involving the `child`axis whose abbreviated form is as given above.For readability, whitespace may be used in selector XPath expressions even though not explicitly allowed by the grammar: [whitespace](#whitespace)may be freely added within patterns before or after any [token](#token).
| **Lexical productions** |
| --- |
| \| <a id="token"></a>[5] \| `token` \| ::= \| `'.' \\| '/' \\| '//' \\| '\\|' \\| '@' \\| NameTest` \| \| --- \| --- \| --- \| --- \| \| <a id="whitespace"></a>[6] \| `whitespace` \| ::= \| `S` \| |

When tokenizing, the longest possible token is always returned.
<a id="key-selector-subset"></a>[Definition:] The subset of XPath defined in [Selector Value OK (§3.11.6.2)](#c-selector-xpath) is called the **selector subset**of XPath.

##### <a id="sec-c-fields-xpaths"></a>3.11.6.3 Fields Value OK

<a id="c-fields-xpaths"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Fields Value OK**
**All**of the following must be true:1 Each member of the [{fields}](#icd-fields) satisfies the constraint [XPath Valid (§3.13.6.2)](#xpath-valid). 2 For each member of the [{fields}](#icd-fields)**one or more**of the following is true:2.1 Its [{expression}](#x-expression) conforms to the extended BNF given above for [Selector](#Selector), with the following modification:
| **Path in Field XPath expressions** |
| --- |
| \| <a id="fPath"></a>[7] \| `Path` \| ::= \| `('.' '//')? ( Step '/' )* ( Step \\| '@' NameTest )` \| \| --- \| --- \| --- \| --- \| |

This production differs from the one above in allowing the final step to match an attribute node. 2.2 Its [{expression}](#x-expression) is an XPath expression involving the `child`and/or `attribute`axes whose abbreviated form is as given above.For readability, whitespace may be used in field XPath expressions even though not explicitly allowed by the grammar: [whitespace](#whitespace)may be freely added within patterns before or after any [token](#token). When tokenizing, the longest possible token is always returned.
<a id="key-field-subset"></a>[Definition:] The subset of XPath defined in [Fields Value OK (§3.11.6.3)](#c-fields-xpaths) is called the **field subset**of XPath.

### <a id="cTypeAlternative"></a>3.12 Type Alternatives

3.12.1 [The Type Alternative Schema Component](#TA_details)
3.12.2 [XML Representation of Type Alternative Schema Components](#declare-ta)
3.12.3 [Constraints on XML Representations of Type Alternatives](#sec-src-ta)
3.12.4 [Type Alternative Validation Rules](#sec-cvc-ta)
3.12.5 [Type Alternative Information Set Contributions](#sec-sic-ta)
3.12.6 [Constraints on Type Alternative Schema Components](#coss-ta)
Type Alternative components provide associations between boolean conditions (as XPath expressions) and [Type Definition](#td)s. They are used in conditional type assignment.

#### <a id="TA_details"></a>3.12.1 The Type Alternative Schema Component

The type alternative schema component has the following properties:

Schema Component: <a id="tac"></a>Type Alternative, a kind of [Annotated Component](#ac)<a id="tac-annotations"></a>{annotations} A sequence of [Annotation](#a) components. <a id="tac-test"></a>{test} An [XPath Expression](#x) property record. Optional.<a id="tac-type_definition"></a>{type definition} A [Type Definition](#td) component. Required.
Type alternatives can be used by an [Element Declaration](#ed) to specify a condition ([{test}](#tac-test)) under which a particular type ([{type definition}](#tac-type_definition)) is used as the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) for element information items governed by that [Element Declaration](#ed). Each [Element Declaration](#ed)may have multiple [Type Alternative](#tac)s in its [{type table}](#ed-type_table).
