#### <a id="declare-ta"></a>3.12.2 XML Representation of Type Alternative Schema Components

The XML representation for a type alternative schema component is an [<alternative>](#element-alternative) element information item. The correspondences between the properties of that information item after the appropriate [·pre-processing·](#key-pre-processing) and the properties of the component it corresponds to are as follows:

XML Representation Summary: `alternative`Element Information Item<a id="element-alternative"></a><alternative
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
test = *an XPath expression*
type = [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
xpathDefaultNamespace = ([anyURI](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anyURI) | (*##defaultNamespace*| *##targetNamespace*| *##local*))
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, ([simpleType](#element-simpleType) | [complexType](#element-complexType))?)
</alternative>
Each [<alternative>](#element-alternative) element maps to a [Type Alternative](#tac) component as follows.

XML Mapping Summary for [Type Alternative](#TA_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{test}](#tac-test) If the `test`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is not present, then [·absent·](#key-null); otherwise an [XPath Expression](#x) property record, as described in section [XML Representation of Assertion Schema Components (§3.13.2)](#declare-assertion), with [<alternative>](#element-alternative) as the "host element" and `test`as the designated expression [[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element). [{type definition}](#tac-type_definition) The type definition [·resolved·](#src-resolve) to by the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `type`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if one is present, otherwise the type definition corresponding to the `complexType`or `simpleType`among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the [<alternative>](#element-alternative) element. [{annotations}](#tac-annotations) The [·annotation mapping·](#key-am-one) of the [<alternative>](#element-alternative) element, as defined in [XML Representation of Annotation Schema Components (§3.15.2)](#declare-annotation).
#### <a id="sec-src-ta"></a>3.12.3 Constraints on XML Representations of Type Alternatives

<a id="src-ta"></a>**Schema Representation Constraint: Type Alternative Representation OK**
<a id="anchor11230"></a>In addition to the conditions imposed on [<alternative>](#element-alternative) element information items by the schema for schema documents, each [<alternative>](#element-alternative) element must have one (and only one) of the following: a `type`attribute, or a `complexType`child element, or a `simpleType`child element.
#### <a id="sec-cvc-ta"></a>3.12.4 Type Alternative Validation Rules

<a id="key-cta-ta-select"></a>[Definition:]A [Type Alternative](#tac)*A***successfully selects**a [Type Definition](#td)*T*for an element information item *E*if and only if *A*.[{test}](#tac-test) evaluates to `true`and *A*.[{type definition}](#tac-type_definition) = *T*.<a id="anchor6465"></a> The [{test}](#tac-test) is evaluated in the following way:1<a id="hack0810-20"></a> An instance of the [[XDM]](#bib-xdm) data model is constructed as follows: 1.1<a id="hack0810-21"></a> An information set is constructed by copying the base information set properties (and not any of the properties specific to [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi)) of the following information items: 1.1.1<a id="hack0810-22"></a>*E*itself. 1.1.2 *E*'s [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) (but not its [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)). 1.1.3 *E*'s [[inherited attributes]](#e-inherited_attributes) which do not have the same [expanded names](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) as any of *E*'s [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element). They are copied as if they were among *E*'s [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and had *E*as their [[owner element]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute). When an attribute with a non-empty [[namespace name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute) is copied, [·namespace fixup·](#term-ns-fixup) may need to be performed on the resulting information set to ensure that a prefix *P*is bound to the [[namespace name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute) and the [[prefix]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute) of the copied attribute is set to *P*. <a id="key-base-infoset-props"></a>[Definition:]By **base information set properties**are meant the properties listed in [Required Information Set Items and Properties (normative) (§D)](#infoset). 1.2 In the copy of *E*, the [[parent]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) property is modified to have no value. The [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) property is modified to have the empty list as its value. 1.3 An [[XDM]](#bib-xdm) data model instance is constructed from that information set, following the rules given in [[XDM]](#bib-xdm). 2 The XPath expression which is the value of the [{test}](#tac-test), is evaluated as described in [XPath Evaluation (§3.13.4.2)](#cvc-xpath). If a [dynamic error](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-dynamic-error) or a [type error](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-type-error) is raised during evaluation, then the [{test}](#tac-test) is treated as if it had evaluated (without error) to `false`. <a id="anchor11073a"></a>**Note:**[Dynamic errors](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-dynamic-error) and [type errors](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-type-error) in the evaluation of [{test}](#tac-test) expressions cause neither the schema nor the document instance to be invalid. But conforming processors may issue a warning if they occur. However, if type errors are detected statically by the XPath processor, the effect is the same as for other static errors in the XPath expression. **Note:**As a consequence of the rules just given, the root node of the [[XDM]](#bib-xdm) instance is necessarily constructed from *E*; the ancestors, siblings, children, and descendants of *E*are not represented in the data model instance, and they are thus not accessible to the tests expressed in the [{test}](#tac-test)s in the [{type table}](#ed-type_table). The element *E*and its [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) will be represented in the data model instance by nodes labeled as untyped. If the [{test}](#tac-test) expressions being evaluated include comparisons which require type information, then explicit casts will sometimes be necessary.
#### <a id="sec-sic-ta"></a>3.12.5 Type Alternative Information Set Contributions

None.

#### <a id="coss-ta"></a>3.12.6 Constraints on Type Alternative Schema Components

All type alternatives (see [Type Alternatives (§3.12)](#cTypeAlternative)) must satisfy the following constraints.

<a id="ta-props-correct"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Type Alternative Properties Correct**
**All**of the following must be true:1 The values of the properties of a type alternatives are as described in the property tableau in [The Type Alternative Schema Component (§3.12.1)](#TA_details), modulo the impact of [Missing Sub-components (§5.3)](#conformance-missing). 2<a id="c-cta-static"></a> If the [{test}](#tac-test) is not [·absent·](#key-null), then it satisfies the constraint [XPath Valid (§3.13.6.2)](#xpath-valid). The [function signatures](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-function-signature) in the [static context](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-static-context)must include signatures for **all**of the following:2.1 The [fn:not](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath-functions/#func-not) function defined in the [[Functions and Operators]](#bib-fno) specification. 2.2 Constructor functions for the built-in datatypes. The further contents of [function signatures](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-function-signature) are [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined). A conforming processor must accept and process any XPath expression conforming to the "required subset" of [[XPath 2.0]](#bib-xpath2) defined by the following grammar. <a id="anchor11073b"></a>**Note:**Any XPath expression containing no static errors as defined in [[XPath 2.0]](#bib-xpath2) may appear in a conforming schema. Conforming processors may but are not required to support XPath expressions not belonging to the required subset of XPath. An XPath expression belongs to the required subset of XPath if and only if **all**of the following are true:1<a id="anchor11073c"></a> The [{expression}](#x-expression) property of the [XPath Expression](#x) is an XPath expression containing no static errors, as defined in [[XPath 2.0]](#bib-xpath2). 2 **One or more**of the following is true:2.1 It conforms to the following extended BNF:
| **Test XPath expressions** |
| --- |
| \| <a id="ta-Test"></a>[8] \| `Test` \| ::= \| `OrExpr` \| \| --- \| --- \| --- \| --- \| \| <a id="ta-OrExpr"></a>[9] \| `OrExpr` \| ::= \| `AndExpr ( 'or' AndExpr )*` \| \| <a id="ta-AndExpr"></a>[10] \| `AndExpr` \| ::= \| `BooleanExpr ( 'and' BooleanExpr )*` \| \| <a id="ta-BooleanExpr"></a>[11] \| `BooleanExpr` \| ::= \| `'(' OrExpr ')' \\|` \| \| \| \| \| `BooleanFunction \\|` \| \| \| \| \| `ValueExpr ( Comparator ValueExpr )?` \| \| <a id="ta-BooleanFunction"></a>[12] \| `BooleanFunction` \| ::= \| `QName '(' OrExpr ')'` \| \| <a id="ta-Comparator"></a>[13] \| `Comparator` \| ::= \| `'=' \\| '!=' \\| '<' \\| '<=' \\| '>' \\| '>='` \| \| <a id="ta-ValueExpr"></a>[14] \| `ValueExpr` \| ::= \| `CastExpr \\| ConstructorFunction` \| \| <a id="ta-CastExpr"></a>[15] \| `CastExpr` \| ::= \| `SimpleValue ( 'cast' 'as' QName '?'? )?` \| \| <a id="ta-SimpleValue"></a>[16] \| `SimpleValue` \| ::= \| `AttrName \\| Literal` \| \| <a id="ta-AttrName"></a>[17] \| `AttrName` \| ::= \| `'@' NameTest` \| \| <a id="ta-ConstructorFunction"></a>[18] \| `ConstructorFunction` \| ::= \| `QName '(' SimpleValue ')'` \| |

2.2 It is an XPath expression involving the `attribute`axis whose abbreviated form is as given above. **Note:**For readability, [[XPath 2.0]](#bib-xpath2) allows whitespace to be used between tokens in XPath expressions, even though this is not explicitly shown in the grammar. For details of whitespace handling, consult [[XPath 2.0]](#bib-xpath2). 3 Any strings matching the [BooleanFunction](#ta-BooleanFunction) production are function calls to [fn:not](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath-functions/#func-not) defined in the [[Functions and Operators]](#bib-fno) specification. Any strings matching the [ConstructorFunction](#ta-ConstructorFunction) production are function calls to constructor functions for the built-in datatypes. **Note:**The minimal content of the [function signatures](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-function-signature) in the [static context](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-static-context) is given in clause [2](#c-cta-static) of [Type Alternative Properties Correct (§3.12.6)](#ta-props-correct): `fn:not`and constructors for the built-in datatypes. **Note:**The above extended BNF is ambiguous. For example, the string "`a:b('123')`" has 2 paths in the grammar, by matching either [BooleanFunction](#ta-BooleanFunction) or [ConstructorFunction](#ta-ConstructorFunction). The rules given here require different function names for the productions. As a result, the ambiguity can be resolved based on the function name. 4 Any explicit casts (i.e. any strings which match the optional "`cast as`" *QName*in the [CastExpr](#ta-CastExpr) production) are casts to built-in datatypes. **Note:**Implementations may choose to support a bigger subset of [[XPath 2.0]](#bib-xpath2). **Note:**The rule given above for the construction of the data model instance has as a consequence that even when implementations support full [[XPath 2.0]](#bib-xpath2) expressions, it is not possible to refer successfully to the children, siblings, ancestors, etc. of the element whose type is being selected.
### <a id="cAssertions"></a>3.13 Assertions

3.13.1 [The Assertion Schema Component](#Assertion_details)
3.13.2 [XML Representation of Assertion Schema Components](#declare-assertion)
3.13.3 [Constraints on XML Representations of Assertions](#sec-src-assertion)
3.13.4 [Assertion Validation Rules](#assertion-validation)
3.13.4.1 [Assertion Satisfied](#sec-cvc-assertion)
3.13.4.2 [XPath Evaluation](#sec-cvc-xpath)
3.13.5 [Assertion Information Set Contributions](#sec-sic-assertion)
3.13.6 [Constraints on Assertion Schema Components](#coss-assertion)
3.13.6.1 [Assertion Properties Correct](#sec-as-props-correct)
3.13.6.2 [XPath Valid](#sec-xpath-valid)
Assertion components constrain the existence and values of related elements and attributes.

Example
```
<xs:assert test="@min le @max"/>
```

The XML representation for assertions.The [<assert>](#element-assert) element requires that the value of the `min`attribute be less than or equal to that of the `max`attribute, and fails if that is not the case.
#### <a id="Assertion_details"></a>3.13.1 The Assertion Schema Component

The assertion schema component has the following properties:

Schema Component: <a id="as"></a>Assertion, a kind of [Annotated Component](#ac)<a id="as-annotations"></a>{annotations} A sequence of [Annotation](#a) components. <a id="as-test"></a>{test} An [XPath Expression](#x) property record. Required.Property Record: <a id="x"></a>XPath Expression<a id="x-namespace_bindings"></a>{namespace bindings} A set of [Namespace Binding](#nb) property records. <a id="x-default_namespace"></a>{default namespace} An xs:anyURI value. Optional.<a id="x-base_URI"></a>{base URI} An xs:anyURI value. Optional.<a id="x-expression"></a>{expression} An [[XPath 2.0]](#bib-xpath2) expression. Required.Property Record: <a id="nb"></a>Namespace Binding<a id="nb-prefix"></a>{prefix} An xs:NCName value. Required.<a id="nb-namespace"></a>{namespace} An xs:anyURI value. Required.
To check an assertion, an instance of the XPath 2.0 data model ([[XDM]](#bib-xdm)) is constructed, <a id="anchor12127"></a> in which the element information item being [·assessed·](#key-va) is the (parentless) root node, and elements and attributes are assigned types and values according to XPath 2.0 data model construction rules, with some exceptions. See [Assertion Satisfied (§3.13.4.1)](#cvc-assertion) for details about how the data model is constructed. When evaluated against this data model instance, [{test}](#as-test) evaluates to either `true`or `false`(if any other value is returned, it's converted to either `true`or `false`as if by a call to the XPath [fn:boolean](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath-functions/#func-boolean) function).

See [Annotations (§3.15)](#cAnnotations) for information on the role of the [{annotations}](#as-annotations) property.

#### <a id="declare-assertion"></a>3.13.2 XML Representation of Assertion Schema Components

The XML representation for an assertion schema component is an [<assert>](#element-assert) element information item. The correspondences between the properties of that information item after the appropriate [·pre-processing·](#key-pre-processing) and the properties of the component it corresponds to are as follows:

XML Representation Summary: `assert`Element Information Item<a id="element-assert"></a><assert
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
**test**= *an XPath expression*
xpathDefaultNamespace = ([anyURI](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anyURI) | (*##defaultNamespace*| *##targetNamespace*| *##local*))
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?)
</assert>
The [<assert>](#element-assert) element maps to an [Assertion](#as) component as follows.

XML Mapping Summary for [Assertion](#Assertion_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{test}](#as-test) An [XPath Expression](#x) property record, as described below, with [<assert>](#element-assert) as the "host element" and `test`as the designated expression [[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element). [{annotations}](#as-annotations) The [·annotation mapping·](#key-am-one) of the [<assert>](#element-assert) element, as defined in [XML Representation of Annotation Schema Components (§3.15.2)](#declare-annotation).
Assertions, like identity constraints and conditional type assignment, use [[XPath 2.0]](#bib-xpath2) expressions. The expression itself is recorded, together with relevant parts of the context, in an [XPath Expression](#x) property record. The mapping is as described below; in each case, the XPath expression itself is given in a **designated attribute**of the appropriate "**host element**".

XML Mapping Summary for [XPath Expression](#x)**Property Record****Property****Representation**[{namespace bindings}](#x-namespace_bindings) A set of [Namespace Binding](#nb) property records. Each member corresponds to an entry in the [[in-scope namespaces]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the host element, with [{prefix}](#nb-prefix) being the [[prefix]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.namespace) and [{namespace}](#nb-namespace) the [[namespace name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.namespace). [{default namespace}](#x-default_namespace) Let **D**be the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `xpathDefaultNamespace`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present on the host element, otherwise that of the `xpathDefaultNamespace`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the [<schema>](#element-schema) ancestor. Then the value is the appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If ****D**is `##defaultNamespace`, **then **the appropriate **case**among the following:1.1 **If **there is an entry in the [[in-scope namespaces]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the host element whose [[prefix]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.namespace) is [·absent·](#key-null), **then **the corresponding [[namespace name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.namespace);1.2 **otherwise **[·absent·](#key-null);2 **If ****D**is `##targetNamespace`, **then **the appropriate **case**among the following:2.1 **If **the `targetNamespace`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is present on the [<schema>](#element-schema) ancestor, **then **its [·actual value·](#key-vv);2.2 **otherwise **[·absent·](#key-null);3 **If ****D**is `##local`, **then **[·absent·](#key-null);4 **otherwise **(**D**is an xs:anyURI value) **D**.[{base URI}](#x-base_URI) The [[base URI]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the host element. [{expression}](#x-expression) An XPath expression corresponding to the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the designated [[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the host element. Example
```
<xs:complexType name="intRange">
 <xs:attribute name="min" type="xs:int"/>
 <xs:attribute name="max" type="xs:int"/>
 <xs:assert test="@min le @max"/>
</xs:complexType>
```

The value of the `min`attribute must be less than or equal to that of the `max`attribute. Note that the attributes are validated before the assertion on the parent element is checked, so the typed values of the attributes are available for comparison; it is not necessary to cast the values to `int`or some other numeric type before comparing them. Example
```
<xs:complexType name="arrayType">
 <xs:sequence>
  <xs:element name="entry" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>
 </xs:sequence>
 <xs:attribute name="length" type="xs:int"/>
 <xs:assert test="@length eq fn:count(./entry)"/>
</xs:complexType>
```

The value of the `length`attribute must be the same as the number of occurrences of `entry`sub-elements.
#### <a id="sec-src-assertion"></a>3.13.3 Constraints on XML Representations of Assertions

None as such.

#### <a id="assertion-validation"></a>3.13.4 Assertion Validation Rules

##### <a id="sec-cvc-assertion"></a>3.13.4.1 Assertion Satisfied

<a id="cvc-assertion"></a>**Validation Rule: Assertion Satisfied**
An element information item *E*is locally [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to an assertion if and only if the [{test}](#as-test) evaluates to `true`(see below) without raising any [dynamic error](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-dynamic-error) or [type error](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-type-error). Evaluation of [{test}](#as-test) is performed as defined in [[XPath 2.0]](#bib-xpath2), with the following conditions:1<a id="c-buildxdm"></a> A data model instance (see [[XDM]](#bib-xdm)) is constructed in the following way: 1.1 *E*is validated with respect to its [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed), as defined in [Element Locally Valid (Element) (§3.3.4.3)](#cvc-elt), if the [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) exists, otherwise against its [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem), as defined in [Element Locally Valid (Complex Type) (§3.4.4.2)](#cvc-complex-type), except that for *E*itself (though not for its element information item descendants), clause [6](#c-as) of [Element Locally Valid (Complex Type) (§3.4.4.2)](#cvc-complex-type) is skipped. (Informally, the element is validated normally, except that assertions are not checked.) **Note:**It is a consequence of this rule that the [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of *E*will be validated in the usual way.1.2<a id="c-as-partialpsvi"></a>A "partial" [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) describing the results of this partial validation of *E*is constructed. The [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) properties of *E*'s [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) are defined in the usual way. On *E*itself, all [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) properties are supplied as described elsewhere in this specification if their values are known. The element's [[validity]](#e-validity) property is given the value ***invalid***if and only if the element is known to be invalid; otherwise it is given the value ***notKnown***. The element's [[validation attempted]](#e-validation_attempted) property is given the value ***partial***.**Note:**Since the assertions of its [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) have not been checked, *E*has been only partially validated, and can be known to be invalid, but not known to be valid. The values of the [[validity]](#e-validity) and [[validation attempted]](#e-validation_attempted) properties are set accordingly. By default, comments and processing instructions are excluded from the partial [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi), but [·at user option·](#dt-useroption) processors may retain comments and processing instructions instead of excluding them. **Note:**If comments and processing instructions are retained, the consequence will be that assertions are able to test for their presence or absence and to examine their contents. 1.3 From the "partial" [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi), a data model instance is constructed as described in [[XDM]](#bib-xdm). The root node of the [[XDM]](#bib-xdm) instance is constructed from *E*; the data model instance contains only that node and nodes constructed from the [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), and descendants of *E*.**Note:**It is a consequence of this construction that attempts to refer, in an assertion, to the siblings or ancestors of *E*, or to any part of the input document outside of *E*itself, will be unsuccessful. Such attempted references are not in themselves errors, but the data model instance used to evaluate them does not include any representation of any parts of the document outside of *E*, so they cannot be referred to. 2 The XPath expression [{test}](#as-test) is evaluated, following the rules given in [XPath Evaluation (§3.13.4.2)](#cvc-xpath), with the following conditions and modifications: 2.1 The root node of the [[XDM]](#bib-xdm) instance described in clause [1](#c-buildxdm) serves as the [context node](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-context-node) for evaluation of the XPath expression.2.2 The [static context](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-static-context) is augmented with the variable "`$value`", as described in [Assertion Properties Correct (§3.13.6.1)](#as-props-correct). 2.3 The variable "`$value`" appears as a member of the [variable values](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-variable-values) in the [dynamic context](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-dynamic-context). The `expanded QName`of that member has no namespace URI and has "`value`" as the local name. The `value`of the member is determined by the appropriate **case**among the following:2.3.1 **If ****all**of the following are true:2.3.1.1 *E*'s [[validity]](#e-validity) in the "partial" [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) is not ***invalid***; 2.3.1.2 *E*'s [[nil]](#e-nil) in the "partial" [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) does not exist or has value ***false***; 2.3.1.3 the [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) of *E*'s [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) has [{variety}](#ct-variety)***simple***, **then **the `value`is the [XDM representation](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-xdmrep) of *E*.[[schema actual value]](#e-schema_actual_value) under the [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) . [{simple type definition}](#ct-simple_type_definition) of *E*'s [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem). **Note:**This clause provides type information to simple contents of elements, to make type-aware comparisons and operations possible without explicit casting in the XPath expressions. **Note:**For complex types with simple content, the element node may be referred to as "`.`", while its content may be referred to as "`$value`". Since the element node, as a consequence of clause [1.2](#c-as-partialpsvi), will normally have the type annotation `anyType`, its [atomized](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-atomization) value will be a single atomic value of type `untypedAtomic`. By contrast, `$value`will be a sequence of one or more atomic values, whose types are the most specific (narrowest) built-in types available.2.3.2 **otherwise **(in the "partial" [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi), *E*.[[validity]](#e-validity) = ***invalid***or *E*.[[nil]](#e-nil) = ***true***or *E*'s [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) does not have [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) . [{variety}](#ct-variety) = ***simple***) the `value`is the empty sequence. 3 The evaluation result is converted to either `true`or `false`as if by a call to the XPath [fn:boolean](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath-functions/#func-boolean) function.**Note:**Although the rules just given describe how an [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) and a [[XDM]](#bib-xdm) instance are constructed, processors are not required to construct actual data structures representing them. However, the result of XPath evaluation must be the same as if such [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) and [[XDM]](#bib-xdm) instance data structures were constructed.
##### <a id="sec-cvc-xpath"></a>3.13.4.2 XPath Evaluation

<a id="cvc-xpath"></a>**Validation Rule: XPath Evaluation**
An [XPath Expression](#x) property record *X*, with a context node *E*, is evaluated as defined in [[XPath 2.0]](#bib-xpath2), with a [static context](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-static-context) as described in [XPath Valid (§3.13.6.2)](#xpath-valid) (unless otherwise specified elsewhere) and with the following [dynamic context](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-dynamic-context) (again, unless otherwise specified elsewhere): 1 The [context item](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-context-item) is *E*.2 The [context position](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-context-position) is 1.3 The [context size](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-context-size) is 1.4 The [variable values](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-variable-values) is the empty set.5 The [function implementations](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-function-implementation) include an implementation of every function in the [function signatures](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-function-signature) of the [static context](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-static-context). See [XPath Valid (§3.13.6.2)](#xpath-valid).6 The [current dateTime](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-date-time) is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined), but is constant during an [·assessment·](#key-va) episode.7 The [implicit timezone](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-timezone) is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined), but is constant during an [·assessment·](#key-va) episode.8 The [available documents](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-available-docs) is the empty set.9 The [available collections](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-available-collections) is <a id="anchor6540"></a> the empty set.10 The [default collection](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-default-collection) is the empty sequence.<a id="anchor11073d"></a>
It is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) (both in this specification and in [[XPath 2.0]](#bib-xpath2)) when type errors are detected and whether, when detected, they are treated as static or dynamic errors.

**Note:**It is a consequence of this rule that a conforming processor which treats a type error in an XPath expression as a dynamic error will treat the expression as having evaluated to false, while a conforming processor which treats type errors as static errors will report an error in the schema.
#### <a id="sec-sic-assertion"></a>3.13.5 Assertion Information Set Contributions

None as such.

#### <a id="coss-assertion"></a>3.13.6 Constraints on Assertion Schema Components

All assertions (see [Assertions (§3.13)](#cAssertions)) must satisfy the following constraints.

##### <a id="sec-as-props-correct"></a>3.13.6.1 Assertion Properties Correct

<a id="as-props-correct"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Assertion Properties Correct**
**All**of the following must be true:1 The values of the properties of an assertion are as described in the property tableau in [The Assertion Schema Component (§3.13.1)](#Assertion_details), modulo the impact of [Missing Sub-components (§5.3)](#conformance-missing). 2 <a id="anchor6541"></a> The [{test}](#as-test) satisfies the constraint [XPath Valid (§3.13.6.2)](#xpath-valid), with the following modifications to the [static context](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-static-context): 2.1 The [in-scope variables](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-in-scope-variables) is a set with a single member. The `expanded QName`of that member has no namespace URI and has `value`as the local name. The (static) `type`of the member is `anyAtomicType*`. 2.2 The [function signatures](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-function-signature) includes signatures for **all**of the following:2.2.1 Functions in the `http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions`namespace as defined in the [[Functions and Operators]](#bib-fno) specification. 2.2.2 Constructor functions for the built-in datatypes. **Note:**The XDM type label `anyAtomicType*`simply says that for static typing purposes the variable `$value`will have a value consisting of a sequence of zero or more atomic values.
##### <a id="sec-xpath-valid"></a>3.13.6.2 XPath Valid

<a id="xpath-valid"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: XPath Valid**
For an [XPath Expression](#x) property record *X*to be valid, **all**of the following must be true:1 The [{expression}](#x-expression) of *X*is a valid XPath expression, as defined in [[XPath 2.0]](#bib-xpath2). 2 *X*does not produce any [static error](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-static-error), under the following conditions (except as specified elsewhere):2.1 The [Static Typing Feature](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-xpath-static-typing-feature) is disabled. 2.2 The [static context](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-static-context) is given as follows: 2.2.1 [XPath 1.0 compatibility mode](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-xpath-compat-mode) is ***false***.2.2.2 The [statically known namespaces](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-static-namespaces) is the [{namespace bindings}](#x-namespace_bindings) of *X*.2.2.3 The [default element/type namespace](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-def-elemtype-ns) is the [{default namespace}](#x-default_namespace) of *X*.2.2.4 The [default function namespace](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-def-fn-ns) is `http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions`.2.2.5 The [in-scope schema definitions](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-issd) are those components that are present in every schema by definition, as defined in [Built-in Attribute Declarations (§3.2.7)](#builtin-ads), [Built-in Complex Type Definition (§3.4.7)](#builtin-ctd) and [Built-in Simple Type Definitions (§3.16.7)](#builtin-stds).2.2.6 The [in-scope variables](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-in-scope-variables) is the empty set.2.2.7 The [context item static type](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-context-item-static-type) is not applicable, because the [Static Typing Feature](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-xpath-static-typing-feature) is disabled.2.2.8 The [function signatures](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-function-signature) are [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined). **Note:**If *X*belongs to an [Assertion](#as) or a [Type Alternative](#tac), [Assertion Properties Correct (§3.13.6.1)](#as-props-correct) and [Type Alternative Properties Correct (§3.12.6)](#ta-props-correct) impose additional constraints on the set of required functions. 2.2.9 The [statically known collations](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-static-collations) are [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined), but always include the [Unicode codepoint collation](https://www.w3.org/TR/xquery-operators/#collations) (`http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/collation/codepoint`) defined by [[Functions and Operators]](#bib-fno).2.2.10 The [default collation](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-def-collation) is the Unicode codepoint collation. 2.2.11 The [base URI](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-base-uri) is the [{base URI}](#x-base_URI) of *X*.2.2.12 The [statically known documents](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-known-docs) is the empty set.2.2.13 The [statically known collections](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-known-collections) is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined).2.2.14 The [statically known default collection type](https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath20/#dt-known-default-collection) is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined).
It is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) (both in this specification and in [[XPath 2.0]](#bib-xpath2)) when type errors are detected and whether, when detected, they are treated as static or dynamic errors.

**Note:**It is a consequence of this rule that a conforming processor which treats a type error in an XPath expression as a dynamic error will treat the expression as having evaluated to false, while a conforming processor which treats type errors as static errors will report an error in the schema.
### <a id="cNotation_Declarations"></a>3.14 Notation Declarations

3.14.1 [The Notation Declaration Schema Component](#Notation_Declaration_details)
3.14.2 [XML Representation of Notation Declaration Schema Components](#declare-notation)
3.14.3 [Constraints on XML Representations of Notation Declarations](#sec-src-notation)
3.14.4 [Notation Declaration Validation Rules](#sec-cvc-notation)
3.14.5 [Notation Declaration Information Set Contributions](#sec-sic-notation)
3.14.6 [Constraints on Notation Declaration Schema Components](#coss-notation)
Notation declarations reconstruct XML NOTATION declarations.

Example
```
<xs:notation name="jpeg" public="image/jpeg" system="viewer.exe">
```

The XML representation of a notation declaration.
#### <a id="Notation_Declaration_details"></a>3.14.1 The Notation Declaration Schema Component

The notation declaration schema component has the following properties:

Schema Component: <a id="nd"></a>Notation Declaration, a kind of [Annotated Component](#ac)<a id="nd-annotations"></a>{annotations} A sequence of [Annotation](#a) components. <a id="nd-name"></a>{name} An xs:NCName value. Required.<a id="nd-target_namespace"></a>{target namespace} An xs:anyURI value. Optional.<a id="nd-system_identifier"></a>{system identifier} An xs:anyURI value. Required if [{public identifier}](#nd-public_identifier) is [·absent·](#key-null), otherwise ([{public identifier}](#nd-public_identifier) is present) optional.<a id="nd-public_identifier"></a>{public identifier} A publicID value. Required if [{system identifier}](#nd-system_identifier) is [·absent·](#key-null), otherwise ([{system identifier}](#nd-system_identifier) is present) optional.
As defined in [[XML 1.0]](#ref-xml-1.0) or [[XML 1.1]](#ref-xml-1.1).

Notation declarations do not participate in [·validation·](#key-vn) as such. They are referenced in the course of [·validating·](#key-vn) strings as members of the [NOTATION](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NOTATION) simple type. An element or attribute information item with its [·governing·](#key-governing) type definition or its [·validating type·](#key-vtype) derived from the [NOTATION](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NOTATION) simple type is [·valid·](#key-vn) only if its value was among the enumerations of such simple type. As a consequence such a value is required to be the [{name}](#nd-name) of a notation declaration.

See [Annotations (§3.15)](#cAnnotations) for information on the role of the [{annotations}](#nd-annotations) property.

#### <a id="declare-notation"></a>3.14.2 XML Representation of Notation Declaration Schema Components

The XML representation for a notation declaration schema component is a [<notation>](#element-notation) element information item. The correspondences between the properties of that information item after the appropriate [·pre-processing·](#key-pre-processing) and the properties of the component it corresponds to are as follows:

XML Representation Summary: `notation`Element Information Item<a id="element-notation"></a><notation
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
**name**= [NCName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NCName)
public = [token](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#token)
system = [anyURI](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anyURI)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?)
</notation>
The [<notation>](#element-notation) element maps to a [Notation Declaration](#nd) component as follows.

XML Mapping Summary for [Notation Declaration](#Notation_Declaration_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{name}](#nd-name)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `name`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element)[{target namespace}](#nd-target_namespace)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `targetNamespace`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the [<schema>](#element-schema) ancestor element information item if present, otherwise [·absent·](#key-null).[{system identifier}](#nd-system_identifier)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `system`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise [·absent·](#key-null).[{public identifier}](#nd-public_identifier)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `public`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise [·absent·](#key-null).[{annotations}](#nd-annotations) The [·annotation mapping·](#key-am-one) of the [<notation>](#element-notation) element, as defined in [XML Representation of Annotation Schema Components (§3.15.2)](#declare-annotation).
Example
```
<xs:notation name="jpeg"
             public="image/jpeg" system="viewer.exe" />

<xs:element name="picture">
 <xs:complexType>
  <xs:simpleContent>
   <xs:extension base="xs:hexBinary">
    <xs:attribute name="pictype">
     <xs:simpleType>
      <xs:restriction base="xs:NOTATION">
       <xs:enumeration value="jpeg"/>
       <xs:enumeration value="png"/>
       . . .
      </xs:restriction>
     </xs:simpleType>
    </xs:attribute>
   </xs:extension>
  </xs:simpleContent>
 </xs:complexType>
</xs:element>

<picture pictype="jpeg">...</picture>
```

#### <a id="sec-src-notation"></a>3.14.3 Constraints on XML Representations of Notation Declarations

None as such.

#### <a id="sec-cvc-notation"></a>3.14.4 Notation Declaration Validation Rules

None as such.

#### <a id="sec-sic-notation"></a>3.14.5 Notation Declaration Information Set Contributions

<a id="sic-notation-used"></a>**Schema Information Set Contribution: Validated with Notation**
Whenever an attribute information item is [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to a [NOTATION](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NOTATION), in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) its parent element information item has the following properties:PSVI Contributions forelement information items
**<a id="e-notation"></a>[notation]**
: An [·item isomorphic·](#key-iso) to the notation declaration [·resolved·](#cvc-resolve-instance) to by the attribute item's [·actual value·](#key-vv)

**<a id="e-notation_system"></a>[notation system]**
: The value of the [{system identifier}](#nd-system_identifier) of that notation declaration.

**<a id="e-notation_public"></a>[notation public]**
: The value of the [{public identifier}](#nd-public_identifier) of that notation declaration.

**Note:**For compatibility, only one such attribute should appear on any given element. If more than one such attribute *does*appear, which one supplies the infoset property or properties above is not defined.**Note:**Element as well as attribute information items may be [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to a [NOTATION](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NOTATION), but only attribute information items cause a notation declaration to appear in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) as a property of their parent.
#### <a id="coss-notation"></a>3.14.6 Constraints on Notation Declaration Schema Components

All notation declarations (see [Notation Declarations (§3.14)](#cNotation_Declarations)) must satisfy the following constraint.

<a id="n-props-correct"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Notation Declaration Correct**
The values of the properties of a notation declaration must be as described in the property tableau in [The Notation Declaration Schema Component (§3.14.1)](#Notation_Declaration_details), modulo the impact of [Missing Sub-components (§5.3)](#conformance-missing).
### <a id="cAnnotations"></a>3.15 Annotations

3.15.1 [The Annotation Schema Component](#Annotation_details)
3.15.2 [XML Representation of Annotation Schema Components](#declare-annotation)
3.15.3 [Constraints on XML Representations of Annotations](#sec-src-annotation)
3.15.4 [Annotation Validation Rules](#sec-cvc-annotation)
3.15.5 [Annotation Information Set Contributions](#sec-sic-annotation)
3.15.6 [Constraints on Annotation Schema Components](#coss-annotation)
Annotations provide for human- and machine-targeted annotations of schema components.

Example
```
<xs:simpleType
       fn:note="special"> <xs:annotation> <xs:documentation>A type for
       experts only</xs:documentation> <xs:appinfo>
       <fn:specialHandling>checkForPrimes</fn:specialHandling>
       </xs:appinfo> </xs:annotation>

```

XML representations of three kinds of annotation.
#### <a id="Annotation_details"></a>3.15.1 The Annotation Schema Component

The annotation schema component has the following properties:

Schema Component: <a id="a"></a>Annotation, a kind of [Component](#c)<a id="a-application_information"></a>{application information} A sequence of Element information items. <a id="a-user_information"></a>{user information} A sequence of Element information items. <a id="a-attributes"></a>{attributes} A set of Attribute information items.
[{user information}](#a-user_information) is intended for human consumption, [{application information}](#a-application_information) for automatic processing. In both cases, provision is made for an optional URI reference to supplement the local information, as the value of the `source`attribute of the respective element information items. [·validation·](#key-vn) does *not*involve dereferencing these URIs, when present. In the case of [{user information}](#a-user_information), indication should be given as to the identity of the (human) language used in the contents, using the `xml:lang`attribute.

[{attributes}](#a-attributes) ensures that when schema authors take advantage of the provision for adding attributes from namespaces other than the XSD namespace to schema documents, they are available within the components corresponding to the element items where such attributes appear.

Annotations do not participate in [·validation·](#key-vn) as such. Provided an annotation itself satisfies all relevant [·Schema Component Constraints·](#gloss-cos) it *cannot*affect the [·validation·](#key-vn) of element information items.

The mapping defined in this specification from XML representations to components does not apply to XML elements contained within an [<annotation>](#element-annotation) element; such elements do not correspond to components, when the mapping defined here is used.

It is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) what effect, if any, the invalidity of a descendant of [<annotation>](#element-annotation) has on the construction of schema components from the enclosing schema document.

The name <a id="ac"></a>[Definition:]**Annotated Component**covers all the different kinds of component which may have annotations.

#### <a id="declare-annotation"></a>3.15.2 XML Representation of Annotation Schema Components

Annotation of schemas and schema components, with material for human or computer consumption, is provided for by allowing application information and human information at the beginning of most major schema elements, and anywhere at the top level of schemas. The XML representation for an annotation schema component is an [<annotation>](#element-annotation) element information item. The correspondences between the properties of that information item after the appropriate [·pre-processing·](#key-pre-processing) and the properties of the component it corresponds to are as follows:

XML Representation Summary: `annotation`Element Information Item et al.<a id="element-annotation"></a><annotation
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([appinfo](#element-appinfo) | [documentation](#element-documentation))*
</annotation><a id="element-appinfo"></a><appinfo
source = [anyURI](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anyURI)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *(*{any}*)*
</appinfo><a id="element-documentation"></a><documentation
source = [anyURI](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anyURI)
xml:lang = [language](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#language)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *(*{any}*)*
</documentation>
The [<annotation>](#element-annotation) element and its descendants map to an [Annotation](#a) component as follows.

XML Mapping Summary for [Annotation](#Annotation_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{application information}](#a-application_information)A sequence of the [<appinfo>](#element-appinfo) element information items from among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), in order, if any, otherwise the empty sequence.[{user information}](#a-user_information)A sequence of the [<documentation>](#element-documentation) element information items from among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), in order, if any, otherwise the empty sequence.[{attributes}](#a-attributes)A set of attribute information items, namely those allowed by the attribute wildcard in the type definition for the [<annotation>](#element-annotation) item itself or for the enclosing items which correspond to the component within which the annotation component is located.
The annotation component corresponding to the [<annotation>](#element-annotation) element in the example above will have one element item in each of its [{user information}](#a-user_information) and [{application information}](#a-application_information) and one attribute item in its [{attributes}](#a-attributes).

Virtually every kind of schema component defined in this specification has an {annotations} property. When the component is described in a schema document, the mapping from the XML representation of the component to the [Annotation](#a) components in the appropriate {annotations} property follows the rules described in the next paragraph.

<a id="key-am-set"></a>[Definition:]The **annotation mapping**of a set of element information items *ES*is a sequence of annotations *AS*, with the following properties: 1<a id="am-set-exp.ann"></a> For every [<annotation>](#element-annotation) element information item among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of any element information item in *ES*, there is a corresponding [Annotation](#a) component in *AS*. **Note:**As noted above, the [{attributes}](#a-attributes) property of each [Annotation](#a) component includes all the attribute information items on the [<annotation>](#element-annotation) element itself, on the XML element which represents (and maps to) the component being annotated, and on any intervening XML elements, if those attribute information items have [[namespace name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute)s different from the XSD namespace.2<a id="am-set-imp.ann"></a> If any element information item *E*in *ES*has any attribute information items *A*such that **all**of the following are true:2.1 *A*is in *E*.[[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).2.2 *A*.[[namespace name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute) is present and not the XSD namespace.2.3 *A*is not included in the [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) property of any annotation component described in clause [1](#am-set-exp.ann). then for each such *E*, an [Annotation](#a) component *C*will appear in *AS*, with *C*.[{application information}](#a-application_information) and *C*.[{user information}](#a-user_information) each being the empty sequence and *C*.[{attributes}](#a-attributes) containing all and only those attribute information items *A*among *E*.[[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).3 *AS*contains no other [Annotation](#a) components.<a id="key-am-one"></a>[Definition:]The **annotation mapping**of a single element information item is the [·annotation mapping·](#key-am-set) of the singleton set containing that element.**Note:**The order of [Annotation](#a) components within the sequence is [·implementation-dependent·](#key-impl-dep). **Note:**When the input set has more than one member, the [Annotation](#a) components in the resulting sequence do not record which element in the set they correspond to. The attribute information items in the [{attributes}](#a-attributes) of any [Annotation](#a) similarly do not indicate which element information item in the schema document was their parent.
#### <a id="sec-src-annotation"></a>3.15.3 Constraints on XML Representations of Annotations

None as such.

#### <a id="sec-cvc-annotation"></a>3.15.4 Annotation Validation Rules

None as such.

#### <a id="sec-sic-annotation"></a>3.15.5 Annotation Information Set Contributions

None as such: the addition of annotations to the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) is covered by the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) contributions of the enclosing components.

#### <a id="coss-annotation"></a>3.15.6 Constraints on Annotation Schema Components

All annotations (see [Annotations (§3.15)](#cAnnotations)) must satisfy the following constraint.

<a id="an-props-correct"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Annotation Correct**
The values of the properties of an annotation must be as described in the property tableau in [The Annotation Schema Component (§3.15.1)](#Annotation_details), modulo the impact of [Missing Sub-components (§5.3)](#conformance-missing).
### <a id="Simple_Type_Definitions"></a>3.16 Simple Type Definitions

3.16.2.2 [Mapping Rules for Atomic Simple Type Definitions](#map.std.atomic)
3.16.2.3 [Mapping Rules for Lists](#map.std.list)
3.16.2.4 [Mapping Rules for Unions](#map.std.union)
