3.16.4 [Simple Type Definition Validation Rules](#sec-cvc-simple-type)
3.16.5 [Simple Type Definition Information Set Contributions](#sec-sic-simple-type)
3.16.6 [Constraints on Simple Type Definition Schema Components](#coss-st)
3.16.6.1 [Simple Type Definition Properties Correct](#sec-st-props-correct)
3.16.6.2 [Derivation Valid (Restriction, Simple)](#sec-cos-st-restricts)
3.16.6.3 [Type Derivation OK (Simple)](#sec-cos-st-derived-ok)
3.16.6.4 [Simple Type Restriction (Facets)](#sec-st-restrict-facets)
3.16.7 [Built-in Simple Type Definitions](#builtin-stds)
3.16.7.1 [xs:anySimpleType](#sec-anySimpleType)
3.16.7.2 [xs:anyAtomicType](#sec-anyAtomicType)
3.16.7.3 [xs:error](#xsd-error)
3.16.7.4 [Built-in primitive datatypes](#sec-builtin-primitives)
3.16.7.5 [Other built-in datatypes](#sec-other-builtins)
**Note:**This section consists of a combination of copies of normative material from [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2), for local cross-reference purposes, and material unique to this specification, relating to the interface between schema components defined in this specification and the simple type definition component.
Simple type definitions provide for constraining character information item [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of element and attribute information items.

Example
```
<xs:simpleType name="celsiusWaterTemp">
 <xs:restriction base="xs:decimal">
  <xs:fractionDigits value="2"/>
  <xs:minExclusive value="0.00"/>
  <xs:maxExclusive value="100.00"/>
 </xs:restriction>
</xs:simpleType>
```

The XML representation of a simple type definition.
#### <a id="Simple_Type_Definition_details"></a>3.16.1 The Simple Type Definition Schema Component

<a id="anchor11103"></a>The simple type definition schema component has the following properties:

Schema Component: <a id="std"></a>Simple Type Definition, a kind of [Type Definition](#td)<a id="std-annotations"></a>{annotations} A sequence of [Annotation](#a) components. <a id="std-name"></a>{name} An xs:NCName value. Optional.<a id="std-target_namespace"></a>{target namespace} An xs:anyURI value. Optional.<a id="std-final"></a>{final}
A subset of {*extension*, *restriction*, *list*, *union*}.

<a id="std-context"></a>{context} Required if [{name}](#std-name) is [·absent·](#key-null), otherwise must be [·absent·](#key-null).
Either an [Attribute Declaration](#ad), an [Element Declaration](#ed), a [Complex Type Definition](#ctd), or a [Simple Type Definition](#std).

<a id="std-base_type_definition"></a>{base type definition} A [Type Definition](#td) component. Required.
With one exception, the [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) of any [Simple Type Definition](#std) is a [Simple Type Definition](#std). The exception is [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType), which has [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType), a [Complex Type Definition](#ctd), as its [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition).

<a id="std-facets"></a>{facets} A set of [Constraining Facet](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#) components. <a id="std-fundamental_facets"></a>{fundamental facets} A set of [Fundamental Facet](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#) components. <a id="std-variety"></a>{variety} One of {atomic, list, union}. Required for all [Simple Type Definition](#std)s except [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType), in which it is [·absent·](#key-null).<a id="std-primitive_type_definition"></a>{primitive type definition} A [Simple Type Definition](#std) component. With one exception, required if [{variety}](#std-variety) is ***atomic***, otherwise must be [·absent·](#key-null). The exception is [·xs:anyAtomicType·](#key-anyAtomicType), whose [{primitive type definition}](#std-primitive_type_definition) is [·absent·](#key-null).
If non-[·absent·](#key-null), must be a [primitive](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-primitive) definition.

<a id="std-item_type_definition"></a>{item type definition} A [Simple Type Definition](#std) component. Required if [{variety}](#std-variety) is ***list***, otherwise must be [·absent·](#key-null).
The value of this property must be a primitive or ordinary simple type definition with [{variety}](#std-variety) = ***atomic***, or an ordinary simple type definition with [{variety}](#std-variety) = ***union***whose basic members are all atomic; the value must not itself be a list type (have [{variety}](#std-variety) = ***list***) or have any basic members which are list types.

<a id="std-member_type_definitions"></a>{member type definitions} A sequence of primitive or ordinary [Simple Type Definition](#std) components.
Must be present (but may be empty) if [{variety}](#std-variety) is ***union***, otherwise must be [·absent·](#key-null).

The sequence may contain any primitive or ordinary simple type definition, but must not contain any special type definitions.

Simple types are identified by their [{name}](#std-name) and [{target namespace}](#std-target_namespace). Except for anonymous simple types (those with no [{name}](#std-name)), since type definitions (i.e. both simple and complex type definitions taken together) must be uniquely identified within an [·XSD schema·](#key-schema), no simple type definition can have the same name as another simple or complex type definition. Simple type [{name}](#std-name)s and [{target namespace}](#ctd-target_namespace)s are provided for reference from instances (see [xsi:type (§2.7.1)](#xsi_type)), and for use in the XML representation of schema components (specifically in [<element>](#element-element) and [<attribute>](#element-attribute)). See [References to schema components across namespaces (<import>) (§4.2.6)](#composition-schemaImport) for the use of component identifiers when importing one schema into another.

**Note:**The [{name}](#std-name) of a simple type is not *ipso facto*the [[(local) name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the element or attribute information items [·validated·](#key-vn) by that definition. The connection between a name and a type definition is described in [Element Declarations (§3.3)](#cElement_Declarations) and [Attribute Declarations (§3.2)](#cAttribute_Declarations).
A simple type definition with an empty specification for [{final}](#std-final) can be used as the [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) for other types [·derived·](#key-derived) by either of extension or restriction, or as the [{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition) in the definition of a list, or in the [{member type definitions}](#std-member_type_definitions) of a union; the explicit values ***extension***, ***restriction***, ***list***and ***union***prevent further [·derivations·](#key-derived) by extension (to yield a complex type) and restriction (to yield a simple type) and use in [·constructing·](#key-constructed) lists and unions respectively.

[{variety}](#std-variety) determines whether the simple type corresponds to an ***atomic***, ***list***or ***union***type as defined by [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2).

As described in [Type Definition Hierarchy (§2.2.1.1)](#Type_Derivation), every simple type definition is a [·restriction·](#key-typeRestriction) of some other simple type (the [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition)), which is [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType) if and only if the type definition in question is [·xs:anyAtomicType·](#key-anyAtomicType) or a list or union type definition which is not itself [·derived·](#key-derived) by restriction from a list or union respectively. A type definition has [·xs:anyAtomicType·](#key-anyAtomicType) as its [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) if and only if it is one of the primitive datatypes. Each *atomic*type is ultimately a restriction of exactly one such primitive datatype, which is its [{primitive type definition}](#std-primitive_type_definition).

The [{facets}](#std-facets) property contains a set of constraining facets which are used to specify constraints on the datatype described by the simple type definition. For ***atomic***definitions, these are restricted to those appropriate for the corresponding [{primitive type definition}](#std-primitive_type_definition). Therefore, the value space and lexical space (i.e. what is [·validated·](#key-vn) by any atomic simple type) is determined by the pair ([{primitive type definition}](#std-primitive_type_definition), [{facets}](#std-facets)).

Constraining facets are defined in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2). All conforming implementations of this specification must support all of the facets defined in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2). It is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) whether additional facets are supported; if they are, the implementation must satisfy the rules for [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) facets described in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2).

As specified in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2), ***list***simple type definitions [·validate·](#key-vn) space separated tokens, each of which conforms to a specified simple type definition, the [{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition). The item type specified must not itself be a ***list***type, and must be one of the types identified in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2) as a suitable item type for a list simple type. In this case the [{facets}](#std-facets) apply to the list itself, and are restricted to those appropriate for lists.

A ***union***simple type definition [·validates·](#key-vn) strings which satisfy at least one of its [{member type definitions}](#std-member_type_definitions). As in the case of ***list***, the [{facets}](#std-facets) apply to the union itself, and are restricted to those appropriate for unions.

[·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType) or [·xs:anyAtomicType·](#key-anyAtomicType)must*not*be named as the [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) of any user-defined atomic simple type definitions: as they allow no constraining facets, this would be incoherent.

See [Annotations (§3.15)](#cAnnotations) for information on the role of the [{annotations}](#std-annotations) property.

#### <a id="declare-datatype"></a>3.16.2 XML Representation of Simple Type Definition Schema Components

As always, the mapping rules given in this section apply after, not before, the appropriate [·pre-processing·](#key-pre-processing).

**Note:**This section reproduces a version of material from [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2), for local cross-reference purposes.XML Representation Summary: `simpleType`Element Information Item et al.<a id="element-simpleType"></a><simpleType
final = (*#all*| List of (*list*| *union*| *restriction*| *extension*))
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
name = [NCName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NCName)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, ([restriction](#element-restriction) | [list](#element-list) | [union](#element-union)))
</simpleType><a id="element-restriction"></a><restriction
base = [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, ([simpleType](#element-simpleType)?, ([minExclusive](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-minExclusive) | [minInclusive](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-minInclusive) | [maxExclusive](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-maxExclusive) | [maxInclusive](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-maxInclusive) | [totalDigits](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-totalDigits) | [fractionDigits](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-fractionDigits) | [length](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-length) | [minLength](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-minLength) | [maxLength](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-maxLength) | [enumeration](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-enumeration) | [whiteSpace](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-whiteSpace) | [pattern](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-pattern) | [assertion](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-assertion) | [explicitTimezone](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-explicitTimezone) | *{any with namespace: ##other}*)*))
</restriction><a id="element-list"></a><list
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
itemType = [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, [simpleType](#element-simpleType)?)
</list><a id="element-union"></a><union
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
memberTypes = List of [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, [simpleType](#element-simpleType)*)
</union>
The [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) element and its descendants normally, when there are no errors, map to a [Simple Type Definition](#std) component. The case in which an [unknown](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-unknown-f) facet is used in the definition of a simple type definition is handled specially: the [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) in question is not in error, but it does not map to any component at all.

**Note:**The effect of the special handling of [unknown](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-unknown-f) facets is to ensure (1) that [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) facets which are not supported by a particular implementation result in the types which depend upon them not being present in the schema, and (2) that the presence of references to [unknown](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-unknown-f) facets in a schema document does not prevent the rest of the schema document being processed and used. The following subsections define one set of common mapping rules for simple type definitions, and three specialized sets of mapping rules for atomic, list, and union datatypes, respectively.
- If the [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) element has a [<restriction>](#element-restriction) element among its children, and the base type definition has [{variety}](#std-variety) = ***atomic***, then the mapping rules in [Common mapping rules for Simple Type Definitions (§3.16.2.1)](#map.std.common) and [Mapping Rules for Atomic Simple Type Definitions (§3.16.2.2)](#map.std.atomic) apply.
- If the [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) element has a [<list>](#element-list) element among its children, or if it has a [<restriction>](#element-restriction) child and the base type definition has [{variety}](#std-variety) = ***list***, then the mapping rules in [Common mapping rules for Simple Type Definitions (§3.16.2.1)](#map.std.common) and [Mapping Rules for Lists (§3.16.2.3)](#map.std.list) apply.
- If the [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) element has a [<union>](#element-union) element among its children, or if it has a [<restriction>](#element-restriction) child and the base type definition has [{variety}](#std-variety) = ***union***, then the mapping rules in [Common mapping rules for Simple Type Definitions (§3.16.2.1)](#map.std.common) and [Mapping Rules for Unions (§3.16.2.4)](#map.std.union) apply.
##### <a id="map.std.common"></a>3.16.2.1 Common mapping rules for Simple Type Definitions

The following rules apply to all simple type definitions.

XML Mapping Summary for [Simple Type Definition](#Simple_Type_Definition_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{name}](#std-name)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `name`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) if present on the [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) element, otherwise [·absent·](#key-null).[{target namespace}](#std-target_namespace)The [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `targetNamespace`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the ancestor [<schema>](#element-schema) element information item if present, otherwise [·absent·](#key-null).[{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the [<restriction>](#element-restriction) alternative is chosen, **then **the type definition [·resolved·](#src-resolve) to by the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `base`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of [<restriction>](#element-restriction), if present, otherwise the type definition corresponding to the [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of [<restriction>](#element-restriction).2 **If **the [<list>](#element-list) or [<union>](#element-union) alternative is chosen, **then **[·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType).[{final}](#std-final) A subset of `{`***restriction***, ***extension***, ***list***, ***union***`}`, determined as follows. <a id="lt-vs"></a>[Definition:]Let **FS**be the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `final`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if present, otherwise the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `finalDefault`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the ancestor `schema`element, if present, otherwise the empty string. Then the property value is the appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **[·FS·](#lt-vs) is the empty string, **then **the empty set;2 **If **[·FS·](#lt-vs) is "`#all`", **then **`{`***restriction***, ***extension***, ***list***, ***union***`}`;3 **otherwise **Consider [·FS·](#lt-vs) as a space-separated list, and include ***restriction***if "`restriction`" is in that list, and similarly for ***extension***, ***list***and ***union***. [{context}](#std-context) The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the `name`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is present, **then **[·absent·](#key-null)2 **otherwise **the appropriate **case**among the following:2.1 **If **the parent element information item is [<attribute>](#element-attribute), **then **the corresponding [Attribute Declaration](#ad)2.2 **If **the parent element information item is [<element>](#element-element), **then **the corresponding [Element Declaration](#ed)2.3 **If **the parent element information item is [<list>](#element-list) or [<union>](#element-union), **then **the [Simple Type Definition](#std) corresponding to the grandparent [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) element information item2.4 **If **<a id="anchor11290a"></a>the parent element information item is [<alternative>](#element-alternative), **then **the [Element Declaration](#ed) corresponding to the nearest enclosing [<element>](#element-element) element information item2.5 **otherwise **(the parent element information item is [<restriction>](#element-restriction)), the appropriate **case**among the following:2.5.1 **If **the grandparent element information item is [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType), **then **the [Simple Type Definition](#std) corresponding to the grandparent2.5.2 **otherwise **(the grandparent element information item is [<simpleContent>](#element-simpleContent)), the [Simple Type Definition](#std) which is the [{content type}](#ctd-content_type).[{simple type definition}](#ct-simple_type_definition) of the [Complex Type Definition](#ctd) corresponding to the great-grandparent [<complexType>](#element-complexType) element information item.[{variety}](#std-variety)If the [<list>](#element-list) alternative is chosen, then ***list***, otherwise if the [<union>](#element-union) alternative is chosen, then ***union***, otherwise (the [<restriction>](#element-restriction) alternative is chosen), then the [{variety}](#std-variety) of the [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition).[{facets}](#std-facets)The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the [<restriction>](#element-restriction) alternative is chosen **and**the children of the [<restriction>](#element-restriction) element are all either [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) elements, [<annotation>](#element-annotation) elements, or elements which specify constraining facets supported by the processor, **then **the set of [Constraining Facet](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#f) components obtained by [·overlaying·](#key-facets-overlay) the [{facets}](#std-facets) of the [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) with the set of [Constraining Facet](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#f) components corresponding to those [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of [<restriction>](#element-restriction) which specify facets, as defined in [Simple Type Restriction (Facets) (§3.16.6.4)](#st-restrict-facets).2 **If **the [<restriction>](#element-restriction) alternative is chosen **and**the children of the [<restriction>](#element-restriction) element include at least one element of which the processor has no prior knowledge (i.e. not a [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) element, an [<annotation>](#element-annotation) element, or an element denoting a constraining facet known to and supported by the processor), **then **the [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) element maps to no component at all (but is not in error solely on account of the presence of the unknown element).3 **If **the [<list>](#element-list) alternative is chosen, **then **a set with one member, a [whiteSpace](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#f-w) facet with [{value}](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#f-w-value) = ***collapse***and [{fixed}](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#f-w-fixed) = ***true***.4 **otherwise **the empty set[{fundamental facets}](#std-fundamental_facets)Based on [{variety}](#std-variety), [{facets}](#std-facets), [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) and [{member type definitions}](#std-member_type_definitions), a set of [Fundamental Facet](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ff) components, one each as specified in [The ordered Schema Component](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dc-ordered), [The bounded Schema Component](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dc-bounded), [The cardinality Schema Component](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dc-cardinality) and [The numeric Schema Component](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dc-numeric).[{annotations}](#std-annotations) The [·annotation mapping·](#key-am-set) of the set of elements containing the [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType), and one of the [<restriction>](#element-restriction), [<list>](#element-list) or [<union>](#element-union)[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), whichever is present, as defined in [XML Representation of Annotation Schema Components (§3.15.2)](#declare-annotation).
##### <a id="map.std.atomic"></a>3.16.2.2 Mapping Rules for Atomic Simple Type Definitions

<a id="std.atomic.p"></a>
The following rule applies if the [{variety}](#std-variety) is ***atomic***

<a id="std-ancestor"></a>[Definition:]The **ancestors**of a [·type definition·](#td) are its [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) and the [·ancestors·](#std-ancestor) of its [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition). (The ancestors of a [Simple Type Definition](#std)*T*in the type hierarchy are themselves [·type definitions·](#td); they are distinct from the XML elements which may be ancestors, in the XML document hierarchy, of the [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) element which declares *T*.)

XML Mapping Summary for [Atomic Simple Type Definition](#Simple_Type_Definition_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{primitive type definition}](#std-primitive_type_definition)From among the [·ancestors·](#std-ancestor) of this [Simple Type Definition](#std), that [Simple Type Definition](#std) which corresponds to a [primitive](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-primitive) datatype.
##### <a id="map.std.list"></a>3.16.2.3 Mapping Rules for Lists

<a id="std.list.p"></a>
If the [{variety}](#std-variety) is ***list***, the following additional property mapping applies:

XML Mapping Summary for [List Simple Type Definition](#Simple_Type_Definition_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition) The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) is [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType), **then **the [Simple Type Definition](#std) (a) [·resolved·](#src-resolve) to by the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `itemType`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of [<list>](#element-list), or (b), corresponding to the [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of [<list>](#element-list), whichever is present. **Note:**In this case, a [<list>](#element-list) element will invariably be present; it will invariably have either an `itemType`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) or a [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType)[[child]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), but not both.2 **otherwise **(that is, the [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) is not [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType)), the [{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition) of the [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition). **Note:**In this case, a [<restriction>](#element-restriction) element will invariably be present.
##### <a id="map.std.union"></a>3.16.2.4 Mapping Rules for Unions

<a id="std.union.p"></a>
If the [{variety}](#std-variety) is ***union***, the following additional property mapping applies:

XML Mapping Summary for [Union Simple Type Definition](#Simple_Type_Definition_details)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{member type definitions}](#std-member_type_definitions)The appropriate **case**among the following:1 **If **the [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) is [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType), **then **the sequence of [Simple Type Definition](#std)s (a) [·resolved·](#src-resolve) to by the items in the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of the `memberTypes`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of [<union>](#element-union), if any, and (b) corresponding to the [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType)s among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of [<union>](#element-union), if any, in order. **Note:**In this case, a [<union>](#element-union) element will invariably be present; it will invariably have either a `memberTypes`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) or one or more [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType)[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), or both.2 **otherwise **(that is, the [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) is not [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType)), the [{member type definitions}](#std-member_type_definitions) of the [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition). **Note:**In this case, a [<restriction>](#element-restriction) element will invariably be present.
#### <a id="sec-src-simple-type"></a>3.16.3 Constraints on XML Representations of Simple Type Definitions

<a id="src-simple-type"></a>**Schema Representation Constraint: Simple Type Definition Representation OK**
In addition to the conditions imposed on [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) element information items by the schema for schema documents, **all**of the following must be true:1 No two elements among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of [<restriction>](#element-restriction) have the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) in the Schema (`xs`) namespace, unless that [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) is one of `xs:enumeration`, `xs:pattern`, or `xs:assertion`. **Note:**That is, most of the facets for simple types defined by this specification are forbidden to occur more than once. But [<enumeration>](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-enumeration), [<pattern>](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-pattern), and [<assertion>](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#element-assertion) may occur multiple times, and facets defined in other namespaces and made available as extensions to this specification may occur multiple times. 2 If the [<restriction>](#element-restriction) alternative is chosen, it has either a `base`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) or a [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) among its [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), but not both.3 If the [<list>](#element-list) alternative is chosen, it has either an `itemType`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) or a [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) among its [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), but not both.4 If the [<union>](#element-union) alternative is chosen, either it has a non-empty `memberTypes`[[attribute]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) or it has at least one `simpleType`[[child]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).
#### <a id="sec-cvc-simple-type"></a>3.16.4 Simple Type Definition Validation Rules

<a id="cvc-simple-type"></a>**Validation Rule: String Valid**
For a string *S*to be locally [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to a simple type definition *T***all**of the following must be true:1 The [·normalized value·](#key-nv) of *S*, *N*, is calculated using the [whiteSpace facet](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#rf-whiteSpace), and any other [pre-lexical facets](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-pre-lexical) associated with *T*, as described in the definition of the term "[·normalized value·](#key-nv)".2 *N*is schema-valid with respect to *T*as defined by [Datatype Valid](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#cvc-datatype-valid) in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2).3 Let *V*be the [·actual value·](#key-vv) of *N*with respect to *T*. Then:Every [·ENTITY value·](#key-TYPE-value) in *V*is a [·declared entity name·](#key-vde).<a id="key-vtype"></a>[Definition:] When a string *N*is schema-valid with respect to a simple type definition *T*as defined by [Datatype Valid](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#cvc-datatype-valid) with the corresponding [·actual value·](#key-vv)*V*, 1 The **validating type**of *V*is *T*if *T*is not a union type, otherwise the validating type is the [basic member](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-basicmember) of *T*'s [transitive membership](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-transitivemembership) which actually [·validated·](#key-vn)*N*. 2 If the [·validating type·](#key-vtype) of *V*is a list type *L*and the [{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition) of *L*is *I*, then the **validating type**of an (atomic) item value *A*occurring in *V*is *I*if *I*is not a union type, otherwise the validating type is the [basic member](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-basicmember) of *I*'s [transitive membership](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-transitivemembership) which actually [·validated·](#key-vn) the substring in *N*that corresponds to *A*. <a id="key-TYPE-value"></a>[Definition:] When the [·validating type·](#key-vtype) of an [·actual value·](#key-vv) is or is [·derived·](#key-derived) from a simple type definition *T*, the value is also referred to as a ***T*value**. For example, an **ENTITY value**is an [·actual value·](#key-vv) whose [·validating type·](#key-vtype) is or is derived from the built-in simple type definition [ENTITY](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ENTITY), and an **ID value**is one whose [·validating type·](#key-vtype) is or is derived from [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID). <a id="key-vde"></a>[Definition:]A string is a **declared entity name**if and only if it is equal to the [[name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.entity.unparsed) of some unparsed entity information item in the value of the [[unparsedEntities]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.document) property of the document information item at the root of the infoset containing the element or attribute information item whose [·normalized value·](#key-nv) the string is.
#### <a id="sec-sic-simple-type"></a>3.16.5 Simple Type Definition Information Set Contributions

None as such.

#### <a id="coss-st"></a>3.16.6 Constraints on Simple Type Definition Schema Components

##### <a id="sec-st-props-correct"></a>3.16.6.1 Simple Type Definition Properties Correct

All simple type definitions must satisfy both the following constraints.

<a id="st-props-correct"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Simple Type Definition Properties Correct**
**All**of the following must be true:1 The values of the properties of a simple type definition are as described in the property tableau in [The Simple Type Definition Schema Component](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dc-defn), modulo the impact of [Missing Sub-components (§5.3)](#conformance-missing).2 All simple type definitions are, or are [·derived·](#key-derived) ultimately from, [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType) (so circular definitions are disallowed). That is, it is possible to reach a primitive datatype or [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType) by following the [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) zero or more times.3 The [{final}](#std-final) of the [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) does not contain ***restriction***.4 There is not more than one member of [{facets}](#std-facets) of the same kind.5 Each member of [{facets}](#std-facets) is supported by the processor.**Note:**As specified normatively elsewhere, all conforming processors must support the facets defined by [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2); support for additional facets is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined). If a schema document applies an [unknown](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-unknown-f) facet, the immediate result will be a violation of this constraint, so that the simple type defined by means of that facet will be excluded from the schema, and any references to it will be treated as undischarged references.
##### <a id="sec-cos-st-restricts"></a>3.16.6.2 Derivation Valid (Restriction, Simple)

<a id="cos-st-restricts"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Derivation Valid (Restriction, Simple)**
<a id="anchor11103a"></a> For any [Simple Type Definition](#std)*D*whose [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) is some [Simple Type Definition](#std)*B*, the appropriate **case**among the following must be true:1 **If ***D*.[{variety}](#std-variety) = ***atomic***, **then ****all**of the following are true:1.1<a id="c-base_atomic"></a>Either *D*is [·xs:anyAtomicType·](#key-anyAtomicType), or else *B*is an atomic simple type definition. **Note:**The type [·xs:anyAtomicType·](#key-anyAtomicType) is an exception because its [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) is [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType), whose [{variety}](#std-variety) is [·absent·](#key-null).1.2 *B*.[{final}](#std-final) does not contain ***restriction***.1.3 For each facet in *D*.[{facets}](#std-facets) (call this **DF**) **all**of the following are true:1.3.1 **DF**is applicable to *D*, as specified in [Applicable Facets](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#cos-applicable-facets) of [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2).1.3.2 **DF**satisfies the constraints on facet components given in the appropriate subsection of [Constraining Facets](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#rf-facets) in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2). 2 **If ***D*.[{variety}](#std-variety) = ***list***, **then ****all**of the following are true:2.1 *D*.[{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition) is not a special type definition and either *D*.[{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition).[{variety}](#std-variety) = ***atomic***or *D*.[{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition).[{variety}](#std-variety) = ***union***and there are no types whose [{variety}](#std-variety) is ***list***among the union's [transitive membership](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-transitivemembership).2.2 The appropriate **case**among the following is true:2.2.1 **If ***B*is [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType) , **then ****all**of the following are true:2.2.1.1 *D*.[{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition).[{final}](#std-final) does not contain ***list***.2.2.1.2 <a id="anchor6735"></a>*D*.[{facets}](#std-facets) contains only the ***whiteSpace***facet component with [{value}](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#f-w-value) = ***collapse***and [{fixed}](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#f-w-fixed) = ***true***.2.2.2 **otherwise ****all**of the following are true:2.2.2.1 *B*.[{variety}](#std-variety) = ***list***.2.2.2.2 *B*.[{final}](#std-final) does not contain ***restriction***.2.2.2.3 *D*.[{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition) is validly [·derived·](#key-derived) from *B*.[{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition), as defined in [Type Derivation OK (Simple) (§3.16.6.3)](#cos-st-derived-ok).2.2.2.4 All facets in [{facets}](#std-facets) are applicable to *D*, as specified in [Applicable Facets](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#cos-applicable-facets).2.2.2.5 All facets in [{facets}](#std-facets) satisfy the constraints on facet components given in the appropriate subsection of [Constraining Facets](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#rf-facets). The first case above will apply when a list is [constructed](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-constructed) by specifying an item type, the second when [·derived·](#key-derived) by restriction from another list.3 **If ***D*.[{variety}](#std-variety) is ***union***, **then ****all**of the following are true:3.1 *D*.[{member type definitions}](#std-member_type_definitions) does not contain a special type definition.3.2 The appropriate **case**among the following is true:3.2.1 **If ***B*is [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType) , **then ****all**of the following are true:3.2.1.1 All of the [{member type definitions}](#std-member_type_definitions) have a [{final}](#std-final) which does not contain ***union***.3.2.1.2 *D*.[{facets}](#std-facets) is empty.3.2.2 **otherwise ****all**of the following are true:3.2.2.1 *B*.[{variety}](#std-variety) = ***union***.3.2.2.2 *B*.[{final}](#std-final) does not contain ***restriction***.3.2.2.3 Each type definition in *D*.[{member type definitions}](#std-member_type_definitions) is validly [·derived·](#key-derived) from the corresponding type definition in *B*.[{member type definitions}](#std-member_type_definitions), as defined in [Type Derivation OK (Simple) (§3.16.6.3)](#cos-st-derived-ok).3.2.2.4 All facets in [{facets}](#std-facets) are applicable to *D*, as specified in [Applicable Facets](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#cos-applicable-facets).3.2.2.5 All facets in [{facets}](#std-facets) satisfy the constraints on facet components given in the appropriate subsection of [Constraining Facets](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#rf-facets). The first case above will apply when a union is [constructed](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-constructed) by specifying one or more member types, the second when [·derived·](#key-derived) by restriction from another union.3.3<a id="no-self-membership"></a> Neither *D*nor any type [·derived·](#key-derived) from it is a member of its own [transitive membership](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-transitivemembership).
<a id="cd-st-restriction"></a>[Definition:]A simple type definition **T**is a **valid restriction**of its [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) if and only if **T**satisfies constraint [Derivation Valid (Restriction, Simple) (§3.16.6.2)](#cos-st-restricts).

##### <a id="sec-cos-st-derived-ok"></a>3.16.6.3 Type Derivation OK (Simple)

The following constraint defines relations appealed to elsewhere in this specification.

<a id="cos-st-derived-ok"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Type Derivation OK (Simple)**
For a simple type definition (call it **D**, for [·derived·](#key-derived)) to be validly [·derived·](#key-derived) from a type definition (call this **B**, for base) subject to a set of blocking keywords drawn from the set {***extension***, ***restriction***, ***list***, ***union***} (of which only ***restriction***is actually relevant; call this set *S*) **one**of the following must be true:1<a id="c-stid"></a>They are the same type definition.2 **All**of the following are true:2.1 ***restriction***is not in *S*, or in **D**.[{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition).[{final}](#std-final);2.2 **One or more**of the following is true:2.2.1 **D**.[{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) = **B**.2.2.2 **D**.[{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) is not [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType) and is validly [·derived·](#key-derived) from **B**given *S*, as defined by this constraint.2.2.3 **D**.[{variety}](#std-variety) = ***list***or ***union***and **B**is [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType).2.2.4<a id="member_as_derived"></a>**All**of the following are true:2.2.4.1 **B**.[{variety}](#std-variety) = ***union***.2.2.4.2 **D**is validly [·derived·](#key-derived) from a type definition **M**in **B**'s [transitive membership](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-transitivemembership) given *S*, as defined by this constraint.2.2.4.3 The [{facets}](#std-facets) property of **B**and of any [intervening union](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-interveningunion) datatypes is empty.**Note:**It is a consequence of this requirement that the [value space](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-value-space), [lexical space](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-lexical-space), and [lexical mapping](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-lexical-mapping) of **D**will be subsets of those of **B**. **Note:**With respect to clause [1](#c-stid), see the Note on identity at the end of [(§3.4.6.5)](#no-identity) above.**Note:**When a simple type definition *S*is said to be "validly [·derived·](#key-derived)" from a type definition *T*, without mention of any specific set of blocking keywords, then what is meant is that *S*is validly derived from *T*, subject to the empty set of blocking keywords, i.e. without any particular limitations.**Note:**It is a consequence of clause [2.2.4](#member_as_derived) that the constraint [(§3.16.6.2)](#cd-st-restriction) can hold between a [Simple Type Definition](#std) in the [transitive membership](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-transitivemembership) of a union type, and the union type, even though neither is actually [·derived·](#key-derived) from the other. The slightly misleading terminology is retained for historical reasons and for compatibility with version 1.0 of this specification.
##### <a id="sec-st-restrict-facets"></a>3.16.6.4 Simple Type Restriction (Facets)

<a id="st-restrict-facets"></a>**Schema Component Constraint: Simple Type Restriction (Facets)**
For a simple type definition (call it **R**) to restrict another simple type definition (call it **B**) with a set of facets (call this **S**) **all**of the following must be true:1 The [{variety}](#std-variety) of **R**is the same as that of **B**.2 If [{variety}](#std-variety) is ***atomic***, the [{primitive type definition}](#std-primitive_type_definition) of **R**is the same as that of **B**.3<a id="c-fr"></a>The [{facets}](#std-facets) of **R**are the [{facets}](#std-facets) of *B*[·overlaid·](#key-facets-overlay) with *S*.Additional constraint(s) sometimes apply depending on the kind of facet, see the appropriate sub-section of [4.3 Constraining Facets](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#rf-facets).<a id="key-facets-overlay"></a>[Definition:] Given two sets of facets *B*and *S*, the result of **overlaying***B*with *S*is the set of facets *R*for which **all**of the following are true:1<a id="fr1"></a>Every facet in **S**is in **R**.2<a id="fr2"></a> Every facet in **B**is in **R**, unless it is of the same kind as some facet in **S**, in which case it is not included in **R**. 3 Every facet in **R**is required by clause [1](#fr1) or clause [2](#fr2) above.
#### <a id="builtin-stds"></a>3.16.7 Built-in Simple Type Definitions

##### <a id="sec-anySimpleType"></a>3.16.7.1 xs:anySimpleType

The [Simple Type Definition](#std) of [anySimpleType](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anySimpleType) is present in every schema. It has the following properties:

<a id="simple-ur-type-itself"></a>Simple Type Definition of anySimpleType**Property****Value**[{name}](#std-name)'`anySimpleType`'[{target namespace}](#std-target_namespace)'`http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema`'[{final}](#std-final)The empty set[{context}](#std-context)[·absent·](#key-null)[{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition)[·xs:anyType·](#any-type-itself)[{facets}](#std-facets)The empty set[{fundamental facets}](#std-fundamental_facets)The empty set[{variety}](#std-variety)[·absent·](#key-null)[{primitive type definition}](#std-primitive_type_definition)[·absent·](#key-null)[{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition)[·absent·](#key-null)[{member type definitions}](#std-member_type_definitions)[·absent·](#key-null)[{annotations}](#std-annotations)The empty sequence
The definition of [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType) is the root of the simple type definition hierarchy, and as such mediates between the other simple type definitions, which all eventually trace back to it via their [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) properties, and [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType), which is *its*[{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition).

##### <a id="sec-anyAtomicType"></a>3.16.7.2 xs:anyAtomicType

The [Simple Type Definition](#std) of [anyAtomicType](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anyAtomicType) is present in every schema. It has the following properties:

<a id="aat-def"></a>Simple Type Definition of anyAtomicType**Property****Value**[{name}](#std-name)'`anyAtomicType`'[{target namespace}](#std-target_namespace)'`http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema`'[{final}](#std-final)The empty set[{context}](#std-context)[·absent·](#key-null)[{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition)[·xs:anySimpleType·](#simple-ur-type-itself)[{facets}](#std-facets)The empty set[{fundamental facets}](#std-fundamental_facets)The empty set[{variety}](#std-variety)***atomic***[{primitive type definition}](#std-primitive_type_definition)[·absent·](#key-null)[{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition)[·absent·](#key-null)[{member type definitions}](#std-member_type_definitions)[·absent·](#key-null)[{annotations}](#std-annotations)The empty sequence
##### <a id="xsd-error"></a>3.16.7.3 xs:error

A [Simple Type Definition](#std) for [·xs:error·](#key-error) is present in every schema by definition. It has the following properties:

<a id="xsd-error-tableau"></a>Simple Type Definition of `xs:error`**Property****Value**[{name}](#std-name)'`error`'[{target namespace}](#std-target_namespace)'`http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema`'[{final}](#std-final){***extension***, ***restriction***, ***list***, ***union***}[{context}](#std-context)[·absent·](#key-null)[{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition)[·xs:anySimpleType·](#simple-ur-type-itself)[{facets}](#std-facets)The empty set[{fundamental facets}](#std-fundamental_facets)The empty set[{variety}](#std-variety)***union***[{primitive type definition}](#std-primitive_type_definition)[·absent·](#key-null)[{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition)[·absent·](#key-null)[{member type definitions}](#std-member_type_definitions)The empty sequence[{annotations}](#std-annotations)The empty sequence**Note:**The datatype `xs:error`has no valid instances (i.e. it has an empty value space and an empty lexical space). This is a natural consequence of its construction: a value is a value of a union type if and only if it is a value of at least one member of the [{member type definitions}](#std-member_type_definitions) of the union. Since `xs:error`has no member type definitions, there can be no values which are values of at least one of its member types. And since the value space is empty, the lexical space is also empty.The type `xs:error`is expected to be used mostly in conditional type assignment. Whenever it serves as the [·governing·](#key-governing) type definition for an attribute or element information item, that item will be invalid.
##### <a id="sec-builtin-primitives"></a>3.16.7.4 Built-in primitive datatypes

Simple type definitions corresponding to all the built-in primitive datatypes, namely ***string***, ***boolean***, ***float***, ***double***, ***decimal***, ***dateTime***, ***duration***, ***time***, ***date***, ***gMonth***, ***gMonthDay***, ***gDay***, ***gYear***, ***gYearMonth***, ***hexBinary***, ***base64Binary***, ***anyURI***, ***QName***and ***NOTATION***(see the [Primitive Datatypes](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#built-in-primitive-datatypes) section of [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2)) are present by definition in every schema as follows:

<a id="dummy-def"></a>Simple Type Definition corresponding to the built-in primitive datatypes**Property****Value**[{name}](#std-name)[as appropriate][{target namespace}](#std-target_namespace)'`http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema`'[{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition)[·xs:anyAtomicType·](#aat-def)[{final}](#std-final)The empty set[{variety}](#std-variety)***atomic***[{primitive type definition}](#std-primitive_type_definition)[this simple type definition itself][{facets}](#std-facets){a [whitespace](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#f-w) facet with [[value]](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#f-w-value) = ***collapse***and [[fixed]](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#f-w-fixed) = ***true***in all cases except [string](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#string), which has [[value]](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#f-w-value) = ***preserve***and [[fixed]](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#f-w-fixed) = ***false***}[{fundamental facets}](#std-fundamental_facets)
[as appropriate]

[{context}](#std-context)[·absent·](#key-null)[{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition)[·absent·](#key-null)[{member type definitions}](#std-member_type_definitions)[·absent·](#key-null)[{annotations}](#std-annotations)The empty sequence
All conforming implementations of this specification must support all the primitive datatypes defined in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2). It is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) whether additional primitive datatypes are supported, and whether, if so, they are automatically incorporated in every schema or not. If [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) primitives are supported, the implementation must satisfy the rules for [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) primitive datatypes described in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2).

<a id="key-automatic"></a>[Definition:] A type about which a processor possesses prior knowledge, and which the processor can support without any declaration of the type being supplied by the user, is said to be **automatically known**to the processor.

**Note:**By their nature, primitive types can be supported by a processor only if [·automatically known·](#key-automatic) to that processor.Types [·automatically known·](#key-automatic) to a processor, whether primitive or derived, can be included automatically by that processor in all schemas, but need not be. It is possible, for example, for a processor to have built-in prior knowledge of a set of primitive and derived types, but to include them in the schema only when the relevant namespace is explicitly imported, or a given run-time option is selected, or on some other conditions; such conditions are not defined by this specification. **Note:**The definition of "[·automatically known·](#key-automatic)" is not intended to prevent implementations from allowing users to specify new primitive types. If an implementation defines a mechanism by which users can define or supply an implementation of a primitive type, then when those mechanisms are successfully used, such user-supplied types are [·automatically known·](#key-automatic) to the implementation, as that term is used in this specification.
##### <a id="sec-other-builtins"></a>3.16.7.5 Other built-in datatypes

Similarly, simple type definitions corresponding to all the other built-in datatypes (see the [Other Built-in Datatypes](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ordinary-built-ins) section of [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2)) are present by definition in every schema, with properties as specified in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2) and as represented in XML in [Illustrative XML representations for the built-in ordinary type definitions](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#drvd.nxsd).

<a id="dummy-ddef"></a>Simple Type Definition corresponding to the built-in ordinary datatypes**Property****Value**[{name}](#std-name)[as appropriate][{target namespace}](#std-target_namespace)'`http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema`'[{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition)[as specified in the appropriate sub-section of [Other Built-in Datatypes](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ordinary-built-ins)][{final}](#std-final)The empty set[{variety}](#std-variety)[***atomic***or ***list***, as specified in the appropriate sub-section of [Other Built-in Datatypes](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ordinary-built-ins)][{primitive type [Other Built-in Datatypes](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ordinary-built-ins)][{context}](#std-context)[·absent·](#key-null)[{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition)if [{variety}](#std-variety) is ***atomic***, then [·absent·](#key-null), otherwise as specified in the appropriate sub-section of [Other Built-in Datatypes](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ordinary-built-ins)][{member type definitions}](#std-member_type_definitions)[·absent·](#key-null)[{annotations}](#std-annotations)As shown in the XML representations of the ordinary built-in datatypes in [Illustrative XML representations for the built-in ordinary type definitions](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#drvd.nxsd)
All conforming implementations of this specification must support all the built-in datatypes defined in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2). It is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) whether additional derived types are [·automatically known·](#key-automatic) to the implementation without declaration and whether, if so, they are automatically incorporated in every schema or not.

### <a id="Schemas"></a>3.17 Schemas as a Whole

3.17.1 [The Schema Itself](#Schema_details)
3.17.2 [XML Representations of Schemas](#declare-schema)
3.17.2.1 [References to Schema Components](#refSchemaConstructs)
3.17.2.2 [References to Schema Components from Elsewhere](#sec-refs-from-elsewhere)
3.17.3 [Constraints on XML Representations of Schemas](#coxr.schemas)
3.17.4 [Validation Rules for Schemas as a Whole](#sec-del-cvc-resolve-instance)
3.17.5 [Schema Information Set Contributions](#sec-sic-schema-all)
3.17.5.1 [Schema Information](#sec-sic-schema)
3.17.5.2 [ID/IDREF Table](#sec-sic-id)
3.17.6 [Constraints on Schemas as a Whole](#coss-schema)
3.17.6.1 [Schema Properties Correct](#sec-sch-props-correct)
3.17.6.2 [QName resolution (Schema Document)](#sec-src-resolve)
3.17.6.3 [QName resolution (Instance)](#sec-cvc-resolve-instance)
A schema consists of a set of schema components.

Example
```
<xs:schema
    xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"
    targetNamespace="http://www.example.com/example">
  . . .
</xs:schema>
```

The XML representation of the skeleton of a schema.
#### <a id="Schema_details"></a>3.17.1 The Schema Itself

At the abstract level, the schema itself is just a container for its components.

Schema Component: <a id="s"></a>Schema, a kind of [Annotated Component](#ac)<a id="s-annotations"></a>{annotations} A sequence of [Annotation](#a) components. <a id="s-type_definitions"></a>{type definitions} A set of [Type Definition](#td) components. <a id="s-attribute_declarations"></a>{attribute declarations} A set of [Attribute Declaration](#ad) components. <a id="s-element_declarations"></a>{element declarations} A set of [Element Declaration](#ed) components. <a id="s-attribute_group_definitions"></a>{attribute group definitions} A set of [Attribute Group Definition](#agd) components. <a id="s-model_group_definitions"></a>{model group definitions} A set of [Model Group Definition](#mgd) components. <a id="s-notation_declarations"></a>{notation declarations} A set of [Notation Declaration](#nd) components. <a id="s-identity-constraint_definitions"></a>{identity-constraint definitions} A set of [Identity-Constraint Definition](#icd) components.
#### <a id="declare-schema"></a>3.17.2 XML Representations of Schemas

A schema is represented in XML by one or more [·schema documents·](#key-schemaDoc), that is, one or more [<schema>](#element-schema) element information items. A [·schema document·](#key-schemaDoc) contains representations for a collection of schema components, e.g. type definitions and element declarations, which have a common {target namespace}. A [·schema document·](#key-schemaDoc) which has one or more [<import>](#element-import) element information items corresponds to a schema with components with more than one {target namespace}, see [Import Constraints and Semantics (§4.2.6.2)](#src-import).

As always, the mapping rules given in this section apply after, not before, the appropriate [·pre-processing·](#key-pre-processing).

XML Representation Summary: `schema`Element Information Item et al.<a id="element-schema"></a><schema
attributeFormDefault = (*qualified*| *unqualified*) : unqualified
blockDefault = (*#all*| List of (*extension*| *restriction*| *substitution*))  : ''
defaultAttributes = [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName)
xpathDefaultNamespace = ([anyURI](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anyURI) | (*##defaultNamespace*| *##targetNamespace*| *##local*))  : ##local
elementFormDefault = (*qualified*| *unqualified*) : unqualified
finalDefault = (*#all*| List of (*extension*| *restriction*| *list*| *union*))  : ''
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
targetNamespace = [anyURI](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#anyURI)
version = [token](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#token)
xml:lang = [language](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#language)
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *(([include](#element-include) | [import](#element-import) | [redefine](#element-redefine) | [override](#element-override) | [annotation](#element-annotation))*, ([defaultOpenContent](#element-defaultOpenContent), [annotation](#element-annotation)*)?, (([simpleType](#element-simpleType) | [complexType](#element-complexType) | [group](#element-group) | [attributeGroup](#element-attributeGroup) | [element](#element-element) | [attribute](#element-attribute) | [notation](#element-notation)), [annotation](#element-annotation)*)*)
</schema><a id="element-defaultOpenContent"></a><defaultOpenContent
appliesToEmpty = [boolean](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#boolean): false
id = [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID)
mode = (*interleave*| *suffix*) : interleave
*{any attributes with non-schema namespace . . .}*>
*Content: *([annotation](#element-annotation)?, [any](#element-any))
</defaultOpenContent>
The [<schema>](#element-schema) element information item maps to a [Schema](#s) component as follows.

XML Mapping Summary for [Schema](#key-schema)**Schema Component****Property****Representation**[{type definitions}](#s-type_definitions)<a id="anchor5918"></a>The simple and complex type definitions corresponding to all the [<simpleType>](#element-simpleType) and [<complexType>](#element-complexType) element information items in the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if any, plus any definitions brought in via [<include>](#element-include) (see [Assembling a schema for a single target namespace from multiple schema definition documents (<include>) (§4.2.3)](#compound-schema)), [<override>](#element-override) (see [Overriding component definitions (<override>) (§4.2.5)](#override-schema)), [<redefine>](#element-redefine) (see [Including modified component definitions (<redefine>) (§4.2.4)](#modify-schema)), and [<import>](#element-import) (see [References to schema components across namespaces (<import>) (§4.2.6)](#composition-schemaImport)). [{attribute declarations}](#s-attribute_declarations) The (top-level) attribute declarations corresponding to all the [<attribute>](#element-attribute) element information items in the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if any, plus any declarations brought in via [<include>](#element-include), [<override>](#element-override), [<redefine>](#element-redefine), and [<import>](#element-import). [{element declarations}](#s-element_declarations)The (top-level) element declarations corresponding to all the [<element>](#element-element) element information items in the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if any, plus any declarations brought in via [<include>](#element-include), [<override>](#element-override), [<redefine>](#element-redefine), and [<import>](#element-import). [{attribute group definitions}](#s-attribute_group_definitions)The attribute group definitions corresponding to all the [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) element information items in the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if any, plus any definitions brought in via [<include>](#element-include), [<override>](#element-override), [<redefine>](#element-redefine), and [<import>](#element-import). [{model group definitions}](#s-model_group_definitions)The model group definitions corresponding to all the [<group>](#element-group) element information items in the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if any, plus any definitions brought in via [<include>](#element-include), [<redefine>](#element-redefine) and [<import>](#element-import). [{notation declarations}](#s-notation_declarations)The notation declarations corresponding to all the [<notation>](#element-notation) element information items in the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if any, plus any declarations brought in via [<include>](#element-include), [<override>](#element-override), [<redefine>](#element-redefine), and [<import>](#element-import). [{identity- constraint definitions}](#s-identity-constraint_definitions) The identity-constraint definitions corresponding to all the [<key>](#element-key), [<keyref>](#element-keyref), and [<unique>](#element-unique) element information items anywhere within the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if any, plus any definitions brought in via [<include>](#element-include), [<override>](#element-override), [<redefine>](#element-redefine), and [<import>](#element-import). [{annotations}](#s-annotations) The [·annotation mapping·](#key-am-set) of the set of elements containing the [<schema>](#element-schema) and all the [<include>](#element-include), [<redefine>](#element-redefine), [<override>](#element-override), [<import>](#element-import), and [<defaultOpenContent>](#element-defaultOpenContent)[[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), if any, as defined in [XML Representation of Annotation Schema Components (§3.15.2)](#declare-annotation).
Note that none of the attribute information items displayed above correspond directly to properties of schemas. The `blockDefault`, `finalDefault`, `attributeFormDefault`, `elementFormDefault`and `targetNamespace`attributes are appealed to in the sub-sections above, as they provide global information applicable to many representation/component correspondences. The other attributes (`id`and `version`) are for user convenience, and this specification defines no semantics for them.

The definition of the schema abstract data model in [XSD Abstract Data Model (§2.2)](#concepts-data-model) makes clear that most components have a {target namespace}. Most components corresponding to representations within a given [<schema>](#element-schema) element information item will have a {target namespace} which corresponds to the `targetNamespace`attribute.

Since the empty string is not a legal namespace name, supplying an empty string for `targetNamespace`is incoherent, and is *not*the same as not specifying it at all. The appropriate form of schema document corresponding to a [·schema·](#key-schema) whose components have no [{target namespace}](#ed-target_namespace) is one which has no `targetNamespace`attribute specified at all.

**Note:**[[XML Namespaces 1.1]](#ref-xml-namespaces-1.1) discusses only instance document syntax for elements and attributes; it therefore provides no direct framework for managing the names of type definitions, attribute group definitions, and so on. Nevertheless, the specification applies the target namespace facility uniformly to all schema components, i.e. not only declarations but also definitions have a {target namespace}.
Although the example schema at the beginning of this section might be a complete XML document, [<schema>](#element-schema) need not be the document element, but can appear within other documents. Indeed there is no requirement that a schema correspond to a (text) document at all: it could correspond to an element information item constructed 'by hand', for instance via a DOM-conformant API.

Aside from [<include>](#element-include) and [<import>](#element-import), which do not correspond directly to any schema component at all, each of the element information items which may appear in the content of [<schema>](#element-schema) corresponds to a schema component, and all except [<annotation>](#element-annotation) are named. The sections below present each such item in turn, setting out the components to which it corresponds.

##### <a id="refSchemaConstructs"></a>3.17.2.1 References to Schema Components

Reference to schema components from a schema document is managed in a uniform way, whether the component corresponds to an element information item from the same schema document or is imported ([References to schema components across namespaces (<import>) (§4.2.6)](#composition-schemaImport)) from an external schema (which may, but need not, correspond to an actual schema document). The form of all such references is a [·QName·](#gloss-QName).

<a id="gloss-QName"></a>[Definition:]A **QName**is a name with an optional namespace qualification, as defined in [[XML Namespaces 1.1]](#ref-xml-namespaces-1.1). When used in connection with the XML representation of schema components or references to them, this refers to the simple type [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) as defined in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2). For brevity, the term [·QName·](#gloss-QName) is also used to refer to [·actual values·](#key-vv) in the value space of the [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) simple type, which are [expanded names](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) with a <a id="q-local"></a>[Definition:]**local name**and a <a id="q-uri"></a>[Definition:]**namespace name**.

<a id="gloss-NCName"></a>[Definition:]An **NCName**is a name with no colon, as defined in [[XML Namespaces 1.1]](#ref-xml-namespaces-1.1). When used in connection with the XML representation of schema components in this specification, this refers to the simple type [NCName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NCName) as defined in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2).

**Note:**It is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) whether a schema processor supports the definitions of [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-names11/#NT-QName) and [NCName](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-names11/#NT-NCName) found in [[XML Namespaces 1.1]](#ref-xml-namespaces-1.1) or those found in [[Namespaces in XML 1.0]](#ref-xml-namespaces-1.0) or both.
<a id="key-resolve"></a>[Definition:] A [·QName·](#gloss-QName) in a schema document **resolves to**a component in a schema if and only if in the context of that schema the QName and the component together satisfy the rule [QName resolution (Schema Document) (§3.17.6.2)](#src-resolve). A [·QName·](#gloss-QName) in an input document, or a pair consisting of a local name and a namespace name, **resolves to**a component in a schema if and only if in the context of that schema the QName (or the name + namespace pair) and the component together satisfy the rule [QName resolution (Instance) (§3.17.6.3)](#cvc-resolve-instance).

In each of the XML representation expositions in the following sections, an attribute is shown as having type `QName`if and only if it is interpreted as referencing a schema component.

Example
```
<xs:schema xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"
            xmlns:xhtml="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml"
            xmlns="http://www.example.com"
            targetNamespace="http://www.example.com">
  . . .

  <xs:element name="elem1" type="Address"/>

  <xs:element name="elem2" type="xhtml:blockquote"/>

  <xs:attribute name="attr1"
                type="xsl:quantity"/>
  . . .
</xs:schema>
```

The first of these is most probably a local reference, i.e. a reference to a type definition corresponding to a [<complexType>](#element-complexType) element information item located elsewhere in the schema document, the other two refer to type definitions from schemas for other namespaces and assume that their namespaces have been declared for import. See [References to schema components across namespaces (<import>) (§4.2.6)](#composition-schemaImport) for a discussion of importing.
##### <a id="sec-refs-from-elsewhere"></a>3.17.2.2 References to Schema Components from Elsewhere

<a id="anchor5167a1"></a> The names of schema components such as type definitions and element declarations are not of type [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID): they are not unique within a schema, just within a symbol space. This means that simple fragment identifiers using these names will not work to reference schema components from outside the context of schema documents.

The preferred way to refer to schema components is to use [[XML Schema: Component Designators]](#ref-scds), which defines a mechanism to designate schema components using the `xscd()`[[XPointer]](#ref-xpointer) scheme.

Alternatively, references to specific element information items in the schema document can be interpreted as to their corresponding schema documents. This can be done using mechanisms supported by [[XPointer]](#ref-xpointer), for example, by using shorthand pointers. It is a matter for applications to specify whether they interpret these references as being to the relevant element information item (i.e. without special recognition of the relation of schema documents to schema components) or as being to the corresponding schema component.

#### <a id="coxr.schemas"></a>3.17.3 Constraints on XML Representations of Schemas

None as such.

#### <a id="sec-del-cvc-resolve-instance"></a>3.17.4 Validation Rules for Schemas as a Whole

None as such.

#### <a id="sec-sic-schema-all"></a>3.17.5 Schema Information Set Contributions

##### <a id="sec-sic-schema"></a>3.17.5.1 Schema Information

<a id="sic-schema"></a>**Schema Information Set Contribution: Schema Information**
Schema components provide a wealth of information about the basis of [·assessment·](#key-va), which can often be useful for subsequent processing. Reflecting component structure into a form suitable for inclusion in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) is the way this specification provides for making this information available.Accordingly, <a id="key-iso"></a>[Definition:] by an **item isomorphic**to a component is meant an information item whose type is equivalent to the component's, with one property per property of the component, with the same name, and value either the same atomic value, or an information item corresponding in the same way to its component value, recursively, as necessary.The [·validation root·](#key-vr) has the following properties:PSVI Contributions forelement or attribute information items
**<a id="e-schema_information"></a>[schema information]**
: A set of **namespace schema information**information items, one for each namespace name which appears as the {target namespace} of any schema component in the schema used for that assessment, and one for [·absent·](#key-null) if any schema component in the schema had no {target namespace}. Each **namespace schema information**information item has the following properties and values: PSVI Contributions fornamespace schema information information items
**<a id="nsi-schema_namespace"></a>[schema namespace]**
: A namespace name or [·absent·](#key-null).

**<a id="nsi-schema_components"></a>[schema components]**
: A (possibly empty) set of schema component information items, each one an [·item isomorphic·](#key-iso) to a component whose {target namespace} is the sibling [[schema namespace]](#nsi-schema_namespace) property above, drawn from the schema used for [·assessment·](#key-va).

**<a id="nsi-schema_documents"></a>[schema documents]**
: A (possibly empty) set of **schema document**information items, with properties and values as follows, for each schema document which contributed components to the schema, and whose `targetNamespace`matches the sibling [[schema namespace]](#nsi-schema_namespace) property above (or whose `targetNamespace`was [·absent·](#key-null) but that contributed components to that namespace by being [<include>](#element-include)d by a schema document with that `targetNamespace`as per [Assembling a schema for a single target namespace from multiple schema definition documents (<include>) (§4.2.3)](#compound-schema)): PSVI Contributions forschema document information items
**<a id="sd-document_location"></a>[document location]**
: Either a URI reference, if available, otherwise [·absent·](#key-null)

**<a id="sd-document"></a>[document]**
: A document information item, if available, otherwise [·absent·](#key-null).

The [[schema components]](#nsi-schema_components) property is provided for processors which wish to provide a single access point to the components of the schema which was used during [·assessment·](#key-va). Lightweight processors are free to leave it empty, but if it *is*provided, it must contain at a minimum all the top-level (i.e. named) components which actually figured in the [·assessment·](#key-va), either directly or (because an anonymous component which figured is contained within) indirectly.
##### <a id="sec-sic-id"></a>3.17.5.2 ID/IDREF Table

<a id="sic-id"></a>**Schema Information Set Contribution: ID/IDREF Table**
In the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) a set of **ID/IDREF binding**information items is associated with the [·validation root·](#key-vr):PSVI Contributions forelement information items
**<a id="e-ii_table"></a>[ID/IDREF table]**
: A (possibly empty) set of **ID/IDREF binding**information items, as specified below.
