For compatibility reasons, such content models are still allowed, but any element instance which matches the wildcard is required to have a governing type definition compatible with the type assigned by the local element declarations matched by the element's expanded name.
2. The elements [<complexType>](#element-complexType) and [<complexContent>](#element-complexContent) are now forbidden to have different values for the `mixed`attribute.
3. The constraint [Element Declarations Consistent (§3.8.6.3)](#cos-element-consistent) now takes open content into account. This change, together with the explicit exclusion of skip wildcards from the constraint, resolves issue [5940 Element Declarations Consistent](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=5940).
4. The rules for restriction of complex types containing local elements with conditional types have been simplified; this resolves issue [12185 Conditional Type Assignment and substitutability](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=12185).
5. The text has been revised to correct the terminology used to describe correct XPath expressions and to specify that it is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) when type errors in XPath expressions are detected and whether they are treated as static or dynamic errors. This change resolves issue [11073 Type errors during XPath evaluation](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=11073).
6. The description of assertions has been revised to make it explicit that the data model instance constructed for testing assertions has a parentless element node as its root, and not an element node with a document node as its parent. This change resolves issue [12127 "Root element" in assertion testing](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=12127).
#### <a id="ch_id"></a>G.1.7 ID, IDREF, and related types

ID, IDREF, and related types:
1. Certain constraints involving [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID) have been extended to include lists of [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID) and unions including [ID](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#ID). See e.g. [Constraints on Attribute Declaration Schema Components (§3.2.6)](#coss-attribute).
2. An element may now have multiple attributes of type `xs:ID`. Elements have always been able to have multiple children of type `xs:ID`, but XSD 1.0 forbad multiple attributes of this type for compatibility with XML DTDs. (Schemas intended to be translatable into DTD form should still avoid the practice.) This change should make it easier for XML vocabularies to support both existing ID attributes and `xml:ID`.
3. The validation rules for values of type `xs:IDREF`, `xs:ENTITY`, or `xs:ENTITIES`are now enforced on default values.
4. Elements and attributes of type `xs:ID`may now have default or fixed values. XSD 1.0 had forbidden this, for compatibility with XML DTDs.
5. The text has been revised to ensure that the special validation rules for `xs:ID`, `xs:IDREF`, `xs:IDREFS`, `xs:ENTITY`, and `xs:ENTITIES`apply not only to the built-in types themselves but also to types derived from them by restriction, list construction, union, and extension. This resolves issue [10662 Should IDREFS and ENTITIES be magic types?](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=10662).
#### <a id="ch_std"></a>G.1.8 Simple type definitions

Changes involving simple type definitions and related constraints:
1. A new type definition called `anyAtomicType`has been introduced into the type hierarchy between `anySimpleType`and all the *atomic*built-in type definitions. See [Built-in Simple Type Definitions (§3.16.7)](#builtin-stds).
2. An error in version 1.0 of this specification relating to the construction of union types from other union types has been corrected. Unions may now appear as members of other unions, and all restrictions of unions are correctly enforced; the rule [Type Derivation OK (Simple) (§3.16.6.3)](#cos-st-derived-ok) has been modified to ensure that member types are not [·validly substitutable·](#key-val-sub-type) for facet-restricted unions. As a result, `xsi:type`may no longer be used, as in XSD 1.0, to subvert restrictions of union types.
3. The requirement that a facet value be a "valid restriction" of another, in the context of simple type restriction, has been clarified.
4. No union type may be a member of its own transitive membership, nor may any type derived from the union. (XSD 1.0 forbad union datatypes to be members of other unions and thus had no need to forbid this explicitly.)
5. Since not all datatypes have a defined canonical representation for all of their values, appeals to the canonical forms of values have been eliminated.
6. Changes have been made to ensure that the descriptions of the [Simple Type Definition](#std) component and of [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType) agree in all details with those of [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2).
7. Equality and identity of lists have been clarified.
8. The fact that [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType) is its own base type [has been clarified](#anchor6204) (addresses issue [6204 anyType/ur-Type: inconsistent whether it has a base-type](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6204)).
9. Enumerations, value constraints, and identity constraints now accept *either*equal *or*identical values as the same. This change resolves issue [9196 Enumeration and NaN](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=9196)
10. Changes have been made in section [Constraints on XML Representations of Simple Type Definitions (§3.16.3)](#sec-src-simple-type) and section [Constraints on XML Representations of Complex Type Definitions (§3.4.3)](#sec-src-ct) to make explicit that XML representations of extension facets (facets in namespaces other than the Schema namespace) are allowed to appear more than once in restrictions of simple types. This resolves issue [11222 src-simple-type.1 should allow duplicate elements from "##other" namespaces](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=11222).
#### <a id="ch_elemdecl"></a>G.1.9 Element declarations

Changes to element declarations:
1. A new form of co-occurrence constraint has now been defined, by allowing the type assigned to element instances to be conditional on properties of the instance (typically attribute values). The addition of conditional type assignment has entailed a number of changes:
  1. Introduction of a [Type Table](#tt) property on element declarations, to hold a sequence of [Type Alternative](#tac)s (condition - selected-type pairs) and a default type definition.
  2. Constraints on that table: all types named in the table (including the default) must be [·validly substitutable·](#key-val-sub-type) for the declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition).
  3. Changes to the XML syntax and XML mapping rules to allow expression of conditional type bindings: the [<alternative>](#element-alternative) element is added, the content model of [<element>](#element-element) is changed.
  4. Validation rules for conditional types: the definition of [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) is adjusted to account for conditional type assignment.
  5. Rules for evaluating the conditional typing tests (more specifically, rules for constructing a temporary infoset and then building the XDM instance and evaluating the XPath expressions as defined elsewhere; priority of tests is given by document order / component order).
  6. PSVI changes to reflect details of the conditional typing: a {type alternative} property is added, and the discussion of [type fallback] now refers to the [·selected type definition·](#key-selected-type) rather than to either the declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) or to the [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) of the element information item
  7. Introduction of some terminology for discussing conditional types (define [·selected type definition·](#key-selected-type), [·conditionally selects·](#key-cta-select), [·validly substitutable as a restriction·](#key-val-sub-type-restricts)).
  8. Rules for checking type restriction in the presence of conditional types.
  9. Introduction of a special [·xs:error·](#key-error) type for use in identifying conditionally assigned types which violate restriction rules
  10. Miscellaneous supporting editorial changes.

2. Element declarations may now specify multiple substitution-group heads.
3. Abstract elements may now appear in substitution groups.
4. The treatment of type tables in [Element Declarations Consistent (§3.8.6.3)](#sec-cos-element-consistent) has been made more specific; this resolves issue [11076 Element Declarations Consistent: comparing type tables](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=11076).
#### <a id="ch_attrdecl"></a>G.1.10 Attribute declarations

Attributes:
1. Attribute declarations can now be marked [{inheritable}](#ad-inheritable) (see [Inherited Attributes (§3.3.5.6)](#Inherited_attributes)) and the values of inherited attributes are accessible in the XDM data model instance constructed for conditional type assignment (see [Type Alternatives (§3.12)](#cTypeAlternative)). Among other consequences, this allows conditional type assignment to be sensitive to the inherited value of the `xml:lang`attribute and thus to the language of the element's contents. This change was introduced to resolve issue [5003 Applicability of <alternative> element to xml:lang](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=5003), raised by the [W3C Internationalization Core Working Group](https://www.w3.org/International/core/).
2. The rules for default attribute values now refer to the [·effective value constraint·](#key-evc), rather than to the [[Value Constraint]](#vc_a); this resolves a bug in the handling of default values for global attribute declarations.
3. The text now makes clear that it is pointless (although not illegal) for schema documents to supply default or fixed values for `xsi:type`and other attributes in the namespace `http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance`, since they will not be applied.
4. Default attribute groups are now supported. The [<schema>](#element-schema) element can carry a `defaultAttributes`attribute, which identifies a named [Attribute Group Definition](#agd); each complex type defined in the schema document then automatically includes that attribute group, unless this is overridden by the `defaultAttributesApply`attribute on the [<complexType>](#element-complexType) element. Default attribute groups make it easier to specify attributes which should be accepted by every complex type in a schema (e.g. `xml:id`and `xml:lang`).
5. All wildcard unions are now expressible; this change resolves issue [6163 3.10.6.3 Attribute Wildcard Union](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6163).
6. As described in [XML Mapping Rule for Named Attribute Groups (§3.6.2.1)](#declare-attributeGroup-core), circular references from [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) to [<attributeGroup>](#element-simpleContent..attributeGroup) are now allowed; all attribute groups in the cycle of references are included.
#### <a id="ch_components"></a>G.1.11 Component structure

Changes in the structure of schema components:
1. Every component now has an {annotations} property whose value is a sequence of annotation elements and out-of-band attributes. See e.g. [The Complex Type Definition Schema Component (§3.4.1)](#Complex_Type_Definition_details).Annotations are no longer allowed to vary in the part of a content model shared by a complex type and its extensions. (This was never possible in components specified using schema documents, but was possible in "born-binary" components.)
2. A [{context}](#ctd-context) property has been defined for the definitions of complex and of simple types; this property simplifies testing for the identity of anonymous type definitions. See e.g. [The Complex Type Definition Schema Component (§3.4.1)](#Complex_Type_Definition_details). The {context} property replaces the {scope} property found in some earlier drafts of this document.
3. The [Schema](#s) component has an additional [{identity-constraint definitions}](#s-identity-constraint_definitions) property containing all the identity constraints in the corresponding schema. See [The Schema Itself (§3.17.1)](#Schema_details) and [XML Representations of Schemas (§3.17.2)](#declare-schema).
4. The underlying basis for the definition of all the different kinds of components has changed to make use of a regular and formal tabulation of their properties. This has been achieved by introducing *property records*wherever version 1.0 had complex property values. For example instead of describing the {scope} property as having "either ***global***or a complex type definition" for its value, a [Scope](#sc_e) property record is called for, which in turn has its own simple properties and values. See e.g. [The Element Declaration Schema Component (§3.3.1)](#Element_Declaration_details).
#### <a id="ch_sva"></a>G.1.12 The process of validation

The process of validation:
1. When an `xsi:type`attribute appears on an element and has a QName as its value, but the QName does not resolve to a known type definition, the element is assigned a "fallback" [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) for validation. The [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) will be the [·selected type definition·](#key-selected-type) or the declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition), if available, and [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType) otherwise. The validation process is now explicitly defined as including the validation of the element and its descendants; processors no longer have the implicit option of skipping the element and its descendants.
2. Element information items which match no particle in a content model are now to be validated using their [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype). Earlier drafts did not specify what happened in such cases.
3. [·Lax assessment·](#key-lva) is now required when an element information item to be validated has neither a [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) nor a [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem); also, lax assessment now requires that the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of the element be assessed as well. In XSD 1.0 and in earlier drafts, lax assessment was optional and did not require the recursive assessment of [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).
4. <a id="anchor11764d"></a> The text now specifies that if an element has an `xsi:type`attribute which does not [·resolve·](#src-resolve) to a type definition, then the `xsi:type`attribute is invalid.
5. The terminology of assessment has been changed to avoid the suggestion that an element information item can be [·strictly assessed·](#key-sva) without being [·assessed·](#key-va).
6. An anomaly in the definition of [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) has been corrected, to ensure that elements matching strict or lax wildcards have the appropriate global element declaration, if it exists, as their [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed). (Resolves issue [7913 Strange result from definition of governing element declaration](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=7913).)
7. The usage of the terms "[·validation·](#key-vn)" and "[·assessment·](#key-va)" has been clarified, and definitions of various forms of document validity have been added for the convenience of users of this specification; see [Schema-validity and documents (§2.5)](#sec-schema-validity-and-docs). (Resolves issues [5164 validation vs assessment](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=5164) and [6015 [schema11] valid (and its derivations) vs assessment as used in text](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6015).)
8. The definition of [Element Locally Valid (Type) (§3.3.4.4)](#cvc-type) has been changed to remove the rule that an element with an [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd) cannot be locally valid against any type definition other than the one specified in the instance. This resolves issue [11764 Unresolved xsi:type should not affect validity against a type](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=11764).
#### <a id="ch_psvi"></a>G.1.13 The ·post-schema-validation infoset·

Changes in the description of the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi):
1. The presentation of the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) has been simplified by removing the suggestion that the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) varies from processor to processor. Instead, the exposition now makes clearer that the body of information available in principle after schema-validity assessment is consistent across all processors; processors may make different subsets of the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) accessible to downstream applications, but when they do so the variation reflects the implementors' decisions about what information to expose, not variation in the information in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi).
2. Terms have been defined to describe different subsets of the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) which may be exposed by processors.
3. Provision is made for exposing the [·actual values·](#key-vv) of elements and attributes in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi), in the {schema actual value} property.
4. The [[element declaration]](#e-declaration) property and various other properties in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) are now described as being present in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) whenever a [·governing·](#key-governing) declaration and/or [·governing·](#key-governing) type definition is known for the item, instead of only when the item is valid.
5. When the [·governing·](#key-governing) type definition of an attribute or element information item is a list type whose item type is a union, the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) now includes the [·validating type·](#key-vtype) for each item in the list.
6. When default values are supplied for attributes with qualified names, [·namespace fixup·](#term-ns-fixup) is performed to ensure that the [[in-scope namespaces]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) property of the attribute's host element has an appropriate binding for the namespace name. It is [·implementation-dependent·](#key-impl-dep) whether [·namespace fixup·](#term-ns-fixup) is also performed on descendants of that element so as to retain consistency of the infoset. [·Namespace fixup·](#term-ns-fixup) may also be helpful if the defaulted value is of type [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) or [NOTATION](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NOTATION); it is [·implementation-dependent·](#key-impl-dep) whether fixup is performed for such values.
7. Annotations given in the XML form of identity-constraint declarations with `ref`attributes are now retained in the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) form of the containing element declaration. This change resolves issue [6144 annotation on IDC with a 'ref' attribute is lost](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6144).
#### <a id="ch_conformance"></a>G.1.14 Conformance

Changes to the description of conformance:
1. The set of conformance classes has been revised and clarified. Instead of "minimally conforming", "schema-document aware", and "Web-aware" processors, this specification now defines (in section [Conformance (§2.4)](#concepts-conformance)) conformance classes for [·general-purpose validators·](#key-gpval), [·general-purpose schema-validity assessors·](#key-gp-assessor), [·special-purpose validators·](#key-spval), and [·other special-purpose tools·](#key-othertools), together with terminology which may be useful in describing particular processors. This change resolves issue [7695 Conformance](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=7695).
2. A checklist has been included listing ways in which conforming processors may vary from each other, and terminology has been provided for some of the more important properties of conforming processors, in an attempt to make it easier for implementors to describe concisely which options their processors exercise, and easier for users to describe what kinds of processor they require.
3. The definition of must and [·error·](#dt-error) have been revised to specify that conforming processors must detect and report error in the schema or schema documents. The quality and detail of the error messages are not constrained.
4. Implementations are now allowed to support primitive datatypes and facets beyond those defined in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2).
5. The validity requirements for schema documents are stated more fully and correctly.
#### <a id="ch_schemacomp"></a>G.1.15 Schema composition

Schema assembly and composition:
1. The [<redefine>](#element-redefine) construct is [·deprecated·](#key-deprecated).
2. An [<override>](#element-override) construct has been defined; in some ways it resembles [<redefine>](#element-redefine), but imposes no constraints on the new definitions provided for components whose definitions are being overridden.
3. The discussion of [<include>](#element-include) and [<override>](#element-override) has been revised to eliminate an ambiguity in earlier versions of this spec regarding the meaning of cyclic dependencies formed by use of [<include>](#element-include) and [<override>](#element-override): such cyclic dependencies are now clearly allowed and have a well defined meaning.
4. When an `xsi:schemaLocation`attribute provides information about a schema document location for a particular namespace, it is no longer an error for it to be encountered after the first occurrence of an element or attribute information item in that namespace. Note, however, that if processing such an `xsi:schemaLocation`attribute causes new components to be added to the schema, then the new components cannot change the assessment outcome of any information items already seen before the element bearing the `xsi:schemaLocation`attribute.
5. No [<import>](#element-import) is needed in a schema document in order to refer to components in namespaces `http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema`or `http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance`. In XSD 1.0, the examples showed no such imports, but there was no rule making it legal to omit the [<import>](#element-import).
6. The handling of "chameleon" inclusion and redefinition in schema documents has been simplified. The new target namespace affects any component or property which would have the target namespace if the schema document specified one. This change makes it easier to write assertions in schema documents without a namespace which are intended to be included by schema documents with varying target namespaces.
7. Section [Identifying how to react to failure (§C.2.5)](#vsc_failure) has now been added, defining the terms **error**and **continue**for use in specifying what a processor does or should do when it seeks components for a given namespace in a given location but fails to find them there.
8. Schema processors are now explicitly recommended to provide a user option to control whether the processor attempts to dereference schema locations indicated in `schemaLocation`attributes in the instance document being validated; this resolves issue [5476 xsi:schemaLocation should be a hint, should be MAY not SHOULD](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=5476).
9. The discussion of `schemaLocation`information in [How schema definitions are located on the Web (§4.3.2)](#schema-loc) has been revised to try to make clearer the motivation for recommending user control over whether schema locations specified in the document instance should or should not be dereferenced. The new text describes some circumstances in which such schema locations typically should be dereferenced and some in which they should not, and attempts to set useful expectations for users and for implementors. These changes are intended to resolve issue [6655](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6655), raised by the W3C Web Accessibility Initiative's Protocols and Formats Working Group.
10. The discussion of schema import in [Licensing References to Components Across Namespaces (§4.2.6.1)](#composition-importLicenseReferences) now states more clearly that references to components in external namespaces are an error if the namespace is not imported; this change addresses issue [5779 QName resolution and xs:import](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=5779).
11. Some errors in the XSLT stylesheet of appendix [Transformation for Chameleon Inclusion (§F.1)](#chameleon-xslt) have been corrected (resolving issues [8573 F.1 Stylesheet for chameleons invalid](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=8573) and [8574 F.1 Stylesheet for chameleons incomplete](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=8574)).
12. The sections on the XML representation of components have been revised to say explicitly that the constraints and mapping rules defined there apply after, not before, pre-processing. This change resolves issues [11179 minor editorial improvement : parent schema components of a named model group](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=11179) and [11354 Mentions of "override" outside of the override section](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=11354)
#### <a id="ch_misc_sub"></a>G.1.16 Other substantive changes

Miscellaneous substantive changes:
1. The discussion of schema-validity assessment and the invocation of conforming processors has been revised; additional invocation patterns have been identified, and names have been given to the different methods of invoking a processor.
2. When an element cannot be strictly validated because no element declaration or type definition is available for it, fallback to lax validation (validating the element against the built-in type [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType)) is now required; in earlier drafts of this document, fallback to lax validation was optional.
3. The XML Representation Constraints no longer refer to the component level; this makes it possible to test a schema document in isolation to determine whether it conforms or fails to conform to these rules.
4. The constraints on the XML representation of schemas have been reformulated to allow them to be checked on schema documents in isolation, rather than requiring knowledge of the rest of the schema into which they will be embedded. The consequence is that some errors are caught not in the XML representation constraints but by having the XML mapping rules generate faulty components so that the error can be detected at the component level. These changes resolve issue [6235 Restriction from xs:anySimpleType](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6235).
5. The [<schema>](#element-schema) element is now declared with open content in the schema for schema documents. This change addresses issue [5930 defaultOpenContent in the S4SD](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=5930).
6. The setting `blockDefault="#all"`has been removed from the schema for schema documents; this change resolves issue [6120 Reconsider blockDefault=#all](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6120).
7. Namespace fixup is now explicitly required in some places where it is needed but was not mentioned before; these changes resolve issue [6445 Namespace fixup and default namespace](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6445) and issue [6465 Namespace fixup and inherited attributes](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6465).
8. In the DTD for schema documents ([DTD for Schemas (non-normative) (§I)](#nonnormative-schemaDTD)), an error has been corrected in the declaration of the `inheritable`attribute of the `xs:attribute`element (resolving issue [11070 DTD for schema documents: inheritable declared as %URIref](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=11070)).
9. In the schema for schema documents, an error in the declaration of `xs:group`within the named model group `xs:allModel`has been corrected to make the type of `xs:all`a legal restriction of its base type and make the schema for schema documents conformant to this specification. This resolves issue [11092 Error in S4SD: complexType name="all" is not a valid restriction](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=11092).
#### <a id="ch_clar"></a>G.1.17 Clarifications and editorial changes

Clarifications and other editorial changes:
1. Each named constraint is now given in a separate section, to simplify reference to them.
2. The XML mapping rules have been reorganized to make them more perspicuous.
3. The keywords defined by [[IETF RFC 2119]](#rfc-2119) to designate different levels of requirement have been marked up to distinguish more consistently between cases where their normative meaning is intended (e.g. "must") and cases where the words are used in its everyday sense without conformance implications (e.g. "must"). See [Documentation Conventions and Terminology (§1.5)](#intro-terminology).
4. A note has been added, warning that the **replace**and **collapse**values for whitespace handling are not a reliable means of neutralizing the effects of word-wrapping and pretty-printing of natural-language data and should not be used for that purpose.
5. Several minor corrections and clarifications have been made. The usage of some technical terminology has been clarified, normalized, and aligned where appropriate with the usage in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2). Conditionals using "if" have been rephrased to use "if and only if" where appropriate.
6. The title of the specification has been changed, and the language defined here is referred to as XSD, not using the name "XML Schema". This may help reduce confusion between the language defined here and the broader class of XML schema languages in general.
7. Conformance-related language has been reviewed to avoid confusion between the conformance-related usage of the verbs may, must, and should, and other usages.
8. Various new terms have been defined, and some existing terms have been redefined, to reduce confusion and improve legibility. In some cases, existing terms which were felt insufficiently informative have been replaced by new terms which may be more useful.
9. Following the example of XQuery 1.0 and XSLT 2.0, the terms "[·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined)" and "[·implementation-dependent·](#key-impl-dep)" have been defined and the two concepts distinguished. The appendix contains lists both of [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) and of [·implementation-dependent·](#key-impl-dep) features.
10. The term "context-determined-declaration" has been replaced with the term [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype); this resolves issue [4690 Editorial: 'context-determined declarations' needs more work](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=4690).
11. The namespace prefixes used to refer to well known namespaces have been changed and are now more consistent; this resolves issue [4316 Make sure namespace prefixes are used appropriately throughout structures](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=4316).
12. Numerous small changes were made in the interests of clarity, completeness, consistency, and precision, and to correct typographical errors. These changes resolve a number of issues, including: [5140 small editorial changes section 3.3](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=5140); [5148 inconsistent target ns description](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=5148); [5639 when is value V a valid restriction of value Y?](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=5639); [5800 Possibly revise list of required infoset properties](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=5800); [5916 Obsolete editorial note](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=5916); [5917 Typo in 3.1.1](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=5917); [5934 Typo concerning <simpleContent mixed="true">](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=5934); [6011 [schema11] base URI comments](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6011); [6156 Typo in 3.4.2](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6156); [6162 <anyAttribute> allows ##definedSibling](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6162); [6165 Constraints on XML representation of anyAttribute](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6165); [6166 Schema Component Model for Wildcards](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6166); [6167 Attribute Wildcard Intersection](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6167); [6170 Wildcards and defaultAttributes](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6170); [6175 Wildcard overlap](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6175); [6227 Type Derivation OK (simple)](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6227); and [6233 Wrong pointer for [nil] PSVI property](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=6233).
13. Discussions of global components now [take <redefine> into account](#anchor5918) (addresses issue [5918 Top level declarations](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=5918)).
14. The usage of "[·validation·](#key-vn)", "[·assessment·](#key-va)", and related terms has been clarified and made more consistent.
15. <a id="anchor10236"></a>It has been clarified that fields of identity constraint definitions can have complex types with simple content.
16. The text concerning `xsi:type`attributes and their effect has been clarified in [Element Locally Valid (Element) (§3.3.4.3)](#cvc-elt). In particular, the revisions attempt to make clearer just what happens in cases where the `xsi:type`attribute in the document instance cannot be used (because the type named is unknown, or otherwise not usable). This resolves issue [11219 Editorial revision of Element Locally Valid (Element)](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=11219).
17. The text has been revised to make clearer that declarations enclosed in an `xs:override`element are handled using the `elementFormDefault`and other schema-document-level defaults from the schema document being overridden, not the schema document containing the `xs:override`element. This resolves issue [10652 xs:override and document-level defaults](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=10652).
18. The constraints forbidding the use of special types as members of unions and item types for lists have been reformulated as part of the definition of the simple type definition component. This resolves issue [11103 Note in section 2.4.1 (Special datatypes as members of a union)](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=11103).
19. The treatment of elements in the Schema namespace occurring within [<annotation>](#element-annotation) elements has been clarified:
  - Such elements do not participate in the mapping from XML representations to components as defined in this specification ([The Mapping between XML Representations and Components (§3.1.3)](#mapping.xr.c), [The Annotation Schema Component (§3.15.1)](#Annotation_details)).
  - Such elements are not required to be valid or to satisfy the XML Representation rules as a condition of conformance for a schema document ([Conformance (§2.4)](#concepts-conformance), [The Annotation Schema Component (§3.15.1)](#Annotation_details)).
  - If such elements are invalid, the effect on schema component construction is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) ([The Annotation Schema Component (§3.15.1)](#Annotation_details), [Checklist of implementation-defined features (§E.1)](#impl-def-list)).
This change resolves issue [10125 Validation of the content of xs:annotation](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=10125).
20. A note has been added to the discussion of [<override>](#element-override) elements to make it easier to see how processors are expected to handle cycles in references from one schema document to another. This change resolves issue [12184 Circularity in xs:override](https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/show_bug.cgi?id=12184).
### <a id="issues"></a>G.2 Issues not resolved

It may be useful to mention some points where possible changes to the specification have been discussed, but on which no changes have, in the end, been made. In some cases, this resulted from the XML Schema Working Group's determination that no change was desirable; in other cases, there was no consensus on the desirability of change, or no consensus on what change should be made.

1. As noted above, some restrictions on ***all***groups have been relaxed; ***all***groups, however, must still be top-level groups; they are not allowed to appear within sequences or choice groups.
2. The namespace-related properties of the basic infoset are [·fixed up·](#term-ns-fixup) when attributes with qualified names are assigned default values. Other kinds of infoset fixup, however, are still not performed. Attributes of type `ID`, `IDREF`, `IDREFS`, and `NOTATION`do not have the same effect on the base infoset as they do if declared in a DTD. (An infoset-to-infoset transformation can be used to migrate the appropriate information into the base infoset.)
3. Some existing implementations (and specifications) assume that elements of type `xs:ID`uniquely identify themselves, instead of uniquely identifying their parent. This version of this specification reaffirms the existing rule, which is that elements and attributes of type `xs:ID`uniquely identify the parent element of the ID attribute or element.
4. The identity of components is still underspecified (although a number of points have been clarified, e.g. by the specification of the {scope} property), with the result that some schemas can be interpreted either as conformant or as non-conformant, depending on the interpretation of the specification's appeals to component identity.
5. The constraint [Element Declarations Consistent (§3.8.6.3)](#cos-element-consistent) has been recast, but not at the higher level of abstraction originally required and expected.
6. The account of schema composition given here has not eliminated all the uncertainties present in XSD 1.0; edge cases remain which different conformant implementations will treat differently.
7. A systematic tabulation of error conditions and definition of a new system of error codes was originally foreseen for XSD 1.1, but has not been completed for inclusion here. No further work in this area is currently anticipated.
## <a id="normative-glossary"></a>H Glossary (non-normative)

The listing below is for the benefit of readers of a printed version of this document: it collects together all the definitions which appear in the document above.

**L(D)**
: For any [Element Declaration](#ed)*D*, the language ***L*(*D*)**[·accepted·](#key-accept) by *D*is the set of all sequences of length 1 whose sole member is an element information item which [·matches·](#key-e-d-match)*D*.

**L(W)**
: For any [Wildcard](#w)*W*, the language ***L*(*W*)**[·accepted·](#key-accept) by *W*is the set of all sequences of length 1 whose sole member is an element information item which [·matches·](#key-wc-match)*W*.

**NCName**
: An **NCName**is a name with no colon, as defined in [[XML Namespaces 1.1]](#ref-xml-namespaces-1.1). When used in connection with the XML representation of schema components in this specification, this refers to the simple type [NCName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#NCName) as defined in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2)

**QName**
: A **QName**is a name with an optional namespace qualification, as defined in [[XML Namespaces 1.1]](#ref-xml-namespaces-1.1). When used in connection with the XML representation of schema components or references to them, this refers to the simple type [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) as defined in [[XML Schema: Datatypes]](#ref-xsp2)

**Schema Component Constraint**
: Constraints on the schema components themselves, i.e. conditions components must satisfy to be components at all. They are located in the sixth sub-section of the per-component sections of [Schema Component Details (§3)](#components) and tabulated in [Schema Component Constraints (§B.4)](#outcome-cos)

**Schema Information Set Contribution**
: Augmentations to [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi)s expressed by schema components, which follow as a consequence of [·assessment·](#key-va). They are located in the fifth sub-section of the per-component sections of [Schema Component Details (§3)](#components) and tabulated in [Contributions to the post-schema-validation infoset (§B.2)](#PSVI_contributions)

**Schema Representation Constraint**
: Constraints on the representation of schema components in XML beyond those which are expressed in [Schema for Schema Documents (Structures) (normative) (§A)](#normative-schemaSchema). They are located in the third sub-section of the per-component sections of [Schema Component Details (§3)](#components) and tabulated in [Schema Representation Constraints (§B.3)](#outcome-src)

**Type Definition Hierarchy**
: Except for [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType), every [·type definition·](#td) is, by construction, either a [·restriction·](#key-typeRestriction) or an [·extension·](#key-typeExtension) of some other type definition. The exception [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType) is a [·restriction·](#key-typeRestriction) of itself. With the exception of the loop on [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType), the graph of these relationships forms a tree known as the **Type Definition Hierarchy**with [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType) as its root

**Validation Rules**
: Contributions to [·validation·](#key-vn) associated with schema components. They are located in the fourth sub-section of the per-component sections of [Schema Component Details (§3)](#components) and tabulated in [Validation Rules (§B.1)](#validation_failures)

**Web-aware**
: **Web-aware**processors are network-enabled processors which are not only [·general-purpose·](#key-general-purpose) but which additionally must be capable of accessing schema documents from the World Wide Web as described in [Representation of Schemas on the World Wide Web (§2.8)](#web-representation) and [How schema definitions are located on the Web (§4.3.2)](#schema-loc).

**XSD schema**
: An **XSD schema**is a set of [·schema components·](#c)

**absent**
: Throughout this specification, the term **absent**is used as a distinguished property value denoting absence

**accept**
: A model group *G*is said to **accept**or **recognize**the members of *L*(*G*).

**accept**
: A particle *P*is said to **accept**or **recognize**the members of *L*(*P*). Similarly, a term *T***accepts**or **recognizes**the members of *L*(*T*).

**actual value**
: With reference to any string, interpreted as denoting an instance of a given datatype, the term **actual value**denotes the value to which the [lexical mapping](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-lexical-mapping) of that datatype maps the string.

**ancestor**
: The **ancestors**of a [·type definition·](#td) are its [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) and the [·ancestors·](#std-ancestor) of its [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition).

**annotation mapping**
: The **annotation mapping**of a set of element information items *ES*is a sequence of annotations *AS*, with the following properties: 1<a id="gl-am-set-exp.ann"></a> For every [<annotation>](#element-annotation) element information item among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of any element information item in *ES*, there is a corresponding [Annotation](#a) component in *AS*. **Note:**As noted above, the [{attributes}](#a-attributes) property of each [Annotation](#a) component includes all the attribute information items on the [<annotation>](#element-annotation) element itself, on the XML element which represents (and maps to) the component being annotated, and on any intervening XML elements, if those attribute information items have [[namespace name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute)s different from the XSD namespace.2<a id="gl-am-set-imp.ann"></a> If any element information item *E*in *ES*has any attribute information items *A*such that **all**of the following are true:2.1 *A*is in *E*.[[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).2.2 *A*.[[namespace name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute) is present and not the XSD namespace.2.3 *A*is not included in the [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) property of any annotation component described in clause [1](#am-set-exp.ann). then for each such *E*, an [Annotation](#a) component *C*will appear in *AS*, with *C*.[{application information}](#a-application_information) and *C*.[{user information}](#a-user_information) each being the empty sequence and *C*.[{attributes}](#a-attributes) containing all and only those attribute information items *A*among *E*.[[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element).3 *AS*contains no other [Annotation](#a) components.

**annotation mapping**
: The **annotation mapping**of a single element information item is the [·annotation mapping·](#key-am-set) of the singleton set containing that element.

**anyAtomicType**
: There is a further special datatype called *****anyAtomicType*****, a [·restriction·](#key-typeRestriction) of [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType), which is the [·base type definition·](#key-baseTypeDefinition) of all the primitive datatypes.

**anySimpleType**
: A special [·restriction·](#key-typeRestriction) of [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType), whose name is *****anySimpleType*****in the XSD namespace, is the root of the [·Type Definition Hierarchy·](#key-typeDefinitionHierarchy) for all simple type definitions. [·xs:anySimpleType·](#key-anySimpleType) has a lexical space containing all sequences of characters in the Universal Character Set (UCS) and a value space containing all [atomic values](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-atomic) and all finite-length lists of [atomic values](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-atomic).

**assessment**
: the word **assessment**is used to refer to the overall process of local validation, recursive determination of validation outcome, and infoset augmentation, i.e. as a short form for "[·schema-validity assessment·](#key-s-v-a)"

**assessor**
: A **schema-validity assessor**(or just **assessor**) is a processor which performs full or partial [·schema-validity assessment·](#key-s-v-a) of an XML instance document, element information item, or attribute information item, with reference to a conforming schema, and provides access to the entirety of the resulting [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi). The means by which an [·assessor·](#key-assessor) provides access to the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined).

**attributed to**
: During [·validation·](#key-vn) of an element information item against its (complex) [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem), associations between element and attribute information items among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) on the one hand, and the attribute uses, attribute wildcard, particles and open content property record on the other, are established. The element or attribute information item is **attributed to**the corresponding component.

**automatically known**
: A type about which a processor possesses prior knowledge, and which the processor can support without any declaration of the type being supplied by the user, is said to be **automatically known**to the processor.

**base particle**
: Let the **base particle**be the particle of the [{content type}](#ctd-content_type) of the [{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition).

**base information set properties**
: By **base information set properties**are meant the properties listed in [Required Information Set Items and Properties (normative) (§D)](#infoset).

**base type definition**
: The type definition used as the basis for an [·extension·](#key-typeExtension) or [·restriction·](#key-typeRestriction) is known as the **base type definition**of that definition

**basic particle**
: A **basic particle**is a [Particle](#p) whose [{term}](#p-term) is a [·basic term·](#key-basic-term).

**basic term**
: A **basic term**is an [Element Declaration](#ed) or a [Wildcard](#w).

**compete**
: Two [Particles](#p)*P*1 and *P*2 contained in some [Particle](#p)*P***compete**with each other if and only if some sequence *S*of element information items has two [·paths·](#key-path) in *P*which are identical except that one path has *P*1 as its last item and the other has *P*2.

**competing paths**
: Two (or more) [·paths·](#key-path) of a sequence *S*in a [Particle](#p)*P*are **competing paths**if and only if they are identical except for their final items, which differ.

**complete path**
: For a model group *M*and a sequence *S*in *L*(*M*), the path of *S*in *M*is a **complete path**; prefixes of complete paths which are themselves not complete paths are **incomplete paths**.

**component name**
: Declarations and definitions may and in some cases must have and be identified by **name**s, which are NCNames as defined by [[XML Namespaces 1.1]](#ref-xml-namespaces-1.1)

**conditionally selects**
: Given a [Type Table](#tt)*T*and an element information item *E*, *T***conditionally selects**a type *S*for *E*in the following way. The [{test}](#tac-test) expressions in *T*'s [{alternatives}](#tt-alternatives) are evaluated, in order, until one of the [Type Alternative](#tac)s [·successfully selects·](#key-cta-ta-select) a type definition for *E*, or until all have been tried without success. If any [Type Alternative](#tac)[·successfully selects·](#key-cta-ta-select) a type definition, none of the following [Type Alternative](#tac)s are tried. Then the type *S***conditionally selected**for *E*by *T*is as described in the appropriate **case**among the following:1<a id="gl-hack-to-force-template-match-2"></a> If at least one [Type Alternative](#tac) in *T*.[{alternatives}](#tt-alternatives)[·successfully selects·](#key-cta-ta-select) a type definition for *E*, then *S*is the type definition selected by the first such [Type Alternative](#tac). 2 If no [Type Alternative](#tac) in *T*.[{alternatives}](#tt-alternatives)[·successfully selects·](#key-cta-ta-select) a type definition, then *S*is *T*.[{default type definition}](#tt-default_type_definition).[{type definition}](#tac-type_definition).

**constructed**
: Datatypes can be **constructed**from other datatypes by ***restricting***the value space or lexical space of a [{base type definition}](#std-base_type_definition) using zero or more [Constraining Facet](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#f)s, by specifying the new datatype as a ***list***of items of some [{item type definition}](#std-item_type_definition), or by defining it as a ***union***of some specified sequence of [{member type definitions}](#std-member_type_definitions).

**contains**
: A model group **contains**the components which it either [·directly contains·](#key-contain-dgp) or [·indirectly contains·](#key-contain-igx).

**contains**
: A particle **contains**the components which it either [·directly contains·](#key-contain-dpt) or [·indirectly contains·](#key-contain-ipx).

**content model**
: A particle can be used in a complex type definition to constrain the [·validation·](#key-vn) of the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of an element information item; such a particle is called a **content model**

**context-determined declaration**
: During [·validation·](#key-vn), associations between element and attribute information items among the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) on the one hand, and element and attribute declarations on the other, are also established. When an item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to an [·element particle·](#key-ep), then it is associated with the declaration which is the [{term}](#p-term) of the particle. Similarly, when an attribute information item is [·attributed to·](#key-att-to) an [Attribute Use](#au), then the item is associated with the [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) of that [Attribute Use](#au). Such declarations are called the **context-determined declarations**.

**declaration**
: **declaration**components are associated by (qualified) name to information items being [·validated·](#key-vn)

**declared entity name**
: A string is a **declared entity name**if and only if it is equal to the [[name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.entity.unparsed) of some unparsed entity information item in the value of the [[unparsedEntities]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.document) property of the document information item at the root of the infoset containing the element or attribute information item whose [·normalized value·](#key-nv) the string is.

**default binding**
: When a sequence of element information items **ES**is [·locally valid·](#loc-locallyValid-seq) with respect to a [Content Type](#ct)**CT**or when a set of attribute information items **AS**satisfies clause [2](#c-aam) and clause [3](#c-ra) of [Element Locally Valid (Complex Type) (§3.4.4.2)](#cvc-complex-type) with respect to a [Complex Type Definition](#ctd), there is a (partial) functional mapping from the element information items *E*in the sequence *ES*or the attribute information items in *AS*to a **default binding**for the item, where the default binding is an [Element Declaration](#ed), an [Attribute Use](#au), or one of the keywords ***strict***, ***lax***, or ***skip***, as follows: 1 When the item has a [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed), the default binding is that [Element Declaration](#ed).2 When the item has a [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad) and it is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to an [Attribute Use](#au), the default binding is that [Attribute Use](#au). 3 When the item has a [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad) and it is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to an attribute wildcard, the default binding is an [Attribute Use](#au) whose [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) is the [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad), whose [{value constraint}](#au-value_constraint) is [·absent·](#key-null), and whose [{inheritable}](#au-inheritable) is the [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad)'s [{inheritable}](#ad-inheritable) (the other properties in the [Attribute Use](#au) are not relevant). 4 When the item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a ***strict***[·wildcard particle·](#key-wp) or attribute wildcard or an [Open Content](#oc) with a ***strict***[Wildcard](#w) and it does not have a [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) or a [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad), then the default binding is the keyword ***strict***.5 When the item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a ***lax***[·wildcard particle·](#key-wp) or attribute wildcard or an [Open Content](#oc) with a ***lax***[Wildcard](#w) and it does not have a [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) or a [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad), then the default binding is the keyword ***lax***.6 When the item is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) to a ***skip***[·wildcard particle·](#key-wp) or attribute wildcard or an [Open Content](#oc) with a ***skip***[Wildcard](#w) then the default binding is the keyword ***skip***.

**defaulted attribute**
: A **defaulted attribute**belonging to an element information item *E*[·governed by·](#key-governing-type-elem) a complex type *T*is any [Attribute Use](#au)*U*for which **all**of the following are true:1 *U*is a member of *T*.[{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses). 2 *U*.[{required}](#au-required) = ***false***. 3 *U*'s [·effective value constraint·](#key-evc) is not [·absent·](#key-null). 4 *U*.[{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) is not one of the [Attribute Declaration](#ad)s from [Built-in Attribute Declarations (§3.2.7)](#builtin-ads). 5 *U*.[{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) does not match any of the attribute information items in *E*.[[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) as per clause [2.1](#c-ctma) of [Element Locally Valid (Complex Type) (§3.4.4.2)](#cvc-complex-type) above.

**definition**
: **definition**components define internal schema components that can be used in other schema components

**definition of anyType**
: A special complex type definition, (referred to in earlier versions of this specification as 'the ur-type definition') whose name is ***anyType***in the XSD namespace, is present in each [·XSD schema·](#key-schema). The **definition of ***anyType*****serves as default type definition for element declarations whose XML representation does not specify one

**derived**
: If a type definition *D*can reach a type definition *B*by following its base type definition chain, then *D*is said to be **derived**from *B*.

**directly contains**
: A model group **directly contains**the particles in the value of its [{particles}](#mg-particles) property.

**directly contains**
: A particle **directly contains**the component which is the value of its [{term}](#p-term) property.

**effective value constraint**
: The **effective value constraint**of an attribute use *U*is *U*.[{value constraint}](#au-value_constraint), if present, otherwise *U*.[{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration).[{value constraint}](#ad-value_constraint), if present, otherwise the **effective value constraint**is [·absent·](#key-null).

**element particle**
: An **element particle**is a [Particle](#p) whose [{term}](#p-term) is an [Element Declaration](#ed).

**element substitution group**
: Through the mechanism of **element substitution groups**, XSD provides a more powerful model than DTDs do supporting substitution of one named element for another

**equivalent type alternative**
: Any [Type Alternative](#tac) is **equivalent**to itself. Otherwise, any [Type Alternative](#tac)*T1*is **equivalent**to a different [Type Alternative](#tac)*T2*if and only if *T1*.[{test}](#tac-test) and *T2*.[{test}](#tac-test) are true for the same set of input element information items and *T1*.[{type definition}](#tac-type_definition) and *T2*.[{type definition}](#tac-type_definition) accept the same set of input information items as valid. In the general case, equivalence and non-equivalence can be difficult to establish. It is [·implementation-defined·](#key-impl-defined) under just what conditions a processor detects that two type alternatives are equivalent, but all processors must detect *T1*and *T2*as equivalent if **all**of the following are true:1 *T1*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{namespace bindings}](#x-namespace_bindings) and *T2*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{namespace bindings}](#x-namespace_bindings) have the same number of [Namespace Binding](#nb)s, and for each entry in *T1*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{namespace bindings}](#x-namespace_bindings) there is a corresponding entry in *T2*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{namespace bindings}](#x-namespace_bindings) with the same [{prefix}](#nb-prefix) and [{namespace}](#nb-namespace). 2 *T1*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{default namespace}](#x-default_namespace) and *T2*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{default namespace}](#x-default_namespace) either are both [·absent·](#key-null) or have the same value. 3 *T1*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{base URI}](#x-base_URI) and *T2*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{base URI}](#x-base_URI) either are both [·absent·](#key-null) or have the same value. 4 *T1*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{expression}](#x-expression) and *T2*.[{test}](#tac-test).[{expression}](#x-expression) have the same value. 5 *T1*.[{type definition}](#tac-type_definition) and *T2*.[{type definition}](#tac-type_definition) are the same type definition. A processor may treat two type alternatives as non-equivalent if they do not satisfy the conditions just given and the processor does not detect that they are nonetheless equivalent.

**equivalent type table**
: A [Type Table](#tt)*T1*is **equivalent**to a [Type Table](#tt)*T2*if and only if **all**of the following are true:1 *T1*.[{alternatives}](#tt-alternatives) has the same length as *T2*.[{alternatives}](#tt-alternatives) and their corresponding entries are [·equivalent·](#key-equiv-ta). 2 *T1*.[{default type definition}](#tt-default_type_definition) and *T2*.[{default type definition}](#tt-default_type_definition) are [·equivalent·](#key-equiv-ta).

**extension**
: A complex type definition which allows element or attribute content in addition to that allowed by another specified type definition is said to be an **extension**

**field subset of XPath**
: The subset of XPath defined in [Fields Value OK (§3.11.6.3)](#c-fields-xpaths) is called the **field subset**of XPath.

**final**
: the complex type is said to be **final**, because no further [·derivations·](#key-derived) are possible

**full instance subset**
: The **full instance subset**of the PSVI includes almost all properties defined by this specification as applying to element and attribute information items, but excludes schema components. It consists of the [·instance-validity subset·](#dt-instance-validity_subset), plus the following properties for elements:

**general-purpose**
: A **general-purpose**processor is a [·validator·](#key-validator) or [·assessor·](#key-assessor) which accepts schemas represented in the form of XML documents as described in [Layer 2: Schema Documents, Namespaces and Composition (§4.2)](#layer2).

**governing**
: The declaration associated with an information item, if any, and with respect to which its validity is [·assessed·](#key-va) in a given assessment episode is said to **govern**the item, or to be its **governing**element or attribute declaration. Similarly the type definition with respect to which the type-validity of an item is assessed is its **governing**type definition.

**governing attribute declaration**
: In a given schema-validity [·assessment·](#key-va) episode, the [·governing·](#key-governing) declaration of an attribute (its **governing attribute declaration**) is the first of the following which applies: 1<a id="gl-c-ad"></a>A declaration which was stipulated by the processor (see [Assessing Schema-Validity (§5.2)](#validation_outcome)).2 Its [·context-determined declaration·](#key-dd);3<a id="gl-c-adbyr"></a>A declaration [·resolved·](#cvc-resolve-instance) to by its [[local name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute) and [[namespace name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute), provided the attribute is not [·skipped·](#key-skipped) and the processor has not stipulated a type definition at the start of [·assessment·](#key-va). If none of these applies, the attribute has no [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad) (or, in equivalent words, the [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad) is [·absent·](#key-null)).

**governing element declaration**
: The **governing element declaration**of an element information item *E*, in a given schema-validity [·assessment·](#key-va) episode, is the first of the following which applies: 1<a id="gl-elem-stipulated"></a>A declaration stipulated by the processor (see [Assessing Schema-Validity (§5.2)](#validation_outcome)).2<a id="gl-elem-cdd"></a>*E*'s [·context-determined declaration·](#key-dd).3<a id="gl-elem-wc-split"></a>A declaration [·resolved·](#cvc-resolve-instance) to by *E*'s [[local name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and [[namespace name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), provided that *E*is [·attributed·](#key-att-to) either to a ***strict***[·wildcard particle·](#key-wp) or to a ***lax***[·wildcard particle·](#key-wp). 4<a id="gl-elem-wc"></a>A declaration [·resolved·](#cvc-resolve-instance) to by *E*'s [[local name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and [[namespace name]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element), provided that **none**of the following is true: 4.1<a id="gl-no-id-if-skipped"></a>*E*is [·skipped·](#key-skipped)4.2 the processor has stipulated a type definition for *E*4.3 a [·non-absent·](#key-null)[·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) exists for *E*If none of these applies, *E*has no [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) (or, in equivalent words, *E*'s [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) is [·absent·](#key-null)).

**governing type definition**
: The **governing type definition**of an element information item *E*, in a given schema-validity [·assessment·](#key-va) episode, is the first of the following which applies: 1<a id="gl-stipulated-plus-xsitype"></a>An [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd) which [·overrides·](#key-overrides) a type definition stipulated by the processor (see [Assessing Schema-Validity (§5.2)](#validation_outcome)).2<a id="gl-stipulated"></a>A type definition stipulated by the processor (see [Assessing Schema-Validity (§5.2)](#validation_outcome)).3<a id="gl-governing-plus-xsitype"></a>An [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd) which [·overrides·](#key-overrides) the [·selected type definition·](#key-selected-type) of *E*.4<a id="gl-gov-no-xsitype"></a>The [·selected type definition·](#key-selected-type) of *E*.5 The value [·absent·](#key-null) if *E*is [·skipped·](#key-skipped). 6 An [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd) which [·overrides·](#key-overrides) the [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype). 7 The [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype).8 An [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd). If none of these applies, there is no [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem) (or, in equivalent words, it is [·absent·](#key-null)).

**governing type definition**
: The **governing type definition**of an attribute, in a given schema-validity [·assessment·](#key-va) episode, is the first of the following which applies: 1 A type definition stipulated by the processor (see [Assessing Schema-Validity (§5.2)](#validation_outcome)).2 The [{type definition}](#ad-type_definition) of the [·governing attribute declaration·](#key-governing-ad). If neither of these applies, there is no [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-att) (or, in equivalent words, it is [·absent·](#key-null)).

**grouping**
: A **grouping**of a sequence is a set of sub-sequences, some or all of which may be empty, such that each member of the original sequence appears once and only once in one of the sub-sequences and all members of all sub-sequences are in the original sequence.

**implementation-defined**
: An **implementation-defined**feature or behavior may vary among processors conforming to this specification; the precise behavior is not specified by this specification but must be specified by the implementor for each particular conforming implementation.

**implementation-dependent**
: An **implementation-dependent**feature or behavior may vary among processors conforming to this specification; the precise behavior is not specified by this or any other W3C specification and is not required to be specified by the implementor for any particular implementation.

**implicitly contains**
: A list of particles **implicitly contains**an element declaration if and only if a member of the list contains that element declaration in its [·substitution group·](#key-eq)

**indirectly contains**
: A model group **indirectly contains**the particles, groups, wildcards, and element declarations which are [·contained·](#key-contain-xpx) by the particles it [·directly contains·](#key-contain-dgp).

**indirectly contains**
: A particle **indirectly contains**the particles, groups, wildcards, and element declarations which are contained by the value of its [{term}](#p-term) property.

**initial value**
: the **initial value**of some attribute information item is the value of the [[normalized value]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.attribute) property of that item. Similarly, the **initial value**of an element information item is the string composed of, in order, the [[character code]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.character) of each character information item in the [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of that element information item

**instance-specified type definition**
: An **instance-specified type definition**is a type definition associated with an element information item in the following way: 1<a id="gl-xsi-type-att"></a>Among the element's attribute information items is one named `xsi:type`.2 The [·normalized value·](#key-nv) of that attribute information item is a qualified name [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to the built-in [QName](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#QName) simple type, as defined by [String Valid (§3.16.4)](#cvc-simple-type). 3 The [·actual value·](#key-vv) (a [·QName·](#gloss-QName)) [·resolves·](#cvc-resolve-instance) to a type definition. It is this type definition which is the **instance-specified type definition**.

**instance-validity subset**
: The **instance-validity subset**of the PSVI consists of the [·root-validity subset·](#dt-root-validity_subset), plus the following properties on elements, wherever applicable:

**item isomorphic to a component**
: by an **item isomorphic**to a component is meant an information item whose type is equivalent to the component's, with one property per property of the component, with the same name, and value either the same atomic value, or an information item corresponding in the same way to its component value, recursively, as necessary

**laxly assessed**
: The schema validity of an element information item *E*is said to be **laxly assessed**if and only if **both**of the following are true:1 *E*has neither a [·governing element declaration·](#key-governing-ed) nor a [·governing type definition·](#key-governing-type-elem).2 *E*is locally [·validated·](#key-vn) with respect to [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType) as defined in [Element Locally Valid (Type) (§3.3.4.4)](#cvc-type), and the schema-validity of *E*'s [[attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) and [[children]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) is assessed as described in clause [2](#c-deepa) and clause [3](#c-deepe) of [Schema-Validity Assessment (Element) (§3.3.4.6)](#cvc-assess-elt).

**locally declared type**
: Every [Complex Type Definition](#ctd) determines a partial functional mapping from element or attribute information items (and their [expanded names](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname)) to type definitions. This mapping serves as a **locally declared type**for elements and attributes which are allowed by the [Complex Type Definition](#ctd).

**locally declared type**
: For a given [Complex Type Definition](#ctd)*CTD*and a given attribute information item *A*, the [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) of *A*within *CTD*is the appropriate **case**among the following:1<a id="gl-dummy3"></a>**If ***CTD*is [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType) , **then **[·absent·](#key-null). 2 **If ***A*has the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) as some attribute declaration *D*which is the [{attribute declaration}](#au-attribute_declaration) of some [Attribute Use](#au) contained by *CTD*'s [{attribute uses}](#ctd-attribute_uses) , **then **the [{type definition}](#ad-type_definition) of *D*. 3 **otherwise **the [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) of *A*within *CTD*.[{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition).

**locally declared type**
: For a given [Complex Type Definition](#ctd)*CTD*and a given element information item *E*, the [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) of *E*within *CTD*is the appropriate **case**among the following:1<a id="gl-dummy1"></a>**If ***CTD*is [·xs:anyType·](#key-anyType) , **then **[·absent·](#key-null). 2 **If ***E*has the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) as some element declaration *D*which is [·contained·](#key-contain-xpx) by *CTD*'s content model, whether [·directly·](#key-contain-dpt), [·indirectly·](#key-contain-ipx), or [·implicitly·](#key-impl-cont) , **then **the declared [{type definition}](#ed-type_definition) of *D*. 3 **otherwise **the [·locally declared type·](#key-ldtype) of *E*within *CTD*.[{base type definition}](#ctd-base_type_definition).

**locally valid**
: A sequence of element information items is **locally valid**with respect to a [Content Type](#ct) if and only if it satisfies [Element Sequence Locally Valid (Complex Content) (§3.4.4.3)](#cvc-complex-content) with respect to that [Content Type](#ct).

**locally valid**
: A sequence *S*of element information items is **locally valid**against a particle *P*if and only if *S*has a [·validation-path·](#key-vpath) in *P*. The set of all such sequences is written *V*(*P*).

**match**
: An element information item *E***matches**an [Element Declaration](#ed)*D*if and only if **one**of the following is true:1 *E*and *D*have the same [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname),2 The [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname) of *E*[·resolves·](#cvc-resolve-instance) to an element declaration *D*2 which is [·substitutable·](#key-validly-sub) for *D*.

**match**
: An [expanded name](https://www.w3.org/TR/2004/REC-xml-names11-20040204/#dt-expname)*E***matches**an [·NCName·](#gloss-NCName)*N*and a namespace name *NS*(or, equivalently, *N*and *NS***match***E*) if and only if all of the following are true:
- The local name of *E*is identical to *N*.
- Either the namespace name of *E*is identical to *NS*, or else *E*has no namespace name (*E*is an unqualified name) and *NS*is [·absent·](#key-null).

**match**
: An element information item *E***matches**a [Wildcard](#w)*W*(or a [·wildcard particle·](#key-wp) whose [{term}](#p-term) is *W*) if and only if *E*is locally [·valid·](#key-vn) with respect to *W*, as defined in the validation rule [Item Valid (Wildcard) (§3.10.4.1)](#cvc-wildcard).

**match**
: Two namespace names *N*1 and *N*2 are said to **match**if and only if they are identical or both are [·absent·](#key-null).

**namespace fixup**
: When default values are supplied for attributes, **namespace fixup**may be required, to ensure that the [·post-schema-validation infoset·](#key-psvi) includes the namespace bindings needed and maintains the consistency of the namespace information in the infoset. To perform namespace fixup on an element information item *E*for a namespace *N*: 1 If the [[in-scope namespaces]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of *E*binds a prefix to *N*, no namespace fixup is needed; the properties of *E*are not changed.2 Otherwise, first select some prefix *P*which is not bound by the [[in-scope namespaces]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of *E*(the choice of prefix is [·implementation-dependent·](#key-impl-dep)).3 Add an entry to the [[in-scope namespaces]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of *E*binding *P*to *N*.4 Add a namespace attribute to the [[namespace attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of *E*.5 Maintain the consistency of the information set by adjusting the namespace bindings on the descendants of *E*. This may be done in either of two ways:5.1 Add the binding of *P*to *N*to the [[in-scope namespaces]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of all descendants of *E*, except where that binding is overridden by another binding for *P*.5.2 Add to the [[namespace attributes]](https://www.w3.org/TR/xml-infoset/#infoitem.element) of each child of *E*a namespace attribute which undeclares the binding for *P*(i.e. a namespace attribute for prefix *P*whose [·normalized value·](#key-nv) is the empty string), unless that child already has a namespace declaration for prefix *P*. Note that this approach is possible only if [[XML Namespaces 1.1]](#ref-xml-namespaces-1.1) is in use, rather than [[Namespaces in XML 1.0]](#ref-xml-namespaces-1.0).The choice between the two methods of maintaining consistency in the information set is [·implementation-dependent·](#key-impl-dep).

**nilled**
: An element information item *E*is **nilled**with respect to some element declaration *D*if and only if **all**of the following are true:1 *E*has `xsi:nil`= ***true***. 2 *D*.[{nillable}](#ed-nillable) = ***true***.

**normalized value**
: The **normalized value**of an element or attribute information item is an [·initial value·](#key-iv) which has been normalized according to the value of the [whiteSpace facet](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#rf-whiteSpace), and the values of any other [pre-lexical facets](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-pre-lexical), associated with the simple type definition used in its [·validation·](#key-vn). The keywords for whitespace normalization have the following meanings: preserveNo normalization is done, the whitespace-normalized value is the [·initial value·](#key-iv)replaceAll occurrences of `#x9`(tab), `#xA`(line feed) and `#xD`(carriage return) are replaced with `#x20`(space).collapseSubsequent to the replacements specified above under **replace**, contiguous sequences of `#x20`s are collapsed to a single `#x20`, and initial and/or final `#x20`s are deleted. Similarly, the **normalized value**of any string with respect to a given simple type definition is the string resulting from normalization using the [whiteSpace facet](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#rf-whiteSpace) and any other [pre-lexical facets](https://www.w3.org/TR/2012/REC-xmlschema11-2-20120405/datatypes.html#dt-pre-lexical), associated with that simple type definition.

**overlay**
: Given two sets of facets *B*and *S*, the result of **overlaying***B*with *S*is the set of facets *R*for which **all**of the following are true:1<a id="gl-fr1"></a>Every facet in **S**is in **R**.2<a id="gl-fr2"></a> Every facet in **B**is in **R**, unless it is of the same kind as some facet in **S**, in which case it is not included in **R**. 3 Every facet in **R**is required by clause [1](#fr1) or clause [2](#fr2) above.

**override**
: An [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd)*S*is said to **override**another type definition *T*if and only if all of the following are true: 1 *S*is the [·instance-specified type definition·](#key-itd) on some element information item *E*.
